Iran–Israel · Conflict Series
Conflict Analysis · Middle East · April 2024

Operation True Promise I

Iran's first-ever direct attack on Israel, 13–19 April 2024. A 320+ projectile barrage in retaliation for Israel's airstrike on the Iranian consulate in Damascus — and Israel's limited counter-strike on Isfahan.
320+
Projectiles launched
~99%
Intercept rate (coalition)
1
Serious injury (Israeli Bedouin girl, 7)
3
Israeli missiles (Apr 19 counterstrike)
Chronological event log
Context — Trigger event
1
Apr
Israel Airstrike · Trigger Event
Israel strikes Iranian consulate complex in Damascus — triggering True Promise
Israeli airstrike destroys the Iranian consulate annex in Damascus, killing IRGC Quds Force commander Gen. Mohammad Reza Zahedi, his deputy, five IRGC officers, and two civilians. Iran vows direct retaliation — the first time it pledges to strike Israel from Iranian soil. Operation True Promise is planned in the two weeks that follow.
📍Iranian Consulate Annex, Damascus, Syria (33.51°N, 36.29°E) ⚔️Precision air-launched munitions (F-35I) 💀16 killed incl. Gen. Zahedi (IRGC Quds Force commander)
Phase I — UAV Swarm Wave (Hours 1–9)
13
Apr
Iran (IRGC) Offensive · UAV Swarm — Wave 1
170 Shahed-136/238 loitering munitions launched — saturating Iron Dome
Iran's IRGC launches 170 Shahed-136 "kamikaze" drones plus 185 faster jet-propelled Shahed-238 variants from Iranian and Iraqi territory. Tactic: overwhelm Iron Dome and David's Sling with a slow-flying saturation wave to clear the path for faster ballistic and cruise missiles. IDF detects the swarm ~5 hours into its flight and issues public alert at 11:30 PM. US forces in the Red Sea and Jordan begin intercepting UAVs long before they reach Israeli airspace.
📍Israel nationwide airspace (31.0°N, 35.0°E) ⚔️Shahed-136 (loitering munition) + Shahed-238 (jet-propelled variant) 🔢Launched: 170 · Intercepted coalition-wide: ~150 💀0 killed
Phase II — Cruise Missile & Ballistic Strike (~2:00 AM Apr 14)
13–14
Apr
Iran (IRGC) Offensive · Cruise Missiles — Wave 2
30 Paveh cruise missiles fired; UK & France assist intercepts over Syria/Iraq
Iran fires 30 Paveh land-attack cruise missiles as the second wave, timed to follow the UAV swarm. The Paveh (also designated Ya Ali) has a range of ~1,700 km and flies at low altitude to evade radar. UK and French air assets intercept several over Syria and northern Iraq before they reach Israeli airspace. David's Sling engages those that penetrate further. 5 of 30 evade interception and reach Israeli territory.
📍Israel nationwide + en route intercepts over Syria/Jordan ⚔️Paveh cruise missile (Ya Ali variant, ~1,700 km range) 🔢Launched: 30 · Intercepted: ~25 💀0 killed
13–14
Apr
Iran (IRGC) Offensive · Ballistic Missiles — Wave 3
120 Fateh-110 / Kheibar Shekan ballistic missiles target Nevatim & Ramon airbases
Iran's third and most consequential wave: 120 ballistic missiles — Fateh-110 (SRBM) and the more advanced Kheibar Shekan (MRBM, ~1,450 km range) — aimed specifically at Nevatim Airbase (home of Israel's F-35 fleet) and Ramon Airbase in the Negev. Arrow-3 intercepts some exo-atmospherically; others are engaged by Arrow-2 and David's Sling. Despite ~110 interceptions, nine missiles strike the two airbases. Five hit Nevatim: one craters a runway, others hit storage and empty facilities. Four hit Ramon. A 7-year-old Bedouin girl is seriously injured by shrapnel near Arad. Some missiles struck exactly at 1:20 AM — the hour of Soleimani's assassination in 2020, a deliberate symbolic gesture.
📍Nevatim AB, Negev (30.88°N, 34.82°E) · Ramon AB (30.78°N, 34.66°E) ⚔️Fateh-110 (SRBM) + Kheibar Shekan (MRBM); 60 tons explosive payload total 🔢Launched: 120 · Intercepted: ~110 · Impacted bases: 9 💀0 killed · 1 seriously injured (7yr-old girl, shrapnel near Arad) · 31 minor
Coalition Air Defense — Operation Iron Shield
13–14
Apr
Israel (IDF) Defensive · Exo-atmospheric intercept
Arrow-3 achieves first combat ballistic missile intercepts in space
Israel's Arrow-3 interceptor — designed for exo-atmospheric intercept of ballistic missiles during the boost/apogee phase — achieves its first-ever combat intercepts, destroying ballistic missiles in space before they re-enter the atmosphere. Approximately 30 intercepts credited to Arrow-3. Arrow-2 handles mid-course interceptions at lower altitudes. Together they form the top two tiers of Israel's "onion" defense. David's Sling and Iron Dome cover lower tiers.
📍Exo-atmospheric (above Israel, 31.5°N, 35.0°E) ⚔️Arrow-3 interceptor (hit-to-kill, exo-atmospheric) 🔢Intercepted: ~30 ballistic missiles
13–14
Apr
Israel (IDF) Defensive · Mid-tier intercept
David's Sling (Stunner) engages cruise missiles & medium-altitude ballistics
David's Sling fills the critical mid-tier gap between Arrow-3 (exo-atmospheric) and Iron Dome (short-range). Its Stunner interceptor engages cruise missiles and medium-to-high-altitude ballistic missiles. Approximately 40 intercepts credited across the overnight attack. First large-scale combat deployment of the system, which had limited prior combat use.
📍Central Israel (31.5°N, 34.8°E) ⚔️David's Sling / Stunner interceptor (endo-atmospheric, 300 km range) 🔢Intercepted: ~40
13–14
Apr
Israel (IDF) Defensive · Short-range intercept
Iron Dome Tamir interceptors engage terminal-phase UAVs & short-range threats
Iron Dome handles the lowest tier — terminal-phase UAVs and short-range threats that evade upper-tier intercepts. ~100 engagements credited to Iron Dome Tamir interceptors over the Tel Aviv district and central Israel. The sheer volume of Shahed drones (170+ slow-flying) is specifically designed to overwhelm this layer, though most are destroyed before reaching Israeli airspace by US and Jordanian assets further out.
📍Tel Aviv District (32.09°N, 34.78°E) ⚔️Iron Dome / Tamir interceptor (short-range, ~70 km) 🔢Intercepted: ~100
13–14
Apr
United States (USN) Defensive · Naval intercept
US Aegis warships fire SM-3 interceptors — Red Sea intercept zone
US Navy Aegis-equipped destroyers and cruisers operating in the Red Sea fire SM-3 Block IIA interceptors against Iranian ballistic missiles and UAVs transiting toward Israel from the south and east. The Intercept reports US forces "did most of the heavy lifting" against Iranian projectiles — intercepting more total threats than Israel itself. ~20 SM-3 intercepts credited. US involvement marks the first US active combat interception of Iranian missiles in defense of Israel.
📍Red Sea / Aegis intercept zone (20.0°N, 38.0°E) ⚔️SM-3 Block IIA (naval ABM, Aegis BMD) 🔢Intercepted: ~20
13–14
Apr
US / Jordan / UK / France Defensive · Forward intercept over Jordan
Patriot PAC-3 & allied air assets intercept over Jordanian airspace
US Patriot PAC-3 batteries based in Jordan engage Iranian UAVs and missiles transiting Jordanian airspace. Jordan's Royal Air Force also independently intercepts threats, and UK (Typhoons from Cyprus) and French (Rafales) join with BVR engagements over Syria and northern Iraq — notably the first coordinated multi-nation air defense operation of this kind in the Middle East. Jordan reports shrapnel falling on its territory from interception debris. ~15 Patriot intercepts credited to US assets; allied total exceeds 100 across all platforms.
📍Jordan airspace (31.24°N, 36.51°E) ⚔️Patriot PAC-3 (US) · Typhoon/Meteor AAM (UK) · Rafale/MICA (France) · RJAF fighters (Jordan) 🔢US Patriot intercepts: ~15 · Allied total: 100+
Phase III — Israeli Counterstrike (Apr 19)
19
Apr
Israel (IAF) Counterstrike · Deliberate restraint
Israel fires 3 precision missiles at Isfahan air defense radar — limited, symbolic response
At 5:23 AM IRST, the IAF launches a deliberately limited counterstrike: 3 air-launched precision munitions (reported Rampage-class) target an S-300 air defense radar site at Shekari Air Base near Isfahan — home to Iran's fleet of F-14 Tomcats and adjacent to nuclear facilities. The target is chosen for symbolic resonance (Isfahan hosts key nuclear infrastructure) while avoiding escalation. Satellite imagery confirms the radar battery is destroyed. Iran initially denies the strike; IRNA describes "minor explosions." A former IRGC commander later publicly acknowledges the strike in October 2024. US and its allies briefed in advance but do not participate. The limited scope is a deliberate signal: Israel "can reach Isfahan" without triggering a full exchange.
📍Shekari Air Base, Isfahan (32.65°N, 51.67°E) ⚔️Air-launched precision munition (reported Rampage anti-radiation missile) 🔢Launched: 3 · Target destroyed: S-300 air defense radar battery 💀0 killed (no escalation sought)

Iranian missiles / drones launched per day — Apr 2024
Apr 13–14
~355
170 Shahed-136/238 UAVs · 30 Paveh cruise missiles · 120 ballistic missiles
All launched in a single ~5-hour window (overnight Apr 13–14)
No further Iranian launches — both sides accepted de-escalation after Apr 19
Israeli strikes launched per day — Apr 2024
Apr 13–18
0 offensive
Apr 19
3
Apr 19: 3 precision missiles → 1 S-300 radar destroyed at Isfahan AB
Deliberately minimal — under US/G7 pressure to avoid escalation
Intended to demonstrate reach without triggering a second Iranian wave
Strategic context
First direct attack: True Promise was the first time in the 45-year history of the Islamic Republic that Iran directly attacked Israeli territory from Iranian soil — a historic escalation crossing a longstanding red line.
Iranian tactic: Deliberate three-wave saturation: slow UAVs first (to drain interceptors), then cruise missiles (evade radar with terrain-following), then ballistic missiles (fastest, hardest to intercept). Designed to overwhelm any single layer of defense.
US role: The Intercept reported US forces intercepted more Iranian weapons than Israel itself. First time the US directly engaged Iranian military assets in defense of Israel — a strategic threshold crossed.
Israeli restraint (Apr 19): Under pressure from Biden and G7 allies, Israel's response was deliberately minimal — 3 missiles, one radar destroyed. Intended to demonstrate reach without triggering escalation. Both sides accepted de-escalation.
Legacy: The intercept of Iran's S-300 batteries in October 2024 (Operation Days of Repentance) was made possible by intelligence gathered during Iron Shield. Lessons from True Promise I directly shaped the IAF's planning for Operation Rising Lion in June 2025.
Sources: Wikipedia (April 2024 Iranian strikes on Israel, 2024 Iran–Israel conflict), BESA Center, The Intercept, IDF spokesperson statements, US DoD, Congress.gov CRS. Some interception figures are approximate due to operational security restrictions.
Conflict Analysis · Middle East · 2025

The Twelve-Day War

Iran–Israel direct conflict, 13–24 June 2025. Israel's Operation Rising Lion, Iran's Operation True Promise III, and US intervention at Fordow, Natanz & Isfahan.
12
Days of combat
610+
Iranian dead (est.)
28
Israeli dead (est.)
1,550+
Iranian missiles & drones fired
3
US nuclear sites struck
Chronological event log
Phase I — Opening Salvo
13
Jun
IsraelOffensive · Air Campaign
Operation Rising Lion — Opening strikes on Iran
200+ IAF fighter jets launch 5 waves of airstrikes before dawn, targeting ~100 Iranian nuclear facilities, missile factories, military bases, and air defense systems. Mossad operatives simultaneously sabotage air defense networks from within. IRGC commander Hossein Salami killed. Natanz Nuclear Facility among primary targets. More than 330 munitions dropped.
📍Natanz, Tehran & facilities across 27 Iranian provinces ⚔️200+ F-35I/F-15 jets, precision munitions, Mossad sabotage 🛡Iranian AD degraded by Mossad prior to strikes
13
Jun
IranRetaliation · Wave 1
Operation True Promise III — First ballistic missile salvos hit Israeli cities
Iran retaliates within hours with ~50 ballistic missiles and drones targeting Tel Aviv district and population centers. Iron Dome and layered air defenses intercept most. One person killed, ~70 wounded. Opening wave of what becomes a sustained nightly barrage over 12 days.
📍Tel Aviv District, Israel-wide (31.77°N, 35.23°E) ⚔️~50 ballistic missiles + drones (Shahab/Fattah series) 🛡Iron Dome, David's Sling, Arrow-3 intercept most 💀1 killed, ~70 wounded
Phase II — Escalation & Economic Targets
14
Jun
IsraelOffensive · Infrastructure
Israel strikes Tehran's Shahran oil depot & 70+ Iranian air defense batteries
First Israeli strike on Iranian critical civilian infrastructure: Shahran oil depot in Tehran. IDF also reports destroying 70+ Iranian air defense batteries so far. Ongoing strikes continue against missile production facilities and IRGC assets.
📍Shahran Oil Depot, Tehran (35.69°N, 51.39°E) ⚔️Precision air-launched munitions 💀Multiple Iranian personnel killed
15
Jun
IranOffensive · Wave 2 (Major Barrage)
Deadliest single day — Mass barrage hits HaSharon, Tel Aviv, Weizmann Institute & Soroka Hospital
Iran's heaviest barrage: ~110 ballistic missiles in salvos of 30–50 striking HaSharon and Tel Aviv subdistricts, plus dedicated strikes on the Weizmann Institute of Science in Rehovot. A missile hits the Soroka Medical Center. Residential buildings struck in Ramat Gan (Diamond Exchange district). A drone intercepted near Be'er Sheva by Barak Magen naval AD. Iranian cluster missiles hit seven Israeli cities simultaneously.
📍HaSharon, Tel Aviv, Ramat Gan, Rehovot, Be'er Sheva ⚔️~111 ballistic missiles + drones; cluster munition variants 🛡Iron Dome intercepts most; Barak Magen intercepts Be'er Sheva drone 💀9 killed, ~259 wounded (deadliest day for Israel)
16
Jun
IranStrategic Strike · Energy Infrastructure
Haifa Oil Refinery struck — Israel's largest refinery shut down for two weeks
Iran retaliates for Shahran by striking the Haifa Refinery (Bazan Group), which supplies 65% of Israel's diesel, 59% of gasoline, and 52% of aviation kerosene. Three impact points confirmed inside the compound. Three workers killed. Facility offline until October. Estimated damages $150–200M.
📍Haifa Refinery (32.82°N, 35.02°E) ⚔️Ballistic missiles 💀3 workers killed; $150–200M damage; offline until Oct
Phase III — Sustained Exchange
18
Jun
IranOffensive · Wave 3
Multiple districts struck; Iran adapts tactics, hit rate rises from 8% to 16%
~20 ballistic missiles and 8 drone raids. Iran begins varying attack patterns and using more advanced longer-range missiles, improving its penetration rate over the course of the war. Multiple light injuries. Trump signals ambiguity: "nobody knows what I'm going to do."
📍Multiple Israeli districts ⚔️~20 ballistic missiles + 8 drone raids 💀Multiple light injuries
19
Jun
IranOffensive · Wave 4
Beersheba & multiple cities struck; Israeli interceptor stocks strained
~25–30 ballistic missiles and 5 drone raids. 200+ wounded, no confirmed deaths. Israeli officials float "regime change" as desirable outcome. WSJ reports Israeli interceptor inventory under strain.
📍Beersheba & surroundings (31.25°N, 34.79°E) ⚔️~25–30 ballistic missiles + 5 drone raids 💀200+ wounded, no confirmed deaths
Phase IV — US Entry & Endgame
21–22
Jun
US + IsraelOffensive · Nuclear Sites
US B-2 bombers strike Natanz, Fordow & Isfahan with GBU-57 bunker-busters
US B-2 Spirit stealth bombers deploy GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrator bunker-busting bombs against Iran's three hardened nuclear sites — including the deeply buried Fordow enrichment facility, unreachable by Israeli munitions alone. Trump declares "major combat operation." IAEA later assesses nuclear program set back by "months." Iran's Wave 22 hits Tel Aviv and Haifa on the same day: 10 of 27 missiles penetrate — Iran's most effective single day of the conflict.
📍Natanz (33.72°N, 51.73°E) · Fordow (34.88°N, 50.99°E) · Isfahan (32.65°N, 51.68°E) ⚔️B-2 Spirit + GBU-57 MOPs (US); F-35I precision munitions (Israel) 💀Multiple senior IRGC killed; 10/27 Iranian missiles hit Israel same day
23
Jun
IsraelOffensive · Regime Target
Israeli airstrike on Evin Prison, Tehran — 79+ killed
Israel strikes Evin Prison in Tehran — Iran's most notorious detention facility housing political prisoners, activists, and dual nationals. 79+ killed. Strike is widely reported as symbolic/strategic, internationally condemned. Iranian air defenses largely degraded at this point.
📍Evin Prison, Tehran (35.75°N, 51.38°E) ⚔️Air-launched precision munitions 💀79+ killed
23
Jun
IranRetaliation · US Forces
Iran fires 14 missiles at Al Udeid Air Base, Qatar (US forces)
In direct retaliation for US nuclear strikes, Iran launches 14 ballistic missiles at Al Udeid — the largest US base in the Middle East (~10,000 personnel). US/Qatari AD intercepts ~13. One missile impacts. Zero casualties. Qatar-mediated ceasefire diplomacy begins immediately.
📍Al Udeid Air Base, Qatar (25.12°N, 51.31°E) ⚔️14 ballistic missiles 🛡US/Qatar AD intercept ~13 of 14 💀0 casualties
23–24
Jun
CeasefireDiplomatic · US-Brokered
Trump announces ceasefire; fighting ends after 12 days
Trump announces ceasefire via social media evening of 23 June. Ceasefire takes effect 24 June, mediated by US and Qatar. Initial violations by both sides; Trump expresses frustration live on TV; Israel carries out reduced retaliatory strike on radar near Tehran — then stands down. Ceasefire holds. Iran claims "great victory"; Netanyahu declares "historic victory." Total cost: 610+ Iranian dead, 28 Israeli dead, $5.9B to Israel's economy.
📍US–Qatar mediated; effective June 24, 2025 💀Total: 610+ Iranian, 28 Israeli dead · $5.9B cost to Israel

★ Jun 13: 200+ jets · 330+ munitions · ~100 targets — largest IAF strike ever (at the time)
508+ total Israeli strikes over 12 days · Evin Prison struck Jun 23 (79+ killed)
US B-2 / GBU-57 strikes on Fordow, Natanz, Isfahan Jun 21–22 (Op. Midnight Hammer)
Strategic context
Trigger: June 12 IAEA declares Iran non-compliant with nuclear obligations for first time in 20 years. Iran announces secret enrichment site. Israel launches pre-dawn strikes the next morning.
Iranian arsenal depletion: Iran began with est. 2,500–3,000 ballistic missiles. 500+ fired, ~1,000 destroyed by Israeli strikes on launchers, ~250 launchers eliminated.
Israeli defense: "Onion" layered system — Arrow-3, Arrow-2, David's Sling, Iron Dome, Barak — intercepts ~90%. Interceptor stocks significantly strained by conflict's end.
US weapons: B-2 Spirit + GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators — the only munition capable of destroying Fordow. First-ever US strike on another state's nuclear program.
Aftermath: 21,000+ detained in Iran. Several Kurds executed. Iran signals possible NPT exit. Ceasefire holds through late 2025. New US-Israel strikes on Iran begin February 28, 2026.
Sources: Wikipedia (Twelve-Day War), ACLED, Al Jazeera, FDD Long War Journal, Brookings, CFR, Al Jazeera Centre for Studies, Congress.gov CRS, The Forward. Data compiled June 2025 – March 2026. Some casualty figures remain disputed.
Conflict Analysis · Middle East · February–March 2026 · Ongoing

Operations Epic Fury / Roaring Lion

Joint US-Israeli offensive against Iran, launched 28 February 2026. Targets: Iranian regime leadership, nuclear infrastructure, missile arsenal, naval assets, and IRGC command network. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed. Iran retaliates across the Middle East and Gulf.
5,300+
Iranian killed (Day 21)
14
Israelis killed (12 civ., 2 mil.)
290+
US soldiers WIA
13,000+

12
countries struck by Iran

Sources: CSIS "3.7 Billion: Estimated Cost of Epic Fury's First 100 Hours" · ABC / CNN / NPR reporting · DOGE.gov · American Enterprise Institute · NPR contract analysis
Chronological event log — ONGOING · Updated daily
⚠ LIVE CONFLICT NOTICE: This tab covers an active, ongoing military operation. All casualty figures, strike counts, and territorial claims are preliminary and subject to revision. Multiple claims are contested between US/Israeli and Iranian sources. Do not treat any figure as confirmed without independent verification. Last data pull: April 10, 2026 · 01:14 UTC.
Context — Road to conflict
6–26
Feb
Diplomacy Nuclear Talks · Collapse
US-Iran nuclear talks in Oman & Geneva collapse — strike decision made
6 Feb: US and Iran hold indirect nuclear talks in Muscat, Oman. 17 Feb: Resumed in Geneva. 22 Feb: Further session. 26 Feb: Oman FM says "significant progress" reached; Iran reportedly agrees to cap enrichment and allow IAEA verification. Despite this, Trump — briefed by envoys Witkoff and Kushner that Iran's latest counter-proposal was "intended only to buy time" — authorises the strike operation. Iran had rebuilt nuclear infrastructure after Operation Midnight Hammer (June 2025). Trump's 10-day deadline to reach a deal expires on 27 February.
Sources: Al Jazeera Timeline Times of Israel Liveblog GlobalSecurity.org
Phase I — Opening strikes (28 Feb, ~1:15 AM EST / 9:45 AM IRST)
28
Feb
US + Israel Offensive · Opening Salvo — Ops Epic Fury / Roaring Lion
Operations Epic Fury & Roaring Lion launched — ~200 IAF jets open with "Genesis"; US strikes separately via Tomahawks, B-2 bombers, HIMARS & LUCAS drones
Joint US-Israeli campaign begins at 9:45 AM Tehran time, but the two nations execute distinct and separately attributed strike packages. Israel (Op. Roaring Lion): The IAF's opening phase — codenamed "Genesis" — is described by the IDF as "the largest strike sortie in the history of the Israeli Air Force." Roughly 200 IAF fighter jets drop 550+ munitions on ~500 targets, focusing on ballistic missile launchers and air defense systems in western and central Iran. IAF Commander Maj. Gen. Tomer Bar tells pilots afterwards: "We have just concluded one of the most significant flyovers in the history of the Israeli Air Force." US (Op. Epic Fury): A separate but coordinated package — B-2 Spirit stealth bombers fly from Whiteman AFB, Missouri and strike hardened missile facilities with 2,000 lb. bombs; US Navy warships launch Tomahawk cruise missiles; HIMARS fires from land; and Task Force Scorpion Strike deploys LUCAS (Low-cost Unmanned Combat Attack System) drones in their first-ever combat use. Two carrier strike groups, USS Abraham Lincoln and USS Gerald R. Ford, support the operation. Combined strikes hit: Tehran (government quarter, presidential palace, intelligence and defense ministries, Khamenei compound), Mehrabad, Isfahan nuclear site, Fordow, Natanz, Qom, Karaj, Kermanshah, Tabriz, Bandar Abbas and Konarak Naval Base (satellite imagery confirms vessels burning). Cyber operations simultaneously disrupt Iranian internet and communications. CENTCOM calls it "the largest regional concentration of American military firepower in a generation."
📍Tehran, Isfahan, Fordow, Natanz, Qom, Karaj, Kermanshah, Tabriz, Bandar Abbas, Konarak ⚔️IAF: ~200 F-35I/F-15 jets, 550+ munitions, ~500 targets · US: B-2 bombers · Tomahawk TLAM · HIMARS · LUCAS drones (first combat use) 🛡Iranians' AD largely pre-suppressed; CENTCOM intercepts hundreds of retaliatory drones/missiles 💀201 Iranian civilians killed, 747 injured (Red Crescent, Day 1) · 40+ senior officials killed (CBS intelligence source)
Sources: Wikipedia USNI News CSIS Aviation Week IDF/Breitbart (IAF figures) FlightGlobal (LUCAS)
28
Feb
Israel (IAF) Targeted Assassination · Supreme Leader
Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei killed — his compound reduced to rubble
An Israeli airstrike hits Khamenei's Tehran compound and official residence. Satellite imagery confirms the building reduced to rubble with black smoke rising. Netanyahu says there are "growing signs" of his death, later confirmed. Khamenei (86) is killed along with his daughter, son-in-law, and grandson. Iranian state media initially denies then confirms death late on 28 Feb / early 1 March. Also confirmed killed: Security Council Secretary Ali Shamkhani, Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh, SPND chief Hossein Jabal Amelian, former SPND head Reza Mozaffari-Nia, and military office chief Mohammad Shirazi. On 1 March, Chief of Staff Gen. Abdolrahim Mousavi also confirmed dead by Iranian state media. Iran declares 40 days of national mourning. Grand Ayatollah Makarem Shirazi (99) issues fatwa calling Khamenei's death "the religious duty of all Muslims" to avenge.
📍Khamenei compound, Tehran (35.69°N, 51.42°E) ⚔️Israeli precision air-launched munitions (confirmed by satellite, Israeli intelligence) 💀Khamenei + family + 6+ top security officials confirmed killed; thousands of IRGC personnel casualties reported
Sources: NPR CBS News Times of Israel Al Jazeera
Phase II — Iranian retaliation (28 Feb, hours after strikes)
28
Feb
Iran (IRGC) Retaliation · Israel
Iran fires missiles at Israel — residential building near Jerusalem struck; one woman killed
Iran launches ballistic missiles at Israeli population centers. A direct hit on a residential building in Beit Shemesh (near Jerusalem) kills one woman in her 50s and injures ~18. Separate missile strikes near Tel Aviv injure ~27 people (2 moderate, 25 mild). Another strike near Jerusalem injures ~20 (including a 10-year-old girl, critically). MDA dispatched to dozens of scenes across Israel. Total Day 1–2: 1 Israeli civilian killed, 121 injured across Israel. Israel's layered air defense intercepts most; IDF says more than 10 Iranian drones also intercepted.
📍Beit Shemesh, Tel Aviv area, Jerusalem area — Israel-wide ⚔️Ballistic missiles + drones 🛡Iron Dome, Arrow, David's Sling; CENTCOM defends regional bases 💀1 killed (Beit Shemesh), 121 injured across Israel (Day 1–2)
Sources: CBS News CNN Live
28
Feb
Iran (IRGC) Retaliation · Regional — US Bases & Gulf States
Iran strikes US bases across Gulf + UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Jordan, Saudi Arabia, Iraq
Iran launches one of the broadest regional retaliatory barrages in its history, targeting US military installations and neighbouring countries simultaneously. Key strikes confirmed: US 5th Fleet HQ, Manama Bahrain (smoke reported, no US casualties per CENTCOM); Al Dhafra AB, UAE; Al Udeid AB, Qatar (11 explosions heard over Doha, 16 Qatari injuries); Ali Al Salem AB, Kuwait (3 US soldiers KIA, 5 seriously wounded — CENTCOM confirms); Bahrain Crowne Plaza Hotel struck by drone (fire, guests evacuated); Dubai International Airport terminal damaged (4 workers injured, flights suspended indefinitely); Kuwait International Airport struck (drone, damage confirmed); Abu Dhabi — drone intercepted near Etihad Towers/Israeli embassy (debris injures a woman and child). Iran also fires on US assets in Jordan, Saudi Arabia, Iraq (2 PMF fighters killed, Jurf al-Sakhar). Oman's Duqm port struck by 2 drones (1 injured). Cyprus — British military bases targeted. Iranian missiles fired at Iraq and Syria.
📍Bahrain · UAE · Kuwait · Qatar · Jordan · Saudi Arabia · Iraq · Oman · Cyprus · Israel ⚔️165 ballistic missiles + 541 drones + 2 cruise missiles (UAE totals alone) · Wider totals still tallying 💀3 US KIA (Kuwait); 1 killed + 32 injured (Kuwait total); 3 killed + 58 injured (UAE); 16 injuries (Qatar); PMF 2+ KIA (Iraq)
Sources: Wikipedia USNI News Al Jazeera Live PBS News Live
28
Feb
Iran Economic Warfare · Strait of Hormuz
Strait of Hormuz closed — 150 oil tankers stranded; Dubai International Airport halted
Iran effectively closes the Strait of Hormuz, through which ~20% of global oil supply transits. 150 freight ships including oil tankers stall behind the strait. Dubai International Airport — the world's busiest for international travel — halts all flights indefinitely. Dubai World Central also closed. Qatar Airways, El Al, Emirates, and most major carriers cancel or suspend Middle East routes. Airspace closed across Israel, UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Iraq, Bahrain, southern Syria. Multiple governments issue evacuation orders for citizens in the region. Saudi Arabia condemns Iranian strikes as a breach of its neighbours' sovereignty.
📍Strait of Hormuz · Dubai International Airport · Regional airspace 💀Economic impact ongoing; oil markets disrupted; 150+ tankers stalled
Sources: Wikipedia PBS Live
Phase III — Day 2 escalation (1 March 2026)
1
Mar
Israel (IAF) Offensive · Wave 2 — "Heart of Tehran"
Israel strikes "heart of Tehran" in second wave — 30+ targets including missile arrays and AD systems
IDF announces first formal strikes under the "Operation Roaring Lion" name label for public communication. Israel strikes 30+ targets in western and central Iran targeting Iran's ballistic missile array and air defense systems. IDF states attacks will continue against "air defence installations, missile sites, military headquarters, and other regime targets." Strikes on Iraq also reported — Israeli attacks on military installations in Jurf al-Sakhar. Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf (highest-ranking official to appear on camera) vows "devastating blows" in retaliation.
📍Tehran (western & central) · Western Iran provinces · Iraq (Jurf al-Sakhar) ⚔️IAF precision munitions; targeting ballistic missile launchers and AD batteries
Sources: Al Jazeera Day 2 Fox News Live
1
Mar
Iran State Media Confirmation · Leadership Vacuum
Iran officially confirms Khamenei dead — 40 days mourning; Chief of Staff Mousavi also confirmed killed
Iranian state media officially confirms Khamenei's death early March 1, after initially denying it. Iranian President Pezeshkian issues a statement calling revenge "legitimate right and duty." Iran's National Security Council vows to "stab America in the heart." Chief of Staff Gen. Abdolrahim Mousavi confirmed killed by Iranian state media. Intelligence head Salah Asadi also confirmed killed. Leadership succession unclear — US intelligence had assessed likely successors will be hardline IRGC figures. Iraq announces 3 days of national mourning. Mourning crowds gather in Tehran; contrast with celebrations in Los Angeles and among Iranian diaspora. IAEA Board of Governors convenes emergency special session Monday (requested by Russia) to address strikes on nuclear sites.
📍Tehran — global diplomatic response 💀Khamenei + daughter + son-in-law + grandson + 8+ top security/military officials confirmed killed
Sources: Al Jazeera NYT Live CFR Analysis
Phase IV — Day 3 · Conflict Widens (2 March 2026)
2
Mar
Hezbollah · Lebanon Offensive · Second front opened
Hezbollah opens Lebanon front — rockets & drones on Israel; ceasefire declared over
Hezbollah, citing Khamenei's assassination, fires rockets and drones at a military base near Haifa — the first Hezbollah attack since the November 2024 ceasefire. Israel responds with strikes on Dahiyeh (Beirut's southern suburbs), 12+ explosions rock the capital, killing at least 31 and wounding 149. IDF strikes 70+ Hezbollah weapons storage facilities in southern Lebanon. Israel warns 40+ villages to evacuate; says no imminent ground invasion. IDF strikes Beirut again targeting a senior Hezbollah operative. Palestinian Islamic Jihad commander Adham al-Othman (41) killed in Dahiyeh. US officials tell MTV Lebanon the 2024 ceasefire is "over" — US will not intervene to stop Israeli strikes in Lebanon. Lebanese government bans Hezbollah's military activities and orders arrests.
📍Dahiyeh (Beirut) · southern & eastern Lebanon · northern Israel (near Haifa) 💀Lebanon: 31+ killed · 149 wounded (Lebanese Health Ministry)
Sources: Al Jazeera Times of Israel NPR
2
Mar
Kuwait · Friendly Fire Incident · Fratricide · All crew ejected safely
Kuwait mistakenly shoots down 3 US fighter jets; all 6 aircrew safely eject
CENTCOM confirms Kuwait's air defenses, overwhelmed by simultaneous Iranian barrages transiting their airspace, mistakenly engaged and destroyed three US fighter jets overnight. All six crew members ejected safely and were recovered. CENTCOM frames it as a tragic error given the extreme pace of the combat environment — hundreds of missiles and drones were in flight simultaneously. The incident exposes a critical fratricide risk in a multi-nation air defense environment with overlapping engagement zones and minimal coordination time windows. Aircraft types not publicly disclosed.
📍Kuwait airspace 💀3 US jets destroyed · 6 aircrew recovered · 0 KIA
Sources: Washington Post CBS News
2
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Expanded regional strikes
4th US KIA · US tanker struck in Bahrain · US Embassy Kuwait compound hit · Iran claims 27 bases struck
CENTCOM (7:30 AM ET) confirms a fourth US service member — wounded in Iran's initial retaliatory salvos — has died, bringing the total US KIA to four (all Kuwait-based sustainment unit). CENTCOM confirms 18 service members seriously wounded overall. US-flagged oil tanker Stena Imperative struck by two Iranian projectiles at Bahrain's Port of Manama — fire erupts, crew evacuates; fire later extinguished (Vanguard Tech / Reuters). Iran or Iran-backed strikes apparently hit the US Embassy compound in Kuwait. Iran's IRGC claims it has now struck 27 US military bases across the Middle East since operations began. Qatar's MoD reports Day 3 intercepts: 2 Iranian warplanes, 7 ballistic missiles, and 5 drones shot down. UAE air defenses intercept 9 ballistic missiles, 6 cruise missiles, and 148 drones on Day 3 (UAE MoD).
📍Kuwait (KIA · Embassy) · Bahrain Port (tanker) · Qatar · UAE 💀US: 4 KIA total · 18 seriously wounded (CENTCOM, 07:30 ET Mar 2) 🚢US tanker Stena Imperative struck; fire extinguished, crew evacuated
Sources: Euronews / CENTCOM CBS News Fox News
2
Mar
Diplomatic · Leadership Governance · Succession · Trump timeline
Iran stands up interim leadership council; UK Akrotiri base struck by Hezbollah drone; Trump signals 4–5 week campaign
Iran's President Pezeshkian announces an interim leadership council — himself, the judiciary head, and a Guardian Council member — has assumed the duties of Supreme Leader. Iran's Guardian Council says a new leader "must be determined as soon as possible." White House official tells Reuters: "new potential leadership in Iran has indicated they want to talk and eventually [Trump] will talk. For now, Operation Epic Fury continues unabated." UK RAF Akrotiri base in Cyprus hit by a Shahed drone assessed by Cypriot officials as Hezbollah-launched from Lebanon, flying at ultra-low altitude — limited damage, no casualties. Two further drones targeting Akrotiri intercepted same day. Trump at the White House says the operation projected 4–5 weeks but is "already substantially ahead of schedule." Defense Secretary Hegseth calls it "the most lethal, most complex, and most-precision aerial operation in history." Trump claims 49 senior Iranian leaders killed in opening strike and 10 Iranian naval vessels sunk.
📍Tehran · Akrotiri Cyprus · Washington DC 🕊Trump: "new leadership wants to talk" — no ceasefire; ops continue "with ferocious, unyielding resolve" 🛡Akrotiri: limited damage, 0 casualties · 2 further inbound drones intercepted
Sources: ABC News Times of Israel Reuters
2
Mar
Iran · Civilian Toll Reported · Civilian casualties escalate
Iranian civilian death toll reaches 555+; 20 killed Niloofar Square Tehran; ~150 killed in Minab girls school strike
Iran's Red Crescent Society reports at least 555 killed in US-Israeli strikes — up from 201 on Day 1. Iranian state media reports 20 civilians killed at Tehran's Niloofar Square on March 2. Iranian-state-affiliated Nour News reports ~150 students killed and 95 injured at a girls' primary school (264 enrolled) in Minab, Hormozgan Province — three strike waves hit the building according to Iran's Education Ministry. Reuters footage showed rescue crews digging through wreckage overnight; NBC News could not independently confirm figures. US and Israeli officials have not publicly addressed the Minab school strike. UNICEF and UNESCO express concern. The IDF reports its air force has dropped more than 1,200 munitions across 24 of Iran's 31 provinces. Iran's internet remains largely collapsed due to cyber operations.
📍Niloofar Square, Tehran · Minab, Hormozgan Province · 130+ cities across Iran 💀Iran total: 555+ killed · 747+ injured (Red Crescent · figures still rising) 💀Minab school: ~150 killed · ~95 injured (Nour News / unverified by Western outlets)
Sources: NPR Washington Post Wikipedia NBC News
Phase V — Day 4 · Overnight escalation (3 March 2026)
3
Mar
Iran · Overnight Strikes Offensive · Embassy + synagogue + Riyadh
US Embassy Riyadh struck; Beit Shemesh synagogue hit (9 killed); US KIA rises to 6; Golestan Palace damaged
Iranian drones strike the US Embassy compound in Riyadh overnight — two drones cause a "limited fire and minor material damage" (Saudi MoD); 8 drones total intercepted near Riyadh and Al-Kharj. The US Embassy confirms the attack and closes to the public until further notice. In Israel, an Iranian missile breaches air defenses and strikes a synagogue in Beit Shemesh, killing 9, leaving 11 missing, and injuring 51 — the single deadliest Iranian strike on Israeli civilians to date. US KIA rises to 6 as two additional service members die overnight (NBC NY / El-Balad). Trump vows to "avenge" American deaths and does not rule out ground troops in Iran "if necessary." CENTCOM confirms 1,250+ targets struck in the first 48 hours, including IRGC HQ, command nodes, ballistic missile sites, naval assets, and communications infrastructure. Semi-official Iranian Mehr News reports Iran's Golestan Palace — a UNESCO World Heritage Site — damaged in the strikes; museum artifacts were pre-moved to a secure vault. US Embassy Kuwait closes until further notice. State Dept orders non-emergency personnel to depart Bahrain, Jordan, and Iraq.
📍Riyadh (Embassy) · Beit Shemesh (synagogue) · Tehran (Golestan Palace) · Kuwait City (Embassy) 💀Israel: 9 killed · 11 missing · 51 injured (Beit Shemesh synagogue) · US total KIA: 6 🏛Golestan Palace (UNESCO) damaged · museum contents pre-evacuated (Mehr News — unverified by West)
Sources: CNN Times of Israel NBC News CBS News
3
Mar
Israel · IAF Offensive · Extensive new wave — Iran + Lebanon
IAF launches "extensive wave" of strikes against Iran and Hezbollah/Beirut simultaneously; 2,000+ bombs in 30 hrs confirmed
The Israeli Air Force announces it has "begun an extensive wave of strikes against the Iranian terror regime and the Hezbollah terrorist organization" — attacking both Iran and Beirut's southern suburbs simultaneously on March 3. Hezbollah overnight claims drone attacks on Israel: targeting Mount Meron air traffic control base, Ramat David Airbase, and an IDF base in the Golan Heights. IDF intercepts two Lebanese drones; no impacts in Israel. Sirens sound in Jerusalem and the Golan Heights. The IDF separately confirms that over 2,000 bombs were dropped on Iran in the first 30 hours of Operation Roaring Lion, and that air supremacy over Tehran was achieved on Day 1. IDF says Iran before the June 2025 war was on pace to build a stockpile of 8,000 missiles, had ~3,000 at the start of that conflict, and had since been producing 12 per month with "clear acceleration" — the stated rationale for the current operation's urgency. Secretary Rubio: "The hardest hits are yet to come from the U.S. military. The next phase will be even more punishing on Iran." Trump separately confirms: "The big wave hasn't even happened. The big one is coming soon."
📍Iran (nationwide) · Dahiyeh Beirut · Golan Heights · Ramat David AB · Mount Meron ⚔️2,000+ bombs dropped on Iran in first 30 hrs (IDF) · air supremacy achieved Day 1 🛡2 Hezbollah drones intercepted over Israel · 0 impacts
Sources: Times of Israel Aviation Week CBS / Rubio
3
Mar
Diplomatic · War Powers Political · Congress · Iran refuses talks
Trump submits War Powers notification; Iran security chief says "no negotiations"; Schumer calls briefing "insufficient"; Dubai flights resume
Trump submits formal War Powers Resolution notification to Senate President Pro Tempore Chuck Grassley (R-Iowa), citing the "untenable" threat from Iran. Senate Minority Leader Schumer calls the Rubio congressional briefing "completely and totally insufficient" and says it "raised more questions than it answered." Schumer urges a war powers vote to curb Trump's authority. Rubio justified the preemptive strike by saying US intelligence knew Iran would automatically retaliate against US forces the moment Israel struck — "it was automatic." Iran's security chief Ali Larijani rejects any talks: "We will not negotiate with the United States." The Quincy Institute's Trita Parsi assesses Iran believes agreeing to a truce now would only invite a later restart of war, and calculates the conflict can only end durably if US casualties rise dramatically. Limited commercial flights resume at Dubai and Abu Dhabi airports late Mar 2 (Emirates flights UAE500/UAE542 depart). State Dept issues departure orders for Bahrain, Jordan, Iraq. US Northern Command raises protective measures at US bases domestically.
📍Washington DC · Dubai/Abu Dhabi (airport reopening) · Tehran 🕊Iran rejects negotiations · Trita Parsi (Quincy): Iran calculating war must cost US more before any truce ✈Dubai/Abu Dhabi: limited flights resume Mon evening after 48+ hrs closure
Sources: CNBC / Rubio NBC News Euronews ABC News / Schumer
3
Mar
US + Israel · CENTCOM Offensive · 72-hour fact sheet — B-52s confirmed; 1,700+ targets struck
CENTCOM 72-hour update: 1,700+ targets struck; B-52 bombers join B-1s and B-2s; 11 Iranian ships destroyed; Assembly of Experts building struck in Qom
The Pentagon releases its 'Operation Epic Fury — First 72 Hours' fact sheet on March 3, confirming that US forces have struck more than 1,700 targets across Iran — up from 1,250 in the first 48 hours. In a significant escalation of air assets, B-52 Stratofortress bombers are confirmed in the strike package for the first time, joining B-1 Lancer and B-2 Spirit bombers already active. Assets listed include B-1, B-2, and B-52 bombers; F-15, F-16, F-18, F-22, and F-35 fighter jets; A-10 attack jets; EA-18G electronic attack aircraft; MQ-9 Reapers; HIMARS; LUCAS drones; Patriot and THAAD missile defense systems; and nuclear-powered aircraft carriers and guided-missile destroyers. CENTCOM confirms 11 Iranian ships have been destroyed: 'Two days ago, the Iranian regime had 11 ships in the Gulf of Oman, today they have ZERO.' Separately, Israel strikes the Assembly of Experts building in Qom — the body convened to elect Iran's next supreme leader — while it was in session. An Israeli official told NBC News the goal was to destroy the building rather than kill the 88 religious scholars present; Iranian state media confirmed the strike and reported no casualties. In the early hours of March 3, the IAF also strikes the IRIB state broadcaster headquarters in Tehran, dropping dozens of bombs and claiming to have 'struck and dismantled' the complex. Iran disputes the extent of the damage; no casualties reported. IDF says it also hit dozens of Ministry of Intelligence command sites and a Quds Force facility in Tehran overnight.
📍Iran nationwide · Qom (Assembly of Experts) · Tehran (IRIB HQ, Ministry of Intelligence) ⚔️B-52 + B-1 + B-2 bombers · F-35/F-22/F-18/F-15/F-16/A-10 jets · Tomahawks · HIMARS · LUCAS drones · MQ-9 Reapers 🚢11 Iranian naval vessels destroyed; CENTCOM confirms 0 remaining in Gulf of Oman 💀No US/Israeli casualties in this wave; Assembly of Experts and IRIB struck — 0 casualties confirmed
Sources: Al Arabiya / CENTCOM NBC News Bloomberg Times of Israel ABC7 / CENTCOM
3
Mar
Iran · Energy Infrastructure Offensive · Saudi Aramco Ras Tanura struck; QatarEnergy LNG halted
Iran strikes Saudi Aramco's Ras Tanura refinery (550,000 bpd); QatarEnergy halts LNG production; Brent crude surges ~10%
Iranian drones target Saudi Aramco's Ras Tanura refinery complex on March 2–3 — one of the world's largest oil export terminals, processing 550,000 barrels per day. Two drones are intercepted and their debris causes a fire at the facility. Saudi Aramco shuts down operations as a precautionary measure; the blaze is brought under control with no reported injuries. Satellite imagery released by Vantor confirms visible structural damage including burn scars at the site. This marks the first major disruption to Gulf energy infrastructure since Iran began its retaliatory campaign. Separately, QatarEnergy halts LNG production at Ras Laffan and Mesaieed Industrial City following Iranian drone strikes on those facilities. Brent crude surges nearly 10% to approximately $79–80/barrel. Analysts warn that combined with the Strait of Hormuz closure, oil supply disruption could intensify. Saudi military sources tell AFP that readiness levels have been raised and any further targeting of oil infrastructure could prompt a military response — signaling Riyadh may be moving toward direct engagement.
📍Ras Tanura, Eastern Province, Saudi Arabia · Ras Laffan & Mesaieed, Qatar ⚔️Iranian drones — two intercepted at Ras Tanura; debris caused fire 💀No casualties at Ras Tanura · QatarEnergy LNG production halted · Brent crude +10% (~$80/bbl)
Sources: Bloomberg The National Argus Media J Post
3
Mar
Iran · Civilian Toll Update Reported · Death toll tops 600+ (Red Crescent); HRANA estimates 742
Iranian civilian death toll rises to 600+ (Red Crescent) / 742 (HRANA); Hengaw estimates 1,300 IRGC/military killed; Al Jazeera: total tops 700
On March 3, Iran's Red Crescent Society reports that over 600 civilians have been killed since the start of the US-Israeli campaign — up from 555 reported on Day 3. The independent human rights group HRANA separately estimates that 742 civilians have been killed. Al Jazeera's running tracker reports the death toll has 'topped 700 people.' Late on March 2, Hengaw — a Kurdish human rights organization — estimated that approximately 1,300 members of Iranian military forces have been killed in the attacks, though this figure is unverified by Western sources. Iranian Foreign Ministry states the military 'has lost control over several units, that are operating according to old general instructions,' suggesting command-and-control degradation. Iran's internet remains in near-total blackout, with connectivity reported at 1–4% of normal levels due to combined Israeli cyberattacks and physical strikes on communications infrastructure. The IAEA confirms that Natanz nuclear enrichment facility suffered significant damage in strikes by March 1–2, with satellite imagery released by Vantor confirming fresh damage.
📍Iran nationwide — 24 of 31 provinces struck (IDF) · Natanz nuclear site confirmed damaged (IAEA/Vantor) 💀600+ civilians killed (Red Crescent, Mar 3) · 742 estimated (HRANA) · ~1,300 IRGC/military KIA (Hengaw, unverified) 🌐Iranian internet at 1–4% of normal · IRIB struck · Command-and-control degraded (Iranian FM)
Sources: Wikipedia / Red Crescent Al Jazeera Day 4 IAEA / Vantor satellite
3
Mar
Diplomatic · Day 4 Political · Trump slams Spain & UK; 19,000 flights canceled; US Embassy Beirut closes; airspace partially reopens
Trump threatens trade war with Spain & UK over Iran war non-cooperation; 19,000 of 32,000 Middle East flights canceled; US Embassy Beirut closed; partial airspace reopens
During a White House meeting with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz on March 3, Trump publicly slams Spain and the United Kingdom for refusing to cooperate with Operation Epic Fury. 'We're going to cut off all trade with Spain,' Trump says, adding he is 'not happy with the U.K. either.' Aviation analytics firm Cirium reports that since Saturday, 19,000 out of 32,000 scheduled Middle East flights have been canceled. People are being evacuated to Frankfurt and other European hubs from Dubai. The US Embassy in Beirut announces it will be closed until further notice. Limited airspace in the UAE and Dubai has begun to reopen, with Virgin Atlantic resuming London–Dubai and London–Riyadh services on March 3. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi tells the Omani counterpart that Tehran is open to 'any serious efforts that contribute to halting escalation and returning to stability' — but security chief Larijani continues to publicly reject any negotiations. Trump in a separate interview with The Atlantic says Iran's new leadership 'want to talk' and he has agreed to talk 'eventually.' No ceasefire timeline exists. The Senate holds its own classified briefing on the war, which Democrats including Schumer continue to call 'insufficient.'
📍Washington DC · Beirut (Embassy closed) · Dubai/Abu Dhabi (partial airspace reopening) · Riyadh ✈19,000 of 32,000 Middle East flights canceled (Cirium) · Virgin Atlantic resumes LHR–DXB/RUH Mar 3 🕊Iran FM: open to 'serious efforts' on de-escalation · Larijani: 'We will not negotiate' · Trump: 'They want to talk'
Sources: ABC7 / Cirium NBC News Live Anadolu Agency CNBC
3
Mar
Israel · IAF Offensive · Tehran Leadership Complex — 100 jets, 250+ bombs
IAF strikes Iran's 'leadership complex' — 100 jets drop 250+ bombs on presidential bureau, SNSC HQ; 4,000 total munitions milestone crossed
Overnight into March 3–4, the Israeli Air Force struck Iran's fortified 'leadership complex' in central Tehran with approximately 100 fighter jets dropping over 250 munitions. Targets included the Presidential Bureau, the Supreme National Security Council (SNSC) headquarters — both destroyed — a compound used for high-level meetings, and an officer training facility. The IDF described it as 'the most important and central headquarters for the Iranian terror regime.' IDF spokesman Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin confirmed the IAF has now dropped over 4,000 munitions on Iran since the war began — surpassing the total used during the entire 12-day June 2025 war — and has conducted over 1,600 sorties. Washington Post reports the IDF separately struck Iran's presidential office and Israel's Defense Minister authorized troops to advance farther into Lebanese territory. Iran fired fresh missile barrages at Israel; the IDF gave the all-clear after no injuries were reported from the latest wave.
📍Central Tehran (Leadership Complex) · Presidential Bureau · SNSC HQ ⚔️~100 IAF jets · 250+ bombs on leadership complex · 4,000+ total munitions (campaign total, IDF) 💀12 Israelis wounded in Iranian retaliatory missile fire same night · 0 casualties confirmed in leadership complex strike
Sources: Times of Israel Washington Post Gateway Pundit / IDF
3
Mar
Israel · IAF Offensive · Covert nuclear site destroyed — 'Minzadehei'
IAF destroys secret underground nuclear weapons development site 'Minzadehei' on outskirts of Tehran; 300+ ballistic missile launchers destroyed since Day 1
On March 3, IDF spokesman Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin revealed in a press conference that the Israeli Air Force had destroyed a covert, partially-underground nuclear weapons development facility codenamed 'Minzadehei' (also reported as 'Min Zadai') on the northeast outskirts of Tehran. According to the IDF, after the June 2025 twelve-day war — during which Iran's known nuclear sites were struck — Iran did not halt its military nuclear activity and instead secretly transferred key infrastructure underground to this site, which was not previously publicly known. The IDF stated that a group of nuclear scientists 'worked secretly to develop capabilities required for nuclear weapons' at the facility. Israeli intelligence monitored the scientists' movements as they traveled clandestinely to the site, eventually enabling a precise strike. The IDF confirmed that since the start of the war, Israeli jets have destroyed over 300 Iranian ballistic missile launchers. Iran's ambassador to the IAEA had previously acknowledged the facility was struck.
📍Northeast outskirts of Tehran (Minzadehei/Min Zadai — partially underground) ⚔️Precision IAF airstrike · site confirmed partially underground · nuclear weapons component development ☢️300+ Iranian ballistic missile launchers destroyed since Day 1 (IDF cumulative total, Mar 3 press conf.)
Sources: Times of Israel Jerusalem Post
3
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · US Consulate Dubai struck; Iran claims 500+ missiles + 2,000+ drones fired since Day 1
US Consulate Dubai parking lot struck by Iranian drone; IRGC claims 'complete control' of Strait of Hormuz; Iran fires 500+ ballistic missiles and 2,000+ drones total; 17 Iranian ships destroyed
On March 3 (Tuesday), a suspected Iranian drone struck the parking lot adjacent to the US Consulate in Dubai, igniting a fire. Secretary of State Marco Rubio confirmed all personnel were accounted for, stating a drone 'unfortunately struck a parking lot adjacent to the chancellery building.' The consulate was operating at bare bones staffing. Separately, CENTCOM commander Admiral Brad Cooper confirmed in a video address that US forces have now struck nearly 2,000 targets with over 2,000 munitions in less than 100 hours, and have destroyed 17 Iranian naval ships — stating 'there is not a single Iranian ship underway in the Arabian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz or Gulf of Oman.' Iran's IRGC separately declared it has 'complete control' over the Strait of Hormuz, with IRGC Navy official Mohammad Akbarzadeh stating no vessel may pass. Cooper confirmed that Iran has launched over 500 ballistic missiles and more than 2,000 drones since the war began, but said Tehran's 'ability to hit us is declining.' Saudi Arabia intercepted two cruise missiles near Al-Kharj (close to Prince Sultan Air Base). Trump announced the US Navy would escort tankers through the Strait 'if necessary' and that the US Development Finance Corporation would provide political risk insurance for Gulf shipping. Dow Jones fell 900+ points at open; Brent crude was up ~2% to $79/barrel on Tuesday after a 6% spike Monday. VLCC supertanker rates hit all-time highs.
📍Dubai (US Consulate) · Strait of Hormuz (IRGC closure declared) · Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia ⚔️Iran: 500+ ballistic missiles + 2,000+ drones total (CENTCOM) · Drone struck US Consulate Dubai parking lot 🚢17 Iranian ships destroyed (CENTCOM) · Strait of Hormuz under declared IRGC closure · Trump orders Navy tanker escorts 💀0 US casualties at Dubai Consulate (Rubio) · Saudi Arabia intercepts 2 cruise missiles near Al-Kharj
Sources: TIME / Rubio CNN (Day 4) CNBC Live
3
Mar
US · CENTCOM Offensive · 7th US KIA confirmed; Pentagon IDs 4 of 6 KIA as 103rd Sustainment Command
Pentagon identifies 4 of 6 US KIA — all Army Reserve, 103rd Sustainment Command (Des Moines); CENTCOM: 50,000+ troops, 200+ jets in operation; IDF carries out overnight 'broad wave' on Day 5
The Pentagon on March 3 (Tuesday) officially identified four of the six US service members killed since the conflict began. All four belonged to the 103rd Sustainment Command, an Army Reserve unit based in Des Moines, Iowa — killed when an Iranian drone hit their makeshift operations center in Kuwait with little overhead protection. CENTCOM commander Adm. Brad Cooper confirmed the operation now involves over 50,000 US troops and more than 200 fighter aircraft conducting 24/7 strikes. In a new development confirmed early March 4, the IDF announced it has carried out a 'broad wave of attacks' across Iran overnight targeting launch sites, defense systems 'and other infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime.' Iran fired fresh missiles at Israel; the IDF gave Israelis the all-clear after intercepting them with no injuries. Israel's Health Ministry reported 219 people treated at hospitals in the past 24 hours of fighting. Non-emergency US Embassy staffers and families authorized to depart Saudi Arabia and Oman. US is in talks with Kurdish groups on a potential military operation inside Iran, per sources cited by Times of Israel. Israel's Defense Minister Katz warns that Khamenei's successor will be 'an unequivocal target for elimination.' Sri Lanka reports rescuing 30 people from a sinking Iranian ship in its waters.
📍Kuwait (US KIA site) · Iran nationwide (overnight IDF wave) · Riyadh/Oman (Embassy departure orders) ⚔️50,000+ US troops · 200+ jets (CENTCOM, Cooper) · IDF: 'broad wave' overnight Day 4–5 targeting launch sites and AD 💀4 of 6 US KIA identified: 103rd Sustainment Command (Army Reserve, Des Moines) · 219 Israelis treated at hospitals past 24 hrs
Sources: Washington Post Times of Israel (Mar 4) CNN (Day 4)
3
Mar
Lebanon · Hezbollah Regional · IDF ground advance into Lebanon; Hezbollah Al-Manar HQ struck; Lebanon death toll 50+
IDF advances into southern Lebanon; Hezbollah Al-Manar TV HQ struck in Dahiyeh; Lebanon death toll tops 50 killed, 335 wounded; full ground invasion held off pending Iran situation
On March 3, Israel's Defense Minister Israel Katz and PM Netanyahu approved the IDF to 'advance and take control of additional areas in Lebanon to prevent firing on Israeli border settlements.' The IDF slightly expanded its security zone in southern Lebanon, striking Hezbollah command centers, weapons storage, and communication infrastructure in Beirut — including Hezbollah's Al-Manar TV headquarters in Dahiyeh, southern Beirut, which was destroyed. Israel issued evacuation orders for dozens of locations in southern Lebanon and two southern Beirut neighborhoods. IDF deployed additional forces and called up reservists near the Lebanese border. However, IDF Northern Command said a full-scale ground invasion was unlikely until the Iran situation was calmer — the limited advance was framed as deterrence. Lebanon's Health Ministry reported at least 50 killed and 335 wounded in Israeli strikes over the past two days. An airstrike in Aramoun (south of Beirut) early March 4 killed at least 6 and wounded 8. IDF confirmed Hezbollah is paying a 'heavy price' and said the group's arsenal has been significantly diminished without Iranian funding and training. Defense Minister Katz declared Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem 'a marked target for elimination,' warning he would end up 'in the depths of hell like Khamenei.'
📍Southern Lebanon · Dahiyeh, Beirut (Al-Manar HQ) · Aramoun (6 killed, Mar 4) · Galilee Panhandle (rocket fire) ⚔️IDF: limited ground advance + extensive airstrikes · Hezbollah: rocket/drone fire on northern Israel (limited impact) 💀Lebanon: 50+ killed · 335 wounded (Health Ministry, 2-day total) · 6 killed Aramoun (Mar 4 early)
Sources: CNN Day 4 Breitbart / IDF Jerusalem Post
3
Mar
Diplomatic · Day 4–5 Political · Markets crash; Iran death toll 787+; Iran retaliation total: 500+ missiles + 2,000+ drones; UK deploys HMS Dragon; France deploys Charles de Gaulle
Markets crash (Dow -900 pts); Iran death toll 787+ (Red Crescent); UK deploys HMS Dragon to region; France repositions Charles de Gaulle to Mediterranean; 1,900 more flights canceled; State Dept orders departures from 6 countries
On March 3, US stock markets suffered steep losses, with the Dow Jones falling over 900 points at the opening bell and the Nasdaq down more than 1.8%. Brent crude rose ~2% to $79/barrel (following a 6% spike the prior session) while VLCC supertanker rates hit all-time highs. The Iranian Red Crescent revised its death toll upward to 787 killed in Iran. Aviation data firm Cirium reported another 1,900 flights in and out of the Middle East canceled on Tuesday, bringing total cumulative cancellations since Saturday to more than 19,000. The State Department ordered non-emergency US government personnel and families to depart Jordan, Bahrain, Iraq, Qatar, Kuwait, and the UAE. British PM Keir Starmer announced the deployment of HMS Dragon (an air defense destroyer) to the region and counter-drone helicopters to Cyprus, where RAF Akrotiri had been struck. France announced it was repositioning the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier group to the Mediterranean. The E3 (UK, France, Germany) confirmed readiness to take 'proportionate defensive action' against Iranian missile and drone sources if necessary. Iran's FM Araghchi told Oman's FM that Tehran was open to 'serious efforts' to halt escalation — but security chief Larijani continued to publicly reject talks and claimed Iran had 'prepared itself for a long war.'
📍Global markets · Cyprus (HMS Dragon) · Mediterranean (Charles de Gaulle) · 6 countries: US departure orders ✈1,900 more flights canceled Mar 3 (Cirium) · Cumulative: 19,000+ of 32,000 scheduled Middle East flights canceled 🕊Iran FM: open to 'serious efforts' · Larijani: 'We will not negotiate' · Trump: 'They want to talk' 💀Iran civilian death toll: 787+ (Red Crescent, Mar 3–4)
Sources: Al Jazeera (Day 4) CNBC Live CNN (Day 4) Times of Israel Mar 3
3
Mar
Israel · IAF Offensive · Day 5 Opening — Wave 10; Isfahan Ghadr missile site struck; 60+ strike waves total
IDF completes 'Wave 10' of strikes on Iran — Isfahan Ghadr missile/storage facility destroyed; IDF threatens Iranian officials in Lebanon within 24 hrs
On March 4 (Day 5), the IDF announced it had completed another wave of airstrikes against Iranian military targets in Tehran and Isfahan — described by Al Jazeera's reporter in Tehran as the '10th wave of attacks against Iran.' In Isfahan, the IDF struck a Ghadr missile storage, production, and launch facility, aiming to reduce Iran's capacity to fire Ghadr-class ballistic missiles at Israel. Strikes in Tehran hit 'infrastructure and sites used by the regime for the production of weapons, with an emphasis on ballistic missile production sites.' The IDF simultaneously issued new evacuation orders for 16 villages in southern Lebanon and launched a new wave of strikes against the Dahiyeh district of Beirut following the orders. Defense Minister Katz issued a warning: 'Any leader appointed by the Iranian terrorist regime will be an unequivocal target for elimination' — an apparent reference to reports of Mojtaba Khamenei's selection as Supreme Leader. The IDF also threatened Iranian officials present in Lebanon, warning them to leave the country within 24 hours. Iran continued firing ballistic missiles at Israel on Day 5; sirens sounded across central Israel and Jerusalem, with Magen David Adom reporting no casualties from the latest intercepts.
📍Isfahan (Ghadr missile site) · Tehran (weapons production) · Dahiyeh, Beirut (new wave) ⚔️IAF precision strikes · 60+ total strike waves (IDF) · Ghadr missile storage/launch facility destroyed 🛡Iranian ballistic missiles intercepted over Israel · Air raid sirens across Jerusalem, Tel Aviv, central Israel 💀No Israeli casualties from latest wave (MDA) · Lebanon evacuation orders for 16 southern villages
Sources: Al Jazeera Day 5 i24 News Times of Israel
3
Mar
Iran · Succession Political · IRGC-pressured hereditary succession — first in Islamic Republic history
Mojtaba Khamenei elected Iran's new Supreme Leader by Assembly of Experts under IRGC pressure — not yet publicly announced; Israel warns he is 'a target'
On March 4, Iran International and multiple outlets reported that Iran's Assembly of Experts has elected Mojtaba Khamenei, the 55–56-year-old son of the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, as the Islamic Republic's third Supreme Leader — under heavy pressure from the IRGC. The decision has not been formally announced by Iranian state media; reports indicate the regime is waiting for a 'security window' to publicize the transition, likely after Ali Khamenei is buried. The selection marks the first hereditary leadership transfer in the history of the Islamic Republic — a system that was explicitly founded to reject monarchical succession. Mojtaba has operated for decades behind the scenes of his father's office (the Beit), building deep ties to the IRGC. He has no formal government title but is described by analysts as having effectively managed the Supreme Leader's office for over 20 years. The IRGC reportedly applied 'heavy pressure' on the Assembly to back Mojtaba, viewing him as a reliable continuation of the 'Axis of Resistance' strategy. Israel's Defense Minister Israel Katz warned that any new Supreme Leader who continues Iran's hostile policies would be 'an unequivocal target for elimination.' Earlier reports had claimed some Assembly of Experts members were killed in the Israeli strike on their Qom building on March 3; the IDF had said it aimed to destroy the building, not kill the 88 scholars. The Wikipedia timeline also notes Iran International reporting Mojtaba's election. Iranian state media had not confirmed as of midday March 4.
📍Tehran (Beit — Office of Supreme Leader) · Assembly of Experts (location undisclosed during wartime) 🕊Mojtaba Khamenei (55–56) — elected 3rd Supreme Leader per Iran International sources · Not yet publicly announced ⚠️First hereditary succession in Islamic Republic history · IRGC drove selection · Qom's clerical establishment skeptical 🎯Israel DM Katz: Mojtaba is 'an unequivocal target for elimination' if he continues hostile policies
Sources: Iran International Khaosod English The Researchers Wikipedia Timeline
4
Mar
Iran · Naval Naval · IRIS Dena frigate sunk off Sri Lanka — 100+ missing
Iranian frigate IRIS Dena sinks 40 nautical miles off southern Sri Lanka — 32 rescued critically wounded; 100+ missing; cause unclear (submarine strike reported)
On March 4, the Iranian Navy Moudge-class frigate IRIS Dena sank approximately 40 nautical miles south of Galle, southern Sri Lanka, after issuing a distress call between 6–7 AM local time (00:30–01:30 GMT). The ship had approximately 180 crew members aboard. Sri Lanka sent ships and aircraft to rescue survivors; Sri Lankan Foreign Minister Vijitha Herath told parliament that 32 'critically wounded' sailors were recovered and transported to Karapitiya Teaching Hospital in Galle. Several bodies were also recovered. Over 100 crew members remain unaccounted for. The cause of the sinking has not been officially confirmed — Sri Lanka's defense ministry offered no immediate explanation, and Herath stated no information was available on what caused the explosion. However, Balkanweb and other outlets cited navy and defense ministry sources telling Reuters that an Iranian submarine had attacked the Iranian warship — possibly a fratricide incident. The frigate was returning from participating in the International Fleet Review 2026 in Visakhapatnam, India in February. The sinking represents a significant geographic expansion of the 2026 conflict into the Indian Ocean, and is described by Wikipedia as 'one of the most significant naval losses for the Islamic Republic of Iran Navy in recent history.' CENTCOM had previously confirmed 17 Iranian ships destroyed as of March 3; it is unclear whether the Dena is included in that count.
📍~40 nautical miles south of Galle, southern Sri Lanka — Indian Ocean 🚢IRIS Dena — Moudge-class frigate, 180 crew · Returning from International Fleet Review 2026, Visakhapatnam, India 💀32 rescued (critically wounded) · several bodies recovered · 100+ missing (Sri Lanka MoD) ⚠️Cause: unknown explosion — possible submarine fratricide (Reuters sources) · Not officially confirmed
Sources: Al Jazeera Arab News PK Balkanweb (Reuters sources) Wikipedia — IRIS Dena
4
Mar
Iran · Regional Strikes Offensive · Turkish airspace breached; NATO intercepts Iranian ballistic missile over eastern Mediterranean
Iranian ballistic missile transits Iraq & Syria, enters Turkish airspace — destroyed by NATO air defenses in eastern Mediterranean; debris falls in Hatay province
On March 4, Turkey's Ministry of Defense announced that a ballistic missile fired from Iran, traveling through Iraqi and Syrian airspace, was intercepted and destroyed by NATO air and missile defense systems deployed in the eastern Mediterranean before it could enter Turkish airspace. Debris from the interception fell in the Dörtyol district of Hatay province, southern Turkey; no casualties or injuries were reported. Turkey's MoD reaffirmed its commitment to national security and its right to 'respond decisively to any threats,' warning all parties against further escalation. The incident marks the first confirmed Iranian ballistic missile interception in or near NATO territory during the current conflict and is a significant escalation in the geographic footprint of Iran's retaliatory strikes — potentially implicating NATO's Article 5 collective defense clause. Ankara said it would continue consultations with NATO allies. Al Jazeera's live tracker also confirmed the interception, noting Turkey had separately condemned the strikes and that President Erdoğan was working toward a ceasefire.
📍Eastern Mediterranean (intercept) · Dörtyol, Hatay Province, Turkey (debris) · Trajectory: Iran → Iraq → Syria → Turkey ⚔️Iranian ballistic missile · NATO air & missile defense systems (eastern Med deployment) 🛡Missile destroyed before entering Turkish airspace · Debris caused no casualties ⚠️First Iranian missile intercepted near NATO territory in current conflict · Potential Art. 5 implications
Sources: Times of Israel Iran International i24 News Haaretz Day 5
4
Mar
Iran · Civilian Toll Reported · Death toll tops 1,000+ (Iranian state media); Al Jazeera tracker: 1,045; live tracker: 1,145
Iran death toll crosses 1,000 — state media reports 1,045+; Al Jazeera live tracker records 1,145; Iran has been 84+ hours without internet; IAEA: no radiological release detected
On March 4, Iranian state media reported the death toll from US-Israeli strikes has surpassed 1,000 — Al Jazeera's running tracker reported 1,045, while its live blog headline cited 1,145 as the latest figure. Iran has now been approximately 84 hours without meaningful internet connectivity (internet at 1–4% of normal, per Netblocks/IRGC data). Separately, the IAEA issued a statement on March 4 confirming that based on the latest satellite imagery, 'there has been no damage to facilities containing nuclear material in Iran, and no radiological release risk has been detected.' Damage was observed at two buildings near the Isfahan nuclear site, but no additional impact was found at Natanz beyond previously reported damage to entrances. The Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant showed no effects. IAEA Director-General Rafael Grossi reiterated calls for 'maximum restraint to avoid any potential radiological incident.' Iranian state media also reported that the farewell ceremony for Ali Khamenei's body has been postponed amid the ongoing conflict. Iranian exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi issued a video message urging the Iranian diaspora to intensify advocacy and contact lawmakers in their countries of residence.
📍Iran nationwide — 24+ of 31 provinces struck (IDF cumulative) · Tehran, Karaj, Isfahan, other cities struck Day 5 💀1,045+ killed (Iranian state media / Al Jazeera tracker) · 1,145 per Al Jazeera live blog headline ☢️IAEA: No radiological release detected · Bushehr NPP unaffected · Buildings near Isfahan site damaged 🌐Iran: 84+ hours without internet (Netblocks) · Khamenei's funeral ceremony postponed
Sources: Al Jazeera Day 5 Al Jazeera Live Iran International / IAEA Wikipedia
4
Mar
Diplomatic · Day 5 Political · Senate war powers vote scheduled; Qatar arrests 10 IRGC spies; UAE intercepts 3 missiles + 121 drones
US Senate schedules war powers vote (Kaine-Paul) to check Trump; Qatar arrests 10 suspected IRGC spies; UAE intercepts 3 missiles + 121 drones on Day 5; vessel struck off Fujairah
On March 4, the US Senate is set to vote on a bipartisan war powers resolution led by Sen. Tim Kaine (D-VA) and Sen. Rand Paul (R-KY) aimed at halting military operations against Iran unless Congress explicitly authorizes them. The vote is expected to fall short of the two-thirds majority needed to override a presidential veto, but puts lawmakers on record. Qatar announced it had arrested 10 suspected spies operating for the IRGC inside the country. The UAE's defense ministry reported intercepting 3 ballistic missiles and 121 drones on Day 5 — with 8 drones falling inside UAE territory. The British maritime security tracker (UKMTO) separately reported that a vessel was struck by an unknown projectile 7 nautical miles east of Fujairah, UAE. Qatar's air defense systems intercepted further projectiles over Doha, causing loud bangs. The US Embassy in Muscat (Oman) instructed all staff and American citizens to shelter in place due to 'ongoing activity.' Iran warned it would take 'similar actions against Israeli centers and personnel in other countries' if Israel targets Iranian diplomatic sites or diplomats in Lebanon. Russia and China both called for an immediate halt to the conflict; Turkish President Erdoğan said Ankara was working toward a ceasefire and sent condolences to the Iranian people.
📍Washington DC (Senate) · Qatar (IRGC spy arrests) · UAE (intercepts + Fujairah vessel strike) · Oman (Embassy shelter-in-place) 🕊Kaine-Paul war powers resolution: vote scheduled Day 5 · Likely veto even if passed · Russia/China call for halt 🛡UAE: 3 ballistic missiles + 121 drones intercepted Day 5 · 8 drones fell inside UAE · Qatar: projectiles over Doha 🚢UKMTO: vessel struck 7nm east of Fujairah, UAE · Cause/nationality unspecified
Sources: Haaretz Day 5 Al Jazeera Day 5 Iran International Al Jazeera Live
4
Mar
US · CENTCOM / Pentagon Briefing · First 100 Hours — 'America is winning decisively'
Hegseth & Caine hold 100-hour Pentagon briefing: ~2,000 targets struck, 20+ ships sunk, Iran ballistic missile launches down 86%; uncontested airspace promised 'in under a week'
At an 8 AM ET Pentagon press conference on March 4 (Day 5), Secretary of War Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs Gen. Dan Caine released the Operation Epic Fury '100 Hours' update. CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper confirmed US and coalition forces have struck nearly 2,000 targets with more than 2,000 munitions in under 100 hours — 'the first 24 hours of this operation were literally double the scale' of Shock and Awe 2003. Hegseth declared 'America is winning — decisively, devastatingly, and without mercy' and stated that 'starting last night and to be completed in a few days, in under a week, the two most powerful air forces in the world will have complete control of Iranian skies — uncontested airspace.' Gen. Caine confirmed Iran's ballistic missile launches are down 86% from the first day of fighting, with a 23% drop in the last 24 hours, and one-way drone attacks are down 73% from opening days. He said CENTCOM would now 'expand inland, striking progressively deeper into Iranian territory.' On munitions strategy, Hegseth confirmed the shift from high-end standoff missiles to GPS- and laser-guided gravity bombs (500–2,000 lb.) 'of which we have a nearly unlimited stockpile' as air superiority over Iran is achieved. Hegseth also confirmed the US Navy submarine sinking of the Iranian warship IRIS Soleimani: 'Last night we sunk their prize ship, the Soleimani — looks like POTUS got him twice.' Fox News confirmed US forces have struck or sunk more than 20 Iranian ships total. Hegseth signaled a potentially longer campaign: 'You can say four weeks, but it could be six, it could be eight, it could be three.' He said Iran's senior leaders are 'dead, missing, or cowering in bunkers.'
📍Pentagon, Washington DC · Iran nationwide (strikes continuing) ⚔️~2,000 targets struck / ~2,000 munitions (CENTCOM, <100 hrs) · Shifting to gravity bombs as air superiority achieved · 50,000+ US troops, 200+ jets, 2 CSGs, B-1/B-2/B-52 bombers 🚢20+ Iranian ships struck or sunk (DoD) · IRIS Soleimani sunk by US submarine torpedo overnight Mar 3–4 — first US sinking of enemy warship since WWII (Hegseth) 📉Iran ballistic missiles down 86% from Day 1; one-way drones down 73% (Caine, JCS) 🛡US/Israel to achieve uncontested Iranian airspace 'in under a week' (Hegseth) · White House: 'complete and total US-Israeli dominance over Iranian airspace anticipated in coming hours' (Leavitt)
Sources: Fox News Live (Mar 4) The Federalist / Hegseth Breitbart / CENTCOM AP / Chicago Tribune Haaretz Day 5 Live
4
Mar
Israel · IAF — Historic First Air-to-Air Combat · World First — F-35 kills manned fighter
WORLD FIRST: Israeli F-35I 'Adir' shoots down Iranian Yak-130 jet over Tehran — first-ever F-35 air-to-air kill of a crewed aircraft; first Israeli aerial kill since 1985
On March 4 (Day 5), the Israeli Defense Forces announced that an IAF F-35I 'Adir' fighter jet shot down an Iranian Air Force Yak-130 combat trainer/light attack aircraft over the skies of Tehran — the first time in history that a crewed manned fighter jet has been shot down by an F-35. It is also the first air-to-air kill by the Israeli Air Force against a manned aircraft since November 24, 1985, when an IAF F-15 downed two Syrian MiG-23s over Lebanon. The IAF airbase commander confirmed the F-35 'fired the missile while it was in the midst of targeting Iranian regime sites in Tehran,' and described the engagement as occurring when the Yak-130 was conducting a counter-drone/counter-UAV mission attempting to intercept Israeli Hermes-900 drones over the capital. Unverified footage posted to social media appeared to show the aircraft crashing into the Lavasan Mountains north of Tehran, with what appeared to be two ejections. Iran holds at least 8 Yak-130s, transferred by Russia beginning in 2023. The IAF Air Force Commander Tomer Bar congratulated the pilot, described as 'relatively junior' in the force, for whom this was not his first flight over Iran. Aviation Week separately noted RAF F-35Bs had recorded their first drone shootdowns in defense of Jordan the prior day — marking a notable week for the platform's combat debut in both air-to-air and air-to-drone roles.
📍Over Tehran / Lavasan Mountains, northern Tehran (engagement and impact) ⚔️IAF F-35I 'Adir' (air-to-air missile) vs. Iranian Air Force Yak-130 (Russian-made combat trainer/light attack) · First F-35 air-to-air kill of crewed aircraft in history 🛡Yak-130 was flying counter-drone mission vs. Israeli Hermes-900 UAVs when intercepted · Two apparent ejections observed (unverified) ⚠️First Israeli aerial kill vs. manned aircraft since Nov. 24, 1985 (Lebanon dogfight) · Iran IRIAF continuing limited air operations despite extensive losses on ground
Sources: Times of Israel The Aviationist Newsweek Aviation Week Israel Hayom
4
Mar
Lebanon · IDF Ground Advance Ground Operations · Lebanon — Sweeping Litani evacuation order; IDF troops advance
IDF issues sweeping evacuation order for ALL of southern Lebanon south of Litani River; troops advance deeper; Lebanon Health Ministry: 72 killed, 437 wounded since war began; new Beirut strikes
On March 4 (Day 5), the IDF dramatically expanded its Lebanon ground operation and issued a sweeping mass evacuation order calling on all civilians south of the Litani River to 'immediately head north of the Litani River.' This expands upon the earlier evacuation orders for 80+ specific villages issued on March 3, and now encompasses the entire southern Lebanon zone. IDF troops from the 91st Division advanced further into southern Lebanon under what the military called 'deepening forward defense,' taking control of 'dominating terrain' to create a security buffer for northern Israeli communities. Lebanese army sources confirmed IDF troops moved into several areas, causing Lebanese military units to redeploy away from border positions 'because of the danger to their safety.' The IDF announced a new wave of strikes against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon on March 4 — targeting weapons storage, missile launchers, command centers, and Hamas/Hezbollah infrastructure in the Tyre and Sidon areas — as well as Beirut's Dahiyeh district. Lebanon's Health Ministry updated total casualties to 72 killed and 437 wounded since hostilities began on March 2. Hezbollah on March 4 fired rockets toward Tel Aviv and the Haifa area — its first long-range attack of the current conflict — with two intercepted by Israeli air defenses and one falling in an open area. IDF confirmed no injuries. Defense Minister Katz warned that there is 'no safe place' for Iranian regime officials in Lebanon, threatening Iranian officers present in the country to leave within 24 hours. Lebanese President Aoun confirmed Hezbollah has been firing rockets from north of the Litani, and the Lebanese government has ordered the army to prevent further attacks.
