📍All of southern Lebanon south of Litani River (mass evacuation) · Dahiyeh, Beirut (airstrikes) · Tyre, Sidon areas (60+ Hezbollah targets) ⚔️IDF ground advance (91st Division) · IAF simultaneous airstrikes on Hezbollah · Hezbollah: first long-range rocket attack on Tel Aviv/Haifa area 🛡2 of 3 Hezbollah rockets toward Tel Aviv/Haifa intercepted · 1 fell in open area (no injuries) 💀Lebanon cumulative: 72 killed · 437 wounded (Lebanese Health Ministry, Day 5) · Tens of thousands displaced
Sources: Times of Israel (IDF Lebanon) Haaretz Day 5 Times of Israel Liveblog Mar 4 CBS News Live (Day 5)
4
Mar
Diplomatic · Congress Political · Senate War Powers Vote DEFEATED 47–53; House WPR debate begins; Brent crude $84/bbl
Senate defeats Kaine-Paul Iran War Powers Resolution 47–53 — Fetterman lone Democrat against; Paul lone Republican for; House debate begins on Khanna-Massie WPR; Brent crude hits $84/bbl; Iran offline 100+ hours
On March 4, the US Senate voted on the Kaine-Paul bipartisan Iran War Powers Resolution, which would have directed the removal of US forces from hostilities with Iran unless Congress specifically authorized them. The resolution was defeated 47–53. Sen. Rand Paul (R-KY) was the only Republican to vote yes, and Sen. John Fetterman (D-PA) was the only Democrat to vote no — mirroring the June 2025 war powers vote pattern. The defeat was expected: with a 53-seat Republican majority, the resolution needed at least 4 GOP crossovers beyond Paul, which it did not achieve. Sens. Collins and Murkowski declined to vote for the measure despite previous support for the Venezuela war powers resolution. In the House, debate began on the parallel Khanna-Massie Iran WPR with a vote expected Thursday March 5. Meanwhile, Brent crude rose to $84/barrel on March 4 — up 3% on the day, and 18% higher than a year ago — as European gas prices climbed more than 70% since Monday due to the Strait of Hormuz closure. Vessel movement in the Strait is down 90% compared to the prior week. NetBlocks reported Iran has now been offline for 100+ hours — the second internet blackout this year (the first was during January's nationwide protests). Iranian state media announced a three-day state funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei. Hegseth confirmed to Congress that the switch to cheaper gravity bombs as Iran's air defenses are suppressed will ease the strain on high-end precision munitions stocks.
📍Washington DC (Senate vote, House debate) · Strait of Hormuz (90% vessel traffic drop) · Iran (100+ hrs internet blackout) 🕊Senate WPR defeated 47–53 · Fetterman (D) only 'no' Democrat · Paul (R) only 'yes' Republican · House WPR Khanna-Massie vote expected Thur Mar 5 💰Brent crude: $84/bbl (+3% Mar 4; +18% year-on-year) · European gas prices +70% since Monday · VLCC supertanker rates at all-time highs 🌐Iran internet blackout: 100+ hours (NetBlocks) · 3-day state funeral for Khamenei announced by Iranian state media
Sources: Action Network / Senate vote result CBS News (Senate WPR) The Globe and Mail Live Washington Post (Senate vote) CBS News Day 5 Live
4
Mar
Iran · Civilian Toll / Judiciary Threat Reported · Death toll 1,145+ · Judiciary warns dissent = death penalty · Khamenei 3-day funeral
Iran death toll 1,145+ (Al Jazeera tracker); Iranian judiciary threatens death penalty for citizens 'supporting the enemy'; 100-hour internet blackout; 3-day Khamenei state funeral announced
On March 4, Iran's confirmed death toll from US-Israeli strikes topped 1,000: Al Jazeera's live tracker recorded 1,045 killed per Iranian state media with its live blog headline citing 1,145 as the latest figure. Iran's judiciary chief Gholam Hosseini Mohseni Ejehei issued a stark warning on Iranian state television that citizens who 'say or do anything in line with the will of America and the Zionist regime are on the enemy's side and must be dealt with on revolutionary, Islamic principles' — raising the possibility of death-penalty charges for those seen as cooperating with the enemy. Iran's three-day state funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei was announced by the official IRNA news agency for Wednesday. Iran has remained in an internet blackout for 100+ hours (NetBlocks), the second such blackout of 2026 after January's protest-related shutdown. Iranian internet remains at approximately 1–4% of normal connectivity. Israeli officials told journalists on March 4 that intelligence assessed Iran could have enriched uranium to 90% (weapons grade) within two weeks before the operation began, though Iran's 'weaponization group was still far from producing a classic nuclear bomb.' Hezbollah on March 4 fired its first long-range rockets of the current conflict toward Tel Aviv and the Haifa area, with the IDF intercepting most and confirming no Israeli casualties. Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf told neighboring countries that Iran uses its missile and drone capabilities 'solely for legitimate' purposes, in a bid to prevent Gulf states from joining the coalition.
📍Iran nationwide · Tehran (strikes on central Tehran Mar 4) · Hezbollah-fired rockets: Tel Aviv/Haifa area 💀1,145+ killed in Iran (Al Jazeera live tracker, Mar 4) · 1,045 per Iranian state media / AJ tracker (lower estimate) ⚖️Iranian judiciary: dissent = enemy cooperation = potential death penalty (Ejehei, state TV, Mar 4) 🌐Iran: 100+ hrs internet blackout (NetBlocks) · 3-day state funeral for Khamenei announced (IRNA) ☢️Israeli officials: Iran was 2 weeks from 90%-enriched uranium before operation; 'still far from classic nuclear bomb'
Sources: Al Jazeera Live The Globe and Mail Live CBS News Day 5 Live Haaretz Day 5 Live
4
Mar
Kurdish Forces · Ground Operation Ground Offensive · Western Iran — PJAK/CPFIK cross-border incursion begins
PJAK Kurdish fighters launch cross-border ground offensive into western Iran from Iraq — CIA actively working to arm them; White House denies Trump authorized arming plan; conflicting reports on scale
On March 4 (Day 5), multiple reports emerged that thousands of Kurdish fighters affiliated with the Kurdistan Free Life Party (PJAK) had begun taking combat positions inside Iranian territory from Iraq's Kurdistan Region as of midnight March 2. A senior official from the Coalition of Political Forces of Iranian Kurdistan (CPFIK) told i24NEWS that 'the ground military movements by Kurdish forces against Iran have already started since the midnight of March 2.' A US official confirmed the cross-border movement to Fox News. CNN separately reported that the CIA is actively working to arm Kurdish forces with the aim of fomenting a popular uprising in Iran, citing multiple people familiar with the plan, with the stated US aim being to use Kurds to stretch Iranian forces, support popular protests, or help them seize and control northern Iran and create a buffer for Israel. Trump personally called Kurdish leaders Masoud Barzani and Bafel Talabani of Iraq's two main factions on March 1, and separately spoke with KDPI head Mustafa Hijri on March 3. On March 4, the Komala Party of Iranian Kurdistan formally joined the CPFIK coalition as its sixth member, alongside PJAK, PDKI, PAK, and Khabat. However, Times of Israel reporter Barak Ravid updated his coverage citing 'conflicting reports' — a senior official in one of the Iranian Kurdish factions denied a ground offensive had formally begun, and Iran's Tasnim news agency denied cross-border incursions in three border provinces. The White House denied Trump had approved any plan to arm Kurdish forces, with Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt calling CIA arming reports 'completely false.' Iraq moved to seal its border after Iran requested action; Iraq's National Security Adviser Qassim al-Araji said reinforcements were sent to prevent infiltrations. The situation remained unresolved and contested as of the morning of March 5.
📍Northwestern Iran (Iranian Kurdistan) · Iraq-Iran border (Zagros Mountains region) ⚔️PJAK (YRK + HPJ armed wings) · thousands of fighters · cross-border from Iraq's Kurdistan Region ⚠️Conflicting reports — US official confirmed to Fox; Kurdish faction official denied to Barak Ravid/Channel 12; Iran denies in 3 border provinces 🕊CIA arming talks confirmed by CNN · White House denies Trump approved plan · Iraq sealing border per Iranian request
Sources: i24 News (exclusive CPFIK official) CNN (CIA arming Kurds) Times of Israel (conflicting reports) Al Jazeera Haaretz JINSA
4
Mar
Diplomatic · Congress Political · House WPR Debate; UK reverses on Diego Garcia; Hegseth visits CENTCOM; Starmer–Trump rift
House begins floor debate on Khanna-Massie Iran WPR (vote expected Thursday Mar 5); UK PM Starmer reverses position — US may use Diego Garcia for 'defensive' strikes; Hegseth to visit CENTCOM HQ Thursday; Iran threatens to strike Dimona nuclear reactor
Floor debate began in the US House of Representatives on March 4 on the Khanna-Massie Iran War Powers Resolution, with a vote expected Thursday March 5. Unlike the Senate measure defeated 47-53, the House resolution faces an even steeper path given Republican control; moderate Democrats including Reps. Josh Gottheimer and Greg Landsman introduced an alternative resolution giving the administration 30 days to end hostilities and banning ground troop deployment for 'regime change or occupation.' House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries supported the Khanna-Massie measure; Speaker Mike Johnson called a war powers vote during active operations 'a frightening prospect.' Separately, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer reversed his earlier refusal to support US operations, announcing the US may use British military bases — including Diego Garcia — for 'defensive' strikes on Iran; Starmer also disclosed that Ukrainian and other specialists would aid Gulf states in countering Iranian drone strikes. Trump had previously criticized Starmer on March 3, calling him 'not Winston Churchill.' Non-essential American workers in Cyprus were asked to evacuate given risk of further Iranian incursion. Defense Secretary Hegseth confirmed he would visit CENTCOM headquarters in Tampa on Thursday. In a significant diplomatic escalation, Iran threatened to strike Israel's Dimona nuclear reactor if the US and Israel sought to topple the Islamic Republic — a threat cited by Times of Israel's live tracker. The E3 (UK, France, Germany) confirmed readiness to take 'proportionate defensive action' against Iranian missile and drone sources if necessary. Italy convened an emergency meeting in Rome with officials considering deploying the destroyer Caio Duilio to Cyprus.
📍Washington DC (House debate) · Diego Garcia (UK reversal) · Tehran (Dimona threat) · CENTCOM HQ Tampa (Hegseth visit Thu) 🕊House WPR vote expected Thu Mar 5 · Gottheimer alternative: 30-day window + no regime-change ground troops · Speaker Johnson opposed ⚠️Iran threatens to strike Dimona nuclear reactor if US-Israel attempt regime change · First explicit nuclear threat against Israeli reactor 🛡UK reversal: Diego Garcia available for defensive strikes (Starmer) · E3 ready for 'proportionate defensive action' · Italy weighing Caio Duilio deployment to Cyprus
Sources: Action Network / House WPR update Jewish Insider (Gottheimer alternative) The Hill (Johnson on WPR) Wikipedia (2026 Iran conflict — E3, Starmer, Diego Garcia) Haaretz Day 5 Live (Dimona threat; Hegseth CENTCOM) Wikipedia (Italy/Caio Duilio)
4
Mar
Lebanon · IDF Ground Advance Ground Operations · Lebanon — IDF troops wounded; Hezbollah clashes in Khiam; 60,000+ displaced; 250+ targets struck
Two IDF soldiers wounded by Hezbollah anti-tank fire in southern Lebanon (401st Brigade); Hezbollah clashes with IDF troops advancing in Khiam; 250+ Lebanon targets struck since Day 2; 60,000+ displaced
On March 4, the IDF confirmed that two soldiers from the 401st Brigade were moderately wounded by Hezbollah anti-tank fire during ground operations in southern Lebanon — the first confirmed IDF ground combat casualties of the current conflict. Hezbollah separately claimed it clashed with Israeli forces attempting to advance on Khiam in southern Lebanon, and launched rocket barrages targeting a concentration of IDF troops in Kiryat Shmona. Since Hezbollah joined the war on March 2, the IDF has struck more than 250 targets in Lebanon, including rocket and missile launch sites, drone production facilities, weapons storage, and command centers south of the Litani River. The IDF has now issued evacuation notices for hundreds of villages across southern Lebanon; the number of officially registered displaced persons has surpassed 60,000 within just a few days, according to Lebanese authorities. A significant spillover effect emerged as approximately 11,000 Syrians crossed from Lebanon back into Syria through the Jdeidet Yabous and Jousieh crossings in a single day — most of them Syrian nationals fleeing the Lebanon escalation. Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun continued diplomatic contacts with international actors to curb escalation and confirmed to the US ambassador that the Lebanese government's ban on Hezbollah's military activities was 'a sovereign and final decision,' though implemention remained in question.
📍Khiam, southern Lebanon (IDF-Hezbollah clash) · Kiryat Shmona (rocket barrage) · Litani River zone (250+ targets struck) · Lebanon-Syria border (11,000 Syrians displaced) ⚔️IDF 91st Division + 401st Brigade in ground advance · Hezbollah: anti-tank fire + rockets at Kiryat Shmona · IDF: airstrikes on 250+ Lebanon targets since Day 2 💀2 IDF soldiers moderately wounded by anti-tank fire (401st Brigade, Mar 4) · Lebanon: 72+ killed, 437+ wounded cumulative (Health Ministry) 🌍60,000+ displaced persons registered in Lebanon (official count) · 11,000 Syrians crossed back into Syria in 24 hours
Sources: Alma Research Center (Day 4 daily report) Wikipedia (2026 Iran conflict — Lebanon section) CNN Day 5
4
Mar
Iran · Dimona Threat Escalatory Threat · Iran warns on Israel's Dimona nuclear reactor; Australia confirms Al Minhad Air Base struck; Anthropic/Claude controversy
Iran threatens to strike Israel's Dimona nuclear reactor if regime-change operation continues; Australia confirms Al Minhad Air Base struck (no casualties); US military reportedly used Anthropic Claude AI in strike planning
Iran issued an explicit threat on March 4 to strike Israel's Dimona nuclear reactor — its sole nuclear research facility — if the US-Israeli coalition continued operations aimed at toppling the Islamic Republic, per Times of Israel and Al Jazeera live trackers. The threat escalated the potential for a radiological incident well beyond anything seen in previous Iran-Israel exchanges. Separately, Australia's Defense Minister Richard Marles confirmed on March 4 that Iranian drones attacked Al Minhad Air Base in the UAE — used by Australia as a long-term staging hub for Middle East military operations — over the weekend; no Australian personnel were injured and all were accounted for. Australia stated it supports the US-Israeli action to kill Khamenei but was not involved in planning or execution. In a domestic controversy, Axios reported that the US military used Anthropic's Claude AI technology to support the Iran strikes; Trump had ordered agencies to 'immediately cease' using Anthropic services on the day of the strikes and subsequently designated Anthropic a 'Supply-Chain Risk to National Security' per Hegseth, prompting Anthropic to announce it would fight the designation in court. Bahrain separately confirmed total cumulative intercepts of 74 missiles and 92 drones since February 28.
📍Dimona, Negev, Israel (Iranian threat) · Al Minhad AB, UAE (Australia confirms strike) · Pentagon/Washington DC (Anthropic controversy) ⚠️Iran explicitly threatens Dimona nuclear reactor strike — first time in current conflict · Potential for radiological incident if executed 🛡Bahrain cumulative intercepts: 74 missiles + 92 drones (Feb 28–Mar 4) · Al Minhad: no Australian casualties 🤖US military reportedly used Anthropic Claude AI in Iran strike planning (Axios) · Trump then banned Anthropic from US government · Anthropic to contest 'Supply-Chain Risk' designation in court
Sources: CNN (Australia Al Minhad confirmation) CNBC (Anthropic/Claude + Hegseth) Times of Israel (Dimona threat) Wikipedia (Bahrain intercept totals)
4
Mar
Diplomatic · Spain / Macron / PrSM Political · Spain reverses; Macron call; CENTCOM deploys PrSMs in combat first
Spain reverses — agrees to cooperate with US operations after Trump trade threat; Macron calls Trump; CENTCOM confirms historic first use of PrSM precision missiles in combat; Spain gov't contradicts White House
White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt announced on March 4 that Spain had 'agreed to cooperate' with US military operations after Trump threatened to cut off trade with Madrid the previous day for refusing to allow US forces to use Spanish bases in support of Operation Epic Fury. However, Iran International and other outlets subsequently reported that Spain's government denied cooperating with US military operations, directly contradicting Leavitt's statement — leaving the Spanish position in public dispute. Separately, US President Trump and French President Emmanuel Macron spoke by phone on Wednesday about US military operations in Iran; the French Elysée source said Macron also raised the situation in Lebanon. In a significant weapons milestone confirmed on March 4, CENTCOM announced that long-range Precision Strike Missiles (PrSMs) were used in combat for the first time during Operation Epic Fury — CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper called it 'an historic first' providing 'unrivaled deep strike capability.' A CENTCOM video of the PrSM launches was released on X.
📍Washington DC (Leavitt briefing) · Paris (Macron-Trump call) · Madrid (Spanish denial) · CENTCOM area of operations (PrSM strikes) 🕊Spain: WH says cooperating; Spanish gov't denies — status unresolved as of Mar 4 evening ⚔️PrSM (Precision Strike Missile) — first-ever combat use confirmed by CENTCOM, March 4
Sources: Fox News Live (Mar 4) Iran International CNBC Live
4
Mar
Iran · Regional Offensive · Mojtaba Khamenei survives airstrike; Kuwaiti girl killed by shrapnel; UAE Burj Al Arab debris hit; Iranian parliament building struck
Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly survives airstrike; 11-year-old Kuwaiti girl dies from Iranian shrapnel; UAE Burj Al Arab damaged by interceptor debris; Iranian parliament building targeted
Iranian sources reported on March 4 that Mojtaba Khamenei — the son of the late Supreme Leader and reportedly newly elected as Iran's third Supreme Leader — survived an airstrike. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz had previously declared Mojtaba 'an unequivocal target for elimination.' An 11-year-old Kuwaiti girl died from her injuries following an incident involving Iranian shrapnel, per Al Jazeera's tracker — raising Kuwait's total civilian death toll from Iranian strikes. The UAE reported that interceptor debris from an intercepted Iranian missile wave fell in Abu Dhabi and Dubai, causing damage to the Burj Al Arab hotel. Iranian state-linked media separately indicated that Iran's parliament building in Tehran was also targeted by airstrikes on March 4, though no casualties were reported. Iran's IRGC announced that 'ground forces entered battlefield operations' in which 230 drones were engaged, and referenced a naval operation targeting US military ships (Al Jazeera/WANA reporting from Tehran).
📍Tehran (Mojtaba airstrike; parliament building) · Kuwait (girl killed by shrapnel) · Dubai/Abu Dhabi (Burj Al Arab debris) 💀Kuwait: 11-year-old girl killed by Iranian shrapnel (Al Jazeera) · UAE: Burj Al Arab sustains debris damage — no reported injuries ⚠️Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly survived Israeli airstrike (Iranian sources, unconfirmed by IDF) · Iranian parliament building struck
Sources: Wikipedia / 2026 Iran conflict Al Jazeera Day 5 CBS News Day 5 Live
4
Mar
Israel · IAF — Day 5 Surge Offensive · 300+ consecutive strike waves in 24 hrs; Basij HQ destroyed; 1,600 total sorties
IDF: 300+ consecutive strike waves in past 24 hours; Basij HQ, missile launchers, and logistics HQ destroyed; 1,600 total IAF strike sorties since Feb 28; Iran fires at least 7 missile waves at Israel on Day 5
The IDF confirmed on March 4 that it has now conducted 1,600 'strike sorties' against Iran since the war began on February 28. In the most recent 24-hour period alone, hundreds of Israeli Air Force fighter jets conducted more than 300 consecutive waves of strikes — IDF International Spokesperson Lt. Col. Nadav Shoshani said this included simultaneous strikes against both Iran and Hezbollah. Among confirmed strikes: Israeli jets struck the Basij paramilitary headquarters, missile launch pads, defense systems, and the supply and logistics directorate affiliated with Iran's ground forces in Tehran. Additionally, Israel struck Azadi Square in Tehran, military facilities, and a metro station. Iran responded by launching at least seven waves of ballistic missiles at Israel on March 4 alone, with the IDF issuing shelter orders across Israel throughout the day — sirens covered Jerusalem, Tel Aviv, and an approximately 1,500 square mile area at 4:16 PM. Magen David Adom reported no casualties from the March 4 waves. The IDF also reiterated the Minzadehei covert nuclear site strike confirmed on March 3.
📍Tehran (Basij HQ; Azadi Square; metro station; logistics HQ) · Iran nationwide (1,600 total IAF sorties) · Israel (7+ missile waves, shelters activated) ⚔️Hundreds of IAF jets · 300+ consecutive strike waves in 24 hrs (IDF, Mar 4) · 1,600 total strike sorties since Feb 28 🛡Iran: 7+ ballistic missile waves at Israel on Day 5 (by 5 PM) · IDF intercepts all · 0 Israeli casualties (MDA)
Sources: FDD Analysis CBS News Day 5 Live Wikipedia (2026 Iran conflict)
4
Mar
Diplomatic · Senate WPR Defeated; House Vote Thursday Political · Senate rejects war powers 47–53; House Khanna-Massie WPR vote set for Thursday Mar 5
US Senate defeats Kaine-Paul Iran War Powers Resolution 47–53 — Fetterman lone Democrat no, Paul lone Republican yes; House Khanna-Massie WPR vote scheduled Thursday March 5; Speaker Johnson calls it 'dangerous'
The US Senate on Wednesday March 4 voted 47–53 to reject the bipartisan Kaine-Paul Iran War Powers Resolution, which would have directed the removal of US forces from hostilities with Iran unless Congress specifically authorized them. Sen. Rand Paul (R-KY) was the only Republican to vote yes, and Sen. John Fetterman (D-PA) was the only Democrat to vote no. Senate Majority Leader John Thune said the president 'is acting in the best interests of the nation.' Some Republicans, including Sen. Todd Young (R-IN), voiced concern about the lack of a clear long-term plan from the administration but still voted against the resolution. Democrats including Sen. Richard Blumenthal said after the classified briefing that he was 'more fearful than ever' about potential US ground troops in Iran. The House of Representatives is set to vote Thursday March 5 on the parallel Khanna-Massie WPR (H.Con.Res.38). Speaker Mike Johnson called it 'a frightening prospect' that would 'empower our enemies.' Republicans hold a razor-thin House majority — only a handful of GOP defections would be needed for the measure to pass. Congresswoman Betty McCollum announced she would vote yes, and House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries backed the measure. The Gottheimer alternative resolution (30-day window, no regime-change ground troops) was also pending.
📍US Senate (vote: 47–53 defeat) · US House (Khanna-Massie WPR vote Thursday Mar 5) 🕊Senate: Paul (R) yes · Fetterman (D) no · All others party-line · House vote outcome pending as of Mar 5 morning ⚠️Sen. Blumenthal: 'more fearful than ever' re: ground troops · Sen. Young: insufficient planning by administration · Johnson: WPR 'frightening'
Sources: ABC News (Senate WPR) Washington Post (Senate vote) McCollum.house.gov The Hill (Johnson WPR)
4
Mar
US · CENTCOM — Updated Naval Count Naval · CENTCOM revises Iranian ships count to 19 destroyed + 1 submarine holed
CENTCOM updates Iranian naval losses to 19 ships destroyed + 1 submarine 'holed'; US military destroyed 19 ships and nearly 2,000 targets total (updated figure from 17)
Updated figures from CENTCOM and Wikipedia's running conflict entry, reflecting CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper's statements as of March 3–4, indicate the US military destroyed 19 Iranian ships and 1 Iranian submarine (described as 'the most operational Iranian submarine that now has a hole in its side'). Additionally, the US submarine sinking of the Iranian warship IRIS Soleimani in international waters off Sri Lanka (confirmed by Hegseth on March 4) is separate from the 17–19 vessels counted in the Gulf/Arabian Sea. Combined naval losses across the campaign now account for 20+ Iranian naval assets. CENTCOM also confirmed that nearly 2,000 targets have been struck in under 100 hours of operations. Rubio announced on March 4 that US-Israeli attacks on Iran are 'going to increase in their intensity' in coming days.
🚢CENTCOM: 19 Iranian ships destroyed + 1 submarine 'holed' as of Mar 3 (Cooper) · IRIS Soleimani separately sunk by US sub (Hegseth, Mar 4) · Total Iranian naval losses: 20+ 📍Arabian Gulf / Gulf of Oman (CENTCOM naval strikes) · Indian Ocean (IRIS Soleimani / IRIS Dena losses) ⚔️Nearly 2,000 targets struck in <100 hours (CENTCOM) · Rubio: intensity of strikes to increase
Sources: Wikipedia (2026 Iran conflict) CNBC Live Fox News Live (Mar 4)
4
Mar
Lebanon · Hezbollah Regional · Hezbollah claims Israeli tank struck; IDF issues Beirut evacuation warning; Greece deploys F-16s to Cyprus
Hezbollah claims it struck an Israeli tank in southern Lebanon; IDF issues new Beirut building evacuation warning; Greek F-16s shoot down drone approaching Cyprus; Iran fires on Israel 7+ times in Day 5; Iran ground forces announce 230-drone engagement
On March 4 (Day 5), Hezbollah claimed it struck an Israeli tank during ground combat in southern Lebanon — the IDF has not confirmed the claim. IDF military spokesman Avichay Adraee issued a new urgent warning on March 4 instructing residents in Beirut's southern suburbs to evacuate a specific building affiliated with Hezbollah ahead of a planned airstrike. Separately, a drone from Lebanon attempting to enter Cypriot airspace was identified and shot down by Greek F-16s that had been deployed to Cyprus following Iranian threats — the Cypriot government confirmed the incident but dismissed reports of full airspace closure. The pro-Iran militia Saraya Awliya al-Dam claimed it struck 'a vital US asset' in Jordan on March 4, though neither independent media nor CENTCOM confirmed the report. Iran's IRGC also announced its 'ground forces entered battlefield operations' engaging 230 drones, and referenced a naval operation targeting US military ships. Iran continued to fire ballistic missiles at Israel throughout Day 5, with at least seven distinct waves detected by 5 PM local time.
📍Southern Lebanon (IDF-Hezbollah ground combat; Hezbollah tank claim) · Dahiyeh Beirut (new evacuation order) · Cyprus (Greek F-16 drone shootdown) · Jordan (Saraya Awliya al-Dam claim — unconfirmed) ⚔️Hezbollah: claims Israeli tank hit in southern Lebanon · IDF: continues advance + airstrikes · Greek F-16s: shoot down Lebanon-launched drone over Cyprus 🛡IDF intercepts all 7+ Iranian ballistic missile waves at Israel Day 5 · 0 Israeli casualties · Greek F-16s active in Cyprus airspace defense
Sources: Wikipedia (2026 Iran conflict) CBS News Day 5 Live Al Jazeera Day 5
5
Mar
Diplomatic · Day 6 — Iran Backchannel Intelligence · Iran Ministry reaches out to CIA via third country
Iran's Ministry of Intelligence secretly reaches out to CIA via third-country channel to discuss end of war — Trump says 'Too Late'; no active negotiations
Operatives from Iran's Ministry of Intelligence quietly signaled openness to the CIA through another country's intelligence service to discuss terms for ending the conflict, according to officials briefed on the matter cited by The New York Times and confirmed by CNN. The messages were conveyed through a third country to the CIA. However, US officials insist they have not been in active talks with Iran since nuclear negotiations fell apart days before the war began. Trump himself pre-empted any diplomatic opening with a Truth Social post stating: 'Their air defense, Air Force, Navy and Leadership is gone. They want to talk. I said Too Late!' A source from Iran's Intelligence Ministry publicly rejected the report as 'absolute lies and psychological warfare.' US officials described entering a 'more intensive' phase of strikes rather than any ceasefire track. Trump separately noted the pool of potential Iranian interlocutors was shrinking fast: 'Most of the people we had in mind are dead. Pretty soon we are not going to know anybody.' Analysts warn the more the Iranian system is degraded, the more any negotiation resembles a scramble to identify a signatory rather than a classic bargaining process.
📍Washington DC · Tehran · third-country intermediary (unnamed) 🕊Iran MoI backchannel to CIA — preliminary, no active negotiations (US officials) · Trump: 'Too Late' · Iran publicly denies ⚠️US officials skeptical Iran can enforce any ceasefire given ongoing leadership decapitation
Sources: Jerusalem Post / NYT CNN Politics Times of Israel
5
Mar
Iran · Regional Expansion Offensive · Azerbaijan, Saudi Arabia, UAE struck on Day 6; Iran launches strikes on Kurdish forces in Iraq
Day 6: Iran strikes Azerbaijan, Saudi Arabia, and UAE; Iran launches retaliatory strikes against Kurdish forces in Iraq; Abu Dhabi airport resumes limited flights; 6 injured in Abu Dhabi debris
On March 5 (Day 6), the Washington Post's live tracker confirmed fresh Iranian strikes on Azerbaijan, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates, further widening the geographic scope of Iran's retaliatory campaign. Iran also announced retaliatory strikes against Kurdish forces in Iraq, directly targeting PJAK positions in response to the cross-border Kurdish ground movements reported on Day 5. In Abu Dhabi, interceptor debris from Iranian drone/missile intercepts fell in two locations, injuring six people, the Abu Dhabi Media Office confirmed. However, in a sign of partial normalization, Abu Dhabi authorities announced that Zayed International Airport had 'resumed limited flight operations' following days of closure — the first major Gulf airport reopening. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi warned the US would 'bitterly regret' the sinking of the Iranian frigate IRIS Soleimani by a US submarine in the Indian Ocean. Meanwhile, the Institute for National Security Studies (Israel) compiled data showing Iran has launched 941 drones, 189 ballistic missiles and 8 cruise missiles at the UAE alone since February 28 — the vast majority intercepted. Italy, Spain, France and the Netherlands were reported by Reuters to be set to deploy naval assets to Cyprus.
📍Azerbaijan · Saudi Arabia · UAE (fresh strikes, Day 6) · Iraq-Iran border (Kurdish strikes) · Abu Dhabi airport (partial reopening) ⚔️Iranian drones/missiles — Azerbaijan, KSA, UAE · Iran strikes Kurdish PJAK positions in Iraq 💀6 injured in Abu Dhabi from interceptor debris · UAE cumulative: 941 drones + 189 ballistic missiles + 8 cruise missiles fired at UAE (INSS) ✈️Abu Dhabi Zayed International Airport resumes limited operations (Day 6) · Italy/Spain/France/Netherlands deploying naval assets to Cyprus
Sources: Washington Post Live CBS News Day 6 Live
5
Mar
Israel · IAF — Day 6 Offensive · Large-scale wave on Tehran infrastructure + Beirut Dahiyeh
IDF launches 'large-scale wave of strikes against infrastructure' in Tehran on Day 6; new total Dahiyeh evacuation order issued covering 400,000 residents; Israel Navy targets Hezbollah in Lebanon
On March 5 (Day 6), the Israeli military announced it had launched targeted attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon alongside a 'large-scale wave of strikes against infrastructure' in Iran's capital Tehran, with explosions heard across multiple Tehran locations. The IAF struck Beirut's Dahiyeh district again in the early morning — at least six Israeli airstrikes hit the southern suburbs, jolting the city awake. The IDF issued an unprecedented sweeping forced displacement order for the entirety of several southern Beirut suburbs — Burj al-Barajneh, Hadath, Haret Hreik and Chiyah — covering an estimated 400,000 residents: 'Save your lives.' IDF Arabic-language spokesperson Ella Waweya confirmed this was the broadest Dahiyeh evacuation order Israel has ever issued. A strike in the Beddawi refugee camp near Tripoli, northern Lebanon — well beyond the main battle zone — killed at least two people, with local sources telling Al Jazeera a Hamas official was the intended target. The Israel Navy announced it was 'targeting Hezbollah's infrastructure in Lebanon and identifying and intercepting aerial threats launched toward the State of Israel on a daily basis,' marking the first formal Navy statement of involvement in the Lebanon front. Iran denied having fired the ballistic missile toward Turkey that NATO intercepted on Day 5.
📍Tehran (large-scale infrastructure wave) · Dahiyeh, Beirut (6 strikes, 400,000-person evacuation) · Beddawi camp, Tripoli (2 killed) · southern Lebanon ⚔️IAF: large-scale strike wave on Tehran infrastructure · IDF Navy: targeting Hezbollah coastal infrastructure Lebanon · Unprecedented full-Dahiyeh evacuation order 🛡Iran denies firing missile toward Turkey that NATO intercepted on Day 5 💀2 killed in Beddawi camp strike (including apparent Hamas official, per local sources — unconfirmed by IDF)
Sources: CBS News Day 6 Live Washington Post Live Day 6 Al Jazeera
5
Mar
Lebanon · Hezbollah Regional · Lebanon death toll 77 killed / 527 wounded (Day 2–4); Day 6 attacks kill 10+ more including children
Lebanon health toll rises: 77 killed / 527 wounded (Day 2–4); Day 6 strikes kill at least 10 more including 7 children; Israeli strike hits family of four in Kfar Tebnit; Hezbollah clashes continue in Dahira
Lebanon's Health Ministry confirmed on March 5 that 77 people had been killed and 527 wounded by Israeli strikes between March 2 and midnight March 4. On March 5 itself, further strikes raised the death toll: Al Jazeera reported at least 10 more people killed on Day 6, including 8 Israeli strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs in the early morning. The Lebanese Health Ministry separately confirmed that those killed in Lebanon in the last 24 hours include seven children — the first specific child casualty breakdown of the conflict (Wikipedia, citing Lebanese Health Ministry). A strike in Kfar Tebnit in southern Lebanon killed four members of the same family — two children and their parents. A separate strike in the Nabatieh region killed the mayor of a village and his wife. An Israeli airstrike on a car on a highway near Zahle in eastern Lebanon killed two people and injured two more. A strike near Tripoli hit the Beddawi Palestinian refugee camp, killing two people including an apparent Hamas official (local sources, unconfirmed by IDF). Hezbollah fighters continued armed confrontations with advancing Israeli forces in the town of Dahira in the far south of Lebanon, with Israeli troops pushing deeper inside Lebanon in their ground offensive. The Lebanese army has been forced to pull back from border positions.
📍Kfar Tebnit (family of 4 killed) · Nabatieh (mayor + wife killed) · Zahle highway (2 killed) · Beddawi camp Tripoli (2 killed) · Dahira (IDF-Hezbollah clashes) · Dahiyeh Beirut (6+ strikes) 💀Lebanon cumulative (Day 2–4): 77 killed · 527 wounded (Health Ministry) · Day 6 adds 10+ more killed including 7 children (Health Ministry, Wikipedia) · 4 family members killed Kfar Tebnit ⚔️IDF ground advance deepening into southern Lebanon · Hezbollah: armed clashes with IDF in Dahira · Israeli Navy active in Lebanon · Lebanese army withdrawn from border positions
Sources: CBS News Day 6 Live Al Jazeera Washington Post Live
5
Mar
Diplomatic · Congress / NATO Political · House WPR vote today; WHO: 13 attacks on Iranian health infrastructure; UK acts on Hormuz; Italy/Spain/France/Netherlands deploy to Cyprus
House narrowly rejects Iran War Powers Resolution 212–219; WHO identifies 13 attacks on Iranian health facilities; Italy/Spain/France/Netherlands deploying naval assets to Cyprus; UK acting on Strait of Hormuz
The US House of Representatives rejected the Khanna-Massie Iran War Powers Resolution (H.Con.Res.38) on March 5 by a vote of 212–219. Four Democrats voted against: Reps. Henry Cuellar, Jared Golden, Greg Landsman, and Juan Vargas. Two Republicans voted in favor: Reps. Thomas Massie and Warren Davidson. Speaker Johnson had predicted defeat, calling it 'a frightening prospect' that would 'empower our enemies.' The measure followed the Senate defeat of a similar resolution 47–53 on March 4. Unlike the Senate version, the House measure was a concurrent resolution that would not have required a presidential signature. Meanwhile, the World Health Organization announced it had identified 13 attacks on Iranian health infrastructure by US and Israeli forces since February 28, plus one attack on a health facility in Lebanon — the first formal WHO accounting of medical facility strikes in the conflict. UK PM Keir Starmer stated Britain is 'acting to support open trade through the Strait of Hormuz,' the first explicit UK statement of intervention in Hormuz shipping. Italian Defense Minister Guido Crusto told Reuters that Italy, Spain, France and the Netherlands were set to deploy naval assets to Cyprus — the first formalized multi-nation European naval deployment of the conflict. Trump claimed he 'must have a role' in choosing Iran's next leader, per CBS News live tracker.
📍Washington DC (House WPR vote) · Strait of Hormuz (UK action) · Cyprus (Italy/Spain/France/NL naval deployment) 🗳House WPR defeated 212–219 · 4 Dems against (Cuellar, Golden, Landsman, Vargas) · 2 Rs in favor (Massie, Davidson) · Senate WPR defeated 47-53 (March 4) 🏥WHO: 13 attacks on Iranian health infrastructure confirmed + 1 in Lebanon (as of March 5) 🛡Italy/Spain/France/Netherlands deploying naval assets to Cyprus (Italian DM Crusto/Reuters) · UK Starmer acting on Hormuz shipping lanes
Sources: US News / AP CBS News Day 6 Live Wikipedia — 2026 Iran war
5
Mar
US · CENTCOM — 5th & 6th KIA Named Personnel · Pentagon names 5th and 6th KIA; all 6 Army Reserve 103rd Sustainment Command
Pentagon identifies 5th and 6th US KIA: CW3 Robert M. Marzan (Sacramento) and Maj. Jeffrey R. O'Brien (Indianola, Iowa) — all 6 KIA confirmed as Army Reserve, Port Shuaiba Kuwait, March 1 drone strike
The Pentagon officially released the names of the fifth and sixth US service members killed in Operation Epic Fury. Chief Warrant Officer 3 Robert M. Marzan, 54, of Sacramento, California, and Major Jeffrey R. O'Brien, 45, of Indianola, Iowa — both Army Reserve — were confirmed as the 5th and 6th KIA. O'Brien died on March 1 at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait, during an unmanned aircraft system attack. Marzan 'is believed to be the individual who perished at the scene' at the same location. All six US KIA are now confirmed as members of the 103rd Sustainment Command (Expeditionary), headquartered in Des Moines, Iowa — killed when an Iranian drone hit their makeshift triple-wide trailer operations center at Kuwait's Shuaiba port on March 1 (Day 2) with no warning and no siren. CENTCOM had previously confirmed 18 troops seriously wounded throughout the operation.
📍Port Shuaiba, Kuwait — Iranian drone strike, March 1, 2026 (Day 2) 💀KIA #5: CW3 Robert M. Marzan, 54, Sacramento CA · KIA #6: Maj. Jeffrey R. O'Brien, 45, Indianola IA — both Army Reserve, 103rd Sustainment Command, Des Moines IA · 18 seriously wounded (CENTCOM)
Sources: CNBC Live CNN Politics
5
Mar
Diplomatic · Congress Political · House Khanna-Massie WPR defeated 212–219; Iran FM: 'Not asking for ceasefire'
House narrowly defeats Khanna-Massie Iran War Powers Resolution 212–219 — Trump retains full authority; Iran FM Araghchi tells NBC: 'We are not asking for a ceasefire and we don't see any reason why we should negotiate'
The US House of Representatives voted 212–219 on March 5 (Thursday) to reject the Khanna-Massie Iran War Powers Resolution (H.Con.Res.38), which would have directed the removal of US forces from hostilities with Iran without congressional authorization. The result was extremely tight: only two Republicans — Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) and Rep. Warren Davidson (R-OH) — voted yes, while four Democrats — Reps. Greg Landsman (OH), Jared Golden (ME), Henry Cuellar (TX), and Juan Vargas (CA) — crossed the aisle to vote no, killing the resolution. The outcome came one day after the Senate defeated its parallel Kaine-Paul WPR 47–53. Combined, both chambers of Congress have now declined to constrain Trump's war authority. Speaker Mike Johnson, who had called the measure 'a frightening prospect,' declared the vote a validation of the president's constitutional authority as commander-in-chief. Rep. Massie, a longtime critic of presidential war powers, described the vote as Congress ceding its Article I authority. Democrats, including Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, had backed the measure. Sen. Tim Kaine said the WPR effort was 'not a one and done' and that Democrats would seek other avenues — including the appropriations process — to impose restraint. A smaller alternative resolution by Rep. Josh Gottheimer (D-NJ), which would have given Trump 30 days before requiring congressional authorization and barred regime-change ground troops, was not brought to a vote. Separately, Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi gave an exclusive interview to NBC Nightly News on March 5 in which he flatly rejected any ceasefire or negotiations: 'We are not asking for a ceasefire, and we don't see any reason why we should negotiate.' He added: 'Negotiate with the US when we negotiated with them twice, and every time they attacked us in the middle of negotiations?' Araghchi also warned that a US ground invasion of Iran would be 'a big disaster for them,' and denied Iran had attacked neighboring countries, insisting all Iranian strikes targeted US military bases and installations on foreign soil.
📍Washington DC (House floor vote) · Tehran (Araghchi NBC interview) 🕊House WPR defeated 212–219 · Massie + Davidson (R) yes · Landsman, Golden, Cuellar, Vargas (D) no · Senate WPR already defeated 47–53 (Mar 4) ⚠️Iran FM Araghchi (NBC): 'We are not asking for a ceasefire and we don't see any reason why we should negotiate' · warns US ground invasion would be 'a big disaster for them'
Sources: Al Jazeera CBS News Fox News CNBC (Araghchi/NBC) NPR
Day 7 — March 6, 2026
6
Mar
US + Israel Offensive · Day 7 Strikes — Near-Complete Air Superiority Declared
B-1B Lancer strikes central Tehran; IDF claims 2,500 cumulative strikes, 80% of Iran's air defenses destroyed; underground leadership bunker dismantled
US Air Force B-1B Lancers conducted strikes on central Tehran at approximately 5:30 AM local time (2:00 AM UTC). Additional strikes were reported in Tehran's Shariati district near Velayat Park at approximately 6:30 AM local time. The Israeli military simultaneously struck over 400 targets in western Iran, including missile launchers and drone manufacturing facilities. IDF announced it had dismantled an underground bunker beneath Tehran's leadership compound using 50 fighter jets in a concentrated strike package. By end of Day 7, the IDF announced it had reached a state of "near-complete air superiority," with 2,500 cumulative strikes conducted and approximately 80% of Iran's air defense systems assessed as destroyed. CENTCOM confirmed continued B-2 Spirit sorties from Diego Garcia and Whiteman AFB alongside Tomahawk strikes from Arleigh Burke-class destroyers in the Arabian Sea and Red Sea.
📍Central Tehran (Shariati district · Velayat Park) · Western Iran (missile/drone sites) · Tehran leadership bunker ⚔️US: B-1B Lancer strikes (5:30–6:30 AM local) · Israel: 50 jets on bunker strike; 400+ targets in western Iran 🛡IDF: 2,500 cumulative strikes since Feb 28 · 80% of Iran's AD systems assessed destroyed · "near-complete air superiority" declared
Sources: Al Jazeera Day 7 CNN Day 7 DefenseScoop
5
Mar
Iran · Regional Expansion Offensive · Iranian drones strike Azerbaijan — Nakhchivan airport terminal hit; 2 injured; Baku threatens retaliation
Iran strikes Azerbaijan's Nakhchivan exclave — drone hits passenger terminal of Nakhchivan International Airport; second drone falls near school in Shakarabad; Azerbaijan brings army to 'full combat readiness' and threatens retaliation; Iran denies involvement
On March 5 (Day 6), Iranian drones — identified by Azerbaijan's defense ministry as Arash-2 loitering munitions — crossed the border from Iran and struck Azerbaijan's Nakhchivan exclave. One drone struck the terminal building of Nakhchivan International Airport (approximately 10 km from the Iranian border), causing visible structural damage and igniting a fire. A second drone fell near a school building in the village of Shakarabad. Two civilians were injured. Azerbaijan's Foreign Ministry formally condemned the attack, summoned Iranian Ambassador Mojtaba Demirchilou, and declared that Azerbaijan 'reserves the right to take appropriate retaliatory measures.' President Ilham Aliyev convened his Security Council and stated: 'Iran committed an act of terror against the territory of Azerbaijan. Our armed forces have been instructed to prepare and implement appropriate retaliatory measures.' Aliyev brought Azerbaijan's army to 'full combat readiness.' Turkey's Foreign Ministry 'strongly condemned' the strikes and warned against spreading the conflict to third countries; Turkish President Erdoğan separately called Aliyev to express solidarity. Iran's armed forces general staff issued a blanket denial: 'The Islamic Republic of Iran has not targeted the Republic of Azerbaijan.' Foreign Minister Araghchi accused Israel of carrying out the strike as a false flag to undermine Iran-Azerbaijan relations. However, Azerbaijan's defense ministry stated that 'technical monitoring systems confirmed that four unmanned aerial vehicles belonging to Iran's armed forces were directed toward Azerbaijan's Nakhchivan region.' The incident opens yet another front in the geographic expansion of Iran's retaliatory campaign, and is particularly sensitive given that Nakhchivan is part of a US-brokered 2025 Armenia-Azerbaijan peace corridor deal ('Trump Route') that Iran has long opposed.
📍Nakhchivan International Airport (terminal hit) · Shakarabad village (drone near school) · Nakhchivan Autonomous Republic, Azerbaijan — ~10 km from Iranian border ⚔️Iranian Arash-2 loitering munitions (confirmed by Azerbaijan MoD technical monitoring) — Iran denies involvement, blames Israel false flag 💀2 civilians injured · airport terminal damaged · fire reported (no fatalities) ⚠️Azerbaijan army at 'full combat readiness' (Aliyev) · threatens retaliation · Turkey condemns · 1st confirmed Iranian strike on a non-Gulf, non-NATO bordering country
Sources: Euronews Al Jazeera Times of Israel NPR Washington Post
5
Mar
Iran · Civilian Toll Update Reported · Death toll 1,230+ (Tasnim); 920+ (Iranian Health Ministry per NPR); Al Jazeera tracker: 1,230
Iran death toll rises to 1,230+ (Tasnim/Al Jazeera) / 920+ (Iranian Health Ministry per NPR) on Day 6; war has now impacted 12 states per OHCHR; WHO condemns strikes on health infrastructure
On March 5 (Day 6), Iran's death toll from US-Israeli strikes rose significantly. Iran's semi-official Tasnim News Agency — controlled by the IRGC — reported the death toll had reached 1,230, a figure also reflected in Al Jazeera's live tracker. NPR, citing Iran's Health Ministry directly, reported the figure at over 920 killed including Supreme Leader Khamenei and more than 160 people in the Minab girls' school strike. The discrepancy reflects differing sourcing between official Iranian health authorities and IRGC-linked media. The UN Human Rights Office (OHCHR) stated the hostilities have now impacted 12 states, and called for an immediate end to hostilities and return to negotiations, saying 'returning to the negotiating table is the one and only way to bring an end to the killing.' The WHO had previously confirmed 13 attacks on Iranian health infrastructure by US and Israeli forces (as of March 5), plus one health facility struck in Lebanon. Iran's internet remained in near-total blackout. The IAEA had previously confirmed no radiological release, with Bushehr NPP unaffected. Iranian state media reported explosions continued to be heard for a sixth day across Iran, Israel, and multiple Gulf states.
📍Iran nationwide — 26+ of 31 provinces struck (ACLED records) 💀1,230+ killed in Iran (Tasnim/Al Jazeera tracker, Day 6) · 920+ per Iranian Health Ministry (NPR) · OHCHR: 12 states impacted 🏥WHO: 13 confirmed attacks on Iranian health infrastructure + 1 in Lebanon (as of Mar 5) 🌐Iran internet blackout continues (Day 6) · Khamenei funeral postponed · IAEA: no radiological release
Sources: Al Jazeera Live Tracker NPR OHCHR
5
Mar
Diplomatic · Trump / Iran Leadership Political · Trump demands role in choosing Iran's next Supreme Leader; rejects Mojtaba Khamenei
Trump tells Reuters and Axios he 'must be involved' in choosing Iran's next Supreme Leader — 'Khamenei's son is unacceptable to me'; Axios: Trump's claim represents 'extraordinary assertion of American power over Iran's political future'
On March 5, President Trump gave exclusive interviews to Reuters and Axios in which he stated he must be personally involved in selecting Iran's next Supreme Leader. 'Khamenei's son is unacceptable to me. We want someone that will bring harmony and peace to Iran,' Trump told Axios, which described the comments as representing 'an extraordinary claim of American power over Iran's political future, further muddying the objectives of the massive US operation.' Trump's statement comes after Iran International reported that Mojtaba Khamenei — son of the late Supreme Leader — had been elected as Iran's third Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts under IRGC pressure (not yet publicly announced by Iranian state media as of Day 5). Israel's Defense Minister Katz had previously declared Mojtaba 'an unequivocal target for elimination.' Israel had also bombed the Assembly of Experts building in Qom on March 3 while it was in session to disrupt the succession process. Trump separately told reporters: 'Everybody that wants to be a leader, they end up dead.' Al Jazeera separately confirmed Trump had expressed interest in the succession process, with an analyst noting Trump 'will not find an Iranian Delcy Rodriguez from within the existing Islamic Republic system.'
📍Washington DC (Trump interviews with Reuters and Axios) · Tehran (succession process unresolved) 🕊Trump: 'Khamenei's son is unacceptable to me. We want someone that will bring harmony and peace to Iran.' (Axios) · Israel: Mojtaba is 'a target for elimination' (Katz) ⚠️Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly elected 3rd Supreme Leader (Iran International, Mar 4) — not yet publicly confirmed by Iranian state media · Assembly of Experts building in Qom struck by IAF Mar 3 during session
Sources: Axios Al Jazeera NPR
5
Mar
US · CENTCOM / Tampa Briefing · Hegseth & Cooper at CENTCOM HQ — 30+ ships sunk; ballistic missiles down 90%; next phase targets missile industry
Hegseth & Adm. Cooper CENTCOM Tampa briefing: 30+ Iranian ships sunk; ballistic missile fire down 90%; drone attacks down 83%; next phase to 'raze' Iran's missile production industry; 'no shortage of munitions'
Secretary of War Pete Hegseth and CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper held a joint press conference at MacDill Air Force Base, Tampa on March 5. Cooper confirmed that US bombers struck nearly 200 targets in Iran in the past 72 hours alone, dropping dozens of 2,000-pound Penetrator bombs on deeply buried ballistic missile launchers. He confirmed the US has now sunk or destroyed more than 30 Iranian ships — up sharply from the 19–20 figure given at the 100-hour briefing on March 4. Iran's rate of ballistic missile fire is down 90% and drone attacks down 83% from the opening days of the campaign. Cooper said the next phase will focus on systematically dismantling Iran's ballistic missile production capability for the future — aiming to 'raze or level' the industry. He confirmed the US also struck Iran's equivalent of Space Command, degrading Iran's satellite and reconnaissance capabilities. Hegseth declared the operation 'advancing decisively,' said the US has 'no shortage of munitions,' and stated: 'Our timeline is ours and ours alone to control.' He cited US air dominance over Iranian skies. Cooper confirmed Iran has attacked 12 countries since February 28 — a new official CENTCOM figure. A Trafalgar Group poll released the same day showed 53.9% of likely US voters approve of Operation Epic Fury, 40.6% disapprove.
📍CENTCOM HQ, MacDill AFB, Tampa, Florida 🚢30+ Iranian ships sunk or destroyed (Cooper, Mar 5) — up from 19+1 sub at 100-hr briefing 📉Iranian ballistic missile fire down 90% from Day 1; drone attacks down 83% (Cooper) ⚔️~200 targets struck by US bombers in past 72 hrs alone (Mar 3–5); 2,000-lb Penetrator bombs on buried launchers; Iran's Space Command struck 🌍Iran has attacked 12 countries since Feb 28 (CENTCOM official count, Cooper)
Sources: Stars and Stripes / CENTCOM CBS News Fox News Live (Mar 5)
5
Mar
Iran · Gulf Strikes Offensive · Bahrain oil refinery hit; Amazon data centers struck UAE & Bahrain; Qatar shoots down two Iranian Su-24 bombers
Iran strikes Bahrain oil refinery; drones hit Amazon data centers in Bahrain and UAE; Qatar F-15s shoot down two Iranian Su-24 fighter-bombers attempting to strike Al Udeid — first manned Iranian aircraft kills of the conflict by a Gulf state
On March 4–5 (Days 5–6), Iran significantly escalated the nature and diversity of its retaliatory strikes. A missile struck an oil refinery in Bahrain, confirmed by government press release and social media video. Iranian forces also launched drone attacks on an Amazon Web Services data center in Bahrain and two Amazon facilities in the UAE, causing service disruptions to cloud infrastructure — a significant first in the conflict, as commercial technology infrastructure became a target. In the most dramatic development, Qatari Air Force F-15s intercepted and shot down two Iranian Air Force Su-24 Fencer fighter-bomber jets attempting to strike Al Udeid Air Base — where approximately 10,000 US troops are stationed — before they reached their target. This marks the first time manned Iranian aircraft were shot down by a Gulf state in combat and the first Iranian attempt to use crewed bombers in an attack on a US base. Explosions were reported in Doha following the interceptions. Iranian drone strikes also targeted Bahrain's Manama financial district area. Separately, an Iranian drone was intercepted over the Bahrain Financial Harbour towers (which houses the Israeli embassy) on March 6 morning, per ABC News. The WHO suspended all activity at its global emergency logistics hub in Dubai amid the airspace crisis.
📍Bahrain (oil refinery, Amazon data center, Bahrain Financial Harbour) · UAE (two Amazon facilities) · Qatar (Al Udeid — Su-24 intercept) ⚔️Iranian drones + Iranian Su-24 Fencer aircraft (2 shot down by Qatar F-15s before reaching Al Udeid) 🛡Qatar F-15s destroy 2 Iranian Su-24s — first Gulf-state manned-aircraft kills of the conflict · US/Gulf air defenses intercept bulk of drone salvo 💀No US casualties confirmed at Al Udeid · Bahrain refinery: damage confirmed · Amazon data centers: significant service disruption
Sources: CNN Day 6 Alma Research Center (Mar 5 daily report) Fox News Live (Mar 5) ABC News Live
5
Mar
Israel · IAF — Day 6 Offensive · Wave 12 complete — Tehran IRGC/Basij HQ, Alborz internal security HQ, Kermanshah missile base destroyed; IDF strikes Tripoli for first time
IDF completes Wave 12 of Tehran strikes — ~90 jets, ~200 munitions on ~40 targets; Kermanshah missile base confirmed destroyed by satellite; Alborz special internal security HQ struck; Israel strikes Tripoli, northern Lebanon for first time in current conflict
On March 5 (Day 6), the IDF announced it had completed its 12th wave of strikes across Tehran, with approximately 90 fighter jets dropping around 200 munitions on approximately 40 targets. Confirmed strikes included: targets belonging to the IRGC and Basij militia; a central command center for Iran's armed Internal Security forces; dozens of command centers and weapons sites across Tehran; and in Alborz province, the headquarters of the regime's special unit responsible for all internal security forces. Satellite imagery confirmed the destruction of the missile base in Kermanshah. Missile launchers in Qom were also destroyed, along with a defense system, control structures, and a radar system in Isfahan. The port of Bandar Abbas was struck overnight. Missile sites were hit in Yazd. Strikes were reported in Sanandaj (western Iran), Shiraz, Karaj, and at Mount Damavand. Strikes also hit Zahedan in eastern Iran near the Pakistan and Afghanistan border, targeting military positions. In Lebanon, Israel struck Tripoli — the largest city in northern Lebanon — for the first time in the current conflict, killing Hamas commander Wasim Attallah Ali, described by the IDF as responsible for training and exercises for Hamas military wings in Lebanon. Israeli airstrikes continued hitting Beirut's Dahiyeh district with fresh evacuation orders. IRGC officers — primarily from the Quds Force serving as military advisers to Hezbollah — began fleeing Lebanon in large numbers, per Axios citing Israeli defense officials; an Israeli official stated 'We expect the IRGC exodus from Lebanon to continue over the next several days.'
📍Tehran (Wave 12: IRGC/Basij/Internal Security HQ) · Alborz (internal security HQ) · Kermanshah (missile base destroyed by satellite confirmation) · Qom · Isfahan · Bandar Abbas · Yazd · Zahedan · Tripoli, northern Lebanon (first strike) ⚔️~90 IAF jets · ~200 munitions · ~40 targets (Wave 12, Tehran) · Separate Lebanon strikes including Tripoli 💀Hamas commander Wasim Attallah Ali killed in Tripoli · 0 Israeli casualties reported from Wave 12 ⚠️IRGC officers fleeing Lebanon en masse — primarily Quds Force advisers to Hezbollah (Axios/Israeli defense officials)
Sources: Fox News Live (Mar 5) Alma Research Center (Mar 5) NBC News Live Times of Israel Liveblog Mar 6
6
Mar
Lebanon · IDF Ground + Air · Lebanon death toll 123 killed; IDF orders second soldier deeper into country; Hezbollah taunts Israel with own evacuation order
Lebanon death toll rises to 123 killed; IDF orders troops to push deeper into Lebanon; second IDF soldier wounded; Hezbollah issues taunting counter-evacuation order for Israeli border towns
By early March 6 (Day 7), the Lebanese government reported the death toll from Israeli strikes had risen to 123 killed since the conflict began March 2 — a significant jump from the 77 reported on March 4. Thousands of people are again fleeing the capital Beirut ahead of new Israeli strikes on the Dahiyeh southern suburbs. The IDF ordered its ground forces to push deeper into Lebanese territory, with a second IDF soldier confirmed wounded in the latest advance. As Israeli strikes hit Beirut's Dahiyeh on March 6 — with flames and smoke visible in AP photographs — Hezbollah responded with a taunting counter-measure: issuing its own evacuation order for Israeli towns near the Lebanon border, a clear psychological operation signaling intent to escalate rocket fire northward. Internal displacement in Lebanon surpassed 83,000 registered displaced persons as of Thursday evening — up from approximately 58,000 on Tuesday — as families continue to flee south and southern Beirut. CNN entered Iran on March 5, becoming the first US news network team on the ground inside the country since the war began.
📍Dahiyeh, Beirut (ongoing strikes, Mar 6 flames confirmed) · Southern Lebanon (IDF ground advance deepening) · Israeli border towns (Hezbollah evacuation warning) ⚔️IDF: ordered to advance deeper into Lebanon · IAF: fresh strikes on Dahiyeh Mar 6 · Hezbollah: issues counter-evacuation order for Israeli border towns 💀Lebanon cumulative: 123 killed (Lebanese government, as of early Mar 6) · 2nd IDF soldier wounded in ground advance 🌍83,000+ displaced in Lebanon (government estimate, Mar 5 evening) · up from 58,000 on Mar 3
Sources: Times of Israel Liveblog Mar 6 NBC News Live CNN Day 6
6
Mar
US + Israel Offensive · Day 7 Strikes — Near-Complete Air Superiority Declared
B-1B Lancer strikes central Tehran; IDF claims 2,500 cumulative strikes, 80% of Iran's air defenses destroyed; underground leadership bunker dismantled
US Air Force B-1B Lancers conducted strikes on central Tehran at approximately 5:30 AM local time (2:00 AM UTC). Additional strikes were reported in Tehran's Shariati district near Velayat Park at approximately 6:30 AM local time. The Israeli military simultaneously struck over 400 targets in western Iran, including missile launchers and drone manufacturing facilities. IDF announced it had dismantled an underground bunker beneath Tehran's leadership compound using 50 fighter jets in a concentrated strike package. By end of Day 7, the IDF announced it had reached a state of "near-complete air superiority," with 2,500 cumulative strikes conducted and approximately 80% of Iran's air defense systems assessed as destroyed. CENTCOM confirmed continued B-2 Spirit sorties from Diego Garcia and Whiteman AFB alongside Tomahawk strikes from Arleigh Burke-class destroyers in the Arabian Sea and Red Sea.
📍Central Tehran (Shariati district · Velayat Park) · Western Iran (missile/drone sites) · Tehran leadership bunker ⚔️US: B-1B Lancer strikes (5:30–6:30 AM local) · Israel: 50 jets on bunker strike; 400+ targets in western Iran 🛡IDF: 2,500 cumulative strikes since Feb 28 · 80% of Iran's AD systems assessed destroyed · "near-complete air superiority" declared
Sources: Al Jazeera Day 7 CNN Day 7 DefenseScoop
6
Mar
Iran (IRGC) Retaliation · 23rd Wave of Strikes; 1,332+ Iranian civilians killed
IRGC announces 23rd wave of strikes against US/Israeli targets; updated casualty toll: 1,332+ killed in Iran, 200+ in Lebanon since Feb 28
The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced the commencement of its 23rd coordinated wave of retaliatory strikes against US bases and Israeli territory. Launches continued against Gulf states and Levant targets. The Iranian Red Crescent updated the civilian death toll inside Iran to at least 1,332 killed since the start of the offensive on February 28, with the wounded count still being tallied across multiple provinces. Lebanese health authorities reported at least 200 killed in Lebanon since February 28, predominantly in areas struck by Israeli Air Force operations targeting Hezbollah infrastructure and Iranian proxy supply lines. Iran's Supreme National Security Council issued a statement vowing continued resistance and denying the legitimacy of any ceasefire framework proposed without the withdrawal of all foreign forces from the region.
💀1,332+ Iranian civilians killed (Red Crescent, Day 7) · 200+ killed in Lebanon (Lebanese health authorities) ⚔️IRGC: 23rd wave of strikes launched · targets: US bases in Gulf + Israeli territory
Sources: Fox News Live Mar 6 Naval News / CENTCOM Newsweek Live CNN Day 7 Alma Research Center Mar 6

Iran (IRGC) Retaliation · 23rd Wave of Strikes; 1,332+ Iranian civilians killed
IRGC announces 23rd wave of strikes against US/Israeli targets; updated casualty toll: 1,332+ killed in Iran, 200+ in Lebanon since Feb 28
The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced the commencement of its 23rd coordinated wave of retaliatory strikes against US bases and Israeli territory. Launches continued against Gulf states and Levant targets. The Iranian Red Crescent updated the civilian death toll inside Iran to at least 1,332 killed since the start of the offensive on February 28, with the wounded count still being tallied across multiple provinces. Lebanese health authorities reported at least 200 killed in Lebanon since February 28, predominantly in areas struck by Israeli Air Force operations targeting Hezbollah infrastructure and Iranian proxy supply lines. Iran's Supreme National Security Council issued a statement vowing continued resistance and denying the legitimacy of any ceasefire framework proposed without the withdrawal of all foreign forces from the region.

6
Mar
Lebanon · IDF · Day 7 Ground + Air · Lebanon death toll 123 killed / 683 wounded; IDF officer seriously wounded; Hezbollah fires at Yoav camp & Haifa naval base; IDF strikes Sidon (5 killed); new Beqaa Valley evacuation order
Lebanon: 123 killed / 683 wounded (Health Ministry); IDF Givati Brigade officer seriously wounded in southern Lebanon; Israel strikes Sidon killing 5; Hezbollah attacks Haifa naval base & Golan; IDF issues new Beqaa Valley evacuation order
By Day 7 (March 6), Lebanon's Ministry of Public Health confirmed the cumulative death toll from Israeli strikes stands at 123 killed and 683 wounded since hostilities began on March 2. Fresh strikes continued on March 5–6: Israeli jets struck Dahiyeh (Beirut's southern suburbs) at dawn on March 6, with smoke visible from AP photographs. Israel also struck Sidon — Lebanon's third-largest city — killing at least 5 people and wounding 7 (Lebanese Health Ministry). Israeli warplanes were reported over Tyre and Bint Jbeil in the south. A new IDF forced evacuation order was issued for Lebanon's eastern Beqaa Valley, further widening the displacement zone. An IDF officer from the Givati Brigade was seriously wounded — the most serious Israeli casualty yet in Lebanon ground operations — with another soldier from the same brigade moderately hurt during fighting in southern Lebanon. Hezbollah also attacked Israel's Yoav military camp in the occupied Golan Heights and the Haifa naval base, with no immediate reports of casualties. UNIFIL reported that since March 2, over 210 missiles have been fired by Hezbollah into Israel. The IDF announced it has now conducted 26 rounds of attacks in Dahiyeh alone. Lebanese government meanwhile hardened its stance against IRGC presence in the country — in a cabinet meeting, PM Nawaf Salam announced a decision ordering all security and military bodies to identify, locate, prevent activities of, arrest, and prepare to deport IRGC personnel from Lebanese territory. Internal displacement in Lebanon surpassed 83,000 registered persons as of Thursday evening.
📍Dahiyeh, Beirut (Dawn strikes Mar 6) · Sidon (5 killed) · Beqaa Valley (new evacuation order) · Haifa naval base (Hezbollah attack) · Golan Heights (Yoav camp attacked) ⚔️IDF: airstrikes on Dahiyeh, Sidon, Tyre, Bint Jbeil + ground advance (Givati Brigade) · Hezbollah: Haifa naval base + Yoav camp + 210+ missiles total since Mar 2 (UNIFIL) 💀Lebanon: 123 killed · 683 wounded (Health Ministry, cumulative Mar 2–6) · 5 killed in Sidon (Mar 6) · IDF: 1 officer seriously wounded, 1 soldier moderately wounded (Givati Brigade) 🌍83,000+ displaced in Lebanon (official count, Mar 5 evening) · Lebanon cabinet orders IRGC arrest and deportation (PM Salam, Mar 6)
Sources: Al Jazeera Lebanon Times of Israel (IDF wounded) Alma Research Center Mar 6 UNIFIL / Wikipedia
Day 8 — March 7, 2026
7
Mar
US + Israel · CENTCOM Offensive · Day 8 — 3,000+ Targets Struck; 43 Iranian Warships Destroyed
CENTCOM: 3,000+ targets struck in Iran since Feb 28, 43 Iranian warships destroyed; Iran's ballistic missiles down 90%, drones down 83% vs Day 1; Israel deploys 80 jets in broad-scale wave
US Central Command released its Day 8 operational summary, reporting that coalition forces had struck more than 3,000 targets inside Iran since the opening of Operation Epic Fury on February 28, and that 43 Iranian naval warships had been destroyed — more than doubling the previously confirmed figure of 20+. CENTCOM reported Iran's ballistic missile attack rate had fallen by 90% versus Day 1, and drone attack rates had dropped by 83%, citing the destruction of Iran's missile production facilities, launcher stockpiles, and command infrastructure. The Israeli Air Force deployed 80 jets in a "broad-scale wave of strikes" against multiple Iranian targets on the morning of March 7. Financial analysis from the Congressional Budget Office assessed the first 100 hours of Operation Epic Fury had cost approximately $3.7 billion (roughly $891 million per day). US Energy Information Administration reported average national gasoline prices had risen to $3.32 per gallon, up $0.34 in a single week — the largest weekly increase since March 2022.
📍Multiple Iranian targets nationwide · Gulf of Oman · Arabian Sea ⚔️CENTCOM: 3,000+ targets struck · 43 warships sunk/destroyed · Israel: 80-jet broad-scale wave 🛡Iran ballistic missiles: down 90% vs Day 1 · Iran drones: down 83% vs Day 1 (CENTCOM) 💰Cost: $3.7B first 100 hrs (~$891M/day, CBO) · US gas avg: $3.32/gal (+$0.34/week, largest jump since Mar 2022)
Sources: Al Jazeera Day 8 CNN Day 8 NBC Live
7
Mar
Diplomatic · Regional Political · Trump demands unconditional surrender; Iran refuses talks; Gulf states hit again; 95,000+ displaced in Lebanon
Trump demands Iran's "unconditional surrender"; Iran's FM Araghchi: "not asking for ceasefire"; deputy FM warns Europe becomes "legitimate targets"; Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Bahrain, UAE hit by fresh Iranian drones; 95,000+ displaced in Lebanon
President Trump stated in a Truth Social post that any resolution to the Iran conflict required "unconditional surrender" from Tehran, calling any negotiated deal "a waste of time." Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian responded that Iran "will never capitulate" and rejected Trump's framing. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi told a press conference that Iran was "not asking for a ceasefire" and saw "no reason why we should negotiate," directly contradicting Trump administration claims that Iranian leaders had privately expressed a desire to talk. Iran's deputy foreign minister warned that European nations would become "legitimate targets" for retaliation if they formally joined US and Israeli military operations. Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Bahrain, and the UAE all experienced fresh waves of Iranian drone and missile attacks on the morning of March 7 — the ninth consecutive day of Iranian retaliatory strikes against Gulf states. UNRWA and Lebanese authorities reported that Israeli strikes in Lebanon had displaced more than 95,000 people, predominantly in southern Lebanon and the Bekaa Valley, with a significant portion of displacement attributed to Israeli Air Force operations targeting Hezbollah command infrastructure and IRGC supply routes.
📍Washington DC · Tehran · Gulf states (SA/Qatar/Bahrain/UAE fresh strikes) · Lebanon (95,000+ displaced) 🕊Trump: "unconditional surrender" only · Iran FM: "not asking for ceasefire" · Deputy FM: Europe = "legitimate targets" if they join conflict 💀95,000+ displaced in Lebanon (UNRWA/Lebanese authorities)
Sources: CNN Day 7 Al Jazeera Day 7 Newsweek Live Wikipedia / Hengaw
7
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Wave 27 / Operation True Promise 4
IRGC Launches 27th Wave with New Solid-Fuel Kheibar-Shekan Missiles
On March 8, the IRGC formally declared the commencement of its 27th offensive wave under Operation True Promise 4, targeting American and Israeli positions. The IRGC's Aerospace Division deployed newly developed solid-fuel Kheibar-Shekan ballistic missiles against Israeli military targets in Haifa, while drone units struck a site housing American personnel near Dubai's Marina district. Simultaneously, the IRGC Navy engaged US Fifth Fleet-linked facilities at Salman Port in Bahrain, claiming hits on command infrastructure for unmanned surface vessels and military support hangars. Iran stated its forces are 'lying in wait for American forces with precision and planning,' signaling readiness for prolonged escalation.
📍Haifa (Israel), Dubai Marina (UAE), Salman Port / Bahrain (5th Fleet area) 🚀New solid-fuel Kheibar-Shekan missiles + drone barrages (Wave 27) 💀Casualties under assessment; no confirmed coalition KIA reported yet
Sources: Tribune India / ANI Tribune India (IDF fuel hubs)
7
Mar
Iran · Diplomacy Political · Pezeshkian Apology + Regime Split
Iran President Apologizes to Gulf Neighbors; IRGC Immediately Contradicts Him
Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian issued a public televised apology to neighboring Gulf countries targeted by Iranian strikes, stating Iran would stop striking neighbors from March 7 onward unless attacked from their territory first. However, within hours, Iranian drones struck Dubai International Airport and a tower facade in Dubai Marina, and sirens sounded again across Bahrain and Qatar. Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf directly contradicted Pezeshkian, declaring that Gulf nations hosting US bases would continue to be targeted. Analysts flagged the contradiction as evidence of a significant fracture in the regime's unified command structure following Khamenei's killing. Trump dismissed the apology, calling Iran the 'loser of the Middle East' and warning Iran would be 'hit very hard.'
📍Tehran (statement); Dubai, Bahrain, Qatar (continued strikes) ⚠️Regime split: Pezeshkian vs. Ghalibaf contradicting ceasefire of neighbor strikes 💬Trump: Iran is 'loser of the Middle East'; vows Iran will be 'hit very hard'
Sources: Fox News Live (Mar 7) Wikipedia – 2026 Iran war Al Jazeera – Missiles intercepted Mar 7
7
Mar
US · Fallen Soldiers Ceremony · Dover AFB Dignified Transfer
VP Vance Receives Remains of 6 KIA Soldiers at Dover; Names Released
Vice President JD Vance attended the dignified transfer ceremony at Dover Air Force Base on March 7 as the flag-draped coffins of the six US service members killed in the Iranian strike on Kuwait's Port of Shuaiba were received. The fallen were identified as US Army Reserve members: Maj. Jeffrey O'Brien, Capt. Cody Khork, Chief Warrant Officer 3 Robert M. Marzan, Sgt. 1st Class Nicole Amor, Sgt. 1st Class Noah Tietjens, and Sgt. Declan Coady. All were killed in a single Iranian retaliatory strike on Kuwait in the opening days of Operation Epic Fury. The ceremony marked a solemn public milestone for American losses in the conflict.
📍Dover Air Force Base, Delaware — dignified transfer 💀6 KIA confirmed: Maj. O'Brien, Capt. Khork, CWO3 Marzan, SFC Amor, SFC Tietjens, Sgt. Coady 🇺🇸All members of the US Army Reserve; killed at Port of Shuaiba, Kuwait
Sources: Fox News Live (Mar 7)
7
Mar
IDF · Strike Offensive · Khamenei Bunker Destroyed / Mehrabad Airport
IDF Destroys Khamenei's Underground Bunker; Strikes Mehrabad Airport, Kills 16 Quds Aircraft
The Israeli military announced it had dismantled the underground bunker built for slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei beneath the leadership compound in central Tehran. Separately, the IDF struck Tehran's Mehrabad International Airport in the early hours of March 7, stating it destroyed 16 Quds Force aircraft on the ground. US and Israeli forces also struck oil facilities in Tehran for the first time, which the IDF claimed were used by the Iranian military. NDTV reported that Iran may now have only approximately 1,000 ballistic missiles remaining. IDF Chief of Staff Zamir confirmed Israel is now in the 'next phase' of the campaign, intensifying strikes on the regime's foundational pillars and military capabilities.
📍Tehran: Khamenei compound bunker; Mehrabad Airport; Tehran oil facilities ✈️16 IRGC Quds Force aircraft destroyed at Mehrabad Airport 📊Iran estimated to have ~1,000 ballistic missiles remaining (NDTV/analysts)
Sources: NBC News Live Blog (Mar 7) Wikipedia – 2026 Iran war (updated) Times of Israel – IDF next stage
7
Mar
US · Naval Naval · USS George H.W. Bush Deployed / 3rd Carrier
US Deploys Third Aircraft Carrier USS George H.W. Bush to Middle East
The United States deployed a third aircraft carrier, USS George H.W. Bush, to the Middle East theater, joining the USS Gerald R. Ford (which had transited from the Mediterranean to the Red Sea via the Suez Canal) and at least one other carrier already operating in the region. The Ford's arrival in the Red Sea was confirmed by Pentagon imagery released on Friday. The deployment dramatically expands US naval air power available for Operation Epic Fury and signals the administration's intent to sustain and escalate the campaign. Hegseth had already previewed a significant surge in firepower, stating more fighter squadrons, capabilities, and bomber pulses were forthcoming.
📍Middle East theater — Red Sea / Persian Gulf / Eastern Mediterranean ⚓3rd carrier: USS George H.W. Bush now deployed; USS Gerald R. Ford in Red Sea 💬Hegseth: 'more fighter squadrons, more capabilities, more bomber pulses more frequently'
Sources: Yahoo News / AP Wikipedia – 2026 Iran war
7
Mar
Lebanon · Displacement Humanitarian · 300,000+ Displaced; IDF Beirut Strikes
300,000+ Displaced in Lebanon; IDF Strikes Beirut Hotel, Dahiyeh as Hezbollah Continues Rocket Fire
NRC estimates at least 300,000 people have been displaced in Lebanon — less than 100 hours after Israel launched renewed airstrikes and mass evacuation orders in South Lebanon, Beirut, and the Bekaa Valley. Lebanese Social Affairs Ministry confirmed 83,847 people had registered as displaced with 399 shelters activated across the country. More than 95,000 people are in collective shelters. The IDF struck a Ramada hotel in Beirut's Rawche seaside area on March 8, and continued strikes on Hezbollah targets in Dahiyeh's southern suburbs. Lebanon's Health Ministry updated the death toll: 123 killed and 683 wounded since the escalation began March 2. An IDF ground incursion using forces from the 91st Division pushed into southern Lebanon to establish a 'security layer,' while Israel ordered the full evacuation of all areas south of the Litani River. Approximately 11,000 people crossed from Lebanon into Syria in a single 24-hour period on March 4 alone.
📍South Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, Beirut Dahiyeh, Rawche (hotel strike Mar 8) 🏠300,000+ displaced (NRC); 83,847 registered in shelters (Lebanese Social Affairs Ministry) 💀123 killed / 683 wounded in Lebanon since Mar 2 (Health Ministry) 🪖IDF 91st Division ground incursion underway south of Litani River
Sources: NRC – Lebanon Displacement Jerusalem Post – Thousands Displaced Fox News Live (Mar 7–8)
7
Mar
Diplomacy · Deadlock Political · Surrender Ultimatum
Trump Demands Iran's 'Unconditional Surrender'; Tehran Rejects Ceasefire, Vows to Fight On
On March 6–7, President Trump posted on Truth Social that there would be 'no deal with Iran except UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER,' dismissing any ceasefire or negotiated off-ramp. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt clarified the demand means Iran must no longer pose a threat before the US stands down. Trump separately told reporters he intended to influence the selection of Iran's next leader and that Mojtaba Khamenei — son of the slain Supreme Leader and a leading successor candidate — would be 'unacceptable.' Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi rejected the terms in an NBC News interview, stating Iran had not requested a ceasefire, was not engaged in back-channel talks, and was prepared for a possible US ground invasion. President Pezeshkian had hours earlier raised the possibility of mediation — only for Araghchi and IRGC-aligned parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf to immediately contradict him, with Ghalibaf insisting Gulf nations hosting US bases remain legitimate targets regardless of any presidential pledge.
