Analysts cited the contradiction as evidence of fracturing Iranian command coherence. Brent crude broke $90/barrel following Trump's surrender ultimatum.
📍Washington D.C. / Tehran — political / diplomatic track ⚠️Iran FM rejects ceasefire; Trump rules out negotiations; IRGC contradicts Pezeshkian apology to Gulf neighbors 💰Brent crude broke $90/barrel on Trump's Truth Social post (CNBC)
Sources: NPR – Trump Demands Unconditional Surrender ABC News – Trump Demands Surrender CNBC – No Deal Without Surrender Wikipedia – 2026 Iran War
7
Mar
Coalition · Naval Offensive · Naval Strike
CENTCOM Confirms 43+ Iranian Vessels Destroyed; Drone Carrier Shahid Bagheri Burns
CENTCOM released footage on March 6 showing the Iranian drone carrier Shahid Bagheri — roughly the size of a WWII aircraft carrier and operating as a floating UAV and ballistic missile launch platform in the Gulf — struck and on fire following US Navy strikes. CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper confirmed the US had destroyed over 30 ships as of March 5, with the running tally climbing to 43+ by Day 8. The Shahid Bagheri had been first struck on March 2, within hours of Operation Epic Fury's launch. CENTCOM separately confirmed the USS torpedo-sinking of the IRIS Dena (Moudge-class frigate) off the coast of Sri Lanka on March 4 — the first ship sunk by a US submarine since World War II — killing over 80 crew members. Secretary of War Hegseth declared the Iranian Navy 'combat ineffective.' The US has also struck Iran's 'equivalent of Space Command' per Cooper, and targeted multiple sites at Bandar Abbas Military Port including drone storage facilities and Artesh Navy dry docks.
📍Persian Gulf (Shahid Bagheri), Indian Ocean off Sri Lanka (IRIS Dena), Bandar Abbas Military Port 🚢43+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed or sunk (CENTCOM Day 8); IRIS Dena sunk by US submarine torpedo 💀80+ Iranian Navy crew killed in IRIS Dena sinking; 32 of 180 sailors rescued, 101 missing (Defense Update)
Sources: Naval News – Drone Carrier Strike Footage Navy Times – CENTCOM Update Mar 5 Defense Update – Epic Fury
7
Mar
Lebanon · Hezbollah Humanitarian · Displacement Update
Lebanon Displaced Tops 300,000+; IDF Tanks Mass Near Border, 13 IDF Soldiers Wounded
As of March 7, humanitarian agencies confirmed over 300,000 people displaced in Lebanon since hostilities resumed on March 2, with 95,773 registered in official shelters and 399 shelters open — 357 already at capacity. The Lebanese Health Ministry reported 339+ injured in Israeli strikes since March 2, with a separate tally of 102+ killed and 638 injured since escalation began (Disaster Risk Management Unit). Lebanon's total civilian death toll reached 123+ with Hezbollah figures still TBD. IDF tanks gathered in the upper Galilee near the Israel-Lebanon border on March 7, raising concerns about a potential ground incursion. Thirteen IDF soldiers were wounded in Hezbollah attacks since hostilities resumed on March 2, including five seriously wounded in a single rocket strike on an IDF position near the border on March 6. Hezbollah launched over 210 missiles at Israel per UNIFIL monitoring, while the IDF struck over 500 targets in Lebanon including 170 rocket launchers. Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem vowed 'We will not surrender no matter the sacrifices,' as Israel issued fresh evacuation warnings to Beirut's southern suburbs including Bourj el-Barajneh, Hadath, Haret Hreik and Chiyah.
📍Southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs (Dahiyeh), northern Israeli border 💀123+ Lebanese civilians killed / Hezbollah figures TBD; 13 IDF soldiers wounded since Mar 2 🏚️300,000+ displaced in Lebanon; 399 shelters open, 357 at capacity (NRC / Lebanese MoSA)
Sources: NRC – Lebanon 300,000 Displaced Times of Israel Liveblog Mar 7 Al Jazeera – Displacement Crisis Wikipedia – 2026 Hezbollah–Israel War
Day 9 — March 8, 2026
8
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile & Drone Wave
Iran Fires 9th Ballistic Missile Wave at Israel in 24 Hours; Gulf States Hit Again
Iranian forces continued their multi-vector retaliation campaign into Day 9, launching a ninth wave of ballistic missiles toward Israel in a single 24-hour period, with sirens sounding across the Galilee and central Israel. No casualties were reported from the latest salvo, which struck open areas. Concurrently, Qatar reported a fresh drone attack wave of 10 drones from Iran, with 9 intercepted. The UAE, Bahrain, and Saudi Arabia also activated air defenses against incoming Iranian missiles and drones. A Saudi interceptor destroyed a drone northeast of Riyadh. IRGC drone attacks targeted al-Dhafra airbase in the UAE, with Iran claiming strikes on US satellite communication centers and radar arrays, though the UAE did not confirm damage. Iran's overall ballistic missile volume has dropped 90% and drone attacks 83% since Day 1, per CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper, but sporadic multi-country barrages continued. NDTV citing intelligence sources estimated Iran may have only ~1,000 ballistic missiles remaining.
📍Israel (Galilee, central), UAE (al-Dhafra AB), Qatar (Doha), Saudi Arabia (Riyadh), Bahrain 💀No casualties reported in latest waves; total Iranian launches since Feb 28: 500+ ballistic missiles, ~2,000 drones (CENTCOM Day 5) 📉Iranian ballistic missile rate down 90%, drone rate down 83% vs. Day 1 (CENTCOM)
Sources: Times of Israel Liveblog Mar 7 Al Jazeera – Iranian Missiles Intercepted Navy Times – CENTCOM Drone Carrier
8
Mar
Coalition · Phase II Strikes Offensive · Strategic Bombing
IDF Strikes IRGC Missile Factory at Shahrud; US B-2s Drop Penetrator Bombs on Buried Launchers
In a major Phase II escalation overnight into March 8, the IDF struck an IRGC ballistic missile production facility at Shahrud — located approximately 2,000 km from Israel — describing it as the site where 'most of the missiles fired at Israel were manufactured.' Hundreds of Israeli Air Force jets participated in the strike package. Simultaneously, US B-2 Spirit stealth bombers dropped dozens of 2,000-pound penetrator bombs on deeply buried Iranian ballistic missile launchers. CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper confirmed that in the prior 72-hour window alone, US bombers struck nearly 200 targets deep inside Iran. The IDF assessed the combined strikes at Parchin and Shahrud set Iran's missile production capability 'back years.' The IDF also confirmed it struck IRGC-linked oil infrastructure in Tehran for the first time and hit additional IRGC internal security sites. IDF Chief of Staff Gen. Zamir confirmed 80% of Iran's air defense systems and 60%+ of ballistic missile launchers had been destroyed by this phase, with the IAF having used over 6,000 munitions across 2,500+ strikes.
📍Shahrud (IRGC missile plant), Parchin Military Complex, Tehran oil infrastructure, western/central Iran 💥IRGC primary ballistic missile factory destroyed; dozens of 2,000-lb penetrator bombs used; 80% of Iran's air defenses destroyed (IDF) 🛩️Hundreds of IAF jets + US B-2 bombers employed; IDF total: 2,500+ strikes, 6,000+ munitions since Feb 28
Sources: Times of Israel Liveblog Mar 7 Stars and Stripes – CENTCOM Update FDD Long War Journal
8
Mar
Iran · Leadership Fracture Diplomatic · Internal Rupture
Pezeshkian Apologizes to Gulf States; IRGC Defies Order, Strikes Continue
On Day 8 (March 7), Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian issued an unprecedented televised apology to Gulf neighbors, stating Iran's Interim Leadership Council had approved a halt to strikes on regional countries unless attacks on Iran originated from their soil. He attributed prior strikes to 'miscommunication in the ranks' following the death of Supreme Leader Khamenei. However, within hours, Iran's IRGC and armed forces spokesman contradicted the president, continuing strikes on Dubai, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, and Kuwait. Hardline cleric and lawmaker Hamid Rasai publicly condemned Pezeshkian's statement as 'unprofessional, weak and unacceptable.' When Pezeshkian later re-posted his statement on social media, the apology had been quietly removed — a visible climbdown. Senior ayatollahs simultaneously urged the Assembly of Experts to urgently name a new supreme leader to restore unified command.
📍Tehran / Gulf-wide — Pezeshkian address broadcast on Iranian state TV ⚠️IRGC defied president's order; strikes on Gulf continued hours after apology 🏛️Iran's Interim Leadership Council: Pezeshkian, Chief Justice Mohseni-Ejei, Ayatollah Arafi
Sources: NPR CBC News Al Jazeera
8
Mar
Israel · Phase 2 Strikes Offensive · Energy Infrastructure
IDF Strikes Tehran & Alborz Oil Depots for First Time; Massive Fires Ignite
On the night of March 7 into Day 9 (March 8), Israel launched strikes on fuel storage and oil infrastructure in Tehran and Alborz Province for the first time since the start of Operation Epic Fury / Roaring Lion. The IDF said it targeted 'fuel storage facilities in Tehran' used to 'operate military infrastructure.' Strikes hit four oil storage facilities and an oil production transfer center, igniting massive fires visible across the capital. At least four tanker drivers were killed in the attacks, per Fars News Agency. A dark haze hung over Tehran on Sunday morning. Iran's National Oil Refining and Distribution Company confirmed multiple facilities were struck. Israeli PM Netanyahu stated Israel and the US had achieved 'almost complete control' over Iranian skies and warned of 'many more targets and surprises prepared.'
📍Shahran oil depot (Tehran outskirts), Karaj (Alborz Province), and four additional fuel storage facilities in Tehran 💀At least 4 tanker drivers killed (Fars News Agency); no IDF casualties reported 🔥Multiple large fires burning across Tehran; black rain reported falling on the city Sunday morning
Sources: Al Jazeera CNN CBS News
8
Mar
Iran · Succession Political · Supreme Leader Selection
Iran's Assembly of Experts Reaches 'Consensus' on New Supreme Leader; IDF Vows to Strike Successor
On March 8 (Day 9), a member of Iran's Assembly of Experts, Ayatollah Mohammad-Mahdi Mirbagheri, announced that the body had reached a 'decisive and unanimous opinion' on Khamenei's successor, without naming the candidate. State media reported the choice was guided by the late Supreme Leader's advice that the new leader should be 'hated by the enemy.' Israel immediately issued a warning that any named successor to Khamenei would be treated as a target, consistent with IDF Chief Zamir's earlier declaration that the next phase of the campaign would strike 'the regime's pillars.' The announcement signals a critical inflection point: Iran's battered clerical structure is attempting to restore centralized command amid an active bombardment campaign targeting its leadership infrastructure.
📍Assembly of Experts — location undisclosed amid ongoing IDF strikes on leadership buildings ⚠️IDF warned: any new supreme leader will be immediately targeted 🏛️Mojtaba Khamenei (Khamenei's son) reported frontrunner; Trump called it 'unacceptable'
Sources: NBC News CNN Al Jazeera
8
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Gulf & Israel Strikes
Iran Fires Continued Waves at Israel, Gulf; Bahrain Desalination Plant Struck; Lebanon Kills ~300+
Despite Pezeshkian's apology, Iranian strikes continued on Day 9 across the region. An Iranian drone attack caused material damage to a water desalination plant in Bahrain. Iran's missiles and drones targeted Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and the UAE. In Israel, CBS News reported very loud explosions in Tel Aviv as Iran launched fresh missile waves, with IDF defense systems working to intercept. Rocket trails were photographed over Netanya. In Lebanon, cumulative IDF strikes since March 2 have killed approximately 300+ people and wounded hundreds more, with nearly 400 casualties per NBC News tally. Al Jazeera reported over 300 dead in Lebanon in total. Iran's FM Araghchi told NBC News' Meet the Press that Iran needs 'to continue fighting for the sake of our people,' and pressed on Russian support, confirmed 'they are helping us in many different directions.' Trump said the war would end only when Iran's leaders 'cry uncle' or their military is no longer functional.
📍Bahrain (desalination plant), Tel Aviv & Netanya (Israel), Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE, Beirut (Lebanon) 💀~300+ killed in Lebanon total (Al Jazeera/NBC); Bahrain desalination plant: material damage, 3 injured 🛡️IDF defense systems active over Tel Aviv; rocket trails visible over Netanya (March 8 morning)
Sources: Al Jazeera Live NBC News CBS News
8
Mar
Iran · Succession Political · Supreme Leader Named
Iran Names Mojtaba Khamenei as Third Supreme Leader; IDF Vows to Target Him
Iran's Assembly of Experts announced on March 8, 2026 — Day 9 of the war — that Mojtaba Khamenei, 57-year-old second son of assassinated Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, had been elected as the Islamic Republic's third Supreme Leader by a decisive majority vote. The announcement came just after midnight local time, following days of internal IRGC pressure on assembly members and significant disagreements over Mojtaba's lack of religious credentials. The IRGC immediately pledged full allegiance. The IDF warned it would target the new leader. Trump called the selection 'unacceptable,' calling Mojtaba a 'lightweight,' and said he 'is not going to last long' without US approval. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi separately rejected any ceasefire on NBC's Meet the Press, saying Iran would 'continue fighting for the sake of our people.'
📍Tehran — Assembly of Experts (online session); announcement broadcast on IRIB state TV ⚠️IDF warns successor is a military target; Trump demands input in leadership selection
Sources: NBC News Reuters / Yahoo News NPR
8
Mar
US · Casualties Defensive · KIA Update
7th US Service Member Dies; Pentagon Confirms Wounds from Saudi Arabia Strike
On March 8, 2026, US Central Command announced the death of a seventh American service member, who succumbed to injuries sustained during an Iranian attack on US troops in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia on March 1. CENTCOM stated: 'Last night, a U.S. service member passed away from injuries received during the Iranian regime's initial attacks across the Middle East.' A separate report indicated the Pentagon simultaneously announced an additional National Guard soldier died from a health-related incident in Kuwait, potentially raising the total further. The name of the service member killed in Saudi Arabia was withheld pending next-of-kin notification. President Trump, who had presided over a dignified transfer ceremony for the six earlier KIAs, acknowledged more casualties were expected, saying 'it's a part of war.'
📍Saudi Arabia (attack origin: March 1) — death announced March 8 💀7+ US KIA confirmed; 18+ seriously wounded (CENTCOM)
Sources: CNN CBS News Live
8
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Wave Strikes on Gulf + Kuwait City
IRGC Drones Strike Kuwait International Airport Fuel Depot; Gulf Wave Continues Despite Pezeshkian Pledge
On the morning of March 8 (Day 9), Iran launched fresh waves of drones and missiles against Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE — directly contradicting President Pezeshkian's apology and pledge to halt attacks on Gulf neighbors made the day prior. In Kuwait, the Kuwaiti Army confirmed a 'wave of hostile drones' targeted fuel storage at Kuwait International Airport, with shrapnel and debris from interceptions damaging civilian infrastructure. Kuwait's Public Institution for Social Security building was separately struck by a drone and caught fire, with a roughly 22-story building filmed in flames. Iran's IRGC described its latest salvo on Israel as the '27th wave of Operation True Promise.' Saudi Arabia reported its first confirmed war deaths: two foreign nationals (Indian and Bangladeshi) were killed when a military projectile struck a residential area in Al-Kharj, with 12 others wounded. Dubai International Airport was also struck. The IRGC's defiance of Pezeshkian's ceasefire gesture underscored continuing fractures in Iranian command coherence.
📍Kuwait City (airport fuel depot + Social Security building); Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia; Dubai, UAE; Bahrain; Qatar 💀2 killed in Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia (first Saudi war deaths); multiple injuries across Gulf states
Sources: CNN Live Al Jazeera PBS NewsHour
8
Mar
Israel · Casualties Defensive · First IDF Soldiers Killed in Lebanon
First Two IDF Soldiers Killed in Southern Lebanon Combat Since Hezbollah Hostilities Resumed
Israel's military confirmed on March 8, 2026 that two IDF soldiers were killed in southern Lebanon — the first Israeli troop fatalities since the renewed Hezbollah conflict began on March 2. The soldiers were engaged in combat against Hezbollah positions south of the Litani River. In parallel, Iran launched multiple ballistic missile salvos at Israel throughout the day, with air raid sirens activated across southern Israel including the Negev Desert. Three Israeli civilians were injured by shrapnel in an afternoon strike. Israel also struck a hotel in Beirut's seafront Raouche district, killing four people and injuring ten, as Israeli forces targeted what they described as IRGC Quds Force commanders planning 'terror attacks against Israel.' Lebanese Health Ministry figures rose to approximately 397 killed and hundreds injured since hostilities began March 2.
📍Southern Lebanon front + Raouche hotel, Beirut (Quds Force targeted); missile impacts in southern Israel 💀2 IDF soldiers KIA (first since Mar 2 escalation); 4 killed in Beirut hotel strike; 3 Israeli civilians injured
Sources: Al Jazeera Day 9 Explainer Times of Israel Liveblog Mar 8 NPR
8
Mar
Iran · Nuclear Intelligence · Enriched Uranium Seizure Discussed
US & Israel Discuss Special Forces Mission to Seize Iran's Enriched Uranium Stockpile
Axios reported on March 8 that the United States and Israel have discussed the possibility of deploying special operations forces to physically seize Iran's stockpile of highly enriched uranium. According to the report, the mission would likely take place 'at a later stage' of the war. Separately, CSIS and IAEA analysts confirmed that US strikes have already struck the Iran Atomic Energy Agency headquarters in Tehran, the Parchin explosive research facility, and conducted further strikes at the Isfahan nuclear complex, though no radiological effects have been confirmed. CENTCOM also confirmed striking Iran's 'equivalent of Space Command.' The potential seizure mission would represent a dramatic escalation of the conflict's nuclear dimension.
📍Iran nuclear sites: Tehran (IAEA HQ), Parchin, Isfahan, Natanz (entrance buildings struck) ⚠️No radiological release confirmed; IAEA monitoring; special forces seizure mission at 'later stage' per Axios
Sources: Axios / Al Jazeera CSIS
8
Mar
Iran · Leadership Political · Succession
Iran Names Mojtaba Khamenei Third Supreme Leader; IRGC Pledges Allegiance
Iran's Assembly of Experts, under significant pressure from the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, formally appointed Mojtaba Khamenei — the 56-year-old son of slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei — as the Islamic Republic's third supreme leader. Iranian state media broadcast the announcement on Sunday, March 8. The IRGC immediately pledged 'complete obedience and self-sacrifice' to the new leader. Trump, who had previously called Mojtaba 'unacceptable,' warned his tenure would be 'short-lived' without U.S. approval. The IDF responded with a statement in Farsi vowing it 'will pursue every successor and every person who seeks to appoint a successor,' calling it an explicit warning to those involved in the selection process.
📍Tehran — Assembly of Experts (online session) ⚠️IDF explicitly threatens Mojtaba Khamenei; Trump calls him a 'lightweight'
Sources: Times of Israel NPR CNBC
8
Mar
US · Casualties KIA · Non-Combat & Combat Wounds
8th U.S. Service Member Dies; Total KIA Rises to 8
The Pentagon confirmed two additional U.S. military deaths on March 8. The seventh casualty was a service member who succumbed to wounds sustained during an Iranian attack on U.S. troops in Saudi Arabia on March 1. The eighth death was Major Sorffly Davius, an NYPD officer serving with the 42nd Infantry Division of the U.S. Army National Guard, who died from a medical episode at Camp Buehring, Kuwait, on March 6. All eight deaths have been Army soldiers. The six soldiers killed in the Kuwait drone strike on March 1 were honored in a dignified transfer at Dover Air Force Base on March 7, attended by President Trump, Vice President Vance, and Defense Secretary Hegseth. CENTCOM continues to report 18+ seriously wounded throughout the operation.
📍Saudi Arabia (wounds) & Camp Buehring, Kuwait (medical) 💀8 total U.S. KIA; 18+ seriously wounded (CENTCOM)
Sources: CNN Anadolu Agency Newsweek
8
Mar
Israel · Strike Offensive · Air Strike
IAF Destroys Iranian F-14 Tomcat Fleet at Isfahan; Strikes IRGC Oil Depots
In a major symbolic and operational blow on March 7–8, the Israeli Air Force conducted a broad wave of strikes against the 8th Tactical Fighter Base at Isfahan Shahid Beheshti International Airport, destroying several Iranian F-14 Tomcat fighter jets — believed to be virtually the entirety of Iran's remaining operational F-14 fleet, the world's last. The IDF said it also hit detection and air defense systems threatening Israeli aircraft operating over Iran. This followed a separate IAF strike on March 7 at Tehran's Mehrabad Airport that destroyed 16 Quds Force aircraft used for weapons transfers to Hezbollah and Hamas. Separately, Israel targeted multiple IRGC-affiliated fuel storage complexes in Tehran on March 8, triggering large fires and toxic smoke across the Iranian capital. The IDF stated the strikes 'significantly deepen the damage to the military infrastructure of the Iranian terrorist regime.'
📍Isfahan Airport (8th TFB) · Mehrabad Airport, Tehran · IRGC fuel depots, Tehran ✈️Iranian F-14 fleet likely destroyed; 16 Quds Force aircraft eliminated at Mehrabad
Sources: The Aviationist CBS News NPR
8
Mar
Israel · Strike Offensive · Targeted Kill
IDF Strikes 5 Senior IRGC Quds Force Commanders in Beirut Hotel
The IDF announced it conducted a targeted strike in Beirut on March 8 against 'key commanders in the IRGC's Quds Force's Lebanon Corps.' Lebanese health authorities reported four people killed in a strike on a hotel in a popular tourist area of Beirut. The IDF confirmed five senior Iranian commanders were eliminated, including Majid Hassini (senior financier of Iranian proxy funds to Hezbollah and Hamas), Ali Reza Bi-Azar (chief of intelligence, Lebanon Corps), Ahmad Rasouli (chief of intelligence, Palestine Corps), Hossein Ahmadlou (intelligence operative, Lebanon Corps), and Abu Muhammad Ali (Hezbollah's representative in the Palestine Corps). IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir stated: 'There is no safe place for the arms of Iranian evil, anywhere in the Middle East.'
📍Beirut, Lebanon — hotel in central tourist district 💀5 senior IRGC Quds Force Lebanon Corps commanders confirmed killed
Sources: Times of Israel ABC News CBS News
8
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Multi-Vector Strike
Iran Strikes Bahrain Desalination Plant, Kuwait Airport, UAE amid Day 9 Barrage
Despite Iranian President Pezeshkian's earlier pledge on March 7 to halt strikes on neighboring Gulf states (unless attacked from their territory), Iranian strikes continued into March 8 across the region. Bahrain reported damage to a desalination plant and a university building. Kuwait confirmed a government building and part of its international airport were hit. The UAE reported incoming missile and drone threats, with Iranian drones again targeting UAE infrastructure. In Bahrain, an Iranian drone attack on Sitra island injured several citizens, one seriously, and damaged houses. Iran launched ten missile attack waves at Israel between March 6 and March 7 alone, according to CTP-ISW. Saudi Arabia also confirmed two civilian deaths from Iranian counterattacks on March 8.
📍Bahrain (Sitra island, desalination plant) · Kuwait International Airport · UAE · Saudi Arabia 💀2 killed in Saudi Arabia; multiple injured in Bahrain; Kuwait airport partially struck
Sources: CNBC Al Jazeera Critical Threats
8
Mar
US · Diplomacy Diplomatic · Evacuation
State Dept. Orders Non-Essential Diplomats Out of Saudi Arabia; 32,000+ Americans Evacuated
The U.S. State Department on March 8 ordered the mandatory evacuation of non-emergency government employees and their families from Saudi Arabia, upgrading earlier voluntary departure guidance due to ongoing Iranian strikes on the Kingdom. Saudi Arabia was designated Level 3 'Reconsider Travel.' Separately, the State Department confirmed that more than 32,000 American citizens have safely evacuated the Middle East since the war began on February 28. The U.S. embassy in Saudi Arabia remains operational with core staff. The State Department also expanded its crisis intake form to now cover Bahrain and Kuwait in addition to UAE, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and Israel.
📍Saudi Arabia (mandatory non-emergency diplomatic evacuation) 🛫32,000+ Americans safely returned to U.S. since Feb. 28
Sources: ABC News ABC News (State Dept.)
Day 10 — March 9, 2026
9
Mar
Iran · Day 10 Barrage Offensive · Multi-Country Missile & Drone Wave
Iran Strikes Bahrain Bapco Refinery, Launches Day 10 Barrage Across Gulf
On March 9 (Day 10), Iran launched a fresh wave of missiles and drones targeting Gulf nations hours after naming Mojtaba Khamenei as the country's new Supreme Leader. Bahrain's state-owned energy company Bapco declared force majeure on its group operations after its refinery complex on Sitra Island was struck and caught fire. Bahrain's state news agency confirmed at least 32 people were injured, including children, in an Iranian strike on the island. Officials in Fujairah, UAE, reported a fire at an oil facility struck overnight. Saudi Arabia said it intercepted several drones attacking the Shaybah oil field and its Foreign Ministry warned it retained 'full right to take all necessary measures to safeguard its security.' Qatar condemned the killing of two civilians in Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia, from an Iranian projectile. Oil prices spiked to nearly $120/barrel on the news.
📍Bahrain (Sitra/Bapco Refinery), UAE (Fujairah oil facility), Saudi Arabia (Shaybah oil field, Al-Kharj residential area) 💀32+ injured in Bahrain; 2 civilians killed in Saudi Arabia; UAE/Qatar casualties under assessment 🛢️Bapco force majeure declared; Fujairah oil facility fire; Shaybah drones intercepted
Sources: Al Jazeera NPR
9
Mar
Iran · Leadership Political · New Supreme Leader Named
Iran Names Mojtaba Khamenei as New Supreme Leader; Oil Spikes to ~$120
On March 8–9, Iran's Assembly of Experts elected Mojtaba Khamenei, son of slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, as Iran's new Supreme Leader. Iran's top political figures — including President Pezeshkian, parliament speaker Ghalibaf, and security chief Larijani — pledged allegiance to him. The announcement immediately triggered a new wave of Iranian strikes on Israel and Gulf states, and sent crude oil prices surging to nearly $120/barrel, the highest since the conflict began. Israel launched a new wave of strikes on Monday targeting infrastructure in central Iran in direct response to the succession. Israel's Defence Minister Katz had previously warned that the new supreme leader would 'become a target.' Trump, who had called Mojtaba Khamenei a 'lightweight' and called his succession 'unacceptable,' said he should have had a say in appointing Iran's new leader.
📍Tehran — Assembly of Experts; oil markets worldwide 👤Mojtaba Khamenei elected Supreme Leader; pledges of loyalty from Pezeshkian, Ghalibaf, Larijani 📈Crude oil spikes to ~$120/barrel; US stocks trim early drop amid market volatility
Sources: NPR AP / Britannica Wikipedia
9
Mar
Israel · Coalition Strike Offensive · IAF Infrastructure Wave
IAF Launches New Wave on Central Iran Infrastructure After Khamenei Succession
Following Iran's naming of Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader, Israel launched a new wave of airstrikes on Monday targeting infrastructure in central Iran. The strikes followed Sunday's IAF bombardment of Iranian oil facilities for the first time in the conflict — strikes on depots near Tehran which caused the city to be engulfed in thick black smoke, producing what Iranian authorities described as 'dangerous black acid rainfall.' Residents were advised to stay indoors and wear masks. The Lebanese parliament also extended its term by two years as Israel intensified attacks on Lebanon. NATO separately confirmed the interception of a ballistic missile that entered Turkish airspace on March 9.
📍Central Iran infrastructure; Tehran oil depots (Day 9); Turkey airspace (NATO intercept, Mar 9) 💀4 killed in first-ever IAF strikes on Iranian oil facilities (Day 8); casualties from Day 9 wave TBD 🛢️Tehran engulfed in black smoke from burning oil depots; acid rain warning issued
Sources: Al Jazeera AP / Britannica
9
Mar
US · KIA Casualty · 7th KIA Identified
Pentagon Identifies 7th KIA: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 1st Space Brigade
The Pentagon on March 9 (Day 10) identified the seventh US service member killed in the Iran war as Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26, of Glendale, Kentucky. Pennington died on Sunday, March 8, from injuries sustained during the Iranian missile attack on a US military base in Saudi Arabia on March 1. He was assigned to the 1st Space Battalion, 1st Space Brigade. His death was announced one day after Trump paid tribute at a dignified transfer ceremony at Dover Air Force Base for the six soldiers of the 103rd Sustainment Command killed in Kuwait. A separate CENTCOM announcement noted that a US National Guard soldier, Maj. Sorffly Davius, 46, of Queens, NY, died in a health-related incident at Camp Buehring, Kuwait, on March 6; that incident remains under investigation. The dashboard KIA figure of 8+ reflects the confirmed combat death of Pennington and the Kuwait health incident.
📍Saudi Arabia (Pennington KIA, Mar 1 wound); Camp Buehring, Kuwait (Davius, health incident Mar 6) 💀7th confirmed combat KIA: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 1st Space Brigade; 8th death: Maj. Sorffly Davius (non-combat, under investigation)
Sources: NPR CNN
9
Mar
Lebanon · Displacement Humanitarian · Mass Displacement Crisis
Lebanon: 700,000 Displaced; Lebanese Parliament Extends Term 2 Years; Israel Intensifies Strikes
As of March 9, nearly 700,000 people — including approximately 200,000 children — have been displaced in Lebanon, according to UNICEF, as Israeli airstrikes continue across the country. Lebanon's Social Affairs Minister confirmed 517,000 people had registered on the government's relief platform as of March 8, with 117,228 in 538 official shelters. Lebanon's parliament voted to extend its term by two years amid the ongoing conflict. Israel launched fresh strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahiyeh) and south Lebanon on March 9. Lebanese Health Ministry figures as of March 6 reported 217 killed and 798 wounded since hostilities escalated on March 2. The figure of 390+ killed in Lebanon as of March 9 per Al Jazeera represents the most current estimate. Fierce clashes were reported around Nabi Chit, where an Israeli operation over the weekend killed 41 people.
📍South Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs (Dahiyeh), Nabi Chit 💀390+ killed in Lebanon, 1,000+ wounded (Al Jazeera, Mar 9); 700,000 displaced (UNICEF) 🏛️Lebanese parliament extends term 2 years; IDF issues third mass evacuation order south of Litani River
Sources: Al Jazeera UN News Xinhua AP / Britannica
9
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Drone & Missile Barrage
Iran Strikes Bahrain Bapco Refinery; Bapco Declares Force Majeure
On March 9, Iran launched a fresh wave of missiles and drones targeting Gulf energy infrastructure. The Bahrain Petroleum Company (Bapco) declared force majeure on all group shipments after its refinery on Sitra Island was struck and caught fire. Smoke was visible for miles as the blaze was eventually brought under control. Bahrain's state news agency confirmed the attack, with images of the burning refinery circulating widely. Qatar, the UAE, and Saudi Arabia also reported additional Iranian drone and missile intercepts during the same barrage. Gulf airspace remained heavily disrupted, with oil production and supply chains under severe strain. The IRGC claimed responsibility for the attack, describing it as retaliation for continued US-Israeli strikes on Iranian soil.
📍Sitra Island, Bahrain — Bapco Refinery Complex 💀No casualties reported; Bapco force majeure declared on all shipments
Sources: Al Jazeera
9
Mar
Iran · Leadership Political · Supreme Leader Succession
Mojtaba Khamenei Elected Supreme Leader; Iran Rejects Surrender Demand
On March 9, Mojtaba Khamenei — eldest son of assassinated Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei — was formally named Iran's new Supreme Leader following a vote by the Assembly of Experts. Iran's top political and military leadership, including President Pezeshkian, IRGC commander Mohammad Pakpour, and Majlis speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, quickly pledged allegiance. Trump, who had previously called Mojtaba a 'lightweight' and stated his selection would be 'unacceptable', renewed his call for Iran's unconditional surrender on Truth Social. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi rejected any ceasefire or negotiations, telling NBC News 'we need to continue fighting for the sake of our people.' Oil prices surged toward $120/barrel before pulling back sharply as markets priced in the succession uncertainty. Russia's President Putin called Trump to discuss the war, with the Kremlin confirming the conversation.
📍Tehran, Iran — Assembly of Experts Session ⚡Oil spiked to ~$120/bbl before retreating below $90; Trump-Putin phone call confirmed
Sources: Britannica NBC News
9
Mar
Israel · Offensive Offensive · Infrastructure Strikes
IAF Launches New Wave on Central Iran Infrastructure After Khamenei Succession
Following the announcement of Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader, Israel launched a fresh wave of airstrikes targeting infrastructure in central Iran. Israeli Defense Minister Katz had previously warned that Iran's new supreme leader would become 'a target,' and the IDF pledged to go after any successor. The weekend strikes (March 6–7) had already included over 400 targets and approximately 1,465 munitions dropped on Iranian regime military sites, including ballistic missile launchers and weapons production facilities. In Tehran, more than 80 IAF fighter jets dropped approximately 230 munitions. The IDF also struck Iran's two most important ballistic missile production sites in the Parchin and Shahroud areas, assessing that the damage set Iran's missile production capability back by years. Strikes on oil depots in Tehran caused a thick cloud of black smoke over the city, prompting Iranian authorities to warn residents to stay indoors.
📍Central Iran — Tehran, Parchin, Shahroud, Karaj oil depots 💥400+ targets hit over Mar 6–7 weekend; 1,465+ munitions; Parchin & Shahroud missile sites heavily damaged
Sources: Alma Research Center Times of Israel
8
Mar
US · KIA Casualty · Identification
Pentagon Identifies 7th KIA: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 1st Space Brigade
CENTCOM confirmed on March 8 that a US soldier wounded during an Iranian attack in Saudi Arabia on March 1 had succumbed to their injuries, bringing the confirmed US KIA total to seven. The service member, identified as Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington of the 1st Space Brigade, passed away after being seriously wounded at the scene of the Iranian strike on US troops in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. CENTCOM also announced separately that Maj. Sorffly Davius, 46, a New York City police officer and Army National Guard soldier from Queens, New York, died at Camp Buehring, Kuwait on March 6 from a health-related incident during a medical emergency; that incident remains under investigation. The six earlier KIA were all assigned to the 103rd Sustainment Command, an Army Reserve unit from Iowa, killed in the Iranian strike on the makeshift operations center at Shuaiba port in Kuwait on March 1.
📍Saudi Arabia (March 1 attack); Kuwait Camp Buehring (Davius, March 6 incident) 💀7 confirmed combat KIA; 18+ seriously wounded (CENTCOM)
Sources: CNN Al Jazeera
9
Mar
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · Mass Displacement
Lebanon: 700,000+ Displaced; 390+ Killed; Israel Intensifies Strikes
The International Rescue Committee issued an emergency flash alert on March 9 confirming that over 700,000 people have been forced from their homes in Lebanon amid escalating hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah. UNICEF reported that approximately 200,000 of the displaced are children. Lebanese health authorities confirmed at least 390 people killed and more than 1,000 injured since the US-Israel war on Iran began on February 28, with the heaviest toll in southern Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs. Israel struck a Beirut hotel on Sunday targeting five IRGC Quds Force commanders who were meeting there, and continued strikes around the town of Nabi Chit. Lebanon's National News Agency reported fierce clashes around Nabi Chit, where an Israeli ground operation over the weekend killed 41 people. The Lebanese Parliament voted to extend its term by two years, citing the security emergency. France's President Macron publicly called on Israel not to expand the war to Lebanon and urged Hezbollah to renounce its weapons.
📍Southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs — nationwide displacement 💀390+ Lebanese killed, 1,000+ injured; 700,000+ displaced (IRC, UNICEF, Mar 9)
Sources: IRC Al Jazeera
9
Mar
CENTCOM · KIA Confirmed Personnel · 8th US KIA Confirmed
Maj. Sorffly Davius Dies at Camp Buehring; US Death Toll Reaches 8
CENTCOM announced Sunday, March 9, that US National Guard soldier Maj. Sorffly Davius, 46, of Queens, New York, died at Camp Buehring, Kuwait, on March 6 following a health-related incident during a medical emergency. The incident is under investigation. Davius was also a New York City police officer since 2014. His death brings the confirmed US military death toll in Operation Epic Fury to eight, one day after Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26, of Glendale, Kentucky — assigned to the 1st Space Battalion, 1st Space Brigade, Fort Carson — was identified as the 7th KIA, having died on March 8 from injuries sustained during an Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia on March 1.
📍Camp Buehring, Kuwait (Davius) / Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia (Pennington) 💀Total US KIA: 8 confirmed — 6 Army Reserve (Kuwait, Mar 1), Sgt. Pennington (Saudi Arabia), Maj. Davius (Kuwait, non-combat health incident)
Sources: CNN Al Jazeera Tracker
9
Mar
Iran · First Strike Under New Leader Offensive · Missile & Drone Wave
Iran Launches First Missile Wave Under Mojtaba Khamenei; Strikes Hit Gulf States
Following the announcement of Mojtaba Khamenei as Iran's new Supreme Leader on March 9, Iran immediately launched a fresh wave of missile and drone attacks across the Gulf. Iranian attacks struck or targeted Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE. In Bahrain, 32 people — including children as young as 2 months old — were injured in an Iranian drone attack on the island of Sitra south of Manama. Fires broke out at an oil facility in Fujairah, UAE. Saudi Arabia intercepted drones attacking its Shaybah oil field. Iranian missiles continued to rain on Israel, with debris from interceptions reported in three areas. Israel said on March 9 that Hezbollah in Lebanon had now launched more attacks against Israel over the past week than Iran itself had.
📍Sitra Island, Bahrain; Fujairah, UAE; Shaybah, Saudi Arabia; Israel 💀32 injured in Bahrain (incl. children); fires at UAE oil facility; no confirmed fatalities in this wave
Sources: NPR Al Jazeera
9
Mar
IDF · Escalation Wave Offensive · Wide-Scale Strike Wave on Central Iran
IDF Destroys IRGC Air Force HQ; Launches 'Wide-Scale' Strikes After Mojtaba Named Leader
In direct response to Iran naming Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader, Israel launched a new broad wave of strikes on central Iran, targeting infrastructure including IRGC command nodes, ballistic missile sites, and the IRGC Air Force headquarters in Tehran, which the IDF announced it had fully destroyed. Israeli strikes also continued in southern Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahiyeh), killing and wounding scores. An Israeli strike hit central Beirut, targeting a building in the Ghobeiry municipality. Human Rights Watch released a report documenting Israeli use of white phosphorus in residential areas of southern Lebanon, which HRW stated violates international humanitarian law.
📍Tehran (IRGC Air Force HQ); Isfahan; central Iran; Dahiyeh, Beirut; Ghobeiry, Beirut 💀Lebanon death toll: 390+ killed, 1,000+ wounded since Feb 28 per Al Jazeera/IRC
Sources: Al Jazeera Gulf News
9
Mar
Trump · Intelligence Disclosure Political · New Nuclear Site Revealed
Trump Reveals Iran Was Building Granite-Hardened New Nuclear Site Before War
At a press conference on the sidelines of a House Republican conference in Doral, Florida on March 9, President Trump disclosed that a key trigger for Operation Epic Fury was Iranian construction of a new nuclear weapons development site 'protected by granite.' Trump stated Iran was reconstituting its weapons program at a new location after Operation Midnight Hammer destroyed its Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan facilities in June 2025. 'They couldn't go back to where they were — the three sites that we obliterated — but they were starting work at another site, a different site, different kind of a site, and that was protected by granite,' Trump said. Trump also stated he was 'disappointed' with Iran's selection of Mojtaba Khamenei as the new Supreme Leader and had a call with Russian President Vladimir Putin to discuss the war.
📍Doral, Florida (press conference); undisclosed site in Iran (new nuclear facility) ⚠️Trump: Iran was 'going to take over the Middle East' without US intervention; called new leader selection a source of disappointment
Sources: Washington Examiner Washington Post
9
Mar
Iran · Diplomatic Diplomatic · FM Rejects Ceasefire on Meet the Press
Araghchi on NBC 'Meet the Press': Iran Rejects Ceasefire, Cites Russian Intelligence Support
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi appeared on NBC's 'Meet the Press' on March 9, again firmly rejecting any ceasefire or negotiations with the United States, declaring Iran 'needs to continue fighting for the sake of our people.' Araghchi also confirmed that Russia is 'helping in many different directions,' including by providing intelligence on the location of US assets. The disclosure of Russian intelligence assistance marks a significant escalation in third-party involvement. Iran's FM rejected Trump's 'unconditional surrender' demand and said Iran is prepared for any US ground invasion scenario, calling such a move a 'waste of time' for the US.
📍NBC 'Meet the Press' / Tehran ⚠️Russia confirmed providing intelligence support to Iran per four NBC News sources; Araghchi: 'They are helping us in many different directions'
Sources: NBC News Soufan Center
9
Mar
Lebanon · Humanitarian Crisis Humanitarian · Displacement Surpasses 700,000
Lebanon: Over 700,000 Displaced; IRC Issues Emergency Alert; 390+ Killed
As of March 9, the International Rescue Committee issued a Watchlist flash emergency alert for Lebanon, reporting that more than 700,000 people — including approximately 200,000 children — have been forced to flee their homes, per IRC and UNICEF figures. The Lebanese Health Ministry confirmed at least 390 people killed and over 1,000 injured in Israeli strikes since hostilities resumed on March 2. Israeli evacuation orders cover the entire area south of the Litani River (home to approximately 500,000 people), Beirut's southern suburbs, and the Bekaa Valley — orders reissued for a third time. Fierce clashes were reported around Nabi Chit in the Bekaa, where an Israeli operation over the weekend killed 41 people. Israel struck a Beirut hotel Sunday, targeting five IRGC Quds Force commanders who had gathered there.
📍South Lebanon, Beirut (Dahiyeh, Ghobeiry), Bekaa Valley; Nabi Chit; Central Beirut hotel 💀390+ civilians killed in Lebanon; 700,000+ displaced; 5 IRGC Quds Force cmds. killed in Beirut hotel strike
Sources: IRC Emergency Alert Al Jazeera
10
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Day 11 Missile & Drone Barrage
Iran Launches New Wave at Israel & Gulf on Day 11; Bahrain Residential Strike Kills One
On the morning of March 10 (Day 11), Iran launched fresh waves of missiles and drones at Israel and multiple Gulf Arab countries. Air-raid sirens sounded in Dubai and Bahrain; in Bahrain, an Iranian strike hit a residential building in the capital Manama, killing a 29-year-old woman and wounding eight others. Saudi Arabia destroyed two drones over its oil-rich eastern region, and Kuwait's National Guard shot down six drones. Sirens later sounded in Jerusalem and explosions were heard in Tel Aviv as Israeli air defenses intercepted incoming fire. Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf posted defiantly: 'We are definitely not looking for a ceasefire.'
📍Manama (Bahrain), Dubai (UAE), Jerusalem & Tel Aviv (Israel), eastern Saudi Arabia, Kuwait 💀1 civilian killed (Bahrain residential strike), 8 wounded
Sources: AP / Military.com 1011 Now
10
Mar
CENTCOM · Day 10 Summary Operational Update · 10-Day Strike Milestone
CENTCOM: 5,000+ Targets Struck, 50+ Iranian Ships Damaged or Destroyed in First 10 Days
U.S. Central Command released a comprehensive 10-day operational summary for Operation Epic Fury, announcing that coalition forces have struck more than 5,000 targets across Iran and damaged or destroyed over 50 Iranian naval vessels since operations began on February 28. Targets included command and control centers, IRGC headquarters and intelligence sites, integrated air defense systems, ballistic missile launch sites, ships and submarines, anti-ship missile positions, military communications, and ballistic missile and drone manufacturing facilities. The summary confirmed the deployment of B-1, B-2, and B-52 bombers; F-15, F-16, F/A-18, F-22, and F-35 fighter jets; A-10s; EA-18G Growlers; MQ-9 Reapers; HIMARS; Patriot and THAAD systems; and nuclear-powered aircraft carriers. Defense Secretary Hegseth stated Tuesday that today would be 'our most intense day of strikes inside Iran — the most fighters, the most bombers, the most strikes.'
📍Iran-wide — Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz, southern Iran and Persian Gulf 🎯5,000+ targets struck; 50+ naval vessels damaged or destroyed (CENTCOM, Day 10)
Sources: Middle East Monitor Fox News Live WION News
9
Mar
Trump · Diplomatic / Strategic Political · Trump Declares War 'Very Complete'
Trump: War 'Very Complete, Pretty Much' — U.S. 'Very Far' Ahead of Schedule
In a phone interview with CBS News on March 9, President Trump declared that the U.S. war against Iran is 'very complete, pretty much,' asserting that Iran 'has no navy, no communications, no air force' and that its missiles are 'down to a scatter.' Trump added that the U.S. is 'very far' ahead of his initial four-to-five-week estimated timetable, while stopping short of declaring a formal end to operations. He also told House Republicans at a conference in Doral, Florida that 'we haven't won enough,' signaling contradictory signals about the conflict's endpoint. Trump threatened that if Iran disrupted oil flow through the Strait of Hormuz, it would be hit 'TWENTY TIMES HARDER.' Separately, Defense Secretary Hegseth said Tuesday that Day 11 would be the operation's 'most intense day of strikes inside Iran.'
📍Trump National Doral, Miami, FL (press conference) / CBS News phone interview 🛢️Brent crude briefly hit $119.50/barrel Monday before dropping ~14% after Trump's CBS remarks
Sources: CBS News NPR Fox News Live
9
Mar
IDF · Strikes Offensive · IDF Renews Wave on Tehran & Southern Iran
IDF Launches 'Extensive' Strikes on Tehran, Isfahan & Southern Iran; US Attacks South
The Israeli Air Force launched a renewed wave of airstrikes on March 9–10 against Iranian regime targets in Tehran, Isfahan, and the Shiraz area, using dozens of fighter jets and over 170 munitions. The IDF stated the strikes targeted IRGC command centers — including a Quds Force command center in Tehran responsible for coordinating Iran's regional proxy groups — and a missile production facility in Isfahan. It marked the 15th wave of IDF strikes on Iran's capital since the war began. Separately, for the first time in the conflict, the IDF announced it was striking targets in southern Iran, a zone until then handled exclusively by U.S. forces. The Israeli government simultaneously announced a two-week extension of emergency wartime restrictions, pushing measures to March 26. Ambassador Zarka said Israel is 'ahead of schedule' in reaching its war objectives.
📍Tehran (IRGC Quds Force HQ), Isfahan (missile production), Shiraz area, southern Iran 🎯170+ munitions dropped; 15th wave on Tehran; first IDF strike in southern Iran
Sources: Haaretz (live) Times of Israel IDF / liveuamap
8
Mar
Iran · Oil Infrastructure Offensive · Iran Strikes Bahrain Bapco Refinery; Bapco Declares Force Majeure
Bahrain's Bapco Refinery Hit by Iranian Missiles; State Oil Firm Declares Force Majeure
Iranian missiles and drones struck Bahrain's Sitra island on March 8–9, hitting the Bapco oil refinery and setting it ablaze. Bahrain's state-owned energy company Bapco declared force majeure on its group operations citing the 'ongoing regional conflict in the Middle East and the recent attack on its refinery complex.' At least 32 people were injured in Iranian strikes on Sitra island, including children. The Bapco refinery fire was the most significant energy infrastructure strike in the Gulf to date. Separately, a fire also broke out at a UAE oil facility after an Iranian overnight attack, and Saudi Arabia intercepted drones targeting its Shaybah oil field. Iran's strikes came despite President Pezeshkian's March 7 announcement that Iran would cease striking Gulf neighbors unless attacked from their territory — a pledge immediately broken.
📍Sitra Island, Bahrain (Bapco refinery); UAE oil facility (Fujairah region); Saudi Shaybah oil field 💀32+ injured in Sitra strikes including children; force majeure declared on Bapco operations
Sources: Al Jazeera NPR
8
Mar
IDF · Controversy Diplomatic Incident · Israeli Fuel Depot Strikes Spark US-Israel Rift
Israel Strikes 30 Tehran Fuel Depots; U.S. Blindsided — 'WTF' Response from Washington
On the night of March 8 (Day 8), the Israeli Air Force struck approximately 30 Iranian fuel depots in and around Tehran without full U.S. coordination, creating the first significant public rift between the two allies since the war began. Large fires raged across Tehran, visible for miles, blanketing the capital in black smoke that triggered acid rainfall warnings. While the IDF notified the U.S. military in advance, a senior U.S. official said Washington was 'surprised by how wide-ranging they were,' and an Israeli official acknowledged the U.S. message was 'WTF.' A Trump adviser told Axios: 'The president doesn't like the attack. He wants to save the oil.' U.S. officials worried that footage of burning depots could spook oil markets — Brent crude had already hit $119.50/barrel — and backfire strategically by rallying the Iranian public to support the regime.
📍Tehran fuel depots — 30 sites struck; fires visible city-wide; acid rainfall advisory issued ⚠️First US-Israel allied friction of the conflict; US concerned over oil market impact and strategic blowback
Sources: Axios PBS NewsHour
9
Mar
US · Casualty Casualty Report · 7th US KIA Identified
Pentagon Identifies 7th U.S. KIA: Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, 1st Space Brigade
The Pentagon announced on March 9 that Sergeant Benjamin N. Pennington, 26, of Glendale, Kentucky, died of wounds sustained in an Iranian strike at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia on March 1. He was assigned to the 1st Space Battalion, 1st Space Brigade. He is the seventh U.S. service member to die in the conflict. The first six were all Army Reservists from the 103rd Sustainment Command, Des Moines, Iowa, killed in a drone strike on a makeshift command center at the civilian port of Shuaiba, Kuwait on March 1. VP Vance said, 'If you are the praying type, I hope you'll spare a prayer for the six souls that we lost, for the seventh soul who will be coming home tonight.' Total confirmed U.S. KIA now stands at 7, with 18+ seriously wounded.
📍Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia (Sgt. Pennington) / Shuaiba Port, Kuwait (first 6 KIA) 💀7 confirmed US KIA total; 18+ seriously wounded (CENTCOM)
Sources: CNN NPR Fortune
10
Mar
US/Israel · Offensive Offensive · Air Strikes
Hegseth: Mar 10 to Be 'Most Intense Day' of Strikes on Iran; Five Iranian Salvos Hit Israel
US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth declared Tuesday the 'most intense day' of strikes on Iran yet, as US forces dramatically surged strike sorties across Iranian territory. CENTCOM confirmed the 10-day cumulative total has now surpassed 5,000 targets struck. In parallel, the IDF reported at least five separate Iranian missile salvos targeting Israel throughout the morning. The IDF confirmed ballistic missile launches from Iran targeting central Israel, with sirens sounding across Tel Aviv, Jerusalem, and parts of the West Bank. Iranian missiles carrying cluster munition warheads — assessed by the IDF at roughly 50% of all ballistic missiles fired at Israel — continued to cause casualties; two workers were killed on March 9 in Yehud from cluster submunition impacts. Hegseth also noted that in the past 24 hours, Iran had fired the lowest volume of missiles since the war began, attributing the decline to the cumulative degradation of Iranian launch capabilities. Joint Chiefs Chairman Gen. Dan Caine separately confirmed that missile attacks are down 90% and one-way attack drones down 83% since Day 1.
📍Iran-wide · Central Israel · Tel Aviv · Jerusalem 💀Iran: 1,255+ killed, ~10,000 injured (Iranian state media, Mar 10); Israel: 14+ killed, 2,339+ injured (Alma Center, Mar 10)
Sources: PBS NewsHour Alma Research Center Al Jazeera
10
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Drone & Missile Strikes
Iran Fires Drones at Saudi Arabia & Kuwait; UAE Intercepts 9 Ballistic Missiles; Haifa Refinery Targeted
On the morning of March 10 (Day 11), Iran continued its retaliatory campaign with drone barrages against Saudi Arabia and Kuwait. UAE air defences intercepted nine Iranian ballistic missiles — eight destroyed, one fell into the sea — plus engaged 35 drones, of which 26 were intercepted and nine fell within UAE territory. A fire broke out at the Ruwais Industrial Complex in Abu Dhabi following a drone strike. The Iranian Army separately announced a drone attack targeting an Israeli oil and gas refinery and fuel tanks in Haifa. Iranian officials also warned that Tehran would deploy naval mines in the Strait of Hormuz, with US intelligence beginning to see preliminary indications of such preparations. The IRGC stated it would 'determine the end of the war' in defiance of Trump's claim that the conflict was nearly over. On the night of March 9, an Iranian UAV struck Bahrain's Millennium Tower in the Al-Seef business district in Manama, killing one woman and injuring eight others.
📍Saudi Arabia · Kuwait · UAE (Ruwais) · Bahrain (Manama, Millennium Tower) · Haifa, Israel · Strait of Hormuz 💀Bahrain: 1 killed, 8 injured in Millennium Tower strike (Mar 9 overnight); UAE: no injuries reported at Ruwais (Mar 10)
Sources: Euronews Live Al Jazeera Day 11 Alma Research Center Haaretz Live
10
Mar
Israel · Offensive Offensive · Air Strikes (Lebanon)
IDF Strikes Hezbollah Stronghold in Beirut; Advances in Southern Lebanon; 486+ Killed in Lebanon
The IDF struck Hezbollah positions in Beirut's southern suburbs on March 10, continuing a sustained campaign across Lebanon as displacement figures neared 700,000. The IDF 36th Division (including the 7th Armored Brigade) conducted a targeted raid in Rab el Thalathine, southern Lebanon, destroying Hezbollah infrastructure and killing Hezbollah fighters. The 401st Brigade (91st Division) also conducted simultaneous operations. Senior Israeli officials warned that Hezbollah seeks to 'wear down' the IDF via increased missile and drone fire in northern Israel, and that the group retains significant launch capability. The Lebanon death toll from Israeli strikes surpassed 486, including 83 children, according to the Lebanese Health Ministry. France called an emergency UN Security Council meeting for March 11, urging Hezbollah to disarm and calling on Israel to avoid land-based interventions. The UN reports close to 700,000 displaced, with 100,000 newly displaced in a single 24-hour period.
📍Beirut southern suburbs (Dahiyeh) · Southern Lebanon (Rab el Thalathine, south of Litani River) 💀Lebanon: 486+ killed (incl. 83 children), ~700,000 displaced (Lebanese MoH / UNICEF, Mar 10)
Sources: Al Jazeera Tracker Times of Israel Liveblog Critical Threats / CTP-ISW UN News
10
Mar
US · KIA Update Personnel · Casualty Update
8th US Soldier Confirmed Killed; 150+ US Troops Wounded — Haaretz/Reuters
An eighth US service member has been confirmed killed in the Gulf region, confirmed by Al Jazeera's casualty tracker on March 10. This follows the March 9 identification of Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26, of Glendale, Kentucky — assigned to the 1st Space Battalion, 1st Space Brigade — who died of wounds sustained in a March 1 Iranian strike on a US base in Saudi Arabia. Separately, two sources reported to Haaretz that 150 US soldiers have been wounded in total since the war began on February 28, significantly higher than the previously stated '18+ seriously wounded' figure. The US has confirmed the six troops killed at Shuaiba port in Kuwait (103rd Sustainment Command, Army Reserve) and a seventh via CENTCOM (a National Guard soldier who died of a health-related incident in Kuwait on March 6), bringing the confirmed KIA total to at least eight.
📍Saudi Arabia (Sgt. Pennington) · Kuwait (6 × 103rd Sustainment Command) · Kuwait (1 × NY National Guard) 💀8+ US KIA confirmed; 150+ US wounded (two sources, Haaretz Mar 10)
Sources: Al Jazeera Casualty Tracker Haaretz Live Mar 10 CNN
10
Mar
US/Israel · Strike Milestone Offensive · CENTCOM Update
CENTCOM: 5,000+ Targets Struck in First 10 Days; 50+ Iranian Vessels Destroyed
CENTCOM officially announced that US forces struck over 5,000 targets in Iran during the first 10 days of Operation Epic Fury. President Trump separately confirmed the figure, stating the US has struck more than 5,000 targets, resulting in the destruction of Iran's navy and air force and 80–90% of its missile capability. CENTCOM also reported damage to or destruction of more than 50 Iranian vessels. Targets encompassed command-and-control centres, IRGC headquarters, IRGC intelligence sites, ballistic missile locations, anti-ship missile installations, nuclear laboratories in Tehran, and drone manufacturing facilities. Among the assets deployed: B-1, B-2, and B-52 bombers, F-15s, F-16s, F-18s, F-22s, Patriot and THAAD air defence systems, and the first-ever combat use of the Low-cost Uncrewed Combat Attack System (LUCAS). A CENTCOM post also confirmed targeting an underground missile launcher with video evidence. Iran's ballistic missile attacks are down 90% and drone attacks down 83% since Day 1, per CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper.
📍Iran-wide · Tehran · Isfahan · Shiraz · Mehrabad · Parchin · IAEA HQ Tehran (unconfirmed) 💀5,000+ targets struck · 50+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed · 90% Iranian missile capability degraded (CENTCOM)
Sources: Air Force Technology WION News Al Jazeera Day 11
9
Mar
Iran · Leadership Political · New Supreme Leader
Mojtaba Khamenei Elected Iran's New Supreme Leader; IRGC and Regime Leadership Pledge Allegiance
On March 8–9, 2026, Mojtaba Khamenei — the 56-year-old son of assassinated Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei — was elected Iran's new supreme leader by the Assembly of Experts, becoming only the third supreme leader in the history of the Islamic Republic. The IRGC, President Masoud Pezeshkian, Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, and Ali Larijani all publicly pledged allegiance. Trump expressed disappointment, saying it would 'lead to just more of the same problem,' and stated it would be 'inappropriate' to say whether the new leader would be targeted. Israel's Defense Minister Israel Katz previously warned that any new supreme leader who continued Iran's agenda 'will be a target for elimination.' Mojtaba has close ties to the IRGC's paramilitary wing and survived an earlier Israeli airstrike during the war.
📍Tehran, Iran · Assembly of Experts session 💀N/A — political transition event
Sources: NPR Times of Israel Al Jazeera
10
Mar
US/Israel · Intelligence Intelligence · Strait of Hormuz
US Intel: Iran May Deploy Naval Mines in Strait of Hormuz; Ship Attacked Off UAE Coast
US intelligence assets began detecting indications on March 10 that Iran may be taking steps to deploy naval mines in the Strait of Hormuz shipping lane, a CBS News reporter reported citing US officials. A separate incident was reported by the UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) centre: a bulk carrier in the Persian Gulf off the UAE coast reported a 'splash and loud bang in close proximity,' suggesting a possible Iranian attack on commercial shipping. The Strait of Hormuz has already been effectively blockaded, with only ships with Iranian connections reported passing as of March 9. Earlier, Iran threatened to block oil flows until attacks ended.
📍Strait of Hormuz · Persian Gulf (off UAE coast) 💀No confirmed casualties in Hormuz mine incident — investigation ongoing
Sources: Haaretz / Reuters Euronews Live
10
Mar
CENTCOM · Strait of Hormuz Offensive · Naval Interdiction
US Destroys 16 Iranian Minelayers Near Strait of Hormuz
The US military announced on March 10 that it had destroyed Iranian naval vessels near the Strait of Hormuz — including 16 minelayers — as Tehran had begun laying mines in the key waterway. CNN reported that Iran's IRGC, which effectively controls the strait alongside Iran's traditional navy, had the capability to deploy a 'gauntlet' of dispersed mine-laying craft, explosive-laden boats, and shore-based missile batteries. The channel has been effectively closed since the start of the war. CENTCOM's action brought the total tally of Iranian vessels damaged or destroyed to over 50, per earlier reporting from Air Force Technology citing CENTCOM's own Day 10 update.
📍Strait of Hormuz, Persian Gulf 🚢16 minelayers destroyed; Iranian Navy total now 50+ vessels sunk/damaged (CENTCOM)
Sources: CNN
10
Mar
US/Israel · Hegseth Offensive · Escalation Declaration
Hegseth Declares March 10 'Most Intense Day of Strikes' on Iran
US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth announced on March 10 that the day would be the 'most intense day of strikes' against Iran since Operation Epic Fury began. The declaration came as Chairman of the Joint Chiefs Gen. Dan Caine confirmed at a Pentagon briefing that the operation had entered its 11th day, with Iranian ballistic missile launches down 90% and one-way attack drones down 83% from Day 1. Hegseth noted that 'the last 24 hours have seen Iran fire the lowest amount of missiles they have fired yet,' signaling continued degradation of Iran's retaliatory capabilities. Strikes on March 10 focused on infrastructure targets across central Iran in response to the election of Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader.
📍Pentagon / Iran — central and infrastructure targets 💀Iranian missile launch rate: down 90% (ballistic) / down 83% (drones) vs. Day 1 (CENTCOM)
Sources: PBS NewsHour NBC News Live
10
Mar
Iran · Hormuz Threat Offensive · Economic Warfare
Iran Threatens Full Strait of Hormuz Blockade; Bapco Declares Force Majeure
As Operation Epic Fury entered its 10th day, Iran intensified economic warfare operations in and around the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian IRGC officials warned that any ship passing through the strait would be attacked, and the channel has effectively been closed since the start of the war. Bahrain's state-owned energy company Bapco declared force majeure on its group operations after waves of Iranian strikes targeted its energy installations and its refinery caught fire. Abu Dhabi's state oil giant was also forced to shutter a refinery following a drone attack. Saudi Aramco CEO Amin Nasser confirmed tankers were being rerouted to avoid the Strait of Hormuz, and warned that the 'situation at the Strait of Hormuz is blocking sizable volumes of oil from the whole region.'
📍Strait of Hormuz; Bahrain (Bapco refinery, Sitra Island); Abu Dhabi ⚠️Bapco force majeure declared; Abu Dhabi refinery shuttered; 150+ freight ships stalled in Hormuz
Sources: Al Jazeera Times of Israel
10
Mar
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · Displacement Crisis
Nearly 700,000 Displaced in Lebanon; 486+ Killed as IDF Expands Operations
The humanitarian situation in Lebanon deteriorated dramatically through March 10. The UN Refugee Agency reported that nearly 700,000 people — including approximately 200,000 children — have been displaced from their homes since Hezbollah joined the war on March 2 following the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei. The Lebanese Health Ministry confirmed the death toll from Israeli strikes rose to 486 with 1,313 people injured. Israel issued a third round of evacuation orders covering the entire area south of the Litani River, while also ordering evacuations of Beirut's southern suburbs for the second time. More than 240 displaced civilians were expelled from the Christian-majority village of Rmeish in south Lebanon after an Israeli soldier warned local leaders via phone call that their village would be struck if any displaced persons communicated with Hezbollah. Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem vowed 'we will not surrender no matter the sacrifices' in a televised address.
📍South Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs (Dahiyeh), Rmeish 💀486+ Lebanese killed, 1,313 injured (Lebanese Health Ministry, Mar 10); ~700,000 displaced (UNHCR/IRC)
Sources: The National UN News IRC
10
Mar
Iran · Diplomatic Diplomatic · Rejection of Ceasefire
Iran Vows to Fight 'As Long As It Takes'; Araghchi Rules Out Negotiations
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi escalated his diplomatic rhetoric on March 10, vowing that Iran was 'well prepared to continue attacking them with our missiles as long as needed and as long as it takes.' Araghchi effectively ruled out any negotiations with Washington, saying Tehran had 'a very bitter experience of talking with Americans' and that 'I don't think talking with Americans anymore would be on our agenda.' Iran's top security official Ali Larijani separately dismissed Trump's threats as 'empty' and issued a personal warning to the US president: 'Watch out for yourself — lest you be eliminated.' This came hours after Trump reiterated on Truth Social that 'if Iran does anything that stops the flow of Oil within the Strait of Hormuz, they will be hit by the United States of America TWENTY TIMES HARDER than they have been hit thus far.'
📍Tehran / Washington — diplomatic exchange via media ⚠️Iran rejects ceasefire; Larijani threatens Trump personally; Trump warns 20x escalation over Hormuz
Sources: Times of Israel NBC News
10
Mar
CENTCOM / Env. · Nuclear Intelligence · Nuclear Sites
Rosatom Evacuates ~100 Staff from Bushehr; US Strikes IAEA HQ & Parchin Reported
As the conflict expanded to peripheral nuclear targets, Russia's Rosatom evacuated nearly 100 staff from Iran's Bushehr nuclear reactor on March 10, though personnel primarily Russian remain on-site to keep the reactor operational. Unconfirmed reports cited by CSIS indicate that the United States struck the Iran Atomic Energy Agency headquarters in Tehran and the explosive research testing facility at Parchin — the latter having been struck a second time since February 28 per satellite imagery confirmed by Critical Threats/ISW on March 4. Further strikes at the Isfahan nuclear complex were also reported. Environmental watchdog CEOBS reported over 300 environmental incidents identified as of March 10, including severe pollution following Israeli attacks on four oil facilities in Tehran over the March 7–8 weekend, exposing the city's nine million residents to dangerous pollution levels.
📍Bushehr (reactor), Tehran (IAEA HQ), Parchin Military Complex, Isfahan nuclear complex ⚠️Rosatom evacuates ~100 staff; Bushehr reactor still operational; Parchin struck 2nd time confirmed by satellite imagery (CEOBS/CTP-ISW)
Sources: CSIS CEOBS
10
Mar
CENTCOM · Naval Action Offensive · Minelayer Destruction
US Destroys 16 Iranian Minelayers Near Strait of Hormuz
On March 10, US Central Command announced the destruction of 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels near the Strait of Hormuz, as Tehran had begun covertly laying mines in the critical waterway. CENTCOM released video footage of the strikes. The action came as Iran continued to threaten a full blockade of the Strait, through which nearly one-fifth of global oil flows, and as Trump warned Iran would be hit 'TWENTY TIMES HARDER' if oil shipments were disrupted. The destruction of the minelayers was part of what Hegseth declared the 'most intense day of strikes' on Iran.
📍Strait of Hormuz, Persian Gulf ⚓16 mine-laying vessels destroyed; Iran had begun active mining of the waterway
Sources: Navy Times CNN Britannica
10
Mar
Iran · IRGC Offensive Offensive · Missile & Drone Barrage
IRGC Declares 'Most Intense' Attack of War; Fires Khorramshahr Ballistic Missiles at Israel
Iran's IRGC announced it had launched its 'most intense and heaviest operation' since the conflict began on the night of March 10–11. State broadcaster IRIB confirmed the salvo included long-range Khorramshahr ballistic missiles targeting Israel and US assets in the region. Gulf states reported interceptions of new drone and missile waves overnight. Saudi Arabia confirmed destruction of at least seven drones in recent hours, with its defense ministry tallying 262 Iranian missiles detected since the war began, of which 241 were destroyed, 19 fell into the sea, and two landed in UAE territory. Israel's military confirmed its defense systems were engaging the incoming threats. The IRGC statement read: 'The war will end only when the shadow of war is removed from our country.'
📍Israel, Gulf states (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait) 🚀Khorramshahr long-range ballistic missiles confirmed; 262 total Iranian missiles detected since Feb 28 per Saudi MoD
Sources: CNN Live NBC News Live
10
Mar
IDF · Beirut Strike Offensive · Beirut Airstrike
IDF Strikes Ghobeiry, Beirut; Israel Hits Second Wave of Targets in Tehran Overnight
On the night of March 10–11, Israel launched a second wave of strikes in Tehran and simultaneously struck the Ghobeiry municipality in Beirut's southern suburbs, with a loud explosion captured on verified footage. Israel also struck areas of Beirut overnight, with CNN's team in Tehran reporting heavy aerial bombardment. The strikes on Lebanon came as Israeli forces continued to expand operations, with Lebanon's Health Ministry recording 486 killed and 1,313 injured since hostilities began on March 2. Nearly 700,000 people have now been displaced in Lebanon according to the UN refugee agency, including approximately 200,000 children. Hezbollah was described by sources as 'rationing munitions' as it braced for the possibility of a full-scale Israeli ground invasion.
📍Ghobeiry, Beirut southern suburbs; Tehran, Iran 💀486 killed, 1,313 injured in Lebanon total (Lebanese Health Ministry, Mar 10); ~700,000 displaced (UNHCR)
Sources: Al Jazeera The National UN News
10
Mar
Pentagon · Casualty Update Administrative · Wounded Figures Released
Pentagon Reports ~140 US Troops Injured in Iran War; War Now in Day 11
The Pentagon disclosed to Congress that approximately 140 US troops have been injured in the Iran war, with 8 severely wounded — the first comprehensive injury tally released since the conflict began. The figure is higher than previous estimates by outside analysts. Seven US soldiers have been confirmed killed in action, including six Army reservists killed in the March 1 drone attack at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait, and Sgt. First Class Pennington, who died on March 8 after being wounded at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia. A dignified transfer for the 7th US KIA was held on March 10. CENTCOM and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs Gen. Dan Caine confirmed at a March 10 press conference that Iranian missile attacks have fallen 90% and one-way drone attacks have decreased 83% since the war's opening day, now in its 11th day.
🏥~140 US troops injured total (Pentagon to Congress); 8 severely wounded 💀7 US KIA confirmed as of March 10; 8th KIA previously reported unconfirmed
Sources: PBS NewsHour Time PBS (Hegseth)
11
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile & Drone Barrages
Iran Fires at Gulf States; Dubai Airport Targeted Again
On March 11, Iran continued its wave of missile and drone strikes against Gulf states. Iran's IRGC targeted Dubai International Airport and commercial shipping, with AP reporting 'Iran fires at Gulf states' as of 06:39 AM ET. Saudi Arabia's Defense Ministry confirmed it had intercepted and destroyed multiple incoming drones in recent hours, with 262 Iranian missiles detected since the start of the war, of which 241 were destroyed. Nearly 1,400 drones have also been intercepted by Saudi defenses. Gulf airspace remained severely disrupted, with passenger planes grounded at Dubai International Airport.
📍Dubai International Airport, Gulf-wide 💥262 Iranian missiles detected by Saudi Arabia; 241 destroyed; ~1,400 drones intercepted region-wide
Sources: AP via Britannica Live Al Jazeera Tracker
11
Mar
IDF · Wide-Scale Strike Wave Offensive · Air Strikes
IDF Begins Wide-Scale Strike Wave on Iran and Hezbollah Targets in Beirut
On March 11, the Israel Defense Forces announced it had begun a 'wide-scale wave' of strikes targeting Iranian terror regime infrastructure across Iran, as well as Hezbollah-related targets in Beirut, Lebanon. The IDF stated: 'The IDF has begun a wide-scale wave of strikes targeting Iranian terror regime infrastructure across Iran.' A fireball was observed rising from an Israeli airstrike that targeted Beirut's southern suburbs overnight March 10–11. CNN's team in Tehran reported heavy aerial bombardment overnight, to which Iran retaliated with fresh strikes against Israel.
📍Tehran, Iran and Ghobeiry / southern suburbs of Beirut, Lebanon 💥Wide-scale IDF wave; overnight bombardment confirmed by CNN and ABC7
Sources: ABC7 News CNN Live Updates
10
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Ballistic Missile Strike
Bahrain: Woman Killed, 8 Injured as Residential Building Struck in Manama
On March 10, a 29-year-old woman was killed and eight people were injured when a residential building in Bahrain's capital Manama was struck during an Iranian attack, according to Bahrain's Ministry of Interior. The strike hit a civilian residential building in Manama, underscoring Iran's continued targeting of Gulf civilian areas. Bahrain's state oil company Bapco separately declared force majeure on its group operations after waves of Iranian strikes targeted its Sitra Island refinery, causing fires.
📍Manama, Bahrain (residential building) & Sitra Island (Bapco refinery) 💀1 killed (29-year-old woman), 8 injured; Bapco force majeure declared
Sources: Al Jazeera Tracker Al Jazeera — Gulf strikes
11
Mar
Lebanon · Displacement Humanitarian · Displacement Crisis
~700,000 Displaced in Lebanon; AP Reports Rare Public Backlash Against Hezbollah
As of March 11, nearly 700,000 people — including approximately 200,000 children — have been displaced in Lebanon since Hezbollah joined the war on March 2, according to the UN refugee agency. Lebanon's Health Ministry confirmed the death toll from Israeli attacks had risen to 570 killed and 1,444 wounded. The Associated Press reported on March 11 that Lebanon's latest conflict has brought 'rare public backlash against Hezbollah as war flares again,' reflecting unusual public anger in Lebanon. Over 240 displaced people were also expelled from the Christian-majority village of Rmeish in south Lebanon after Israeli threats. The Bapco oil refinery in Bahrain and other regional infrastructure continued to burn.
📍Lebanon (south, Bekaa Valley, Beirut suburbs); ~700,000 displaced per UNHCR 💀570 killed, 1,444 wounded in Lebanon (Health Ministry, Mar 10)
Sources: The National AP via Britannica Live UN News
11
Mar
Iran · Wave 37 Offensive · Multi-theater Missile & Drone Barrage
IRGC Declares 'Most Intense Operation' — Wave 37 Strikes Gulf, Ship Hit in Strait of Hormuz
On the morning of March 11 (Day 12), Iran's IRGC announced its 37th wave of attacks, declaring it 'the most intense and heaviest operation since the start of the war.' Missiles and drones struck targets across Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE. In Bahrain, dozens — including children — were wounded near Sitra/Ma'ameer, with a fire breaking out at a facility after a drone strike. In the UAE, two Iranian drones fell near Dubai International Airport for the second time, injuring four foreign nationals. Saudi Arabia intercepted six Iranian missiles near Prince Sultan Air Base, with intercept booms heard near Dammam. Kuwait's National Guard shot down eight drones. Oman downed two drones at sea north of Al Duqm. Separately, the UK Maritime Trade Operations agency reported a container vessel struck by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, some 46km northwest of Ras Al-Khaimah, setting it on fire.
📍Bahrain (Sitra/Ma'ameer), UAE (Dubai Airport), Saudi Arabia (Prince Sultan AB / Dammam), Kuwait, Oman, Strait of Hormuz 💥Wave 37 — self-described by IRGC as most intense yet; container vessel hit and ablaze in Strait of Hormuz 💀Dozens wounded in Bahrain incl. children; 4 injured near Dubai Airport; no confirmed mass-casualty event as of filing
Sources: Al Jazeera Euronews
11
Mar
CENTCOM · Naval Offensive · Iranian Fleet Destruction
CENTCOM: 60+ Iranian Ships Destroyed — Entire Soleimani-Class Warship Class Eliminated
CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper announced on March 11 that since the start of Operation Epic Fury on February 28, US forces have targeted and destroyed more than 60 Iranian naval vessels, significantly weakening Iran's naval combat power. Among the confirmed kills, Cooper stated: 'We also took out the last of four Soleimani-class warships — that's an entire class of Iranian ships now out of the fight.' The Soleimani-class guided-missile catamarans, first delivered to the IRGC Navy in 2022, were the first Iranian warships to carry vertical launch systems capable of medium-range surface-to-air missiles. Separately, CENTCOM reported that on March 10, US forces destroyed 16 Iranian mine-laying boats near the Strait of Hormuz, after US intelligence had observed Iran taking steps to deploy naval mines in the waterway. Earlier in the conflict (March 5–6), CENTCOM also released footage of the destruction of the Iranian drone carrier Shahid Bagheri, a vessel roughly the size of a WWII aircraft carrier that had served as a floating missile-and-drone launch platform in the Gulf.
📍Persian Gulf / Strait of Hormuz / Gulf of Oman ⚓60+ total Iranian vessels destroyed; entire Soleimani-class (4 ships) eliminated; 16 minelayers sunk Mar 10 🚢Iranian drone carrier Shahid Bagheri destroyed (Mar 5–6); IRIS Dena torpedoed in Indian Ocean (USS Charlotte)
Sources: Military Times Navy Times
11
Mar
IDF · Beirut Offensive · Wide-Scale Strike Wave
