IDF Launches Wide-Scale Strikes on Tehran and Beirut's Southern Suburbs — 'Most Intense Day' Declared
On the night of March 10–11, the IDF launched what it described as a major new wave of strikes on Iran and simultaneously struck Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahiyeh). Israeli aircraft targeted IRGC and Hezbollah infrastructure in Beirut. Israel began a second wave of strikes on Tehran on the morning of March 11, with CNN's team in Tehran reporting heavy aerial bombardment overnight. Defense Secretary Hegseth had earlier declared March 10 would be the 'most intense day of strikes inside Iran,' with B-1 and B-52 bombers returning from overnight missions out of RAF Fairford. The IDF separately launched a warning strike on Beirut's southern suburbs and warned it would target Hezbollah infrastructure in Tyre, Sidon, and areas south of the Litani River.
📍Tehran (multiple districts), Beirut southern suburbs (Dahiyeh/Ghobeiry), Tyre and Sidon (Lebanon south coast) ✈️B-1 and B-52 bombers deployed from RAF Fairford; IDF 2nd wave vs. Tehran on Mar 11 morning 💀Lebanon death toll from Israeli strikes since Mar 2 now at 570+ killed, 1,444+ wounded (Gov. Disaster Risk Mgmt Unit)
Sources: CNN The War Zone
11
Mar
Iran · Hormuz Escalation · Strait of Hormuz Minelaying
Iran Moves to Mine Strait of Hormuz; 150 Ships Stalled; Energy Crisis Deepens
US intelligence assets detected Iran taking steps to deploy naval mines in and around the Strait of Hormuz, CBS News reported on March 10. In response, CENTCOM struck and destroyed 16 Iranian mine-laying boats on March 10. Shipping through the Strait has slowed to a near-standstill, with approximately 150 freight ships — including many oil tankers — stalled in the waterway. The IRGC had previously threatened that no oil would leave the region while US-Israeli attacks continued. Iran's blockade has driven Brent crude above $100/barrel. Bahrain's state energy company Bapco declared force majeure on its shipments after its refinery was struck. Abu Dhabi's ADNOC was also forced to shutter a refinery (922,000 bpd capacity) following a drone strike. An unnamed IRGC official had warned that any vessel transiting the Strait would be met with 'lethal force.' The tanker Prima (Malta-flagged) was attacked by an IRGC drone; a bulk carrier also reported being struck by an unknown projectile northwest of Ras Al-Khaimah on March 11.
📍Strait of Hormuz, Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman 🛢️~150 ships stalled; Brent crude past $100; Bapco & ADNOC force majeure; Saudi Aramco warns of 'catastrophic consequences' ⚓16 Iranian minelayers destroyed by CENTCOM Mar 10; Iran's drone carrier Shahid Bagheri previously destroyed
Sources: CNN Al Jazeera
11
Mar
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Political · Trump / Iran Positions
Trump Signals War 'Pretty Much' Complete; IRGC Vows to Fight On; Mojtaba Khamenei Consolidates Power
On March 9–10, US President Trump told CBS News the war was 'very complete, pretty much,' briefly calming markets. However, his aides stated operations would continue until all objectives — including Iran's 'unconditional surrender' — were met. White House spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said the initial timeline was 'four to six weeks.' Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei — elected March 8 by an interim leadership council after his father's assassination — received pledges of loyalty from the IRGC, President Pezeshkian, Ghalibaf, and Larijani. Trump called the outcome 'disappointing,' saying 'it's going to lead to just more of the same problem.' The IRGC responded to Wave 37 by declaring the 'war will end only when the shadow of war is removed from our country.' Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi reiterated Iran has made no ceasefire request and is prepared for a US ground invasion. Jerusalem Post analysts noted Trump may be beginning to frame an exit narrative, while Israel is likely to push for continued operations until Hezbollah and Iran's nuclear/missile capabilities are more fully degraded.
🏛️Mojtaba Khamenei confirmed as new Supreme Leader (Mar 8); IRGC, Pezeshkian, Ghalibaf, Larijani pledge loyalty 🕊️Trump: 'unconditional surrender' only condition; Iran: no ceasefire requested; mediation efforts by unnamed countries ongoing 📉Oil briefly dipped after Trump 'complete' comment before rebounding above $100 as IRGC launched Wave 37
Sources: Jerusalem Post Al Jazeera
11
Mar
CENTCOM · Strike Update Offensive · Nuclear & Peripheral Targets
CENTCOM Confirms 5,500+ Targets Struck; Bushehr Reactor Staff Evacuated as Nuclear Periphery Hit
CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper confirmed that American forces have now struck more than 5,500 targets inside Iran, an increase from the 5,000 figure reported on March 10. Unconfirmed reports indicated US strikes hit the Iran Atomic Energy Agency headquarters in Tehran, the explosive research testing facility at Parchin, and additional strikes at the Isfahan nuclear complex. Russia's state atomic energy company Rosatom evacuated nearly 100 staff from Iran amid the escalating campaign, though personnel remained on-site to keep the Bushehr reactor running. Cooper also confirmed the regime's ballistic missile launch rate has fallen approximately 90% since Day 1, and drone attacks are down 83%.
📍Tehran, Parchin, Isfahan, Bushehr — multiple nuclear-adjacent sites 💥5,500+ total targets struck across Iran (CENTCOM, Mar 11) ☢️Rosatom evacuates ~100 staff from Bushehr reactor amid strikes
Sources: OAN News CSIS Analysis
11
Mar
IDF · Lebanon Front Offensive · Beirut & Southern Lebanon
IDF Launches Extensive Wave on Dahiyeh; 70+ Beirut Targets Hit; Nearly 750,000 Displaced in Lebanon
The Israeli Air Force launched an 'extensive' wave of strikes on Beirut's southern suburb of Dahiyeh, a Hezbollah stronghold, with the IDF announcing it had struck over 70 targets in Beirut's south since the war began — including demolishing 50 multi-story buildings used by Hezbollah. Concurrently, Iran fired ballistic missiles and Hezbollah launched approximately 100–150 rockets at northern Israel in what the IRGC described as a 'joint and integrated operation.' Two people were lightly injured in northern Israel. The humanitarian situation in Lebanon reached a crisis point, with nearly 750,000 people displaced — including approximately 200,000 children — according to UNICEF and NBC News. The Lebanese government had earlier declared Hezbollah's military activities illegal, with all but two of 24 cabinet ministers voting in favor of the measure on March 2.
📍Dahiyeh (Beirut southern suburbs), northern Israel, southern Lebanon 💀570+ Lebanese killed since Mar 2; ~750,000 displaced (UNICEF/NBC) 🚀Hezbollah fires ~150 rockets at northern Israel; 2 lightly injured
Sources: Times of Israel NBC News Al Jazeera
11
Mar
Iran · Wave 37 Offensive · Regional Missile & Drone Barrage
IRGC Wave 37: Khorramshahr Missiles, Gulf-Wide Drone Strikes, Ship Hit in Strait of Hormuz
Iran's IRGC announced its 'most intense and heaviest operation' since the start of the war on Day 12 — Wave 37. Iran claimed it launched long-range Khorramshahr ballistic missiles against Israeli and US targets. A commercial container vessel was struck by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, some 25 nautical miles northwest of Ras Al-Khaimah, setting it ablaze, according to the UK Maritime Trade Operations agency. Across the Gulf: Saudi Arabia intercepted 6 Iranian missiles near Prince Sultan Air Base; Kuwait's National Guard shot down 8 drones; Bahrain sounded air raid sirens; Oman reported 2 drones downed at sea; two Iranian drones fell near Dubai International Airport, injuring four. Iran's IRGC stated: 'The war will end only when the shadow of war is removed from our country.' Despite Trump's claim the war was 'pretty much' complete, Iran sustained its offensive tempo.
📍Strait of Hormuz, Kuwait, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Oman, Qatar, Israel 🚢Container vessel struck and set ablaze in Strait of Hormuz (UKMTO) 🚀Wave 37 — Khorramshahr long-range ballistic missiles deployed; Gulf-wide drone barrage
Sources: Al Jazeera Euronews CNN
11
Mar
IDF · Khamenei Bunker Offensive · Leadership Infrastructure
IDF Destroys Khamenei's Underground Bunker Beneath Tehran Leadership Compound
The Israeli military announced it had dismantled the underground bunker constructed beneath the leadership compound in central Tehran that had been intended for use by slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The destruction of the bunker was presented as part of the broader campaign to eliminate regime leadership infrastructure. Separately, the New York Times reported that preliminary findings of a US internal investigation indicated that a March 1 airstrike on a school in Minab — killing over 168 people, many of them children — was conducted by the US using outdated targeting data, with video showing a US Tomahawk missile striking an adjacent building.
📍Central Tehran — Leadership Compound underground bunker destroyed ⚠️NYT: Minab school strike (168+ killed) attributed to US with outdated targeting data (preliminary)
Sources: NBC News Wikipedia — 2026 Iran War
11
Mar
Pentagon · Casualty Update Administrative · US Casualties
Pentagon: ~140 US Service Members Wounded; 8 KIA Confirmed; 8 Remain Severely Injured
The Pentagon confirmed on March 10 that approximately 140 US service members have been wounded since the start of Operation Epic Fury on February 28 — an increase from the previously reported 18+ seriously wounded. Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell stated that 108 of those have already returned to duty, while 8 remain severely injured. Eight US service members have been confirmed killed in action: six Army reservists died in the March 1 drone strike on a makeshift operations center at a civilian port in Kuwait, a seventh died March 8 from injuries sustained in an Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, and an eighth was confirmed KIA in the Gulf region. Reuters separately cited two officials as saying as many as 150 troops may have been wounded; White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt called that figure 'in the ballpark.'
💀8 US KIA confirmed (CENTCOM) — 6 in Kuwait (Mar 1), 1 in Saudi Arabia (Mar 8), 1 confirmed Mar 10 🏥~140 wounded (Pentagon); 8 remain severely injured; 108 returned to duty
Sources: Al Jazeera Newsweek Military Times
11
Mar
Iran · Hormuz Mining Defensive · Strait Blockade Intensifies
US Destroys 16 Iranian Minelayers Near Strait of Hormuz; CENTCOM Pivots Naval Strategy
The US military announced it had destroyed Iranian naval ships including 16 minelayers near the Strait of Hormuz following intelligence confirming Iran had deployed several dozen mines — ranging from rudimentary contact mines to sophisticated influence mines — using small fast-attack craft. CENTCOM issued an urgent directive for Iranian civilians to avoid all port facilities and warned that civilian ports used for military purposes had lost their 'protected status' under international law. The Strait remains severely disrupted with 150 freight ships stalled. Iran is estimated to retain roughly 80–90% of its small-boat fleet and a stockpile of between 2,000 and 6,000 mines. President Trump issued a 'remove them immediately' ultimatum to Tehran, warning of military consequences 'at a level never seen before.'
📍Strait of Hormuz — 25 nautical miles northwest of Ras Al-Khaimah and surrounding waters ⚓16 Iranian minelayers destroyed (CENTCOM, Mar 10–11); 150 ships stalled in Hormuz 🛢️Brent crude past $100/bbl; Bapco declares force majeure; ADNOC refinery shut (922k bbl/day)
Sources: CNN OAN News
11
Mar
Iran · Political Political · Leadership & Diplomacy
UN Security Council Passes Resolution Demanding Iran Halt Gulf Attacks; Mojtaba Khamenei Rallies Support in Tehran
The UN Security Council passed a resolution by 13 votes with 2 abstentions calling for Iran to immediately halt its attacks on Gulf states — Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Jordan — saying they breach international law and pose a serious threat to international peace and security. Meanwhile, rallies in Tehran showed public support for new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, who was elected on March 8 to succeed his father and received pledges of allegiance from the IRGC, Ghalibaf, Larijani, and Pezeshkian. Trump said Mojtaba's selection was 'disappointing' and 'going to lead to just more of the same problem.' The NYT also reported on March 11 that Iranian attacks caused damage to at least 17 US sites in the Middle East.
🌐UNSC resolution: 13–0–2, demands Iran immediately cease all Gulf state attacks 👤Mojtaba Khamenei (56) consolidates power; IRGC, top leaders pledge loyalty 📊NYT: Iranian attacks damaged at least 17 US sites across the Middle East
Sources: Times of Israel Wikipedia — 2026 Iran War Times of Israel — March 10
11
Mar
CENTCOM · Update Offensive · Strike Milestone
CENTCOM: 5,500+ Targets Struck, Entire Soleimani-Class Warship Class Destroyed
CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper confirmed in a video message posted to X on March 11 that US forces have now struck more than 5,500 targets inside Iran and destroyed over 60 Iranian naval vessels. Cooper notably announced the destruction of the last of four Soleimani-class guided-missile catamarans — the first Iranian warships equipped with vertical launch systems — declaring 'that's an entire class of Iranian ships now out of the fight.' Cooper also reaffirmed air superiority over vast swaths of Iran and stated that Iran's ballistic missile launch rate had fallen approximately 90% from Day 1. A CENTCOM urgent directive was simultaneously issued warning Iranian civilians to evacuate all port facilities, stating that civilian ports used for military purposes lose protected status under international law.
📍CENTCOM HQ / Iran-wide 🚢60+ Iranian ships destroyed; entire Soleimani-class (4 vessels) eliminated 🎯5,500+ total targets struck inside Iran
Sources: Military Times Stars and Stripes OAN
11
Mar
Iran · Wave 37 Offensive · Missile & Drone Barrage
IRGC Declares 'Most Intense Operation': Wave 37 Hits Kuwait, Bahrain, Gulf; Ship Struck in Strait of Hormuz
Iran's IRGC announced its 37th wave of attacks on Day 12 of the war, declaring it the 'most intense and heaviest operation' since the conflict began, according to Iranian state media. Targets included a US base in Kuwait, oil and energy infrastructure in Bahrain — where a drone struck a facility at Ma'ameer on Sitra Island causing a large fire — and the UAE, where two drones fell near Dubai International Airport injuring four people. The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations confirmed that an unknown projectile struck a container vessel in the Strait of Hormuz approximately 25 nautical miles northwest of Ras Al-Khaimah, setting it on fire. Saudi Arabia intercepted six Iranian missiles near Prince Sultan Air Base. Kuwait's National Guard shot down eight drones. The IRGC declared 'the war will end only when the shadow of war is removed from our country.'
📍Kuwait, Bahrain, UAE (Dubai Airport), Strait of Hormuz, Saudi Arabia, Oman 💀Dozens wounded in Bahrain Sitra; 4 injured in Dubai; ship crew evacuated in Hormuz 🚀Khorramshahr ballistic missiles + drones deployed; Saudi Arabia reports 262 total Iranian missiles detected since war began
Sources: Al Jazeera Euronews CNN
11
Mar
Pentagon · Casualties Admin · Casualty Report
Pentagon Confirms ~140 US Troops Wounded; 8 KIA Confirmed; 8 Remain Severely Injured
Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell issued the most detailed public US casualty accounting to date on March 10–11, confirming that approximately 140 US service members have been wounded across 10 days of sustained Iranian attacks since the start of Operation Epic Fury on February 28. Of those, 108 have already returned to duty. Eight service members remain listed as severely injured and are receiving intensive medical care. Seven confirmed KIA have been officially named by the Pentagon as of March 11: six Army Reserve soldiers killed in the March 1 drone strike on a makeshift command center at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait, and a seventh who succumbed on March 8 to injuries sustained in the March 1 Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia. Reuters cited two officials putting the wounded figure at 150, which White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt called 'in the ballpark.' Secretary Hegseth declared Tuesday March 10 the 'most intense day of strikes inside Iran.'
📍Port Shuaiba (Kuwait), Prince Sultan AB (Saudi Arabia), regional US bases 💀8 KIA confirmed; ~140 wounded total (8 severe, 108 returned to duty) 🏥Pentagon: vast majority of injuries minor; Hegseth says strike tempo surging
Sources: Al Jazeera Newsweek Military Times
11
Mar
NYT / Pentagon · Investigation Admin · Accountability
NYT: Preliminary Findings Indicate US Responsible for Minab School Strike Using Outdated Targeting Data
The New York Times reported on March 11 that preliminary findings in the ongoing US internal investigation indicate the deadly strike on the Shajareh Tayyebeh elementary school in Minab, Iran — carried out on February 28, Day 1 — was conducted by the United States using outdated targeting data. A video obtained by investigators showed a US Tomahawk cruise missile striking a building adjacent to the school. Iranian state media reported approximately 180 people, mostly school children, were killed and 95 wounded; CENTCOM had previously said it was investigating the incident. Iranian foreign ministry had called the strike a 'war crime.' The investigation remains ongoing.
📍Minab, Hormozgan Province, southern Iran 💀~180 killed (mostly children), 95 wounded per Iranian state media; figures unverified independently ⚠️Preliminary US investigation points to Tomahawk strike with outdated targeting data
Sources: New York Times (via Wikipedia / CENTCOM) NBC News
11
Mar
IDF · Lebanon Offensive · Strike
IDF Destroys Hezbollah Missile Launchers in Lebanon; 570+ Civilians Killed; 750,000 Displaced
On March 11–12, the IDF confirmed fresh strikes destroying Hezbollah missile launchers across Lebanon, as fighting between the IDF and Hezbollah continued in southern Lebanon near Khiam. Lebanon's Health Ministry confirmed 570 people have been killed in Lebanon since the conflict began on February 28. A humanitarian catastrophe is now unfolding: NBC News reported nearly 750,000 people have been displaced inside Lebanon, with the UN citing approximately 700,000 displaced including 200,000 children. The number of registered displaced rose by 100,000 on Monday and Tuesday alone. UNICEF reported over 1,100 children killed or injured across the conflict zone (91 in Lebanon). Human Rights Watch accused Israel of using white phosphorus over residential areas in the southern Lebanese town of Yohmor on March 3. Lebanon's government — in a rare move — voted on March 2 to declare Hezbollah's military activities illegal, with only Hezbollah's two cabinet ministers voting against.
📍Southern Lebanon (Khiam, Litani River zone), Beirut suburbs (Dahiyeh, Ghobeiry) 💀570+ Lebanese civilians killed; 750,000+ displaced (NBC News / UN estimates) 🔥HRW: white phosphorus used over Yohmor residential area (March 3); Lebanon bans Hezbollah military activities
Sources: Jerusalem Post / IDF Stars and Stripes NBC News UN News
12
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Wave 37 / IRGC 'Most Intense' Op
IRGC Declares Wave 37 'Most Intense': Hormuz Ship Hit, Gulf Struck; Iranian Missiles Intercepted Over Sharon
Iran's IRGC declared its 37th wave of Operation True Promise 4 on Day 12, describing it as the 'most intense and heaviest operation' since the start of the war. The wave targeted US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, the UAE, and Israel. A container vessel was struck by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz approximately 25 nautical miles northwest of Ras Al-Khaimah, setting it on fire. In Bahrain, attacks wounded dozens including children near Sitra, and a drone struck a residential building in Manama, killing one woman and injuring eight. The Dubai Media Office reported two drones fell near Dubai International Airport, wounding four. Iranian missiles were intercepted over the Sharon region of Israel. The IRGC stated the war would end 'only when the shadow of war is removed from our country.' Iran used Khorramshahr long-range ballistic missiles among the projectiles fired at Israel.
📍Strait of Hormuz, Kuwait, Bahrain, UAE, Israel (Sharon), Qatar 💀1 killed (Manama), dozens wounded in Bahrain incl. children; 4 wounded near Dubai airport; ship crew status unknown 🚢Container vessel struck and set ablaze in Strait of Hormuz — 25nm NW of Ras Al-Khaimah
Sources: Al Jazeera Jerusalem Post CNN
11
Mar
CENTCOM / IDF · Strikes Offensive · 'Most Intense Day' + IRGC Drone Cell Strike
Hegseth: 'Most Intense Day of Strikes'; IAF Hits IRGC Drone Operatives Mid-Launch in Western Iran; Bombers Deploy to RAF Fairford
US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth declared March 11 the 'most intense day of strikes inside Iran' since the operation began. The Israel Air Force launched a 'wide-scale wave of strikes' against IRGC infrastructure across Iran, simultaneously striking Hezbollah targets in Beirut. Critically, Israeli jets struck a group of Iranian drone operatives at a western Iran launch site shortly before they were set to launch drones toward Israel — killing the cell inside the IRGC command center. The IDF also struck several long-range ballistic missile launch sites and IRGC military infrastructure, and continued targeting missile-related infrastructure in western Iran. Additionally, 11 B-1 and 3 B-52 US bombers were photographed being prepared at RAF Fairford in the UK, signaling a surge in heavy bomber capacity. CENTCOM simultaneously warned Iran's civilian ports that use for military purposes would lose protected status under international law, and urged dockworkers to distance from IRGC naval assets ahead of anticipated expanded naval strikes.
📍Western Iran (drone launch site), Tehran, IRGC HQ at Fardis, Beirut (Hezbollah targets), RAF Fairford UK (bomber staging) 💥IRGC drone cell eliminated at launch site; IRGC Fardis HQ heavily damaged; multiple IRGC launch sites destroyed ✈️11 B-1 + 3 B-52 bombers staged at RAF Fairford; IAF conducted simultaneous multi-target wave
Sources: Jerusalem Post NBC News Live Blog ABC7 / AP
11
Mar
CENTCOM Update Administrative · Targets / Naval Tally
CENTCOM: 5,500+ Targets Struck, Entire Soleimani-Class Warship Class Destroyed; 60+ Ships Sunk
CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper released a video update on March 11 confirming US forces have now struck more than 5,500 targets inside Iran — up from the 5,000 reported on March 10. Cooper confirmed the destruction of all four Soleimani-class warships, stating 'that's an entire class of Iranian ships now out of the fight.' The total tally of Iranian naval vessels struck or sunk exceeded 60. Cooper also reported that Iran's ballistic missile launch rate has dropped approximately 90% since Day 1 and drone attacks have fallen 83%. CENTCOM simultaneously issued an urgent directive warning Iranian civilians to avoid all port facilities, stating civilian ports used for military purposes lose protected status under international law. Cooper emphasized US forces 'have achieved air superiority over vast swaths of Iran.'
📍Iran-wide; Strait of Hormuz naval theater; CENTCOM HQ briefing 🚢60+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed; all 4 Soleimani-class warships eliminated 📊Iranian ballistic missile launches down ~90%; drone launches down ~83% vs Day 1
Sources: Stars and Stripes Military Times OAN
12
Mar
CENTCOM · Naval Offensive · Minelayer Interdiction
US Destroys 16 Iranian Minelayers Near Strait of Hormuz; Iran Mining Campaign Ongoing
The US military confirmed it destroyed 16 Iranian minelayers operating near the Strait of Hormuz after intelligence confirmed Iran had begun deploying naval mines in the key waterway. CNN had earlier reported that Tehran began laying mines in the strait. CENTCOM's naval strategy shifted to aggressively target Iranian mine-laying operations, described as small fast-attack craft operating in 'swarms,' each capable of carrying two to three mines. US intelligence estimated Iran had deployed several dozen mines — ranging from contact to influence mines — using craft difficult to track. President Trump issued an ultimatum to Tehran demanding mines be 'removed immediately,' warning of military consequences 'at a level never seen before' if the waterway remains obstructed. Experts estimated Iran retains 80–90% of its small-boat fleet and a stockpile of 2,000–6,000 mines. Shipping through the Strait had already slowed dramatically, with Brent crude oil briefly topping $100/barrel.
📍Strait of Hormuz — approximately 25nm NW of Ras Al-Khaimah and surrounding waters 💥16 Iranian minelayer vessels destroyed by US forces ⛽Brent crude briefly tops $100/bbl; Maersk suspended cargo to/from 7 Middle Eastern nations; Hormuz shipping near standstill
Sources: CNN OAN / CENTCOM CNBC
12
Mar
Iran · Leadership Political · New Supreme Leader Confirmed
Mojtaba Khamenei Confirmed as Iran's New Supreme Leader; Pledges Allegiance from IRGC, Top Officials
Mojtaba Khamenei, the 56-year-old son of slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, was elected on March 8, 2026, as Iran's third supreme leader. President Masoud Pezeshkian, IRGC commander Mohammad Pakpour, and top political figures including Ali Larijani and Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf all pledged allegiance. He has close ties to the IRGC, which has continued firing missiles and drones since his father's killing on February 28. Israel had already described Mojtaba as a potential target; Trump called him a 'lightweight' and said the outcome was 'disappointing' and would 'lead to just more of the same problem.' Mojtaba survived an earlier Israeli airstrike. Iran-aligned proxy groups and regional allies also publicly declared their support for the new supreme leader. His appointment came despite an Israeli airstrike on March 3 that destroyed the Assembly of Experts' building as members were meeting to discuss succession, temporarily delaying the process.
📍Tehran, Iran 👤Mojtaba Khamenei — 56-year-old cleric, son of Ali Khamenei; closely tied to IRGC ⚠️Israel declared new supreme leader a potential strike target; Trump expressed displeasure with selection
Sources: Wikipedia / 2026 Iran War Times of Israel Al Jazeera
12
Mar
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · Displacement Crisis
Lebanon Displacement Tops 750,000; 570+ Civilians Dead; HRW Accuses Israel of White Phosphorus Use
The humanitarian crisis in Lebanon has dramatically worsened. NBC News and UN officials confirmed nearly 750,000 people have been displaced as of March 12, with the UN's humanitarian coordinator in Lebanon stating the overall figure was likely even higher as not all displaced have registered. UNICEF reported nearly 700,000 displaced including approximately 200,000 children. The Lebanese Health Ministry confirmed 570 civilians killed and 1,444 wounded since Israeli strikes resumed on March 2. The number registered as displaced rose by 100,000 on Monday and Tuesday alone. Human Rights Watch issued a report accusing the Israeli military of illegally using white phosphorus munitions over homes in the southern Lebanese town of Yohmor on March 3, verifying at least eight images showing the incendiary substance over a residential area. Over 30,000 people crossed from Lebanon into Syria as of March 6. Lebanon's government voted on March 2 to declare Hezbollah's military activities illegal, with all but two Cabinet ministers — the two Hezbollah ministers — voting in favor.
📍Southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs, Dahiyeh 💀570+ Lebanese civilians killed; 1,444+ wounded (Lebanon Health Ministry, Mar 11) 🏠750,000+ displaced; 700,000 confirmed by UNICEF; 120,000+ in formal shelters; shelters at full capacity
Sources: NBC News UN News Stars and Stripes / Lebanon Health Min. Human Rights Watch
12
Mar
Nuclear · CSIS / CENTCOM Strike Campaign · Nuclear Periphery
US-Israeli Strikes Expand to Iran Atomic Energy Agency HQ, Parchin Explosive Research Site; Rosatom Evacuates ~100 Staff from Bushehr
CSIS analysis and unconfirmed reports indicate that US and Israeli strikes have broadened to target Iran's peripheral nuclear infrastructure. Unconfirmed reports state the US struck the Iran Atomic Energy Agency headquarters in Tehran and the explosive research testing facility at Parchin — the complex previously associated with Iran's pre-2003 nuclear weapons program and struck again on March 4. Further strikes at the Isfahan nuclear complex were also reported. The most acute escalatory risk centers on the Bushehr reactor, which Iran operates with Russian assistance. Rosatom evacuated nearly 100 staff from Iran, though personnel remain on-site to keep the reactor running. Satellite imagery from March 8 confirmed the combined US-Israeli force struck underground facilities at the Isfahan Missile Complex, and cratered the runway of the IRGC Aerospace Force's Ali Akbar Drone Base in Ahvaz. Iran's nuclear status as of March 2026 remains unverifiable — the IAEA has been unable to conduct in-field verification since June 2025 Israeli strikes.
📍IAEA HQ Tehran (unconfirmed), Parchin Military Complex, Isfahan Nuclear Complex, Bushehr Reactor, Ahvaz Drone Base ☢️Rosatom evacuated ~100 staff from Bushehr; reactor still operational; IAEA cannot verify enrichment levels ⚠️Strikes unconfirmed by CENTCOM; CSIS flags Bushehr as most acute escalatory nuclear risk
Sources: CSIS Critical Threats / CTP-ISW Epic Fury Live
11
Mar
US Casualties · Pentagon Administrative · Casualty Update
NYT: Iranian Attacks Damaged 17 US Sites; Pentagon Confirms 8 KIA, ~140 Wounded (8 Severely)
The New York Times reported on March 11 that Iranian attacks have caused damage to at least 17 US sites in the Middle East. The Pentagon confirmed approximately 140 US service members have been wounded since February 28 — 108 have returned to duty, 8 remain severely injured. Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell confirmed the wounded figure in a formal statement. Seven confirmed US KIA total: six Army reservists killed in the March 1 Iranian drone strike on a makeshift operations center at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait, and one additional soldier who died March 8 from wounds sustained at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia on March 1. An eighth death — a National Guard soldier from a health-related incident in Kuwait on March 6 — was also reported by CENTCOM. White House spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt confirmed the 4-to-6-week operational timeline and stated Trump will determine when Iran has reached a point of 'unconditional surrender.'
📍Port Shuaiba Kuwait (6 KIA), Prince Sultan Air Base Saudi Arabia (1 KIA), Kuwait (1 health-related KIA), 17 US sites damaged across region 💀8 US KIA confirmed; ~140 wounded total (108 returned to duty; 8 remain severely injured) 📋NYT: 17 US military sites damaged across the Middle East by Iranian attacks through Day 11
Sources: Pentagon / Al Jazeera Newsweek Military Times
11
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Wave Attack
Iran Launches 'Most Intense' IRGC Operation Yet; Cargo Ship Struck in Strait of Hormuz
On March 11 (Day 12), Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps declared it had launched its 'most intense and heaviest operation' since the start of the war, firing its 37th wave of attacks across the region, including against US targets in Kuwait and regional civilian and energy infrastructure. A commercial cargo vessel was struck by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, approximately 25 nautical miles northwest of Ras Al-Khaimah, UAE, setting it on fire. The UAE scrambled fighter jets to intercept ballistic missiles and drones. Two Iranian drones fell near Dubai International Airport, injuring four foreign nationals. Bahrain sounded air-raid sirens amid drone strikes wounding dozens including children near Manama. Saudi Arabia intercepted six Iranian missiles near Prince Sultan Air Base. Kuwait's National Guard shot down eight drones. The IRGC stated: 'The war will end only when the shadow of war is removed from our country.' Iran also employed its long-range Khorramshahr ballistic missile in overnight salvos against Israel and US regional assets.
📍Strait of Hormuz · UAE · Bahrain · Kuwait · Saudi Arabia · Qatar · Israel 💀Multiple civilian and military casualties across Gulf; 4 wounded near Dubai airport; dozens wounded in Bahrain
Sources: Al Jazeera Fox News Euronews
11
Mar
CENTCOM · Strike Update Offensive · BDA Report
CENTCOM: 5,500+ Targets Struck, All Soleimani-Class Warships Destroyed, Iran Fleet 'Out of the Fight'
In a video statement posted to X on March 11, CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper confirmed that US forces have now struck more than 5,500 targets inside Iran, including over 60 Iranian naval vessels. Cooper announced the destruction of all four of Iran's Soleimani-class guided-missile catamarans — an entire class of warships eliminated. He stated that strike waves were occurring 'nearly every hour' in recent operations, including the destruction of the last Soleimani-class ship. The admiral reaffirmed air superiority 'over vast swaths of Iran' and stated that Iran's ability to launch ballistic missiles had dropped approximately 90% since Day 1, with drone attacks down 83%. CENTCOM also issued an urgent directive warning Iranian civilians and dockworkers to avoid all port facilities, stating that civilian ports used for military purposes lose protected status under international law. The Pentagon simultaneously confirmed that Secretary Hegseth declared Tuesday would be the 'most intense day of strikes' on Iran.
📍Iran-wide · Strait of Hormuz · CENTCOM HQ Tampa 💀60+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed; all 4 Soleimani-class warships eliminated; missile launch capability down ~90%
Sources: Stars and Stripes OAN Janes
11
Mar
Iran · Cluster Bomb Escalation Offensive · Escalation
Iran and Hezbollah Launch Coordinated Cluster Bomb Strikes on Israel
On March 11, Iran and Hezbollah reportedly coordinated a cluster munitions attack on Israeli territory in what was described as a major escalation of the conflict. The IDF responded by launching a wide-scale wave of strikes against IRGC infrastructure across Iran simultaneously with strikes on Hezbollah targets in Beirut. Israeli fighter jets also struck a group of Iranian drone operatives at a launch site in western Iran shortly before they were set to launch drones toward Israel. The IDF said it struck several long-range ballistic missile launch sites and IRGC military infrastructure throughout the country. Separately, the IDF struck IRGC command infrastructure in Fardis and completed waves of strikes across Tehran, Isfahan, and Shiraz targeting command centers and manufacturing sites.

11
Mar
Iran · Funeral Event · Military Funeral
Mass Funeral in Tehran for Fallen IRGC Commanders, Including IRGC C-in-C Pakpour and Armed Forces Chief Mousavi
On March 11, massive crowds gathered in Tehran for a state funeral honoring Iranian military commanders killed in the joint US-Israeli conflict. Among those being mourned were Abdolrahim Mousavi, chief of staff of the Iranian Armed Forces, and Mohammad Pakpour, commander-in-chief of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps — two of the highest-ranking military officers in Iran. The IDF had previously described Mousavi as 'one of the highest senior military ranking officials' and stated that Pakpour was a key leader of the 'destruction of Israel' plan. The funeral underscored the scale of Iran's military leadership losses since the operation began on February 28.
📍Tehran, Iran 💀Abdolrahim Mousavi (Armed Forces Chief of Staff) · Mohammad Pakpour (IRGC C-in-C) · multiple senior commanders
Sources: Fox News IranWire
11
Mar
US · Energy Response Diplomatic · Economic
Trump Announces IEA Coordination of Record 400M-Barrel Oil Reserve Release to Counter Iran War Energy Shock
On March 11, President Trump announced that the International Energy Agency (IEA) had agreed to coordinate the release of a record 400 million barrels of oil from national petroleum reserves around the world in a coordinated effort to stabilize global energy markets rattled by the Iran war. Trump made the announcement during remarks at Verst Logistics in Hebron, Kentucky. The move came as Brent crude briefly topped $100 per barrel on March 12 amid ongoing Iranian attacks on Gulf shipping and energy infrastructure, including Bahrain's Bapco refinery (which declared force majeure) and repeated strikes near Saudi Aramco's Ras Tanura facility. Iran's internet blackout had by this point reached 240 hours, with Iran spending 'a third of 2026 offline' according to cybersecurity watchdog NetBlocks.
📍IEA / Global · Hebron, Kentucky (announcement) 💀No casualties; economic escalation — Brent crude briefly >$100/bbl
Sources: Fox News NBC News
12
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Wave Attack
Iran Launches Day-13 Wave Attacks on Gulf; Bahrain Fuel Tanks Hit, Saudi Arabia Shoots Down Drones Near Oilfield
On March 12 (Day 13), Iran launched a fresh wave of drone and missile attacks on Gulf countries. Bahrain reported Iranian strikes on fuel storage tanks. Saudi Arabia shot down drones headed toward an oilfield. Iranian missiles were also intercepted over Israel's Sharon region. The IDF simultaneously struck and destroyed Hezbollah missile launchers in Lebanon. Gulf states across the region — Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, and Qatar — continued to intercept incoming Iranian drones and missiles. Brent crude oil briefly topped $100 per barrel as Iranian attacks on shipping compounded supply concerns.
📍Bahrain · Saudi Arabia · Israel (Sharon region) · Lebanon · UAE · Kuwait · Qatar 💀Casualties under assessment; fuel infrastructure struck in Bahrain
Sources: Al Jazeera Jerusalem Post Britannica
12
Mar
US Air Force · Equipment Loss Accident · Western Iraq
KC-135 Stratotanker Crashes in Western Iraq — All 6 Crew Killed
A U.S. Air Force KC-135 Stratotanker supporting Operation Epic Fury crashed in western Iraq after a mid-air incident with a second KC-135. All six crew members were killed. The second aircraft declared an emergency (squawk 7700) and landed safely at Ben Gurion Airport with visible tail fin damage. CENTCOM stated the crash was "not due to hostile fire or friendly fire" and opened an investigation. The Islamic Resistance in Iraq claimed responsibility, alleging it shot down the aircraft. The KC-135 was assigned to the 940th Air Refueling Wing at Beale Air Force Base, California. This is the first KC-135 lost in 13 years and the deadliest Air Force mishap since the CV-22 Osprey crash off Japan in November 2023. The loss brings confirmed U.S. KIA in the war to 13.
📍Western Iraq 💀6 USAF crew KIA — total US war KIA now 13 ✈️KC-135 Stratotanker, 940th ARW, Beale AFB ⚠️CENTCOM: not hostile fire — under investigation
Sources: The War Zone CNN Air & Space Forces Washington Post
13
Mar
CENTCOM · Strike Update Offensive · Record Strike Tempo
Hegseth Declares 'Highest Volume' Day of Strikes; CENTCOM Confirms 15,000+ Targets Hit
At a Pentagon press conference on March 13, Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs Gen. Dan Caine announced that the US-Israeli campaign had now struck over 15,000 targets across Iran since February 28. Hegseth declared that the day would be 'the highest volume of strikes that America has put over the skies of Iran and Tehran,' with the number of sorties and bomber pulses described as the highest yet. CENTCOM confirmed Iranian one-way attack drones were down 95% and missile volume down 90% from Day 1 levels. Hegseth declared Iran to have 'no air defenses, no air force, no navy.' Approximately 200 US service members have been wounded since the start of the operation, with nearly 170 already returned to duty.
📍Tehran, Iran — plus simultaneous strikes in Shiraz, Ahvaz, Isfahan 💀US wounded total raised to ~200; 13 KIA confirmed (7 enemy fire + 6 KC-135 crash) 📊Iranian drone attacks down 95%; missile volume down 90% vs. Day 1
Sources: DefenseScoop WND / CENTCOM
13
Mar
IAF · Strike Wave Offensive · Wide-Scale Air Strikes
IAF Launches Wide-Scale Strikes on IRGC Infrastructure; 200+ Targets Hit in Western & Central Iran in 24 Hours
The Israel Air Force launched another wide-scale wave of strikes against IRGC infrastructure across Iran on March 13, simultaneously striking Hezbollah targets in Beirut. The IDF stated it struck more than 200 targets over the past day in western and central Iran, including ballistic missile launchers, air defense systems, and weapons manufacturing sites. Strikes occurred simultaneously in Tehran, Shiraz, and Ahvaz. One strike in Tehran caused an explosion near a state-organized demonstration in support of Palestinians, according to IRIB. Israel's military issued prior warnings to Iranians to evacuate military infrastructure zones. The IDF also continued targeting IRGC drone array operatives at launch sites in western Iran, striking operatives shortly after their arrival at a launch site.
📍Tehran, Shiraz, Ahvaz, western Iran — ballistic missile launchers and air defense sites 💥200+ targets struck in 24 hours; IRGC drone operatives eliminated pre-launch
Sources: NPR Jerusalem Post
13
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Wave Strike
New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei Vows to Continue War; Iran Fires on Israel & Gulf on Day 14
Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, in his first public statement since taking office on March 8, vowed to continue the war and promised to maintain pressure on the Strait of Hormuz, declaring that Iran's retaliation against US-Israeli strikes was 'not complete.' Iranian missiles and drones continued striking Israel and Gulf states on Day 14, with oil prices remaining above $100/barrel. An Iranian ballistic missile struck the northern Israeli town of Zarzir, leaving dozens lightly wounded according to Magen David Adom, with one person in moderate condition from shrapnel and 57 others treated for minor injuries. Israel's military confirmed its air defense and strike operations were responding across both fronts simultaneously. Hezbollah also continued firing into northern Israel overnight.
📍Zarzir, northern Israel — ballistic missile strike; Gulf states continuing under drone/missile attack 💀Dozens lightly wounded in Zarzir; 1 moderate shrapnel injury; 57 minor injuries 🚢Strait of Hormuz remains under pressure; new supreme leader pledges continued blockade
Sources: CFR Daily Brief NPR
13
Mar
US · Economic Measure Policy · Sanctions Waiver
US Temporarily Lifts Russian Oil Sanctions to Counter Iran War Energy Shock
On March 13, the United States temporarily lifted restrictions on the sale of Russian oil to help counter the global energy crisis triggered by the Iran war. The move allowed Indian refineries and other buyers access to Russian crude that had previously been under US sanctions. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated the measure was 'deliberately short-term' and would not provide significant financial benefit to the Russian government, as it only authorized transactions involving oil already stranded at sea. The decision came as Aramco CEO Amin Nasser warned that the Strait of Hormuz situation was 'blocking sizable volumes of oil from the whole region,' and oil prices remained above $100/barrel.
📍Washington D.C. / Global energy markets 💰Oil prices above $100/barrel; Strait of Hormuz near-blockade ongoing
Sources: Wikipedia / 2026 Iran War NPR
13
Mar
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · Mass Displacement
Lebanon Death Toll Reaches 773; Over 830,000 Displaced as Israel Expands Ground Operations
Israel's assault in Lebanon has killed 773 people and displaced more than 830,000, according to the Lebanese government's disaster management office as of March 13. Among those killed are 98 children, according to Lebanon's Information Minister. The IDF is preparing to expand its ground activity in southern Lebanon, with the Israeli military ordering civilians to evacuate areas south of the Litani River. As of March 12, almost 100,000 people crossed from Lebanon into Syria following the latest escalation, according to the IRC. Israel also widened its evacuation order to now cover around a tenth of the entire country. Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem demanded Israel end its military aggression and withdraw from southern Lebanon, declaring 'we will not surrender no matter the sacrifices.'
📍Southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs 💀773 killed in Lebanon (incl. 98 children); 830,000+ displaced 🚶~100,000 crossed from Lebanon into Syria as of Mar 12 (IRC)
Sources: NPR IRC / Lebanon Crisis

Mar
Iran · Drone/Missile Barrage Offensive · Wave Attack
Iran Launches Day 14 Barrage on Gulf States; Fuel Tanks Hit in Bahrain
On Day 14 of Operation Epic Fury, Iran launched a new wave of drone and missile attacks across Gulf countries, with Bahrain reporting direct strikes on fuel tanks. Saudi Arabia intercepted six Iranian missiles near Prince Sultan Air Base, with interception booms reported near Dammam. Kuwait's National Guard shot down eight drones. Bahrain sounded air raid sirens, and Oman reported two Iranian drones downed at sea north of Al Duqm commercial port. Qatar's military intercepted incoming missiles. The IRGC described the assault as its most intense and heaviest operation since the start of the war, with Iranian state media stating 'the war will end only when the shadow of war is removed from our country.'

Sources: Al Jazeera Euronews
14
Mar
US · Casualties Incident · Aircraft Loss
All 6 KC-135 Crew Confirmed Dead; US Death Toll Rises to 13
CENTCOM confirmed on March 13 that all six crew members were killed when a KC-135 refueling aircraft went down over western Iraq, raising the total US military death toll to 13. Two aircraft were involved; one went down while the other landed safely. Officials stated the incident was not caused by hostile or enemy fire and is under investigation. Seven US service members have been killed in action by Iranian attacks — six in the March 1 Kuwait port drone strike and one who died of wounds from the Prince Sultan Air Base attack in Saudi Arabia on March 8. Approximately 5,000 additional Marines and sailors are reportedly deploying to the Middle East according to US officials.
📍Western Iraq (KC-135 crash); Kuwait & Saudi Arabia (KIA) 💀13 US KIA total (7 enemy fire + 6 KC-135 crash); ~200 wounded (CENTCOM Day 13)
Sources: NPR NBC Washington DefenseScoop
14
Mar
CENTCOM · Strike Milestone Offensive · Record Volume
Hegseth Declares Heaviest Strike Day Yet; CENTCOM Confirms 15,000+ Targets, Iranian Drones Down 95%
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, appearing alongside Chairman of the Joint Chiefs Gen. Dan Caine at the Pentagon on March 13, announced that the day would mark the highest volume of US strikes over Iran since the start of Operation Epic Fury. 'Today will be, yet again, the highest volume of strikes that America has put over the skies of Iran and Tehran. The number of sorties and number of bomber pulses the highest yet — ramping up and only up,' Hegseth said. He confirmed Iranian missile volume is down 90% and one-way attack drones are down 95% compared to Day 1. CENTCOM separately confirmed that US and Israeli forces have now struck over 15,000 targets combined since February 28. Adm. Brad Cooper also confirmed that as of Day 13, over 6,000 targets had been struck by US forces alone, including more than 60 naval vessels and 30+ minelayers. Iran has no functioning air defenses, air force, or navy according to Hegseth.
📍Tehran, western & central Iran — B-2 bombers, fighter jets, cruise missiles 📊15,000+ combined targets struck (CENTCOM); Iranian drone attacks down 95% vs. Day 1
Sources: DefenseScoop WND / CENTCOM Stars and Stripes

Mar
IDF · Lebanon Escalation Offensive · Ground Expansion Planned
Israel Plans Massive Lebanon Ground Invasion; 800,000 Displaced, Lebanon Proposes Ceasefire
Israel is planning to significantly expand its ground operation in Lebanon, aiming to seize the entire area south of the Litani River and dismantle Hezbollah's military infrastructure, according to Israeli and US officials. The IDF has issued evacuation orders across southern Lebanon and — for the first time — to villages north of the Litani River and to Hezbollah's stronghold in Beirut's southern suburbs. Around 800,000 Lebanese civilians have been displaced and at least 773 people killed. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun presented a four-point ceasefire proposal on March 11 calling for a 'complete truce' and deployment of the Lebanese Army to disarm Hezbollah; Israel rejected the proposal. The IDF also eliminated several senior Hezbollah commanders on March 13 including Abu Dharr Mohammadi (IRGC missile unit operations commander in Beirut), Ali Muslim Tabaja (senior Imam Hossein Division commander), and Abu Ali Riyan (Radwan Force southern Lebanon unit commander). Netanyahu warned he 'wouldn't issue life insurance policies on any of the leaders of the terrorist organizations,' a direct threat to new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei.
📍Southern Lebanon, Beirut suburbs, north of Litani River — full evacuation orders issued 💀773+ Lebanese killed; 800,000+ displaced; 3 senior Hezbollah/IRGC commanders eliminated

Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued a statement vowing to continue the war and promising to keep pressure on the Strait of Hormuz, declaring Iran's retaliation against US-Israeli strikes was 'not complete.' Defense Secretary Hegseth confirmed Mojtaba Khamenei had been injured in an airstrike. Separately, the Israeli military announced it had dismantled the underground bunker built for the slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei beneath the leadership compound in central Tehran. IDF also struck the 'Taleghan' compound — a covert site used to advance nuclear weapons capabilities and develop advanced explosives as part of the AMAD project — destroying it entirely. Meanwhile, Trump posted on Truth Social reiterating there would be 'no deal with Iran except UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER' and that Iran has 'practically nothing left to target.'

Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth, alongside Chairman of the Joint Chiefs Gen. Dan Caine, declared March 13 would be 'the highest volume of strikes that America has put over the skies of Iran and Tehran,' with the most sorties and bomber pulses of the war. CENTCOM confirmed that between US and Israeli air forces, more than 15,000 enemy targets have been struck since Feb. 28. The IDF separately announced it had carried out more than 7,600 strikes against Iran over the first two weeks of Operation Roaring Lion, including more than 2,000 directed at regime headquarters and assets, and approximately 4,700 targeting Iran's missile program. Hegseth also reiterated that Iran has 'no air defenses, no air force, no navy,' with missile volume down 90% and one-way attack drones down 95% from Day 1. CENTCOM additionally released B-2 stealth bomber footage conducting strike missions over Iran.

US Bombs Kharg Island Oil Hub; IAF Hits 200+ Targets Including Underground Missile Sites
President Trump announced on Friday that the US military had 'totally obliterated every MILITARY target in Iran's crown jewel, Kharg Island,' Iran's primary oil export terminal. Simultaneously, Israel's air force reported striking more than 200 targets over the preceding 24 hours in western and central Iran, including ballistic missile launchers, air defense systems, and weapons manufacturing sites. The IDF reported simultaneous strikes in Tehran, Shiraz, and Ahvaz, targeting an underground site used to produce and store ballistic missiles as well as a central air-defense base. One strike near a state-organized demonstration in Tehran caused an explosion; Israel said it was destroying military infrastructure and had issued warnings to civilians.

Iran · Wave 37+ Offensive · Missile & Drone Barrage
Iran Launches Most Intense Wave Yet; Bahrain Fuel Tanks Hit, Ship Struck in Strait of Hormuz
On Day 13, the IRGC announced it had launched its 'most intense and heaviest operation' since the start of the war, targeting Gulf countries with waves of missiles and drones. Bahrain reported strikes on fuel storage tanks at Sitra Island, with fires breaking out at the Ma'ameer energy facility. A container vessel was struck and set ablaze in the Strait of Hormuz, approximately 25 nautical miles northwest of Ras Al-Khaimah. Bahrain's Air Defence Systems reported intercepting 203 drones and 124 missiles since February 28. Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued a statement vowing to continue the war and pledging to keep pressure on the Strait of Hormuz, declaring Iran's retaliation 'is not complete.' Turkey's defense ministry also intercepted a ballistic missile fired from Iran. Oil prices rose back above $100/barrel amid the escalating attacks.

IAF · Lebanon Escalation Offensive · Ground Operation Imminent
Israel Plans Massive Lebanon Ground Offensive; IDF Sends Reinforcements to Border, 820,000+ Displaced
Israel announced plans to significantly expand its ground operation in Lebanon, aiming to seize the entire area south of the Litani River and dismantle Hezbollah's military infrastructure. The IDF has three armored and infantry divisions on the Lebanese border and is sending additional reinforcements and mobilizing reserves. A senior Israeli official stated 'We are going to do what we did in Gaza,' targeting Hezbollah weapons storage and launch infrastructure. As of March 14, more than 820,000 Lebanese civilians have been displaced, including over 200,000 children, according to the International Rescue Committee. The Lebanese government, led by President Joseph Aoun, proposed a four-point ceasefire plan including a complete truce, Lebanese Army deployment to disarm Hezbollah, and direct negotiations under international auspices — but Israel rejected the proposal. Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem declared 'there is no solution except resistance.' The death toll in Lebanon reached 773 killed and 1,933 injured as of the latest Health Ministry figures, with the IDF reporting 380+ Hezbollah fighters killed and 1,100+ strikes in Lebanon.

Mar
Iran · Mojtaba Khamenei Political · War Vow
New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei Vows to Continue War; Pledges Strait of Hormuz Pressure
A statement attributed to Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei — elected on March 8 following the killing of his father Ali Khamenei — declared Iran would continue the war and pledged to maintain pressure on the Strait of Hormuz, stating that Iran's retaliation against US-Israeli strikes 'is not complete.' The statement came as Iran launched what the IRGC described as its most intense barrage of the conflict. Khamenei had survived an earlier airstrike on March 4. Iran's top political figures, including President Pezeshkian, Ghalibaf, Larijani, and IRGC leaders, had all pledged allegiance to Mojtaba after his election. Foreign Minister Araghchi separately confirmed Iran had made 'no request for a ceasefire' and denied any behind-the-scenes contact with US officials. Meanwhile, Trump continued to demand unconditional surrender as the sole condition for ending the war, with the White House stating operations would continue until Iran 'no longer poses a credible and direct threat.'
📍Tehran — Mojtaba Khamenei's statement issued from undisclosed location ⚔️Iran declares retaliation 'not complete'; IRGC vows heaviest wave yet 🕊️No ceasefire request from Tehran; Trump demands unconditional surrender only

Mar
US · Strike Offensive · Economic Infrastructure
US Obliterates Kharg Island; Trump Declares Every Military Target Destroyed
President Trump announced on Friday, March 14, that the US military had 'totally obliterated every MILITARY target in Iran's crown jewel, Kharg Island,' the primary terminal handling Iran's oil exports. The strikes were part of what Hegseth confirmed was the highest-volume strike day of the operation, with the greatest number of sorties and bomber pulses yet recorded. Simultaneously, the IDF struck over 200 targets across western and central Iran including underground ballistic missile production and storage sites, air defense bases, and regime infrastructure in Tehran, Shiraz, and Ahvaz. A US-flagged strike on Ferdowsi Square in central Tehran caused an explosion near a state-organized Quds Day rally attended by thousands; Israel had warned Iranians to clear the area beforehand. The IDF later posted that the head of Iran's judiciary was present at the rally. No immediate casualties were reported from the Ferdowsi Square strike.
📍Kharg Island, Persian Gulf + Tehran, Shiraz, Ahvaz (simultaneous waves) 💥200+ IAF targets struck; Kharg Island military infrastructure obliterated per Trump 📢Hegseth: 'Highest volume of strikes yet — ramping up and only up'

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile & Drone Wave
Iran Fires Ballistic Missiles at Zarzir; Hezbollah Continues Northern Israel Barrage
On March 14, an Iranian ballistic missile struck the northern Israeli town of Zarzir, leaving dozens lightly wounded according to Magen David Adom. One person was reported in moderate condition after being hit with shrapnel, while 57 others were treated for minor injuries, mostly from glass shards. Hezbollah also continued firing into northern Israel overnight, with Israel's military stating its air defense and strike operations were responding across both fronts simultaneously. Turkey's defense ministry separately announced it intercepted a ballistic missile fired from Iran, while in the UAE, smoke was seen near Dubai's Burj Khalifa following an Iranian drone interception. Iran's IRGC claimed its missile and drone assault capacity remained active despite US and Israeli claims of a 90% reduction in ballistic missile volume and 95% drop in one-way attack drones.

31st MEU and USS Tripoli Ordered to Middle East; 5,000 Additional Marines and Sailors Deploying
Elements from the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit (MEU) and the amphibious assault ship USS Tripoli have been ordered to the Middle East, US officials confirmed on March 14. Separately, approximately 5,000 additional Marines and sailors will be deploying to the region, according to three US officials, bringing total US personnel supporting Operation Epic Fury to over 50,000 service members. The deployment signals a potential shift toward amphibious and ground-capable forces in the theater as the air campaign enters its third week. The Department of War confirmed on March 13 that more than 50,000 US service members are currently supporting Operation Epic Fury.

Iran · Leadership Political · War Posture
New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei Vows Hormuz Pressure Will Continue; Retaliation 'Not Complete'
New Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued a statement vowing Iran would continue the war and keep pressure on the Strait of Hormuz, declaring Iran's retaliation against US-Israeli strikes was 'not complete.' Elected on March 8 to replace his assassinated father, Mojtaba's statement was reported by CFR and aligned with IRGC messaging that the 'war will end only when the shadow of war is removed from our country.' Iran's ambassador to the UN simultaneously argued that Tehran would not fully close the Strait of Hormuz, while the semi-official Fars News Agency warned ships passing through 'could be at risk from missiles or rogue drones.' Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf had warned the previous week that attacks on Iran's southern maritime frontier would cause Iran to 'abandon all restraint.' Lebanon's Lebanese President Aoun proposed a four-point ceasefire deal for the Lebanon front on March 13, which Israel rejected.

IAF Strikes 200+ Targets in Western & Central Iran; Tehran, Shiraz, Ahvaz Simultaneously Hit
Israel's Air Force launched another intensive wave of strikes across western and central Iran on March 13–14, hitting more than 200 targets in a single operational period. Simultaneous strikes were conducted in Tehran, Shiraz, and Ahvaz. Targets included an underground ballistic missile production and storage facility, a central air-defense base, and additional regime infrastructure. One strike in Tehran caused an explosion near a state-organized pro-Palestinian demonstration in Enghelab Square, though no immediate casualties were reported by the Associated Press. The IDF stated it issued warnings to Iranians to vacate areas surrounding military infrastructure before strikes commenced. CENTCOM simultaneously confirmed that between US and Israeli forces, over 15,000 enemy targets have now been struck since February 28.

Coalition · Strike Offensive · Strategic Strike
CENTCOM Confirms 90+ Iranian Naval Vessels Destroyed; Entire Soleimani-Class Warship Class Eliminated
CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper confirmed that US forces have destroyed more than 60 Iranian ships as of March 11, with the total rising to over 90 naval vessels destroyed by the time of the Day 13–14 update. Cooper specifically announced the destruction of the last of four Soleimani-class guided-missile catamarans — the first Iranian warships capable of vertical launch systems — marking the elimination of an entire Iranian warship class. CENTCOM also issued urgent directives for Iranian civilians to vacate all port facilities, warning that civilian ports used for military purposes lose their protected status under international law. Iranian mine-laying operations in the Strait of Hormuz remain an ongoing threat, with the US pivoting naval strategy to aggressively target IRGC mine-laying vessels.

Mar
Coalition · Ground Threat Escalation · Lebanon Ground Operation
Israel Plans Massive Lebanon Ground Invasion; 800,000+ Civilians Displaced, Lebanese Government Proposes Ceasefire
Axios and multiple Israeli and US officials confirmed on March 14 that Israel is planning a significantly expanded ground operation in Lebanon, aiming to seize all territory south of the Litani River and dismantle Hezbollah's military infrastructure — potentially the largest Israeli ground invasion of Lebanon since 2006. The IDF has issued evacuation orders across southern Lebanon and — for the first time — to villages north of the Litani River and Hezbollah's stronghold in Beirut's southern suburbs. The IRC and Lebanese President Joseph Aoun confirmed that approximately 820,000 people, including over 200,000 children, have been displaced. At least 773 Lebanese have been killed. Aoun presented a four-point ceasefire proposal — including a complete truce and Lebanese Army disarmament of Hezbollah — which Israel rejected. Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem declared 'there is no solution except resistance.' The IDF has three armored and infantry divisions on the Lebanese border and is mobilizing additional reserves.

14
Mar
CENTCOM · Precision Strike Offensive · Strategic Infrastructure
CENTCOM Strikes 90+ Military Targets on Iran's Kharg Island; Oil Infrastructure Preserved
In the early hours of March 14 (Day 15), U.S. Central Command executed what President Trump described as 'one of the most powerful bombing raids in the History of the Middle East' against Kharg Island — Iran's primary oil export hub responsible for approximately 90% of the country's crude exports. CENTCOM confirmed that more than 90 Iranian military targets were destroyed, including naval mine storage facilities, missile storage bunkers, air defenses, a naval base, and airport facilities, while oil infrastructure was deliberately preserved. Trump announced the strike via Truth Social, warning that if Iran interfered with free passage through the Strait of Hormuz, he would 'immediately reconsider' sparing oil infrastructure. Iran's semi-official Fars News Agency reported over 15 explosions on the island but confirmed no damage to oil infrastructure. Iran's armed forces responded with a threat to destroy 'all oil and gas infrastructure in the region' tied to U.S. allies if energy assets on Kharg were hit. Tehran separately threatened to attack three major UAE ports — Jebel Ali, Khalifa, and Fujairah — claiming they were used to launch the Kharg strikes.
📍Kharg Island, Persian Gulf — 15 miles off Iran's southern coast 💥90+ Iranian military targets destroyed; oil infrastructure preserved ⚠️Iran threatens UAE port infrastructure; vows to destroy regional energy assets if oil struck
Sources: NPR NBC News CNBC
14
Mar
Pentagon · Operational Update Briefing · Capability Assessment
Hegseth: 15,000+ Targets Hit; Iran Missiles Down 90%, Drones Down 95%; Mojtaba Khamenei 'Wounded and Likely Disfigured'
At a Pentagon press conference on March 13–14 (Day 14–15), Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs Gen. Dan Caine confirmed that the U.S.-Israeli coalition has now struck more than 15,000 enemy targets — more than 1,000 per day since February 28. Hegseth declared Iran's missile volume is down 90% and one-way attack drones are down 95% compared to Day 1. He stated flatly: 'Iran has no air defenses. Iran has no air force. Iran has no navy.' Hegseth also disclosed that new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is 'wounded and likely disfigured,' questioning why Khamenei's first public message was delivered in written form rather than on camera: 'His father: dead; he's scared, he's injured, he's on the run, and he lacks legitimacy.' CENTCOM separately confirmed approximately 200 U.S. service members have been wounded since the start of the operation, with the vast majority sustaining minor injuries; nearly 170 have already returned to duty, and 10 remain seriously wounded in stable condition.
📍Pentagon, Washington D.C. 📊15,000+ targets struck; missiles -90%, drones -95% vs. Day 1 🤕~200 U.S. wounded total; 10 seriously wounded, stable; 13 KIA confirmed
Sources: DefenseScoop PBS NewsHour GlobalSecurity.org
15
Mar
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Talks Diplomatic · Stalemate
Trump Rejects Ceasefire Deal; Iran Hardens Position; IRGC Blocks All Talks
As of March 15, ceasefire diplomacy remains fully deadlocked. President Trump stated on Saturday that he is 'not ready' to negotiate a deal, saying 'the terms aren't good enough yet.' On the Iranian side, two senior Iranian sources told Reuters that Tehran has rejected all mediation efforts — from Egypt, Oman, and others — and will not engage in ceasefire discussions until U.S. and Israeli strikes permanently end and Iran receives compensation. The IRGC has gone further, internally blocking even back-channel discussions: 'The Guards strongly believe that if they lose control over the Strait of Hormuz, Iran will lose the war,' one senior Iranian source said, adding that 'Iran's political leaders will not engage in such talks despite attempts by several countries.' Iran's Ministry of Intelligence had previously reached out indirectly to the CIA through a third country's intelligence service to discuss terms, but the contact produced no results as Iran's leadership structure remained in disarray. Trump, meanwhile, continued calling for Iran's 'unconditional surrender,' while also saying he heard Mojtaba Khamenei 'is not alive' — though he called that a rumor — and urged him to 'do something very smart for his country, and that's surrender.'
🏛️Mediators: Egypt, Oman, Qatar — all stalled 🚫IRGC blocks all ceasefire talks; Iran demands end to strikes + compensation 🗣️Trump: 'Terms aren't good enough yet'; still demanding unconditional surrender
Sources: The Manila Times / Reuters NBC News The Week
14
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Escalation Threat
Iran Threatens UAE Port Cities; Strikes US Embassy Helipad in Baghdad Green Zone; Fire at UAE Oil Terminal
Following the U.S. Kharg Island strikes, Iran dramatically escalated its rhetoric on March 14–15, threatening for the first time to attack three major UAE civilian port cities — Jebel Ali, Khalifa, and Fujairah — claiming U.S. forces used them to launch the Kharg strikes, and urging civilians to evacuate those areas. A fire broke out at an oil terminal in Fujairah, UAE, following a drone attack. Separately, an Iranian missile struck a helipad inside the U.S. Embassy compound in Baghdad's heavily guarded Green Zone, destroying an air defense system component and prompting a new U.S. Embassy emergency warning for Americans to leave Iraq immediately. Iran's armed forces warned that if any oil infrastructure on Kharg Island is struck, 'all oil and gas infrastructure in the region in which the U.S. and its allies have interests will be set on fire and destroyed.' Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi also vowed retaliation for the Kharg Island strike, saying Iran's armed forces would target any energy infrastructure tied to American companies in the region.
📍UAE (Fujairah oil terminal fire); Baghdad Green Zone (US Embassy helipad struck) ⚠️Iran threatens Jebel Ali, Khalifa, Fujairah ports; urges civilian evacuation 💀No confirmed casualties in Baghdad Embassy strike; Green Zone air defense component destroyed
Sources: France 24 CBS News NPR / ABC7
14
Mar
IDF · Lebanon Offensive · Ground Escalation Preparation
Israel Finalizes Plans for Massive Lebanon Ground Invasion; 820,000+ Displaced as Humanitarian Crisis Deepens
Israel is finalizing plans for a large-scale ground invasion of southern Lebanon aimed at seizing the entire area south of the Litani River and dismantling Hezbollah's military infrastructure, according to Israeli and U.S. officials. The operation is expected to be the largest Israeli ground invasion of Lebanon since 2006. A senior Israeli official stated the trigger was a massive Hezbollah coordinated attack launching more than 200 missiles in a single barrage in coordination with Iranian strikes: 'Before this attack we were ready for a ceasefire in Lebanon, but after it there is no way back from a massive operation.' The IDF has three armored and infantry divisions positioned on the Lebanese border. The Trump administration backs the major operation to disarm Hezbollah but is pressing Israel to spare Beirut's international airport and Lebanese state infrastructure. Lebanon's Health Ministry confirmed at least 773 people have been killed (including more than 100 children) and 1,933 injured since fighting erupted. The International Rescue Committee reported more than 820,000 people — including over 200,000 children — have been displaced. Washington Post reported nearly a million Lebanese have been displaced, with aid groups warning of a deepening humanitarian catastrophe.
📍Southern Lebanon, Beqaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs — full south-of-Litani evacuation orders 💀773 killed in Lebanon (incl. 100+ children, 18 paramedics); 1,933 injured 🏚️820,000+ displaced; stadiums, schools, churches converted to shelters; ~100,000 crossed to Syria
Sources: Axios Washington Post IRC
14
Mar
Pentagon · KIA Casualty · KC-135 Crash
Pentagon Identifies All 6 Air Force Airmen Killed in KC-135 Crash Over Western Iraq
The Pentagon formally identified the six U.S. Air Force airmen killed when a KC-135 refueling aircraft crashed in western Iraq on March 12 (Day 13). Three were assigned to the 6th Air Refueling Wing at MacDill Air Force Base, Florida: Maj. John A. Klinner, 33, of Auburn, Alabama; Capt. Ariana G. Savino, 31, of Covington, Washington; and Tech. Sgt. Ashley B. Pruitt, 34, of Bardstown, Kentucky. The other three were assigned to the 121st Air Refueling Wing at Rickenbacker Air National Guard Base in Columbus, Ohio: Capt. Seth R. Koval, 38, of Mooresville, Indiana; Capt. Curtis J. Angst, 30, of Wilmington, Ohio; and Tech. Sgt. Tyler H. Simmons, 28, of Columbus, Ohio. Gen. Caine confirmed the crash occurred 'over friendly territory in western Iraq' and was not the result of hostile or enemy fire. The crash brought total confirmed U.S. KIA in the Iran war to at least 13.
📍Western Iraq — non-hostile crash during refueling operations 💀6 airmen KIA — 6th ARW (MacDill AFB, FL) and 121st ARW (Rickenbacker ANGB, OH) 📊Total U.S. KIA: at least 13 confirmed (7 by enemy fire + 6 in KC-135 crash)
Sources: ABC7 / AP NBC Washington
15
Mar
Iran · Missile Barrage Offensive · Cluster-Munition Ballistic Missiles
Iran Fires 7+ Ballistic Salvos at Israel Since Midnight; Cluster Munitions Suspected
On the morning of March 15 (Day 16), Iran launched at least seven ballistic missile salvos at Israel since midnight, wounding at least five people — one moderately — in central Israel. Police and rescue services confirmed that one impact in a central city appeared to involve cluster bomb munitions, with shrapnel igniting a vehicle fire and damaging an apartment building in Holon. Sirens sounded across a wide arc from northern towns including Kiryat Shmona and Metula down through Tel Aviv, Netanya, Holon, Petah Tikva, and Eilat. The IDF said it suspected Iran had again used cluster munition warheads on its ballistic missiles. Israel's air defense systems engaged throughout the night and morning. Qatar separately reported intercepting four ballistic missiles and several drones launched from Iran overnight.
📍Central & northern Israel; Tel Aviv metro; Holon; Petah Tikva; Eilat; Qatar (4 missiles intercepted) 💀5 injured in Israel since midnight (1 moderately); no fatalities reported in this wave
Sources: Times of Israel Haaretz
15
Mar
IDF · Airstrikes Offensive · Multi-Front Strike Wave
IDF Intensifies Strikes on Iran & Hezbollah; Hits Iranian Space Agency Research Center
In the early hours of March 15, heavy airstrikes were reported across Iran's Isfahan area, with 20 explosions heard near Shiraz, heavy explosions in southern Tehran, and strikes on Dezful Air Base and Khomein. Late on March 14 into the 15th, the IDF announced it had struck the primary research center of the Iranian Space Agency and an aerial defense system production factory. Simultaneously, the IDF issued evacuation orders for residents in Beirut's southern suburbs including the Dahiya district, and conducted air and ground operations in southern Lebanon targeting Hezbollah launch sites, weapons depots, command centers, and Radwan Force facilities. The IDF's 7th Brigade conducted counterterrorism operations against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon. Overnight Israeli strikes in Lebanon killed at least four people, including one person in a residential building in Sidon. Israel also approved a 2.6 billion shekel emergency defense budget for urgent procurement of armaments and advanced weapons.
📍Isfahan, Shiraz, southern Tehran, Dezful Air Base, Khomein (Iran); Dahiya/southern Beirut, Sidon (Lebanon) 💥Iranian Space Agency research center destroyed; air defense production factory struck; 4 killed in Lebanon overnight
