15
Mar
Iran · Threats Escalatory · IRGC Death Threat vs. Netanyahu
IRGC Vows to 'Pursue and Kill' Netanyahu; Araghchi Accuses US-Israel of Striking Arab States
On March 15, the IRGC issued an explicit threat via Iran's state-affiliated Mehr news agency vowing to 'pursue' and then 'kill' Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. Separately, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that Tehran had received information that the U.S. and Israel were also conducting attacks in Arab countries, and confirmed ongoing talks with Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Oman, Bahrain, and the UAE about forming a joint committee to investigate regional strikes. Araghchi told France that countries must avoid actions that escalate the conflict. Meanwhile, Saudi Arabia reported shooting down 26 Iranian drones over its territory, and the UAE reported a missile attack. Air raid sirens sounded in Bahrain and Tel Aviv as the conflict entered its third week with no ceasefire in sight.
📍Tehran (IRGC statement); Gulf-wide (KSA: 26 drones downed; UAE: missile attack; Bahrain: sirens) ⚠️Explicit IRGC death threat against Netanyahu; no ceasefire talks underway
Sources: NPR CNBC Haaretz
15
Mar
Diplomacy · Pope / UN International · Ceasefire Calls
Pope Leo Calls for Immediate Ceasefire; UN Chief Guterres Visits Beirut, Launches $325M Appeal
On March 15, Pope Leo made an impassioned plea for an immediate ceasefire in the expanding Iran war, lamenting 'atrocious violence' that he said had killed thousands of non-combatants. The first U.S. pope warned that violence would not bring justice, stability, or peace to the region. This followed a March 14 visit to Beirut by UN Secretary-General António Guterres, who called for an immediate end to hostilities, insisting 'there is no military solution, only diplomacy.' Guterres launched a $325 million humanitarian appeal to support Lebanon, warning that the south of the country 'risks being turned into a wasteland.' He noted that well over 800,000 Lebanese had been displaced and that hundreds had been killed, including many children. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun proposed negotiations with Israel but had yet to receive a response. Trump, meanwhile, said on Saturday that the terms for a deal were 'not good enough yet' and signaled the war would continue.
📍Vatican (Pope Leo statement); Beirut (Guterres visit); Washington (Trump comments) 🕊️$325M UN humanitarian appeal launched for Lebanon; 800,000+ displaced; Trump rejects current ceasefire terms
Sources: Euronews Haaretz The Week India UN News
15
Mar
US · Force Posture Defensive · Missile Interceptor Warning
US Officials: Israel Running Critically Low on Ballistic Missile Interceptors; IDF Denies
U.S. officials warned on March 15 that Israel is running critically low on ballistic missile interceptors, amid the sustained Iranian barrage now in its 16th day. The IDF denied the reports, stating it was not running 'critically low' on interceptors. Israel separately approved a 2.6 billion shekel emergency defense budget for urgent procurement of armaments and advanced weapons, suggesting supply concerns are real. The warning comes as Iran has fired at least seven salvos at Israel since midnight alone on March 15, with cluster munitions suspected. Approximately 5,000 additional Marines and sailors were also reported deploying to the Middle East to bolster U.S. force posture.
📍Israel (interceptor stocks); MacDill AFB / CENTCOM AOR (5,000 additional Marines/sailors deploying) ⚠️IDF approves 2.6B shekel emergency defense procurement; US warns of interceptor shortfall
Sources: Haaretz NBC Washington
15
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Ballistic Missile Salvo
Iran Fires New Ballistic Missile Barrage at Israel; Cluster Munitions Impact Holon & Eilat
On the morning of March 15 (Day 16), Iran launched at least one confirmed new barrage of ballistic missiles toward Israel. Magen David Adom paramedics responded to shrapnel-hit sites in central Israel following the strike. Iranian missiles wounded at least 3 people in Eilat, including a child. A firefighter was photographed extinguishing a blaze in a vehicle following the impact of what appeared to be a cluster munition from an Iranian ballistic missile strike. Israel's military confirmed the launches and instructed residents in affected areas to head to shelters immediately. The IRGC's ballistic missile launch rate has declined roughly 90% from Day 1, per CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper, but Iran continues to conduct targeted high-impact penetration strikes.
📍Eilat, Holon, and central Israel 💀3+ wounded including a child (Eilat); vehicle fire in Holon from cluster munition impact 🚀Iran's IRGC ballistic missile rate down ~90% from Day 1 per CENTCOM
Sources: Times of Israel Haaretz
15
Mar
IDF · Offensive Offensive · Wide-Scale Air Strike
IDF Launches Broad Wave of Strikes on Western Iran; Hits IRGC Command Centers in Hamadan Province
On Sunday, March 15, the Israeli Air Force announced it had begun a wide-scale wave of strikes targeting Iranian regime infrastructure in western Iran. According to an IDF statement, several IRGC command centers and Basij headquarters were struck in Hamadan province. Explosions were also reported near Isfahan, Shiraz, Dezful Air Base, Khomein, and southern Tehran in the early hours of March 15. A video circulated on social media showing extensive damage from an airstrike at Jask port in Hormozgan Province. CENTCOM separately confirmed on the evening of March 15 that U.S. forces struck another Iranian military capability, posting that 'U.S. forces continue to degrade Iranian military capability.'
📍Hamadan Province, Isfahan, Shiraz, Dezful, Khomein, S. Tehran, Jask port (Hormozgan) 🎯IRGC command centers, Basij HQ, aerial defense infrastructure, port military facilities

Mar
Iran · Statement Diplomatic · Defiance
Araghchi: Iran 'Never' Asked for Ceasefire, Ready to Fight Until Trump Admits Defeat
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi appeared on CBS News' Face the Nation on Sunday, March 15, forcefully rejecting any suggestion Iran had sought a ceasefire or negotiations. Araghchi defended Iran's strikes on Gulf states hosting U.S. forces, stating that those countries had 'given their soil to American forces to attack us.' He told CBS that Iran would continue to fight until U.S. President Donald Trump 'comes to the point that this is an illegal war with no victory.' Earlier, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian's comments echoed Araghchi's defiance, hours after Araghchi also ruled out negotiations with the U.S. on a separate NBC News appearance the previous day. Qatar separately reported it had intercepted four ballistic missiles and several drones launched from Iran on March 15.
📺CBS News Face the Nation — FM Araghchi interview 🛡️Qatar intercepted 4 ballistic missiles + several drones launched from Iran (Mar 15)
Sources: Haaretz CNBC
15
Mar
US · Escalation Warning Strategic · Policy
UN Amb. Waltz: Trump Weighing Strikes on Kharg Island Oil Infrastructure
U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations Mike Waltz said on CNN's State of the Union on Sunday, March 15, that President Trump is actively weighing strikes on the oil infrastructure at Kharg Island, Iran's primary crude export hub accounting for approximately 90% of the country's oil exports with a loading capacity of roughly 7 million barrels per day. Waltz stated that 'President Trump's not going to take any options off the table.' Trump had previously directed CENTCOM to carry out a large-scale precision strike on Kharg Island's military targets on March 14, striking more than 90 military sites — including naval mine storage facilities and missile storage bunkers — while deliberately sparing the oil infrastructure. The IEA announced on March 15 that emergency stocks of oil 'will soon start flowing to global markets,' updating last week's 400 million barrel release figure to nearly 412 million barrels.
📍Kharg Island, Hormozgan Province — Iran's primary oil export terminal ⚠️Trump considering strikes on oil infrastructure; oil prices up 40%+ since Feb 28 🛢️IEA releases ~412M emergency barrels to global markets
Sources: CNBC Middle East Eye / CENTCOM
15
Mar
IDF · Lebanon Intel · Leadership Strike
IDF Kills Senior Hezbollah Badr Unit Commander; Brother of Detroit Synagogue Shooter
The Israeli military announced on Sunday, March 15, that it had killed Ibrahim Mohamad Ghazali in a strike last week. Ghazali was identified as a weapons operations commander in Hezbollah's Badr unit. The IDF stated that Ghazali is the brother of the Detroit synagogue shooter, Ayman Mohamad Ghazali. The U.S. Department of Homeland Security had previously flagged both brothers as known Hezbollah members. Israel is also planning a major ground expansion in Lebanon, with the IDF issuing evacuation orders covering roughly a tenth of the country, including the entire area south of the Litani River and Beirut's southern suburbs. Around 831,000+ Lebanese civilians have been displaced as of March 14–15 per multiple humanitarian organizations, with 773+ killed and 1,933+ injured per Lebanon's Health Ministry.
💀Ibrahim Mohamad Ghazali — Hezbollah Badr Unit weapons ops commander, killed in IDF strike 📍Southern Lebanon / Beirut southern suburbs 🏠831,000+ Lebanese civilians displaced (Axios/IRC/Brussels Morning, Mar 14–15)

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Ballistic Missile / Drone Barrage
Iran Warns Civilians to Evacuate Areas of Dubai & Doha Ahead of Imminent Strikes
Iran's Media Operations Center issued an urgent warning to civilians in specific districts of Dubai and Doha — including the Ain Khaled neighborhood, Lusail, and Al Waab district — urging residents to evacuate immediately. Iran claimed US military personnel were concealed in those locations and that strikes were imminent. The warning came as Iran continued its wave of daily drone and missile salvoes against Gulf states. Qatar confirmed it had intercepted four ballistic missiles and several drones launched from Iran. The IRGC's vow to 'pursue and kill' Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was also reiterated, with Iran's state-affiliated Mehr news agency reporting the IRGC statement directly. Trump dismissed reports of an alleged Iranian attack on the USS Abraham Lincoln as 'fake news,' threatening FCC scrutiny and treason charges against outlets that broadcast the claims.
📍Dubai (Al Waab / DXB vicinity), Doha (Ain Khaled / Lusail), Persian Gulf 💀No confirmed casualties from Mar 16 warning wave; Qatar intercepted 4 ballistic missiles + several drones
Sources: Times of Israel Haaretz ABC News
15
Mar
IDF · Strike Wave Offensive · Air Strike
IAF Completes 400th Strike Wave; IDF Says Thousands of Targets Remain
The Israeli Air Force announced the successful completion of its 400th wave of strikes against Iran under Operation Roaring Lion on Saturday, March 15. Targets included missile stockpiles and IRGC personnel. In the past 24 hours, the IAF struck over 200 targets in western and central Iran, including command centers manned by Iranian soldiers, air defense systems, and weapon storage and production sites. IDF Spokesman Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin stated in a televised briefing: 'We have a precise plan. We still have thousands of targets in Iran, and we are identifying new targets every day.' According to IDF intelligence, over 6,000 IRGC members have been killed and approximately 15,000 wounded. The IDF also noted it continued to degrade Iran's ballistic missile production infrastructure throughout the country. Israel additionally confirmed it was moving additional troops north toward Lebanon in preparation for an expanded ground operation, with three armored and infantry divisions already positioned on the Lebanese border.
📍Western and central Iran; Iranian airspace (400th IAF wave) 💀6,000+ IRGC killed / 15,000+ wounded (IDF intelligence estimate)
Sources: Times of Israel Jerusalem Post NPR
14
Mar
CENTCOM · Kharg Island Offensive · Strategic Bombing
Trump Orders Massive Strike on Kharg Island Military Targets; 15,000+ Total Targets Struck
On Day 15 (March 14), Trump directed a major bombing raid against military targets at Iran's Kharg Island oil hub, deliberately sparing civilian oil infrastructure but using the strike as an explicit warning: if the Strait of Hormuz shipping blockade continued, oil infrastructure would be next. Secretary of Defense Hegseth simultaneously reported that coalition forces had now struck over 15,000 enemy targets combined, with Iranian ballistic missile launch volumes down 90% and drone launches down 95% from Day 1 peaks. Hegseth also stated that Mojtaba Khamenei was 'likely disfigured' by earlier strikes. A separate Iranian missile struck the helipad inside the US Embassy compound in Baghdad in the second attack on the embassy since the war began. Saudi Arabia intercepted 51 Iranian drones in the largest single-day barrage targeting the kingdom. Israel destroyed the Zrarieh Bridge over the Litani River to interdict Hezbollah supply lines.
📍Kharg Island (Iran); US Embassy Baghdad (Iraq); Saudi Arabia (multi-site) 💀No confirmed US/coalition KIA from Kharg raid; Baghdad embassy helipad destroyed, no casualties confirmed
Sources: GlobalSecurity.org WND / CENTCOM ABC News
15
Mar
Lebanon · Civilian Crisis Humanitarian · Mass Displacement
Lebanon Death Toll Hits 850+; 820,000+ Civilians Displaced as Israel Plans Major Ground Invasion
Lebanon's Health Ministry reported on Sunday, March 15, that 850 people have been killed and over 2,000 injured since the war began on February 28. The Lebanese displacement crisis has reached catastrophic levels, with more than 820,000 civilians — including over 200,000 children — forcibly displaced from their homes, according to the International Rescue Committee. The IDF has issued evacuation orders across southern Lebanon, the Beqaa Valley, and Beirut's southern suburbs, with orders now extending north of the Litani River for the first time. Israel is simultaneously planning a massive ground invasion to seize the entire area south of the Litani River and dismantle Hezbollah's military infrastructure — potentially the largest Israeli ground operation in Lebanon since 2006. The Trump administration has urged Israel to spare Beirut's international airport and Lebanese state infrastructure, but Israel has stopped short of a full commitment. The IDF also struck Hezbollah's Radwan Force headquarters in Beirut and destroyed several launch sites in the Al-Qataraani area on Saturday.
📍Southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs; IDF northern border staging areas 💀850+ killed / 2,000+ wounded in Lebanon (Health Ministry, Mar 15); 820,000+ displaced (IRC)
Sources: NPR Axios IRC
15
Mar
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Rejected Political · Statement
Araghchi: Iran 'Never' Asked for Ceasefire; Mojtaba Khamenei Vows Hormuz Will Stay Closed
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi firmly reiterated on Sunday, March 15, that Iran has not requested a ceasefire and is not seeking negotiations with the United States, telling NPR: 'We never asked for a ceasefire.' Meanwhile, new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei — who has made no public video or live appearance since his selection on March 8 — issued a written statement vowing that the Strait of Hormuz would remain closed and that Iran's retaliation against US-Israeli strikes was 'not complete.' French President Emmanuel Macron spoke with Iranian President Pezeshkian and urged Iran to immediately stop all direct and proxy attacks, warning that escalating tensions were pushing the region toward chaos and calling for a new political and security framework to prevent Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons. Pope Leo XIV also made his most forceful public statement of the war, calling on all parties to 'cease the fire' in the name of Christians of the Middle East and all people of goodwill.
📍Tehran (IRGC statement); Paris (Macron call); Vatican (Pope Leo XIV) 💀N/A — diplomatic/political developments
Sources: NPR CFR Haaretz
15
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · IRGC 'Most Intense' Wave; Dubai & Doha Strikes
Iran Launches Strikes Near Dubai Airport & Across Gulf; IRGC Claims 'Most Intense' Operation Yet
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced its 'most intense and heaviest operation' since the war began on March 11, unleashing coordinated waves of missiles and drones across the Gulf. Two Iranian drones fell in the vicinity of Dubai International Airport, injuring four foreign nationals — the second such strike on the airport. Bahrain reported intercepting 114 missiles and 190 drones cumulatively since February 28, with a drone strike sparking a fire at the Ma'ameer facility near Manama. Kuwait's National Guard shot down 8 drones, Bahrain sounded air raid sirens, and Oman downed two Iranian drones north of Al Duqm port. Iran also struck a container vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, setting it ablaze roughly 25 nautical miles northwest of Ras Al-Khaimah. A senior IRGC commander declared Iran was now only firing missiles with payloads of 1,000 kg or more — a shift from saturation to high-impact penetration strikes. Iran warned Dubai and Doha civilians ahead of further imminent strikes by March 16.
📍Dubai Intl Airport vicinity, Bahrain Ma'ameer, Strait of Hormuz (25nm NW of Ras Al-Khaimah), Kuwait, Oman Al Duqm 💀4 injured at Dubai Airport; dozens wounded in Bahrain Sitra; 1 container ship on fire in Hormuz
Sources: Al Jazeera Euronews

Mar
IDF · Strike Wave Offensive · IDF Says Campaign Ahead of Schedule; 3 More Weeks Planned
IDF: Campaign Running Ahead of Schedule; 3+ More Weeks of Strikes Planned Against Iran's Defense Industry
Israeli military officials confirmed on March 15 that the joint campaign against Iran is proceeding faster than initially expected, yet the IDF is planning at least three more weeks of operations as thousands of additional targets remain. The IDF has struck more than 1,700 assets tied to Iran's military industry since Feb. 28. Military officials said Israeli Air Force now holds air supremacy across most of Iran's airspace after destroying more than 100 air defense systems and about 120 detection and radar systems. IDF officials estimated between 4,000–5,000 Iranian soldiers had been killed in strikes, with tens of thousands more wounded, and noted signs of declining morale and desertion within Iranian ballistic missile units. IDF Spokesman stated: 'We have thousands of targets ahead.' The campaign has also caused significant damage to Iran's ballistic missile production industry; officials said Iran currently lacks the ability to manufacture any new missiles. Early on March 15, heavy airstrikes were reported in the Isfahan area, with 20 explosions near Shiraz, heavy explosions in southern Tehran, Dezful Air Base, Khomein, and Hamadan. A video showed extensive damage from a strike at the Jask port in Hormozgan Province.
📍Isfahan, Shiraz, Tehran (south), Dezful Air Base, Khomein, Hamadan, Jask port (Hormozgan) 💀IDF estimates 4,000–5,000 Iranian soldiers killed total; missile production industry severely damaged
Sources: Times of Israel VINnews

Mar
CENTCOM · Strike Confirmation Offensive · CENTCOM Confirms Continued Degradation of Iranian Military
CENTCOM Confirms Continued Strikes on Iranian Military Capabilities; Saturday Evening Statement Issued
U.S. Central Command announced on Saturday evening, March 15, that it had struck another Iranian military capability, issuing a statement that 'U.S. forces continue to degrade Iranian military capability.' This followed the broader March 13 CENTCOM briefing in which Secretary Hegseth and Chairman Caine confirmed over 15,000 total enemy targets struck between U.S. and Israeli forces. CENTCOM has maintained a near-continuous strike tempo, with B-2 stealth bombers conducting long-range missions to eliminate Iranian military infrastructure, including underground missile sites and UAV production facilities. Cooper previously confirmed air dominance over Iran, with Iranian ballistic missile attack rates down 90% and drone attacks down 83% from Day 1. More than 50,000 U.S. military personnel are supporting Operation Epic Fury.
📍Multiple locations across Iran; B-2 strikes on underground missile sites and UAV production facilities 💀15,000+ total targets struck (CENTCOM, Mar 13); Iranian missile launch rate down ~90% from Day 1

Mar
Iran · Leadership Political · New Supreme Leader Khamenei Vows Hormuz Blockade Continues
Mojtaba Khamenei: Iran Will Not Surrender; Hormuz Pressure to Continue; Trump Uncertain if New Supreme Leader Is Alive
In a statement attributed to new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran vowed to continue the war, promising to maintain pressure on the Strait of Hormuz and declaring that Iran's retaliation against U.S.-Israeli strikes 'was not complete.' President Trump told NBC News on March 15 that he was not sure whether Mojtaba Khamenei was still alive, saying 'so far n[ot confirmed].' The IRGC separately vowed to 'pursue' Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu and 'kill him,' according to Iran's state-affiliated Mehr news agency. Iran's security forces stepped up domestic surveillance, with Hengaw Organization for Human Rights documenting widespread phone checks at checkpoints and text-message warnings to citizens not to share information with foreign media. Dozens across Iran have been arrested on suspicion of spying for Israel since the war began.
📍Tehran — statement attributed to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei; Strait of Hormuz 💀Dozens arrested inside Iran on espionage charges; IRGC threatens Netanyahu's life

Lebanon · Humanitarian Crisis · UN Flash Appeal Issued; 820,000+ Displaced
UN Issues $308M Flash Appeal for Lebanon; 820,000+ Displaced as Israel Plans Full-Scale Ground Invasion
UN Secretary-General António Guterres visited Beirut on March 14, announcing a $308.3 million flash humanitarian appeal after roughly 816,000–820,000 Lebanese civilians were internally displaced — a figure the UN warned would continue to rise. Guterres warned that southern Lebanon 'risks being turned into a wasteland' and called the Israeli bombing campaign 'devastating' and 'rendering large portions of Lebanon uninhabitable.' The IRC reported over 820,000 displaced, including 200,000+ children. Meanwhile, Israel is planning a massive ground invasion aiming to seize all territory south of the Litani River and dismantle Hezbollah's military infrastructure — potentially the largest Israeli ground incursion into Lebanon since 2006. The IDF has issued evacuation orders to villages and towns north of the Litani River for the first time, and sent reinforcements and additional reserves to the Lebanon border. Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem declared 'there is no solution except resistance.' Lebanon's death toll stands at 850+ killed and over 2,000 injured.

US–Israel · Strikes Offensive · Multi-City Airstrike Wave
IDF Strikes Isfahan, Shiraz, Tehran, Hamadan & Dezful; 200+ Targets Hit in 24 Hours
In the early hours of March 15–16, the Israeli Air Force launched a wide-scale wave of strikes across western and central Iran. Heavy airstrikes were reported in the Isfahan area, with approximately 20 explosions heard near Shiraz, heavy explosions in southern Tehran, and strikes on Dezful Air Base, Khomein, and Hamedan. The IDF confirmed hitting over 200 targets in the past 24-hour period, including command centers manned by Iranian soldiers, air defense systems, and weapon storage and production sites. CENTCOM separately confirmed a continued US strike on Iranian military capabilities on Saturday evening. Israel stated it still has 'thousands of targets' ahead, with new ones identified daily, and is preparing at least three more weeks of strikes to systematically degrade Iran's entire defense industry.

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile & Drone Strikes
Iran Fires 4 Missile Salvos at Israel; Dubai Airport Fuel Tank Fire; Abu Dhabi Casualty Reported
On March 16, Iran fired four missile salvos at Israel, injuring at least one person in what appeared to be a cluster bomb attack; a missile also struck and damaged a house in Rishon Lezion. Simultaneously, a fuel tank near Dubai International Airport caught fire following a drone-related incident, prompting a temporary flight suspension. In Abu Dhabi, one person was killed after a missile struck a vehicle. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi declared that the Strait of Hormuz is 'open, but closed to our enemies,' as Iran continued to restrict passage for US-allied vessels. Tehran also flatly rejected Trump's claim that Iran 'wants to make a deal,' with Araghchi reiterating that Iran has not requested a ceasefire.

Mar
IDF · Ground Ops Ground · Limited Incursion South Lebanon
IDF Launches 'Limited and Targeted' Ground Operation in South Lebanon to Expand Buffer Zone
On March 16, the Israeli Defense Forces announced a 'limited and targeted' ground operation in southern Lebanon, with the stated objective of expanding Israel's buffer zone and ensuring Hezbollah no longer poses a threat to residents of northern Israel. IDF troops of the 91st 'Galilee' Regional Division were deployed in the operation. Defense Minister Israel Katz stated the operation would continue until Hezbollah no longer threatens northern Israel. The IDF is simultaneously preparing for a significantly larger ground invasion, aiming to seize the entire area south of the Litani River and dismantle Hezbollah's military infrastructure. Hundreds of thousands of Lebanese civilians have now been evacuated from southern Lebanon amid the escalation.

Sources: CNN Times of Israel Axios

Mar
Diplomacy · Hormuz Diplomatic · Strait of Hormuz Coalition
Trump Calls on China, UK, France, Japan, S. Korea to Send Warships to Hormuz; No Firm Commitments Yet
As the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively shut to US-allied tanker traffic — with Iran only permitting vessels from non-belligerent nations to pass — President Trump appealed publicly to China, France, Japan, South Korea, the UK and other countries to dispatch warships to the strait to restore passage. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer confirmed the UK is working with allies on a plan to reopen the strait. Trump said he received 'some positive response' but noted a few countries 'would rather not get involved.' No country has yet made a firm public commitment. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi simultaneously declared that the strait is 'open, but closed to our enemies,' and said Iran has no hesitation in continuing the war. Iran said about 700 missiles and 3,600 drones have been fired at US and Israeli targets since the war started February 28.
📍Strait of Hormuz — effectively closed to US-allied shipping; ~20% of global oil supply at risk 📊Iran claims ~700 missiles + 3,600 drones fired total since Feb 28; Brent crude above $106/barrel
Sources: CNN Al Jazeera Live CNN Live
16
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile & Drone Barrage
Iran Fires on UAE, Saudi Arabia & Gulf States; Dubai Fuel Tank Fire Extinguished; Fujairah Port Hit
On Day 17 (March 16), Iran continued its degraded but persistent missile and drone campaign across Gulf states. The UAE announced it intercepted six Iranian ballistic missiles and 21 drones. Saudi Arabia's defense ministry reported it intercepted and destroyed 64 drones since midnight, with 61 targeting its eastern region and three downed near Riyadh. Iran targeted the Port of Fujairah along the UAE's east coast with a drone strike, sparking a fire in its petroleum industrial zone and temporarily halting port operations. In Kuwait, two missiles struck the perimeter of Ahmad al-Jaber airbase, wounding three soldiers, while drones struck facilities at Kuwait's international airport, damaging part of its radar system. Bahrain's international airport remained shuttered with airspace closed, sirens activated numerous times overnight. Qatar's interior ministry described its situation as 'stable.' The IRGC Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council Ali Larijani issued a six-point message to Muslims worldwide calling for 'unity of the Islamic nation.' Iran's Foreign Minister stated the country does not seek a ceasefire but the war 'must end,' vowing to continue fighting until future attacks are prevented.
📍UAE (Fujairah Port, Al-Dhafra AB), Saudi Arabia (Eastern Region, Riyadh), Kuwait (Ahmad al-Jaber AB, airport), Bahrain (Manama), Qatar 💀3 Kuwaiti soldiers wounded at Ahmad al-Jaber; multiple injuries across region; no confirmed new fatalities this reporting period
Sources: Haaretz Live (Day 17) Al Jazeera – How is Iran still shooting? Jerusalem Post Live
16
Mar
IDF · Space & Anti-Satellite Strike Offensive · Precision Strike
IDF Destroys Iranian Space Research Compound Used to Develop Anti-Satellite Weapons in Tehran
On March 16 (Day 17), the Israel Defense Forces announced that an Israeli airstrike destroyed a compound in Tehran that Iran had been using to develop weapons to threaten Israeli and allied satellites. The facility was used to develop Iranian military space programs, including the Chamran-1 satellite launched by the IRGC in September 2024. The IDF stated the strike 'adds to' a previous strike conducted on Friday against a separate space-related research compound belonging to the Iranian Space Organization, also in Tehran. The IDF declared it 'will continue to act to defend its capabilities across all domains — on the ground, the air, at sea, and in space.' IDF spokesman Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin reiterated the military still has 'thousands of targets in Iran' with new ones identified every day, and that plans run 'through at least the Jewish holiday of Passover, about three weeks from now.' The IAF now claims air supremacy over most of Iran's airspace after destroying more than 100 air defense systems and around 120 detection systems.
📍Tehran, Iran — IRGC-linked space weapons research compound 💥Compound destroyed; linked to Chamran-1 IRGC satellite program and anti-satellite weapons development
Sources: Washington Times Haaretz Day 17 Live Times of Israel – Mar 15 IDF Briefing
16
Mar
CENTCOM · Naval Milestone Assessment · Naval Destruction Update
CENTCOM: 100+ Iranian Naval Vessels Destroyed, 6,000+ Combat Flights Flown — 'We Aren't Done'
On March 16/17 (Day 16/17), CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper released a video update stating that the U.S. military has now destroyed more than 100 Iranian naval vessels. 'To date, our Air Force, Navy and Marine Corps aviators have collectively flown more than 6,000 combat flights,' Cooper said, adding 'we aren't done.' The White House declared on Saturday that 'Iran's ballistic missile capacity is functionally destroyed. Their navy assessed combat ineffective. Complete and total aerial dominance over Iran.' Despite these claims, Iran continued firing residual missiles and drones at Gulf states on Day 17. The U.S. military update follows the large-scale Kharg Island strike on Friday that destroyed 90+ military targets including naval mine storage and missile bunkers while 'preserving oil infrastructure.' President Trump on Monday quoted Cooper's update, declaring Iran's air force, navy, anti-aircraft, and radar were 'gone.' The IDF and CENTCOM confirmed missile launch rates are down approximately 90% from Day 1 and drone launches down ~86–95%, while Iran retains asymmetric attack capacity via remaining drone stocks and Strait of Hormuz mine threat.
📍CENTCOM HQ / Iran-wide — strikes ongoing across all 31 provinces 💀100+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed or damaged; 6,000+ US combat flights; Iran missile salvo rate down ~90% from Day 1
Sources: Stars and Stripes – CENTCOM Day 16 Al Jazeera – How is Iran still shooting? RedState – New from CENTCOM
16
Mar
Iran · Diplomacy Political · Ceasefire Stance
Iran FM: 'We Are Not Seeking a Ceasefire But War Must End'; IRGC Vows to 'Pursue and Kill' Netanyahu
On March 16, Iran's Foreign Minister stated that Iran is not seeking a ceasefire but that the war 'must end,' saying Iran will continue fighting until future attacks are prevented. In an earlier Sunday interview with CBS, FM Abbas Araghchi declared: 'We never asked for a ceasefire, and we have never asked even for negotiation. We are ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes.' The IRGC separately vowed via Iran's state-affiliated Mehr news agency to 'pursue and kill' Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei—whose status Trump said he was unsure of, adding 'so far not'—has yet to make a video or public appearance, having only issued a written statement. Meanwhile, President Trump, speaking from the East Room on March 16, declared Iran's 'Air Force is gone, the Navy is gone, many, many ships have been sunk... their radar is gone, and their leaders are gone.' Trump confirmed he is working to assemble a Strait of Hormuz coalition, with Axios reporting an announcement could come later in the week, while the EU on Monday announced €458 million ($525 million) in humanitarian aid for the Middle East and said member states will not participate in a military Hormuz mission.
📍Tehran / Washington D.C. / Brussels 🗣️Iran FM rules out ceasefire or negotiations; IRGC threatens Netanyahu; EU declines Hormuz mission; Trump eyes Hormuz coalition announcement
Sources: Iran International – Iran FM statement Mar 16 ABC7 – Live Updates Mar 16 Haaretz Day 17 Live

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile & Drone Barrage
Iran Targets Jerusalem Area; Missile Debris Hits Holon Home; 10 Attack Waves vs. Israel on Mar 15
On March 15–16, Iranian forces launched 10 identified attack waves against Israel using ballistic missiles, with one barrage targeting the Jerusalem area on March 16 — debris from an intercepted missile fell on a residential home and injured two people. Israeli air defenses, including the F-35I 'Adir' fleet, continued round-the-clock intercept operations. The IDF confirmed that more than 85% of Iran's detection and air defense capabilities and over 70% of its missile launchers have been neutralized since February 28. Iran has now fired an estimated 290+ ballistic missiles and 500+ drones at Israel since the conflict began, roughly half carrying cluster munitions. Iran simultaneously continued striking Gulf states; fuel storage facilities at Dubai Airport were reported damaged on March 16, one person was killed in Abu Dhabi, and attacks were reported in Bahrain and at the US Embassy in Baghdad.

16
Mar
IAF · Strike Wave Offensive · Air Campaign
IDF Strikes Khamenei's Personal Aircraft at Mehrabad; Hits Nuclear Concealment Sites; 7,600+ Total Strikes
During the night of March 15–16, the IDF carried out a wide wave of strikes in Tehran targeting infrastructure linked to the Iranian regime. The Israeli Air Force specifically struck a plane used by Iran's supreme leader at Tehran's Mehrabad International Airport and hit sites where Israel assesses the regime is attempting to fortify and conceal components related to the nuclear program. Additionally, more than 100 targets connected to the regime's production systems were struck in recent days. Since the start of the conflict, the IDF has now conducted more than 7,600 strikes across Iran as part of nearly 5,000 aerial sorties, of which approximately 4,700 were directed at Iran's missile program. More than 700 missile launcher targets have been neutralized in real time, and the IDF says it still has 'thousands of targets' remaining, with the campaign expected to continue for at least three more weeks.
📍Mehrabad Airport (Tehran), nuclear concealment sites (Tehran), western & central Iran 🎯7,600+ total IDF strikes; 4,700 against missile program; 85%+ of Iranian air defenses neutralized
Sources: FDD Long War Journal (Mar 16) Times of Israel (Mar 15) Alma Research Center (Mar 16)
16
Mar
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Talks Political · White House
Trump: 'Not Ready to Deal' — Doubts New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei Is Even Alive
As the war entered its 17th day, the White House confirmed Trump is 'willing to talk, but not at this time,' insisting Operation Epic Fury continue unabated. Trump told reporters he was not ready to make a deal, saying the 'terms aren't good enough yet,' and cast doubt on whether Iran's newly appointed Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei — who has not appeared publicly since being named — is even alive. Defense Secretary Hegseth described the younger Khamenei as 'wounded and likely disfigured,' and said 'his father: dead; he's scared, he's injured, he's on the run, and he lacks legitimacy.' White House officials added that part of the reason Trump is not engaging with Iran is because the administration is not confident Khamenei 'is actually in charge.' US allies in the Middle East have offered to mediate but have been rebuffed. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi reiterated on CBS that Tehran has 'never asked for a ceasefire' and is 'ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes.'
🏛️White House, Washington D.C. / CBS News interview (Araghchi) 📌Trump declines talks; questions if Mojtaba Khamenei is alive; Iran FM rejects ceasefire framing
Sources: CNN Live (Mar 16) NBC News (Mar 16) Iran International (Mar 16)
16
Mar
Lebanon · Ground Ops Offensive · Ground Expansion
Israel Expands S. Lebanon Buffer Zone; 1M+ Lebanese Displaced; IDF Plans Major Ground Invasion
Israel formally expanded its ground operations in southern Lebanon, with Defense Minister Israel Katz ordering Israeli forces to systematically destroy Hezbollah infrastructure in villages close to the Israeli border and barring displaced Shiite residents from returning south of the Litani River until Israeli border communities are secured. The IDF has now issued evacuation orders for the entire area south of the Litani River and for the first time extended them to villages north of the river and Hezbollah's stronghold in Beirut's southern suburbs. The war has displaced more than 1 million Lebanese civilians — roughly 14% of the country's entire population — with over 130,000 sheltering in schools and collective centers. Lebanon's Health Ministry reported more than 880 people killed in Israeli attacks. Israeli military estimates indicate more than 400 Hezbollah fighters have been killed since the start of March. Israel is planning a significant expansion of its ground operation to seize the entire area south of the Litani River, which could be the largest Israeli ground invasion of Lebanon since 2006. The UN Secretary-General visited Beirut on March 14 and launched a $308.3 million flash humanitarian appeal, warning southern Lebanon 'risks being turned into a wasteland.'
📍Southern Lebanon (south of Litani River), Beirut southern suburbs, Bekaa Valley 💀880+ killed (Lebanon Health Ministry); 1M+ displaced (14% of population); 400+ Hezbollah fighters killed (IDF est.)
Sources: Axios (Mar 14) YourNews (Mar 16) UN News (Mar 14)
17
Mar
IDF · Simultaneous Strikes Offensive · Air Strikes
IDF Launches Simultaneous Strikes on Tehran & Beirut; Iran Fires Back Over Israel
On March 17, the Israel Defense Forces launched what it described as 'extensive' strikes simultaneously against targets in Tehran and Beirut. In the Iranian capital, the IDF said it was hitting 'infrastructure of the terror regime,' while in Beirut it targeted Hezbollah positions. The dual strikes came as Iran and Hezbollah launched concurrent missile attacks on Israel — which they said were coordinated. Rocket trails were visible over the Israeli coastal city of Netanya. Magen David Adom reported no direct impact casualties from the latest Iranian missile attack, treating several people for anxiety and shelter-rush injuries. The IDF subsequently lifted the shelter order. Separately, a US-Israeli missile strike on a house in Baghdad's al-Jadiriyah neighborhood killed at least two Iranian advisers to Tehran-backed groups, per AFP and two Iraqi security sources. Rockets and at least five drones also targeted the US Embassy in Baghdad in what Iraqi security sources described as the most intense assault on the embassy since the war began.
📍Tehran · Beirut · Baghdad · Central & Coastal Israel 💀2 Iranian advisers killed in Baghdad strike; no direct Israeli casualties from missile barrage 🚀5+ drones + rockets fired at US Embassy Baghdad — most intense assault to date
Sources: Times of Israel Haaretz
17
Mar
UAE · Airspace Closure Escalation · Regional
UAE Closes Airspace as Iran Launches New Missile & Drone Wave; Abu Dhabi Targeted
On March 17, the UAE General Civil Aviation Authority announced a full temporary closure of the country's airspace as an 'exceptional precautionary measure' amid rapidly evolving regional security developments. The UAE Defence Ministry said it was responding to incoming missile and drone threats from Iran. The closure followed a series of attacks on UAE territory on March 16: a drone strike ignited a fire at a fuel tank near Dubai International Airport — forcing the temporary suspension of flights — and a separate drone strike caused a fire at an industrial zone in Fujairah. A missile also struck a car in Abu Dhabi, killing a Palestinian resident. Dubai airport later resumed a limited flight schedule on March 16, before the full airspace closure was declared on March 17.
📍Dubai International Airport · Fujairah Industrial Zone · Abu Dhabi 💀1 Palestinian resident killed in Abu Dhabi missile strike; 4 injured near Dubai airport ✈️UAE full airspace closed March 17 — exceptional precautionary measure
Sources: Haaretz Liveblog Al Jazeera
16
Mar
Iran · Regional Escalation Offensive · Multi-Front
Saudi Arabia Intercepts 37 Drones; IRGC Spokesperson Claims Most Missile Cache Intact
On March 16, Saudi Arabia's air defenses intercepted 37 drones in its eastern region. Bahrain, Kuwait, and Qatar also reported drone interceptions. Meanwhile, IRGC spokesperson Brigadier-General Ali Mohammad Naini told a local broadcaster that most of the IRGC's weapons cache remains intact, claiming the missiles being used in the current war are 'from a decade ago' and that Iran has not yet deployed missiles produced since the June 2025 Twelve-Day War. The assertion pushes back against US claims that Iran's missile capacity has been 90% degraded. Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya Headquarters separately issued a public threat toward Saudi Arabia, warning that the presence of the US aircraft carrier Gerald Ford in the Red Sea constitutes a direct threat.
📍Saudi Arabia (eastern region) · Bahrain · Kuwait · Qatar · Red Sea 🛡️37 Iranian drones intercepted by Saudi Arabia on Mar 16 ⚠️IRGC claims newer-generation missiles not yet deployed
Sources: Al Jazeera Alma Research Center
16
Mar
IDF · Space Domain Offensive · Precision Strike
IDF Destroys Iranian Space Research Center Used to Develop Anti-Satellite Weapons
On March 16, the Israeli Air Force struck and destroyed a compound in Tehran that Iran was using to develop weapons designed to threaten Israeli and allied satellites. The IDF stated the strike complemented a prior attack on a separate space-related research compound belonging to the Iranian Space Organization in Tehran carried out on March 14. Israel assessed that Iran had invested 'significant' resources in developing space warfare capabilities to undermine regional and international stability. The IDF declared it would 'continue to act to defend its capabilities across all domains — on the ground, the air, at sea, and in space.' Israel's satellite network is critical to real-time intelligence gathering as the IDF continues its strike campaign in Iran.
📍Tehran, Iran — Iranian Space Organization compound 🛰️Second space-related research site struck in two days; first hit March 14
Sources: Washington Times
17
Mar
Iraq · Militia Leader Killed Targeted Killing · IRGC Proxy
Kata'ib Hezbollah Security Commander Abu Ali al-Askari Confirmed Killed in Baghdad Airstrike
On March 17, it was announced that Abu Ali al-Askari, the security commander of Kata'ib Hezbollah — one of the most powerful Iran-backed militias in Iraq — was killed in an airstrike on Baghdad on March 14. Al-Askari had been a prominent IRGC-linked figure and a key node in the Iraqi proxy network responsible for coordinating drone and missile attacks against US forces. The announcement came as US-Israeli strikes against Iran-backed PMF infrastructure across Iraq continued, with Iraqi security sources reporting additional drone activity near the US Embassy in Baghdad and Balad airbase in Salah al-Din governorate.
📍Baghdad, Iraq — struck March 14; confirmed March 17 💀Abu Ali al-Askari, Kata'ib Hezbollah security commander, killed
Sources: Wikipedia / 2026 Iran War Al Jazeera
17
Mar
IDF · Ground Escalation Offensive · Ground Operation
Israel Launches Ground Invasion of Southern Lebanon; 1 Million Displaced
On March 17, Israel launched a ground invasion of southern Lebanon, with the IDF describing it as a 'limited and targeted' operation against key Hezbollah strongholds. Defense Minister Israel Katz said the operation would resemble the Gaza war and that displaced Lebanese civilians would not be permitted to return south of the Litani River until northern Israeli border communities are secure. Lebanese authorities simultaneously confirmed that more than one million people — representing roughly one-fifth of Lebanon's entire population — have now registered as displaced since the start of the conflict. More than 120,000 of the displaced are sheltering in collective shelters, with many sleeping in the streets or in cars. A joint statement from Canada, France, Germany, Italy, and the UK warned of 'devastating humanitarian consequences.' The Israeli army also issued fresh evacuation orders to residents south of the Zahrani River ahead of additional operations, while IDF jets struck Bint Jbeil and al-Jumayjimah, killing at least five people.
📍Southern Lebanon — Litani River zone, Bint Jbeil, al-Jumayjimah, Khiam 💀880+ Lebanese killed (Health Ministry); 1,000,000+ displaced (Lebanese govt) ⚔️400+ Hezbollah fighters killed since start of March (IDF estimate)
Sources: TIME Al Jazeera Wikipedia — 2026 Iran war
17
Mar
CENTCOM · Strike Milestone Offensive · Air Campaign Update
CENTCOM: 15,000+ Enemy Targets Struck; Iran Missile Launch Rate Down 90%
CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper confirmed that between US Air Force and Israeli Air Force operations, over 15,000 enemy targets have been struck since the start of Operation Epic Fury on February 28. Cooper stated the coalition has achieved air superiority over vast swaths of Iran, and that Iran's ballistic missile launch rate has dropped approximately 90% from Day 1. The IDF separately reported carrying out more than 7,600 strikes across Iran in nearly 5,000 aerial sorties, of which about 4,700 targeted the Iranian regime's missile program. More than 85% of Iran's air defense and radar capabilities have been neutralized. The Pentagon also confirmed that the number of US troops wounded now exceeds 200 across seven countries, as Iran continued launching waves of missiles and one-way attack drones. On March 14, US forces conducted a large-scale precision strike on Kharg Island destroying more than 90 military targets, including naval mine storage bunkers and missile infrastructure.
📍Iran-wide; Kharg Island, Tehran, Tabriz, Shiraz, Hamadan, Isfahan 💥15,000+ total targets struck (CENTCOM, Mar 13); 90+ targets on Kharg Island alone (Mar 14) 📉Iran ballistic missile launch rate down ~90% from Day 1; drone launches down ~86%
Sources: WND / CENTCOM RedState / CENTCOM Alma Research Center Washington Post
16
Mar
Iran · FM Statement Diplomatic · Ceasefire Rejection
Iran FM Araghchi: 'We Never Asked for a Ceasefire' — Will Fight Until Attacks Are Prevented
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi firmly rejected Trump's claims that Iran had sought a ceasefire or negotiations, stating on CBS News on March 15–16: 'We never asked for a ceasefire, and we have never asked even for negotiation. We are ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes.' On March 16, Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman Baghaei reinforced this position, saying Tehran is not seeking a ceasefire but that 'the war must end in a way that it will not be repeated.' Iran entered negotiations in earlier weeks 'with open eyes' but in 'absolute distrust,' he said. President Trump, meanwhile, told NBC News that Iran was ready to 'make a deal,' but that 'the terms aren't good enough yet.' Trump continues to demand Iran's unconditional surrender as a prerequisite for ending the war.
📍Tehran / Diplomatic — CBS News, NBC News statements 🗣️FM Araghchi: 'We never asked for a ceasefire' — Iran vows to continue resistance
Sources: NPR Iran International NBC News
16
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Drone/Missile Wave
Iranian Drones Hit Dubai Airport Fuel Storage, Abu Dhabi; US Embassy Baghdad Targeted
On March 16, Iran launched fresh waves of drone and missile attacks across the region. In the UAE, Iranian drones damaged fuel storage facilities at Dubai International Airport and killed one Palestinian national in Abu Dhabi's Al Bahia area in a missile strike on a civilian vehicle, according to the Abu Dhabi Media Office. Attacks were also reported in Bahrain, though details remained unclear at time of reporting. Separately, Iranian-aligned militias launched an attack targeting the US Embassy in Baghdad, while rockets were also fired at a US logistical facility near Baghdad International Airport. On March 15, Alma Research Center tracked 10 distinct Iranian ballistic missile attack waves against Israel — 5 directed at the Tel Aviv area and 5 at southern Israel. Since the start of the campaign, a cumulative total of 245 Iranian attack waves against Israel have been identified.
📍Dubai (UAE), Abu Dhabi, Bahrain, Baghdad (Iraq), Israel 💀1 killed in Abu Dhabi; casualties reported at Dubai Airport; US Embassy Baghdad targeted 🚀10 Iranian ballistic missile waves vs. Israel on Mar 15; 245 total waves vs. Israel since Feb 28
Sources: CBS News Alma Research Center
17
Mar
IDF · Nuclear Targeting Offensive · Airstrike
IDF Strikes Iranian Nuclear Concealment Sites; 70%+ of Missile Launchers Neutralized
During the night between March 15 and 16, the IDF carried out a wide wave of strikes in Tehran targeting infrastructure linked to the Iranian regime. According to an IDF spokesperson, the Air Force attacked sites where Iran is assessed to be attempting to fortify and conceal components related to its nuclear program. In the preceding days, more than 100 targets connected to the regime's weapons production systems were struck. As of March 16, IDF military sources reported that more than 70% of Iran's missile launchers have been neutralized, over 85% of Iran's air defense and radar systems have been destroyed, and the IDF has now conducted more than 7,600 strikes in nearly 5,000 aerial sorties. The main operational effort remains focused on thwarting missile launches and striking IRGC headquarters.
📍Tehran, Tabriz, Shiraz, Isfahan Air Force Base 💥7,600+ IDF strikes in ~5,000 sorties; 70%+ missile launchers neutralized; 85%+ air defenses destroyed
Sources: Alma Research Center Times of Israel
17
Mar
Iran · Leadership Strike Offensive · Targeted Assassination
IDF Kills Ali Larijani & Basij Chief Gholamreza Soleimani in Overnight Strikes
In the early morning hours of March 17, Israeli Air Force jets carried out simultaneous precision strikes against high-value Iranian leadership targets in Tehran, Shiraz, and Tabriz. The IDF and Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz confirmed that Ali Larijani — secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council and widely regarded as Iran's 'de facto leader' following the killing of Ayatollah Khamenei — was eliminated in a strike near Tehran. The IDF also confirmed the killing of Gholamreza Soleimani, commander of the IRGC's all-volunteer Basij paramilitary force. Iran has not officially confirmed the deaths. The IDF stated it has been instructed to 'continue hunting down' Iranian leadership. These are the highest-profile leadership eliminations since the opening-day assassination of Khamenei on February 28.
📍Tehran, Shiraz, Tabriz — simultaneous multi-city wave 💀Ali Larijani (SNSC chief, de facto leader) + Gholamreza Soleimani (Basij commander) confirmed killed by IDF; Iran has not confirmed
Sources: ABC News NPR Just Security
17
Mar
US · Domestic · Dissent Political · Senior Official Resignation
Senior Trump Official Resigns Over Iran War, Citing Lack of Imminent Threat
On March 17, Joe Kent, Director of the National Counterterrorism Center, publicly resigned from his position, stating he 'cannot in good conscience support the ongoing war in Iran.' Kent wrote on social media that 'Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation,' becoming one of the most senior Trump administration officials to break with the president over the conflict. His resignation comes as U.S. intelligence assessments continue to indicate the Iranian regime is likely to remain in place, with the IRGC exerting greater control. The resignation adds to mounting political pressure on the Trump administration as the war enters its third week with no clear end in sight.
📍Washington D.C. — National Counterterrorism Center ⚠️First senior Trump official to resign over the Iran war; cited absence of imminent threat
Sources: NPR Times of Israel
17
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile/Drone Wave
Iranian Missile Strikes Central Israel Train Station; Hezbollah Prepares Major Barrage
On March 17, Iranian ballistic missiles struck a train station in central Israel, with rescue services responding to the impact site. Separately, rocket trails were photographed over the coastal city of Netanya during an Iranian ballistic missile attack. The IDF issued a warning that Hezbollah was likely planning a major rocket barrage on central and northern Israel within hours. Hezbollah launched 37 attack waves against Israel on March 16 alone, averaging 100 rockets per day, according to Al Jazeera. The IDF's ground invasion of southern Lebanon, announced on March 17, aims to create an expanded forward defense buffer zone to push Hezbollah away from the border. Iran's IRGC spokesperson meanwhile claimed most of its weapons cache remains intact, and that missiles used so far are from 'a decade ago,' with newer post-2025 production missiles not yet deployed.
📍Central Israel (train station impact); Netanya (ballistic missile intercept); southern Lebanon (IDF ground advance) 💀Casualties from train station strike pending; 6 wounded in Nahariya from Hezbollah rocket, Mar 16
Sources: Times of Israel Al Jazeera Al Jazeera
17
Mar
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · Displacement Crisis
Lebanon Displaced Persons Surpass 1,049,328 — One in Five Citizens Forced from Homes
Lebanese authorities confirmed on March 17 that the number of displaced persons in Lebanon has surpassed one million, reaching an official tally of 1,049,328, representing approximately one-fifth of the country's entire population. Reception centers are at full capacity, with many displaced individuals sheltering in tents, vehicles, or open areas. The crisis has been driven by sweeping Israeli evacuation orders now covering more than 1,470 square kilometers (14% of Lebanese territory), intensive bombing of southern Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs, and the IDF's newly launched ground invasion. Israel's Defense Minister Katz stated that displaced civilians will not be permitted to return to areas south of the Litani River as long as Hezbollah remains a threat. The Lebanese Health Ministry's toll since March 2 stands at 886 killed — including 111 children and 38 health workers — and 2,141 wounded. UK, France, Canada, Germany, and Italy issued a joint statement calling on Israel to refrain from a broad ground maneuver in Lebanon.
📍Southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, southern Beirut suburbs — 1,470 sq km under evacuation orders 💀886 killed (incl. 111 children, 38 health workers), 2,141 wounded since Mar 2 (Lebanon Health Ministry) 🏚️1,049,328 officially displaced; 130,000+ sheltering in schools and collective centers
Sources: Al Jazeera Alma Research Center Time
17
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Drone/Missile Regional Wave
Iran Strikes Israeli Train Station; UAE Closes Airspace; Baghdad Hotel & US Embassy Targeted
In the early hours of March 17, Iran launched continued drone and missile salvos across the region. The UAE temporarily closed its entire airspace as 'an exceptional precautionary measure' in response to missile and drone threats; airspace was subsequently reopened. Saudi Arabia intercepted a dozen drones. Iraqi security sources confirmed that drones targeted the US Embassy in Baghdad and the Balad airbase in Salah al-Din. A drone also struck Baghdad's Rasheed Hotel — used by Iraqi officials and foreign delegations — causing damage but no casualties. In Abu Dhabi, a Pakistani national was killed after shrapnel fell during the interception of a ballistic missile. Iran's IRGC said it struck targets in Israel and across the Gulf. The IDF confirmed intercepting a missile fired from Iran, with no injuries reported from that specific intercept.
📍UAE (airspace closure); Baghdad (US Embassy, Rasheed Hotel, Balad airbase); Abu Dhabi (missile shrapnel); Saudi Arabia (12 drones intercepted) 💀1 Pakistani national killed in Abu Dhabi from missile intercept shrapnel; no US Embassy casualties reported
Sources: NPR Haaretz Just Security
18
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile & Cluster Munition Strike
Iran Fires Cluster Munitions at Central Israel; Couple Killed in Ramat Gan on Day 19
In the early hours of Wednesday, March 18 (Day 19), Iran launched a new wave of ballistic missiles and cluster munitions at central and northern Israel, triggering sirens across the Tel Aviv metropolitan area, the West Bank, and surrounding cities. Two people — a couple in their seventies — were killed in Ramat Gan when their apartment block was struck by fragments of a cluster bomb fired from Iran. Shrapnel also fell in Holon, Or Yehuda, Tel Aviv, and Abu Ghosh west of Jerusalem, causing fires and damage to roads, a bus, and nearby buildings. No further wounded were immediately reported. Bahrain, Qatar, UAE, and Saudi Arabia each reported intercepting additional Iranian missiles and drones overnight. The IDF responded by striking ten Basij positions in Tehran and completing a new wave of airstrikes on 'Iran Regime Command Centers' in the capital. An Israeli airstrike also flattened an apartment building in Bachoura in central Beirut, with no injuries immediately reported. Rockets were fired at the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad for the second time in 24 hours. Australian PM Albanese confirmed that an Iranian projectile hit near its Al Minhad Air Base in the UAE, with all Australian personnel safe.
📍Ramat Gan (central Israel); shrapnel in Holon, Or Yehuda, Tel Aviv, Abu Ghosh; IDF strikes in Tehran; Bachoura, Beirut; U.S. Embassy Baghdad; Al Minhad AB, UAE 💀2 Israeli civilians killed (elderly couple, Ramat Gan); multiple interceptions across Gulf states; no coalition casualties reported in immediate update
Sources: Haaretz Al Jazeera Live Fox News Live ABC News Live
18
Mar
CENTCOM · Strike Update Offensive · Deep Penetrator Bomb Strike
CENTCOM Drops Multiple 5,000-lb Bunker Busters on Hardened Iranian Missile Sites Along Hormuz Coast
On Tuesday, March 17 into the early hours of March 18, U.S. Central Command announced that U.S. aircraft had dropped 'multiple 5,000-pound deep penetrator munitions' on hardened Iranian missile sites along Iran's coastline near the Strait of Hormuz. The bombs — officially designated bunker busters — are designed to penetrate reinforced and deeply buried targets. The strike follows the ongoing CENTCOM campaign that has now struck over 15,000 combined U.S.-Israeli targets across Iran since February 28. Meanwhile, the IDF struck ten Basij positions in Tehran and completed a new wave against 'Iran Regime Command Centers.' The IDF also initiated a series of strikes targeting Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon after issuing fresh evacuation warnings. Saudi Arabia hosted a consultative meeting of foreign ministers from Arab and Islamic countries in Riyadh to discuss regional security. The UN launched a formal probe into the deadly March 1 strike on a girls' school in southern Iran that killed at least 175 people, mostly children.
📍Strait of Hormuz coastline missile sites (Iran); Tehran Basij positions; southern Lebanon; Riyadh (Arab FM meeting) 💥5,000-lb bunker busters deployed; 15,000+ coalition targets struck to date; 70%+ Iranian missile launchers neutralized per Israeli intel
Sources: ABC News Live Haaretz WND / CENTCOM
18
Mar
Iran · Leadership Diplomatic · Regime Response
Iran FM Araghchi: Tehran's Political System 'Remains Strong'; IRGC Vows to 'Hunt Down' Netanyahu
On March 18, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated publicly that Tehran's political system remains strong and that the assassination of senior leaders including Ali Larijani and Basij Chief Gholamreza Soleimani will not set the country back. Iran's Supreme National Security Council confirmed Larijani died alongside his son, a security deputy, and several protection team members. Separately, the IRGC issued a statement vowing to 'pursue' Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and 'kill him,' according to Iran's state-affiliated Mehr news agency. Iran has rejected all ceasefire proposals, with Araghchi reiterating the country did not request a ceasefire even in the previous 12-Day War. Meanwhile, the IDF warned that new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei 'is not safe' and threatened to hunt him down as well. Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard publicly backed President Trump's authority to determine what constitutes an imminent threat, in response to the resignation of NCTC Director Joe Kent.
📍Tehran (political statements); IDF communiqué re: Mojtaba Khamenei; Washington DC (Gabbard statement) 🎯Ali Larijani confirmed dead (son + protection team also killed); IRGC threatens Netanyahu; Mojtaba Khamenei declared a target
Sources: Al Jazeera Live ABC News Live J.Post Live

Mar
Israel · Naval Operational · Carrier Incident
USS Gerald R. Ford to Temporarily Withdraw to Crete After On-Board Fire
On Day 18/19, U.S. officials confirmed that the aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford — America's newest and the world's largest carrier, currently operating in the Red Sea in support of Operation Epic Fury — is expected to temporarily pull into port at Souda Bay, Crete, Greece, following an on-board fire. The warship has been deployed for nine months, including prior participation in operations against Venezuela in the Caribbean before arriving in the Middle East. The carrier's temporary withdrawal marks a notable operational development, though U.S. officials indicated the pause would be brief. The ship's departure does not halt ongoing coalition air operations, which continue at high intensity across Iran.
📍Red Sea → Souda Bay, Crete (temporary port call) ⚠️On-board fire; no casualties reported in immediate disclosures; temporary withdrawal only — operations continue via other assets

Hezbollah Fires Dozens of Rockets at Northern Israel; IDF Strikes Bachoura, Warns of 'Expanded Preparations'
On March 18, Hezbollah fired dozens of rockets at Israeli forces in southern Lebanon and northern Israeli communities. The IDF warned of 'expanded preparations' by the organization and struck several Hezbollah launchers, infrastructure sites, and a logistics site in Beirut. An IDF airstrike flattened an apartment building in Bachoura in central Beirut in the early hours of Wednesday. The IDF said it had initiated a series of strikes targeting Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon after urging residents to evacuate. According to Al Jazeera, Hezbollah has been firing an average of 100 rockets into Israel per day, at times in tandem with Iranian salvos. Israel is simultaneously planning a massive ground invasion of Lebanon aimed at seizing the entire area south of the Litani River. Lebanon's health ministry reports that Israeli attacks have now killed at least 912 people, including 111 children, and wounded more than 2,200 since the fighting began on March 2. Saudi Arabia hosted an emergency consultative meeting of Arab and Islamic foreign ministers in Riyadh to discuss ways to support regional security and stability.

CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper confirmed in a Day 16 update (March 16) that US Air Force, Navy, and Marine Corps aviators have flown more than 6,000 combat flights, with over 100 Iranian naval vessels destroyed — 'and we aren't done.' Between US and Israeli forces, over 15,000 enemy targets have been struck. Cooper stated that Iran's ballistic missile launch rate has fallen approximately 90% from Day 1, and drone launch rates have fallen 86%. Over One Million Displaced in Lebanon as IDF Expands Ground Operation South of Zahrani River
Lebanese authorities confirmed that more than one million people — nearly one-fifth of Lebanon's entire population — have been registered as displaced since fighting resumed on March 2. Israel's military issued new evacuation orders for residents south of the Zahrani River on March 17, pushing its displacement zone approximately 40 km north of the Israeli border and covering over 1,470 sq km of Lebanese territory. More than 120,000 displaced persons are sheltering in collective centers, with over 300,000 children among the displaced.
