Mar
Iran · Political Diplomatic · Ceasefire Stance
Trump Demands Unconditional Surrender; Iran FM Araghchi Says 'We Never Asked for Ceasefire'
On March 16–17, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi told CBS News that Tehran 'never asked for a ceasefire' and would 'continue this resistance without any hesitation,' flatly rejecting negotiations. Separately, Iran's foreign ministry spokesman Baghaei said the war 'must end' but ruled out any ceasefire request, saying it was 'ridiculous' to ask a country under attack to stop fighting. President Trump, meanwhile, posted on Truth Social that 'There will be no deal with Iran except UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER,' and told NBC News he was not yet ready to make a deal because 'the terms aren't good enough yet.' The White House Press Secretary stated US forces expect the war to continue approximately four to six more weeks. Trump also questioned on March 15 whether Iran's newly elected Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei was still alive, saying 'I'm hearing he's not alive,' while Defense Secretary Hegseth called the younger Khamenei 'wounded and likely disfigured.'
📍Washington D.C. / Tehran — diplomatic/political front 💀No direct casualties; political escalation; no ceasefire or negotiations underway
Sources: NBC News Iran International
17
Mar
US · Political Political · Allies
Trump Scolds NATO Allies, Japan, South Korea, Australia for Not Joining Iran Campaign
On March 17, President Trump issued a statement on Truth Social renouncing NATO's support and calling out US allies in the Indo-Pacific — Japan, South Korea, and Australia — for failing to join US-led strikes against Iran. Trump declared that the United States 'does not need the help of anyone' in the ongoing war. The statement escalated tensions with US treaty allies and followed earlier complaints that Gulf states were not doing enough to protect Strait of Hormuz shipping lanes.
📍Washington D.C. — political/diplomatic 💀N/A — political statement
Sources: Wikipedia / 2026 Iran War
16
Mar
CENTCOM · Strike Offensive · Kharg Island
US Strikes 90+ Military Targets on Kharg Island, Spares Oil Infrastructure
US forces conducted a large-scale bombing raid on Kharg Island, Iran's primary oil export hub handling approximately 90 percent of Iran's oil exports. CENTCOM confirmed approximately 90 military targets were struck, including naval mine storage facilities and missile storage bunkers, while oil infrastructure was deliberately preserved. President Trump stated on Truth Social that he chose 'NOT to wipe out the Oil Infrastructure on the Island' for 'reasons of decency,' though warned the infrastructure could still be targeted. Oil prices have risen over 40 percent since the war's start, with the International Energy Agency calling it the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.
📍Kharg Island, Persian Gulf — ~21 miles off Iran's coast 💀No civilian casualties reported; military targets only
Sources: Stars and Stripes Wikipedia / 2026 Iran War
18
Mar
Iran · Escalation Threat Offensive · IRGC Evacuation Warning
IRGC Threatens Gulf Energy Infrastructure; Issues Evacuation Warning
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps issued a public evacuation threat to civilians living near energy facilities in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and the United Arab Emirates, warning of imminent strikes on oil and gas infrastructure. The threat came in direct retaliation for an Israeli strike on Iran's South Pars natural gas field in the Persian Gulf — a major escalation struck with U.S. coordination on March 18. Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei declared that Israel 'will pay' following the assassination of three senior Iranian security officials over the preceding two days, including Intelligence Minister Esmaeil Khatib, whose killing was confirmed by Iranian President Pezeshkian on March 18.
📍South Pars Gas Field, Persian Gulf; Threats directed at Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE energy sites 💀Esmaeil Khatib (Intelligence Minister) confirmed killed in Israeli overnight airstrike, Mar 18
Sources: Al Jazeera Live Wikipedia – 2026 Iran war
18
Mar
Israel · Strike Offensive · IAF Strategic Strike
Israel Strikes South Pars Gas Field and Neighboring Refineries
In a significant escalation on March 18, Israel struck the South Pars natural gas field in the Persian Gulf — Iran's largest gas production complex and a critical piece of its energy and export infrastructure — along with neighboring refineries, in a coordinated operation with the United States. The strike prompted immediate fury from Iran's IRGC, which threatened retaliatory strikes on Gulf energy infrastructure and issued evacuation warnings to civilians near oil and gas facilities in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and the UAE. Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei vowed Israel 'will pay' for the strike and for a string of assassinations of senior Iranian officials over the previous 48 hours.
📍South Pars Gas Field, Persian Gulf, Iran; coordinated with U.S. forces ⚡Triggered IRGC evacuation threat to Gulf energy facility workers across KSA, Qatar, UAE
Sources: Wikipedia – 2026 Iran war Al Jazeera Live
17
Mar
Iran · Leadership Strike Defensive · Senior Officials Assassinated
Israel Kills Ali Larijani and Basij Chief Gholamreza Soleimani in Overnight Strikes
In the early morning hours of March 17, Israeli airstrikes targeted and killed two of Iran's most senior remaining officials: Ali Larijani, Iran's top security official and head of the Interim Leadership Council who had publicly announced the council on March 1, and General Gholamreza Soleimani, the commander of the Basij paramilitary force. Both deaths were confirmed by Iranian state media. In response, Iran launched a missile barrage that killed two Israeli civilians in Ramat Gan. The same day, Israel simultaneously launched a ground invasion of southern Lebanon, compounding the escalation across the regional front.
📍Tehran, Iran — targeted in overnight Israeli airstrikes 💀Ali Larijani (Interim Leadership Council head) and Gen. Gholamreza Soleimani (Basij commander) confirmed killed; 2 Israeli civilians killed in Ramat Gan retaliatory strike
Sources: Wikipedia – 2026 Iran war Al Jazeera – Mar 17 liveblog
18
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile/Drone Launch
Iran Fires Solid-Fuel Sejjil Missile in 54th Wave; CENTCOM Strikes Strait of Hormuz Missile Sites
Iran's IRGC launched its 54th wave of strikes against Israel under Operation True Promise 4, deploying a solid-fuel Sejjil medium-range ballistic missile alongside Khorramshahr, Kheibar Shekan, Qadr, and Emad systems targeting Israeli air command centers and defense industrial facilities. Simultaneously, CENTCOM announced strikes on Iranian missile sites stationed along the Strait of Hormuz coastline, as part of ongoing efforts to restore freedom of navigation through the critical waterway — through which roughly 20% of global oil supply passes. Satellite imagery showed dozens of vessels paralyzed outside the strait. The Trump administration confirmed 5,000 additional Marines are being deployed to the region, and Trump and Netanyahu agreed by phone to cooperate on the Hormuz issue. The Pentagon confirmed missile launches are down 90% and drone attacks down 86% from Day 1 levels, yet Iran retains residual capacity for continued harassment strikes.
📍Strait of Hormuz coastline / Israel (nationwide alert) 💥54th IRGC wave under Op. True Promise 4; Sejjil + 4 other ballistic systems confirmed ⚓5,000 US Marines deployed to region; possible Kharg Island ground op under consideration
Sources: Jerusalem Post The Defense News
18
Mar
US · Diplomatic Diplomatic · Endgame Options
Trump Presented Daily Escalate-or-Exit Options as Iran Vows No Ceasefire
The US military is presenting Trump with a daily menu of escalation and off-ramp options for the Iran war, NBC News reported. Trump told NBC over the weekend that Iran was ready to end the war but 'the terms aren't good enough yet,' while reiterating his demand for unconditional surrender and regime change. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi flatly rejected a ceasefire on Monday, saying Tehran 'did not request a ceasefire' and would 'continue this resistance without any hesitation.' Iran's spokesman Baghaei echoed that position, calling it 'ridiculous' to ask a country under attack to halt hostilities. The International Energy Agency warned the war is 'creating the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market,' while Trump's AI czar David Sacks became the first senior White House figure to publicly urge the president to end the conflict.
📍Washington DC / Tehran 🛢️Oil prices up 40%+ since war began; IEA calls it largest supply disruption in history 🕊️Iran FM: 'We never asked for a ceasefire… We are ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes'
Sources: NBC News Iran International
18
Mar
Lebanon · Ground War Offensive · IDF Ground Operation
Israel Launches Ground Operations in South Lebanon; 1 Million+ Displaced
Israel formally announced 'limited and targeted ground operations' in southern Lebanon against Hezbollah on March 17–18, with IDF Chief of Staff Eyal Zamir vowing to 'deepen' the operation until all objectives are achieved. The IDF warned residents south of the Zahrani River — roughly 40km north of the Israeli border — to evacuate ahead of a new operation, expanding the forced displacement zone beyond the Litani River. Lebanon's Ministry of Social Affairs confirmed more than one million civilians have been displaced, representing approximately one-fifth of the country's total population, with over 132,000 registered as formal refugees. Lebanese officials reported at least 912 people killed since March 2, including 111 children and 67 women, with 2,221+ wounded. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz vowed displaced Lebanese would not return south of the Litani until Israeli border security is guaranteed, and threatened escalating damage to Lebanese national infrastructure. Hezbollah's Naim Qassem has been designated a target for elimination. Hezbollah's political ally the Amal Movement voted symbolically to outlaw Hezbollah's military activities on Lebanese soil amid domestic Lebanese backlash.
📍Southern Lebanon / Bekaa Valley / Beirut southern suburbs — IDF ground forces active 💀912+ Lebanese killed (incl. 111 children); 2,221+ wounded; 1,000,000+ displaced 🪖IDF pushing into new areas beyond Litani; Hezbollah Radwan Force reinforcing; Naim Qassem designated for elimination
Sources: Al Jazeera Breitbart Time
19
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Cluster Munition Missile Strike
Iran Fires Cluster-Warhead Missiles at Tel Aviv Suburb; Couple Killed in Ramat Gan
In retaliation for the Israeli killing of Ali Larijani, Gholamreza Soleimani, and Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib, Iran's IRGC fired multiple-warhead missiles — including cluster munitions — at the Tel Aviv metropolitan area on March 18. A man and woman in their 70s were killed in their apartment in Ramat Gan, a suburb of Tel Aviv, with impact damage also reported at a Tel Aviv train station and in other parts of central Israel. The IRGC claimed its missiles struck over 100 military and security targets in Israeli territory as 'revenge' for the Larijani killing. CNN correspondents witnessed what appeared to be cluster munitions over central Israel during the salvo.
📍Ramat Gan (Tel Aviv suburb), central Israel 💀2 killed (elderly couple), extensive property damage across central Israel 🚀Multiple-warhead / cluster munition ballistic missiles; IRGC claimed 100+ Israeli targets struck
Sources: CNN NPR CNBC
18
Mar
Israel · Strike Offensive · Targeted Assassination
Israel Kills Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib in Overnight Tehran Strike
Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz announced on March 18 that Iranian Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib had been killed in an Israeli airstrike the previous night — the third senior Iranian official eliminated within approximately 24 hours, following the earlier killings of Ali Larijani and Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian confirmed Khatib's death on social media. Katz stated that Khatib was 'responsible for the regime's internal apparatus of repression and assassination, as well as for advancing external threats,' and declared Israel authorized to kill 'any senior Iranian figure' without additional approval. Iran's president called it a 'cowardly assassination.'
📍Tehran, Iran 💀Esmail Khatib (Iranian Intelligence Minister) killed; third senior official eliminated within ~24 hours ⚔️Israeli Air Force targeted strike; Katz authorized standing order to kill any senior Iranian official on sight
Sources: NPR Iran International RFERL
18
Mar
Israel · Strike Offensive · Naval Strike / New Front
IDF Strikes Iranian Naval Targets in Caspian Sea for First Time; Hits Bandar Anzali
In a significant geographic expansion of the war, the IDF struck Iranian Navy vessels at the port city of Bandar Anzali on the Caspian Sea coast of northern Iran on the evening of March 18 — the first Israeli strikes in northern Iran since the war began. The IDF stated its Air Force, guided by Navy and Military Intelligence, struck targets in northern Iran 'for the first time in Operation Rising Lion.' Bandar Anzali hosts the main base of Iran's northern naval fleet and serves as a critical node in the Iran-Russia maritime trade corridor used to smuggle military equipment to Russia since 2022. Simultaneously, the IDF confirmed over 200 strikes in western and central Iran over the previous 24 hours targeting missile launch sites, air defense systems, and weapons production facilities.
📍Bandar Anzali, Caspian Sea coast, northern Iran (first IDF strike in northern Iran) 🚢Iranian Navy vessels at Bandar Anzali port struck; Iran-Russia supply corridor targeted ⚔️IAF guided by Navy/Military Intelligence; expands maritime war beyond Persian Gulf/Strait of Hormuz
Sources: Israel Hayom CNN Jerusalem Post
18
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Energy Infrastructure Strike
Iran Retaliates Against South Pars Strike; Fires on Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG Terminal
After Israel struck facilities linked to Iran's South Pars natural gas field — the world's largest — on March 18, Iran's IRGC issued evacuation warnings for energy facilities across Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Qatar and threatened to target them 'in the coming hours.' Hours later, Qatar reported a fire at its massive Ras Laffan industrial city, home to QatarEnergy's LNG export terminals, after an Iranian missile strike. QatarEnergy reported 'extensive damage.' Qatar's Civil Defense controlled two of three fire sites with no injuries. In response, Qatar's foreign ministry declared Iran's military and security attachés persona non grata and ordered them to leave within 24 hours. Saudi Arabia shot down five Iranian drones targeting an energy facility in its Eastern Province. Oil prices surged above $108/barrel on global markets.
📍Ras Laffan, Qatar (LNG terminal); Saudi Arabia Eastern Province energy facilities 🔥Extensive damage at QatarEnergy's Ras Laffan; Qatar expels Iran's military attachés 💹Brent crude surged to $108.28/barrel; Asian markets fell 1.4–2.7% on energy shock
Sources: Iran International CBS News RFERL
18
Mar
CENTCOM · Strike Offensive · Deep Penetrator Munitions
CENTCOM Drops 5,000-lb Deep-Penetrator Bombs on Hormuz Missile Sites; HIMARS Fired from Bahrain
U.S. Central Command announced on March 18 that American forces had 'successfully employed multiple 5,000-pound deep penetrator munitions on hardened Iranian missile sites along Iran's coastline near the Strait of Hormuz.' The strikes targeted underground and hardened launch infrastructure in a continued effort to suppress Iranian missile fire and reopen the strait to international shipping. Separately, video verified by NBC News showed a US-built M142 HIMARS system firing missiles at Iran from near Bahrain International Airport — potentially the first documented use of American ballistic systems launched from a Gulf nation during the conflict. Saudi Arabia also stated it 'reserved the right to take military action' against Tehran following continued Iranian attacks on its territory.
📍Strait of Hormuz coastline (Iran); Bahrain (HIMARS launch site) 💣Multiple 5,000-lb GBU-57-class deep penetrators employed on hardened underground missile sites 🛡️Saudi Arabia declares right to military action against Iran; Kuwait arrests 10 Hezbollah operatives
Sources: CBS News Iran International Live CNN Day 19
18
Mar
Iran · Nuclear Incident · Nuclear Plant Strike Report
Strike Reported at Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant; IAEA Says No Damage
Iran's Atomic Energy Organization reported on March 18 that a 'hostile projectile' struck the site of the Bushehr nuclear power plant in southern Iran along the Persian Gulf coast — the country's only operational nuclear reactor. The organization said no casualties were reported and no damage was caused to the facility itself. The UN nuclear watchdog (IAEA) confirmed Iran's notification and also stated the strike caused no damage. Neither Iran nor the IAEA attributed the strike to a specific country. The incident added to global concern about the safety of nuclear infrastructure during the ongoing air campaign.
📍Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant, southern Iran (Persian Gulf coast) ☢️IAEA confirms: no damage to reactor; no casualties; perpetrator not formally attributed ⚠️IAEA chief Rafael Grossi warned war cannot entirely eliminate Iran's nuclear program
Sources: CNN NPR
19
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Energy Infrastructure Strikes
Iran Unleashes Massive Wave on Gulf Energy Sites; Ras Laffan LNG Suffers 'Extensive Damage'
Following Israel's bombing of Iran's South Pars gas field on March 18, the IRGC launched its most consequential retaliatory wave yet against Gulf energy infrastructure. Iran fired ballistic missiles and drones at Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG terminal — one of the world's largest, handling roughly one-fifth of global LNG supply — causing what QatarEnergy described as 'extensive damage' to the Pearl GTL facility and setting multiple LNG installations ablaze. Qatar was forced to halt all gas production. Separately, Iranian drones struck a Kuwaiti refinery, missiles targeted Saudi Arabia's Yanbu refinery on the Red Sea, and a gas field and facility in the UAE was also targeted. European natural gas futures surged ~24% and Brent crude hit $115–$116 per barrel. Qatar expelled Iranian military attachés and condemned the strikes as 'a dangerous escalation and flagrant violation of state sovereignty.' Saudi Foreign Minister Faisal bin Farhan said 'what little trust there was has been completely shattered.' Trump threatened to 'massively blow up' Iran's South Pars Gas Field entirely if attacks on Qatar continued, while also saying the US 'knew nothing' about Israel's prior South Pars strike.
📍Qatar (Ras Laffan), Kuwait, Saudi Arabia (Yanbu), UAE — energy hubs 💥'Extensive damage' at Pearl GTL; multiple LNG facilities ablaze; Qatar halts all gas production 💰Brent crude hits $116/bbl; European gas futures +24%
Sources: NPR CNN ABC7
19
Mar
Israel · Strike Offensive · South Pars Gas Field
IAF Strikes Iran's South Pars Gas Field — World's Largest; Trump Distances US from Attack
Israeli Air Force jets struck Iran's South Pars gas field in Bushehr Province on March 18, marking the first time Israel had directly targeted Iranian gas production infrastructure during the current war. Iran confirmed damage to the facility. Israeli Defense Minister Katz had foreshadowed 'significant surprises' hours before the strike. The attack prompted immediate condemnation from Qatar, which shares the underwater field with Iran. US President Trump said the US had no advance knowledge of the strike and declared there would be 'no more' Israeli attacks on South Pars. However, Trump warned that if Iran continued attacking Qatar, the US itself would 'massively blow up the entirety of the South Pars Gas Field' at a scale 'Iran has never seen or witnessed before.'
📍South Pars Gas Field, Bushehr Province, Iran (world's largest gas field) ⚡First Israeli strike on Iranian gas production infrastructure in current war 🛢️Brent crude surges; global LNG markets severely disrupted
Sources: Times of Israel CBS News CNN
19
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Cluster Bomb Strike on Israel
Iran Fires Cluster Munitions at Central Israel; Foreign Worker Killed at Moshav Adanim
In retaliation for the killing of Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib and the South Pars strike, Iran launched a ballistic missile salvo containing cluster munitions targeting central Israel in the early hours of March 19. Multiple apparent cluster munition impacts were reported across central Israel. A 30-year-old foreign worker was killed at Moshav Adanim after sustaining critical shrapnel injuries. Several other impacts were reported in the wider central Israel area. The Palestinian Red Crescent also reported at least three killed and 13 injured in the West Bank from falling missile fragments from the same salvo. Israel's air defense and early warning systems intercepted much of the barrage, but defense officials noted that without those systems the casualty toll would have been far higher.
📍Moshav Adanim, central Israel; West Bank (shrapnel) 💀1 killed (foreign worker, 30); 3 killed + 13 injured in West Bank (Palestinian Red Crescent) ⚠️Cluster munitions confirmed; several impact sites across central Israel
Sources: Times of Israel CBS News
19
Mar
Israel · Strike Offensive · Naval Strike / New Front
IAF Strikes Iranian Navy in Caspian Sea for First Time — 5 Missile Boats Destroyed at Bandar Anzali
In a historic expansion of the war's maritime arena, the Israeli Air Force struck the Iranian Navy's Fourth Naval District headquarters and vessels at the port of Bandar Anzali on the Caspian Sea in northern Iran — the first IDF strike in northern Iran and the first time the war has reached the Caspian. The IDF said the operation was guided by Naval Intelligence and the Military Intelligence Directorate. Arab-sourced reports indicated at least five missile boats and several smaller vessels were hit and destroyed. Bandar Anzali serves as the main base of the IRGC's northern fleet and is a critical node in the Iran–Russia weapons and cargo smuggling corridor. The strikes came approximately 1,300 km from Israeli territory and were confirmed by the IDF, which said the aim was to expand aerial and maritime superiority and deny Iran any safe harbour on any coast.
📍Bandar Anzali, Gilan Province, Caspian Sea coast — ~1,300 km from Israel 🚢5+ missile boats destroyed; Iranian Navy's Northern Fleet HQ struck 🔗Iran–Russia smuggling corridor disrupted
Sources: Times of Israel JNS JFeed
19
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Energy Infrastructure Strike
Iran Missiles Knock Out 17% of Qatar's LNG Export Capacity; Ras Laffan Suffers Severe Damage
Following Israel's strike on Iran's South Pars gas field on March 19, Iran launched retaliatory missiles at energy infrastructure across Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE. Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City — the world's largest LNG export terminal — sustained the most severe damage, with QatarEnergy's CEO confirming the attacks knocked out approximately 17 percent of Qatar's LNG export capacity, representing an estimated $20 billion in lost annual revenue. Qatar subsequently expelled Iranian military and security diplomatic attachés, declaring them persona non grata and ordering them out within 24 hours. Saudi Arabia's Foreign Minister warned that 'nonpolitical' options were on the table if attacks continued, and declared that 'the little trust that remained in Iran has been completely shattered.' Strikes on Saudi and UAE energy sites were largely intercepted. Global oil prices surged to $115 per barrel on the day, as markets reacted to the loss of major LNG supply. Trump stated that Israel would not strike South Pars again, but warned the US would 'massively blow up' the entire field if Iran attacked Qatar.
📍Ras Laffan Industrial City, Qatar; Saudi Arabia; UAE 💥17% of Qatar LNG capacity destroyed; ~$20B annual revenue at risk 📈Brent crude hits $115/barrel; IEA calls it largest supply disruption in history
Sources: Al Jazeera Live CNN Live CBS News
19
Mar
Israel · Leadership Strike Offensive · Targeted Assassination
IDF Kills Intelligence Minister Esmaeil Khatib in Tehran; Iran Confirms Death
Israel announced Wednesday, March 19, that it had killed Iranian Minister of Intelligence Esmaeil Khatib in an overnight airstrike on Tehran, a day after Israel killed security chief Ali Larijani and Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian confirmed Khatib's killing. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz stated he had authorized the military to eliminate 'any senior Iranian official' when the opportunity arose. The killing of Khatib, Larijani, and Soleimani in rapid succession within 48 hours — combined with the earlier assassination of Supreme Leader Khamenei on Day 1 — has all but guttered Iran's senior security command. Netanyahu called Larijani the 'boss of the Revolutionary Guards gang' and framed the killings as an effort to give Iranians a path to overthrowing the regime. Iran's Army Chief Amir Hatami warned Tehran's response 'will be decisive and regrettable.'
📍Tehran, Iran 💀Khatib (Intelligence Minister) confirmed killed; Larijani + Soleimani killed Mar 17 ⚠️Iran vows 'decisive' retaliation; most of senior security leadership now eliminated
Sources: CNN Fox News Al Jazeera
19
Mar
US · Diplomatic/Economic Political · War Funding + DNI Testimony
Trump Seeks $200B Pentagon Supplemental; Gabbard Testifies Iran Nuclear Program 'Obliterated'; Oil at $115
On March 19, President Trump confirmed he is seeking $200 billion in new Pentagon funding to replenish munitions expended in Operation Epic Fury, calling it a 'small price to pay.' Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard testified before the Senate, reiterating that Iran's nuclear enrichment program was 'obliterated' in last year's strikes, though she separately told senators the Iranian regime 'appears to be intact, although largely degraded.' Gabbard also faced accusations of altering her Senate testimony by omitting intelligence details that contradicted Trump's 'imminent threat' justification for the war. Global oil prices hit $115 per barrel on Day 20, the highest since the conflict began, as the International Energy Agency warned the war was 'creating the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.' The US also signalled it could 'unsanction' some Iranian oil and release more from its strategic reserve to ease prices.
💰$200B Pentagon supplemental funding requested 📈Oil hits $115/barrel; IEA: largest supply disruption in history 🏛️Gabbard Senate testimony: Iran regime 'intact but largely degraded'
Sources: CNN Live Al Jazeera Day 20
19
Mar
US/Israel · Strike Offensive · Leadership Decapitation
IDF Kills IRGC Spokesman Ali Mohammad Naini in Tehran Dawn Strike
In a pre-dawn strike on March 20, US-Israeli forces killed IRGC Spokesman and Head of the IRGC Public Relations Array, Brigadier General Ali Mohammad Naini, 68, in Tehran. The IRGC confirmed Naini 'was martyred in the criminal cowardly terrorist attack by the American-Zionist side at dawn.' Naini had appeared on national television just hours before his death insisting Iran still maintained full missile production capacity. In the same strike wave, the IDF also confirmed the killing of Esmail Ahmadi, Head of the Intelligence Division of Iran's Basij Force, previously announced as killed alongside Basij Commander Gholamreza Soleimani earlier in the week. The IDF simultaneously struck more than 130 regime targets across western and central Iran overnight, including ballistic missile launchers, UAV systems, and air defense networks.
📍Tehran, Iran — dawn strike on IRGC PR headquarters 💀Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC Spokesman, Brig. Gen.) confirmed killed; Esmail Ahmadi (Basij Intel Chief) also confirmed killed in related strike
Sources: Al Jazeera The National News JNS / Cleveland Jewish News
20
Mar
Iran · Energy Strike Offensive · Infrastructure Attack
Iranian Drones Strike Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi Refinery on Eid al-Fitr, Sparking Fires
In the early hours of March 20 — coinciding with Eid al-Fitr, the celebration marking the end of Ramadan — Iranian drones struck Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery, the country's largest, processing approximately 730,000 barrels per day, sparking fires at multiple operational units. A second strike also targeted the Mina Abdullah refinery (490,000 bpd capacity), bringing the combined refining capacity at risk to roughly 1.2 million barrels per day. Kuwait Petroleum Corporation confirmed emergency teams extinguished the fires with no reported injuries. The Kuwait military confirmed its air defenses were simultaneously intercepting 'hostile missile and drone attacks' targeting multiple locations. The strikes are part of Iran's expanding energy infrastructure campaign following Israel's bombing of Iran's South Pars gas field on March 18, after which Iran vowed 'zero restraint.' Brent crude briefly spiked to $119 per barrel on the news before settling near $114.
📍Mina Al-Ahmadi & Mina Abdullah refineries, south of Kuwait City — combined ~1.2M bpd capacity 🔥Fires at multiple units; contained by emergency teams; no injuries reported 💰Brent crude spiked to $119/bbl; oil prices up ~45% since war began
Sources: AP / US News Al Jazeera NPR
19
Mar
US · Aircraft Incident Defensive Incident · Aircraft Damage
USAF F-35A Hit by Suspected Iranian Fire Over Iran, Makes Emergency Landing — First Crewed Aircraft Hit by Enemy Fire in War
A US Air Force F-35A Lightning II was struck by what two sources described to CNN as Iranian fire while conducting a combat mission over Iran on March 19, forcing an emergency landing at a US air base in the Middle East. CENTCOM spokesman Capt. Tim Hawkins confirmed the aircraft 'landed safely' and 'the pilot is in stable condition,' adding the incident is under investigation. The IRGC posted video purportedly showing its air defense system striking the F-35 at approximately 2:50 a.m. local time, claiming it 'severely damaged' the aircraft. Iran has developed passive infrared sensor-based air defense systems capable of targeting stealth aircraft without radar emissions. If confirmed as a hostile-fire hit, this marks the first time an F-35 has been struck by enemy fire in any conflict. It comes as the US has also lost three F-15Es to Kuwaiti friendly fire (March 1) and a KC-135 in a crash over western Iraq (March 12, killing 6 crew). Air & Space Forces Magazine reported the F-35 is now one of roughly 20 US aircraft known damaged or destroyed in the war.
📍Airspace over Iran — emergency landing at undisclosed US regional air base ✈️F-35A damaged; pilot stable; IRGC claims strike at ~02:50 local; incident under investigation ⚠️First crewed US aircraft confirmed struck by hostile (Iranian) fire in Operation Epic Fury
Sources: CNN Air & Space Forces Magazine The Aviationist
19
Mar
US/Israel · Diplomacy Political · Energy Ceasefire / Alliance Friction
Netanyahu Agrees to Halt Strikes on Iranian Gas Infrastructure After Trump Rebuke; US Lifts Iranian Oil Sanctions
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed at a press conference on March 19 that Israel 'acted alone' in striking Iran's South Pars gas field on March 18 — the world's largest natural gas reserve — and agreed to honor President Trump's direct request to stop further attacks on Iranian energy infrastructure. 'President Trump asked us to hold off on future attacks, and we're holding off,' Netanyahu said. Trump told reporters he 'told him, don't do that,' marking the most significant public rift between the two leaders since the war began. The strike had triggered Iranian retaliation against Ras Laffan (Qatar), Saudi Aramco's Ras Tanura refinery, and UAE gas facilities, sending oil prices surging. DNI Tulsi Gabbard told the House Intelligence Committee that US and Israeli war objectives differ: the US is focused on destroying Iran's ballistic missile program and navy, while Israel is focused on 'disabling the Iranian leadership.' Separately, the US Treasury temporarily lifted sanctions on Iranian oil already loaded on ships through April 19, 2026, in a bid to ease global energy prices. Netanyahu also claimed Iran now has 'no ability to enrich uranium' or 'produce ballistic missiles' — a claim disputed by IAEA Director-General Rafael Grossi.
📍Jerusalem (Netanyahu press conference) / Washington D.C. (Trump Oval Office) ⚡Trump-Netanyahu public split over South Pars strike; energy ceasefire agreed 💰US lifts Iranian oil sanctions through Apr 19; Brent crude above $110/bbl; IEA urges work-from-home globally
Sources: NPR Bloomberg Washington Post
20
Mar
US/Israel · Strikes / Day 21 Offensive · Continued Bombardment
IAF Launches Fresh Waves on Tehran as Iranians Mark Nowruz; Trump Mulls 'Winding Down' War
Israel launched additional waves of strikes on Tehran in the early hours of March 20 — Day 21 of the conflict — as Iranians marked Nowruz (Persian New Year) and Muslims globally celebrated Eid al-Fitr. The IDF said strikes targeted 'infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime across Tehran,' with a second and third wave hitting the Nur area east of Tehran, Isfahan, and western Iran. Iran responded with missile barrages that triggered air-raid sirens in Tel Aviv, with air defense interceptors echoing across the city. The UAE's air defenses also responded to Iranian missile and drone threats, with explosions reported across Dubai. The UK's Royal Air Force continued defensive patrols over Jordan, Qatar, the UAE, and Bahrain. President Trump told reporters he is considering 'winding down' the military campaign even as additional US Marines were dispatched to the Middle East. CENTCOM confirmed oil prices now exceed $110 per barrel, up approximately 45% since the war began, with Goldman Sachs warning prices may remain above $100 through 2027.
📍Tehran, Isfahan, western Iran (IAF); Tel Aviv, Gulf states (Iranian retaliation) 💀Casualties ongoing; Lebanon death toll surpasses 1,001 (Health Ministry); 1M+ Lebanese displaced 💰Oil above $110/bbl; IEA urges work-from-home; Goldman Sachs warns $100+ oil through 2027
Sources: CNN Al Jazeera NPR

Mar
CENTCOM · Day 22 Update Offensive · Strategic Assessment
CENTCOM: 8,000+ Targets Struck, 130 Vessels Destroyed — 'Largest Navy Elimination Since WWII'
In a video message posted to X on Saturday March 21, CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper confirmed that U.S. forces have now struck over 8,000 Iranian targets and destroyed 130 Iranian naval vessels across 22 days of combat operations — constituting what Cooper described as 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' Cooper stated that U.S. forces have flown over 8,000 combat flights and maintain full air superiority over Iran. He also confirmed the use of multiple 5,000-pound bombs on an Iranian hardened underground facility used to store anti-ship cruise missiles and mobile launchers that threatened the Strait of Hormuz. 'Their navy is not sailing, their tactical fighters are not flying, and they've lost the ability to launch missiles and drones at the high rates seen at the beginning of the conflict,' Cooper said. He added that intelligence support sites and missile radar relays monitoring ship movements in the Strait have also been destroyed. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz separately issued a statement assessing that both U.S. and Israeli operations would continue to ramp up.
📍Strait of Hormuz / Iran-wide 🎯8,000+ targets struck total; 130 vessels destroyed; 8,000+ combat sorties flown 💣Multiple 5,000-lb bombs dropped on hardened Hormuz missile/radar facility
Sources: Stars and Stripes Washington Examiner RedState
20
Mar
Iran · IRGC Wave Strike Offensive · Ballistic Missile / Hypersonic
IRGC Launches 54th Wave — Deploys Solid-Fuel Sejjil MRBM Alongside Hypersonic Missiles at Israel
The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps conducted its 54th wave of strikes under Operation True Promise 4, deploying Iran's solid-fuel Sejjil medium-range ballistic missile — a two-stage domestically produced weapon — alongside the Khorramshahr, Kheibar Shekan, Qadr, and Emad ballistic missile systems. In a separate wave, the IRGC announced the launch of hypersonic missiles and drones against American and Israeli targets across the region. The IRGC framed the 53rd wave as being carried out in honor of the 84 sailors killed when the Iranian destroyer Dena was sunk by a U.S. submarine off Sri Lanka. Despite the degradation of Iranian launch capacity — the IDF estimates 60–70% of launchers neutralized — Iran demonstrated an ability to continue multi-system salvos. The rate of fire has slowed to approximately 10–20 missiles per day at Israel, down from 20+ distinct barrages on Day 1.
📍Launched from Iran — targeting Israel and US bases across the region 🚀Sejjil MRBM + Khorramshahr, Kheibar Shekan, Qadr, Emad + hypersonic missiles deployed 📊54th wave total; rate of fire down ~90% vs. Day 1 peak
Sources: The Defense News GlobalSecurity.org

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · IRBM Strike
Iran Fires Two IRBMs at Diego Garcia Joint US-UK Base — First Operational Use at That Range
Iran fired two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at the joint US-UK military base at Diego Garcia on the Chagos Islands in the British Indian Ocean Territory, some 2,500 miles from Iran. According to Israel, Iran used a two-stage weapon for the strike. Neither missile struck the base, but the incident marked Iran's first operational use of a ballistic missile at that range — suggesting Iran's missile inventory has greater reach than Tehran had previously acknowledged. Diego Garcia serves as a hub for long-range U.S. bombers, warships, and submarines likely involved in ongoing Operation Epic Fury strikes. The UK government called the attack 'a threat to British interests.' The Wall Street Journal first reported the strike on Friday March 20.
📍Diego Garcia, British Indian Ocean Territory — 2,500 mi from Iran 🚀Two IRBMs fired — both missed; first Iranian operational IRBM use at this range 🇬🇧UK calls attack 'a threat to British interests'

Mar
IDF · Roaring Lion Offensive · Air Strike
IDF Conducts Five-Strike Wave on Large-Scale Ballistic Missile Array in Western Iran; Hits Nuclear R&D Facility in Tehran
On March 21, the IDF announced that its air force conducted five successive strikes on a large-scale ballistic missile array site in western Iran, releasing footage of the sequential strikes. Separately, the Israeli Air Force struck an IRGC nuclear weapons component research and development facility in Tehran, along with a central IRGC ballistic missile production and development compound, a missile component storage facility, an Iranian Defense Ministry missile fuel production site, and a missile component production site. The IDF also reported that Iran fired a surface-to-air missile at an IAF aircraft which missed, with the aircraft sustaining no damage. A CNN investigation published Friday revealed that the US and Israel had bombed 77% of tunnel entrances at 27 Iranian underground missile bases — though satellite imagery also showed rapid Iranian efforts to dig out buried munitions, in some cases within 48 hours of strikes.
📍Western Iran (missile array) + Tehran (nuclear R&D + IRGC missile production) 🎯5-strike wave on western missile array; nuclear R&D + missile fuel/component sites hit in Tehran 🛡️Iranian SAM fired at IAF aircraft — missed; no damage sustained
Sources: Jerusalem Post Times of Israel
21
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Energy Infrastructure Strike
Iran Strikes Ras Laffan LNG Hub in Qatar — Cuts 17% of Global LNG Output for Up to Five Years
Iran struck Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG facility, the world's largest liquefied natural gas complex. The CEO of QatarEnergy confirmed the strike cut approximately 17 percent of output and could affect production for as long as five years. Qatar supplies roughly 20 percent of global LNG, and the disruption is expected to trigger force majeure declarations on contracts with Belgium, Italy, South Korea, and China. Qatar's prime minister and Turkey's foreign minister held a joint news conference condemning the strike as a 'dangerous escalation.' Qatar's defense forces had previously reported ballistic missile attacks on Al Udeid Air Base. The Ras Laffan strike also prompted Gulf-wide energy infrastructure alerts: Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and the UAE all reported Iranian attacks on energy sites in the same period, as oil and gas prices surged globally following Israel's earlier strike on Iran's South Pars gasfield.
📍Ras Laffan Industrial City, Qatar — world's largest LNG hub 💥~17% of QatarEnergy LNG output cut; impact up to 5 years; force majeure on contracts to Belgium, Italy, S. Korea, China 📈Global LNG and oil prices spike; supply disruptions across Europe and Asia
Sources: Al Jazeera Gulf News
21
Mar
IDF · Lebanon Ground Ops Offensive · Ground Operation
Israel Launches Lebanon Ground Operation; 1M+ Displaced — Katz: No Return South of Litani Until Border Secured
The Israeli military launched what it described as a 'limited and targeted ground operation' against Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon, with IDF Chief of Staff Eyal Zamir stating the army is determined to 'deepen' operations until 'all objectives are achieved.' Lebanon's Ministry of Social Affairs confirmed more than one million people — approximately one fifth of the country's entire population — have been displaced, with over 130,000 sheltering in collective centers and many sleeping in the streets. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz stated that displaced Lebanese residents will not be permitted to return south of the Litani River until Israeli border communities are secured, and separately warned that Hezbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem could face the same fate as his predecessor. Israeli jets struck Bint Jbeil, al-Jumayjimah, Qaqaiyat al-Jisr and multiple Beirut southern suburb areas. Lebanon's Health Ministry reported 1,001+ killed and 2,584+ wounded since March 2. As of March 19, approximately 140,000 people had crossed from Lebanon into Syria. The IDF stated that over 400 Hezbollah fighters had been killed since early March.
📍Southern Lebanon (south of Litani/Zahrani Rivers), Bekaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs 👥1,000,000+ displaced (one-fifth of Lebanese population); 140,000 crossed into Syria as of Mar 19 💀1,001+ killed in Lebanon (Health Ministry); 400+ Hezbollah fighters killed (IDF est.); 2,584+ wounded
Sources: Al Jazeera Time IRC
17
Mar
IDF · Leadership Strike Offensive · Targeted Killing
IDF Assassinates Ali Larijani — Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council
On March 17, Israel assassinated Ali Larijani, the secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council — one of the most senior security officials in the Iranian regime. Larijani, who had previously ruled out ceasefire talks following Trump's early March overtures, was a central figure in Iran's national security decision-making apparatus. The killing represents the highest-profile confirmed assassination since the March 19 killing of IRGC spokesman Ali Mohammad Naini. Larijani's death further degrades the coherence of Iran's military command structure, compounding earlier losses including Supreme Leader Khamenei, Defense Minister Nasirzadeh, IRGC Commander Pakpour, and Defense Council Secretary Shamkhani. Hengaw Organization for Human Rights estimated on March 18 that over 5,300 Iranian military personnel had been killed since the war's start.
📍Tehran, Iran 💀Ali Larijani — Secretary, Supreme National Security Council — confirmed killed 📊Iranian military death toll: 5,300+ (Hengaw, Mar 18); IDF estimated 3,000–4,000 on Mar 13

Iran · Long-Range Strike Offensive · IRBM Launch at Diego Garcia (2nd)
Iran Fires 70th Attack Wave; Cluster Bomb Strike Hits Tel Aviv & Southern Cities
On March 21–22, Iranian armed forces announced their 70th wave of attacks, launching missiles and drones toward Israel and US bases in the Gulf. On the Israeli home front, an Iranian missile equipped with a cluster bomb warhead struck central Tel Aviv, injuring 15 people — one seriously after being downgraded from moderate condition. Separate ballistic missile strikes hit the southern cities of Dimona and Arad, causing residential damage; IDF Spokesperson Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin confirmed these were direct missile hits, and an IDF probe found the two missed interceptions were unconnected. Iran also fired a sixth confirmed ballistic missile at Eilat, likely intercepted. Schools across southern and central Israel were closed, and Prime Minister Netanyahu visited Arad, urging world leaders to join the coalition and warning that Iran's Diego Garcia IRBM launch proved Tehran's missiles could now 'reach deep into Europe.'
📍Tel Aviv (cluster munition), Dimona, Arad, Eilat — Israel; ongoing strikes vs. Gulf US bases 💀15 injured in Tel Aviv cluster strike (1 serious); residential buildings damaged in Arad & Dimona; no fatalities confirmed in latest wave

CENTCOM · Day 22 Update Offensive · 8,000+ Targets Struck; Strait of Hormuz Degradation Ops
CENTCOM: 8,000+ Targets & 130 Vessels Struck; Drops 5,000-lb Bombs on Hormuz Missile Sites
In a Saturday video update posted to X, CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper confirmed that US forces have now struck more than 8,000 Iranian targets and 130 Iranian naval vessels across 22 days of combat operations — constituting what Cooper called 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' The US has flown over 8,000 combat flights and launched the longest field artillery strike in Army combat history. Cooper announced the US dropped multiple 5,000-pound bombs on a hardened underground facility near the Strait of Hormuz used to store anti-ship cruise missiles, mobile launchers, and intelligence radar relays tracking ship movements — directly degrading Iran's ability to threaten freedom of navigation. Iran's tactical fighters are 'not flying' and their ability to launch missiles and drones at high rates has been eliminated. Israeli Defense Minister Katz simultaneously vowed that joint US-Israeli strikes would 'increase significantly' in the coming days. President Trump stated the US has achieved its objectives 'weeks ahead of schedule' and said he was considering 'winding down' military operations while ruling out a ceasefire.

Iran · Missile Strike Offensive · Ballistic Missile Strikes on Southern Israel
Iranian Missile Directly Strikes Dimona & Arad; Netanyahu Visits Damage Sites
On the night of March 21 into March 22, Iranian ballistic missiles achieved direct hits on the southern Israeli cities of Dimona and Arad, damaging residential buildings. IDF Spokesperson Effie Defrin confirmed both were direct strikes and that an army probe found the two missed interceptions were separate, unconnected incidents. Most of those injured in the Arad strike were not in shelters at the time. The IDF chief of staff subsequently approved strikes on 'all fronts' in response. In-person classes were canceled nationwide, and Prime Minister Netanyahu visited the Arad strike site, using Tehran's earlier Diego Garcia IRBM launch as evidence that Iran can now threaten deep into Europe, and calling on allied nations to commit to the joint coalition.

Mar
Trump · Ultimatum Diplomatic · 48-Hour Strait of Hormuz Ultimatum
Trump Threatens to Strike Iran's Power Plants Unless Strait of Hormuz Opened Within 48 Hours
On March 21–22, President Trump posted on Truth Social that if Iran does not 'fully open, without threat, the Strait of Hormuz, within 48 hours,' the United States will hit and obliterate Iranian power plants, 'starting with the biggest one first.' Trump simultaneously stated he 'doesn't want a ceasefire' but is considering 'winding down' military operations, saying from the South Lawn: 'You don't do a ceasefire when you're literally obliterating the other side.' Iran has effectively blocked the strait since the war's start, choking energy flows vital to Asian markets. The Dallas Fed warned the closure will have global economic effects. Twenty-two countries have now signaled readiness to help defend Hormuz shipping after Trump called on allied nations to step up. Iran's Houthis allies in Yemen warned they would retaliate against any effort to reopen the strait.

Mar
Iran · Escalation Offensive · IRBM Strike on Diego Garcia
Iran Fires Ballistic Missiles at US-UK Diego Garcia Base in Indian Ocean
On Friday, March 21, Iran launched two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia, the joint US-UK military base in the Chagos Islands, located approximately 4,000 km (2,500 miles) from Iran — double the range Iran had publicly acknowledged. One missile failed in flight; a US warship fired an interceptor at the other. Neither struck the base. IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir confirmed the attack and warned that the same missiles put European capitals — Berlin, Paris, and Rome — within range. The UK's Ministry of Defence condemned the strike as 'reckless' and confirmed the base had been unsuccessfully targeted. The attack came hours before London announced it would permit US forces to use UK bases, including Diego Garcia, for operations targeting Iranian missile sites near the Strait of Hormuz.
📍Diego Garcia, Chagos Islands, Indian Ocean — ~4,000 km from Iran 🚀2 IRBMs fired; 0 impacts on base — one failed in flight, one intercepted/missed ⚠️First Iranian use of missiles at this range; IDF says Europe now within strike range

Mar
Iran · Wave 70 Offensive · Mass Missile & Drone Salvo
Iran's 70th Attack Wave Targets Southern Israel; Arad & Dimona Mass Casualty Event
On Saturday evening, March 21–22, Iran declared its 70th wave of attacks under Operation True Promise 4, launching Khorramshahr-4 and multi-warhead Qadr ballistic missiles at more than 55 locations. Two missiles penetrated Israeli air defenses and directly struck the southern cities of Arad and Dimona — near Israel's Negev Nuclear Research Center — injuring over 100 people in what Israel declared a mass casualty event. At least 74 were injured at two impact sites in Arad alone, including 7 seriously. A daycare was struck by cluster munitions in Rishon Lezion. Netanyahu visited both cities and condemned Tehran for targeting civilian areas. The IAEA confirmed no radiation release from the Dimona strike. Iran also struck five US military installations across Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Kuwait, Iraq, and Bahrain in the same wave. Israel subsequently closed all schools for Sunday and Monday.
📍Arad, Dimona (near Negev Nuclear Research Center), Rishon Lezion — southern & central Israel 💀100+ Israelis injured (84+ in Arad, several in Dimona); 8 serious, 16 moderate 🚀Khorramshahr-4 and Qadr multi-warhead systems used; cluster munition confirmed in Rishon Lezion

Coalition · IDF Offensive · Wide-Scale Strike Package
IDF Launches Wide-Scale Simultaneous Waves on Iran & Lebanon; Hits 200+ Targets
On Sunday, March 22, the Israeli military launched simultaneous 'wide-scale' waves of airstrikes on Iran and Hezbollah in Lebanon, striking more than 200 targets in a single operational cycle. In Iran, the IDF struck dozens of compounds storing weapons and ballistic missiles, including a large-scale ballistic missile array site in western Iran hit with five successive airstrikes. In Lebanon, two waves of strikes were completed targeting key Hezbollah command centers in Beirut and additional areas. Israel also struck the Qasmiya Bridge over the Litani River — a critical transit link between southern and northern Lebanon — prompting Lebanese President Joseph Aoun to condemn the strikes as 'unjustified' and a 'prelude to a ground invasion.' Iran's air defenses claimed to have intercepted a US-Israeli armed drone over Tehran and claimed to have shot down an Israeli fighter (the third such claim during the war; not confirmed by Israel).

Iran Vows to 'Irreversibly Destroy' Gulf Energy Infrastructure if Power Plants Struck
Following Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to open the Strait of Hormuz or face destruction of Iranian power plants, Tehran issued its sharpest counter-threat of the war. Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf said critical energy infrastructure across the Middle East could be 'irreversibly destroyed' if Iran's power plants are targeted. Iranian Armed Forces spokesman Lt. Col. Ebrahim Zolfaqari added that all US and Israeli energy infrastructure, IT systems, and water desalination facilities in the region would be struck. Iran's military separately declared readiness to close the Strait of Hormuz 'indefinitely' if the US follows through. Iran's representative to the IMO clarified that the strait remains open to all except Tehran's 'enemies.' Bahrain has become the first Gulf state to signal willingness to help reopen the strait, joining 22 nations total including the UAE, Australia, Japan, Canada, and several EU members.

CENTCOM: 8,000+ Targets Struck, Iran's Navy 'Dead in the Water'; 8,000 Combat Sorties Flown
Admiral Brad Cooper, CENTCOM commander, released a sweeping Day 22 operational update declaring that U.S. forces have now struck over 8,000 Iranian military targets and destroyed 130 Iranian naval vessels — what he called 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' Cooper confirmed that the U.S. has flown over 8,000 combat sorties and maintains full air superiority over Iran. He added that the strikes have targeted and destroyed thousands of Iranian missiles, advanced attack drones, and the entirety of Iran's navy, which Iran had used to harass international shipping. Cooper emphasized that Iranian tactical fighters are not flying and that Iran has 'lost the ability to launch missiles and drones at the high rates seen at the beginning of the conflict.' The CENTCOM chief also disclosed that multiple 5,000-pound bombs were dropped on a hardened underground Iranian facility used to disrupt Strait of Hormuz transit, destroying anti-ship cruise missiles, mobile missile launchers, and missile radar relays used to monitor ship movements. Cooper said the U.S. has built 'the most extensive air defense umbrella in the world over the Middle East right now' in coordination with Gulf partners. President Trump also publicly boasted that Iran 'had a navy two weeks ago — they have no navy anymore. It's all at the bottom of the sea.'

Iran Warns Gulf States Will Be 'First to Lose' if Hormuz Coalition Joins; Threatens Bahrain & UAE Directly
Following Trump's 48-hour ultimatum threatening to strike Iranian power plants if the Strait of Hormuz was not reopened, Iran's IRGC specifically warned that the two Arab countries offering to join the Strait of Hormuz defense coalition — Bahrain and the United Arab Emirates — 'will be the first to lose in this battle.' The warning came as over 20 nations issued a joint statement condemning Iran's attacks on civilian shipping and energy infrastructure, affirming readiness to support efforts to ensure safe passage through the Strait. Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araghchi simultaneously reiterated that Iran had 'never asked for a ceasefire' and 'never asked even for negotiation,' telling CBS News that 'we are ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes.' Iran also launched continued waves of drones and missiles at Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and Gulf states. Saudi Arabia intercepted multiple waves of Iranian drones; Kuwait Petroleum Corporation reported that its Mina al-Ahmadi refinery was targeted by drone attacks, forcing some units offline.

IDF Strikes Large-Scale Ballistic Missile Array in Western Iran; IAF Hits IRGC Nuclear R&D Site in Tehran
On March 22, the IDF announced a series of five consecutive airstrikes against a 'large-scale ballistic missiles array site in western Iran,' releasing footage of the sequential precision strikes. Separately, the Israeli Air Force struck an IRGC research and development facility in Tehran used to develop nuclear weapons components, as well as a central IRGC ballistic missile production and development compound, a missile component storage facility, an Iranian Defense Ministry missile fuel production site, and a missile component production facility. The IDF also confirmed that Iran fired a surface-to-air missile at an IAF aircraft, but the projectile missed and the aircraft sustained no damage. CNN analysis of satellite imagery covering 27 Iranian underground missile bases revealed that 77% of their tunnel entrances had been bombed by the U.S.-Israeli campaign to bury munitions underground, though the investigation also found evidence of rapid Iranian efforts to dig out stored munitions at some sites within 48 hours of strikes. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz vowed that joint offensive operations 'will increase significantly' in the coming week, and the IDF stated it still has thousands of targets remaining across Iran.

IDF · Lebanon Offensive · Ground Operations
IDF Expands Lebanon Ground Operation; Death Toll Surpasses 1,000; Over 1 Million Displaced
As of March 22, Israel's escalating military campaign in Lebanon has killed more than 1,000 people including at least 118 children, wounded over 2,584, and displaced more than one million Lebanese — representing approximately one-fifth of the country's entire population. The IDF expanded its ground troop presence in southern Lebanon, with the army chief stating the operation would 'deepen' until 'all objectives are achieved.' Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz declared that displaced Lebanese south of the Litani River would not be permitted to return home until Israeli northern communities' safety was guaranteed. Evacuation orders now cover over 1,470 sq km (14% of Lebanese territory), encompassing more than 100 towns and villages. Approximately 140,000 people crossed from Lebanon into Syria by March 19. More than 120,000 of the displaced are sheltering in collective shelters, with many sleeping in the streets of Beirut. Hezbollah has been launching an average of 100 rockets per day into Israel, with Secretary-General Naim Qassem designated as an IDF elimination target.

Iran Fires Cluster-Munition Ballistic Missiles at Central Israel; IDF Retaliates With 'Wide-Scale' Tehran Strikes
In the early hours of March 23 (Day 24), Iran launched a fresh wave of ballistic missiles at central Israel, with cluster munition submunitions impacting multiple locations in the Petah Tikva area. Rescue services confirmed property damage at several sites but reported no injuries. In direct retaliation, the IDF launched what it described as a 'wide-scale' wave of airstrikes in Tehran targeting infrastructure of Iran's 'terror regime.' Separately, one person was hurt following a missile interception over the UAE, and rocket alarms blared in Lebanon border towns as Hezbollah continued harassing fire. Israel simultaneously announced it would slash outbound flights at Ben Gurion Airport to one per hour with a maximum of 50 passengers aboard, citing continued missile threat to the airport. Trump reportedly indicated he was 'leaning toward' traveling to Israel in April to receive the Israel Prize at the Independence Day ceremony on April 22.

Coalition · Gulf Dynamics Diplomatic · Gulf States Shift Position
UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar & Bahrain Now Pushing to Continue War Until Iran Military Is Degraded; Some Consider Joining Offensive
In a significant diplomatic shift reported by the Times of Israel on March 23, officials in the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Bahrain — states that initially opposed the war — told ToI that after absorbing sustained Iranian missile and drone attacks, all four Gulf states now believe Iran's military must be substantially cut down before any ceasefire can be called. Some officials indicated their governments are now actively considering joining the US-Israeli offensive. The shift reflects the scale of Iranian attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure, airports, and military installations over the preceding three weeks. Iran had by March 17 fired 314 ballistic missiles, 1,672 drones, and 15 cruise missiles at the UAE alone. The IEA chief separately warned that the global economy faces a 'major threat' from the ongoing Hormuz crisis.

Following an Israeli strike on Iran's South Pars natural gasfield — one of the world's largest — Iran launched retaliatory strikes targeting the Haifa port area in Israel and the Ras Laffan LNG complex in Qatar on or around March 20–22. QatarEnergy's CEO confirmed the Ras Laffan strike cut approximately 17% of output, potentially for up to five years, with force majeure likely on contracts to Belgium, Italy, South Korea, and China. Qatar's prime minister and Turkey's foreign minister held a joint news conference condemning the attack as a 'dangerous escalation.' On Day 22 (March 21), the Iranian armed forces announced their 70th wave of attacks, launching missiles and drones at Israel and US bases in the Gulf. Iran also threatened 'zero restraint' and to 'irreversibly destroy' Gulf energy infrastructure if its own power plants are struck. CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper released a video update on March 21 (Day 22) confirming that US forces had struck over 8,000 Iranian targets and destroyed 130 Iranian naval vessels — the 'largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' Cooper confirmed the US maintains air superiority over Iran with over 8,000 combat sorties flown. He touted the recent firing of 'the longest field artillery strike in Army combat history.' He also confirmed the use of 5,000-pound bombs to strike missile sites, and emphasized ongoing efforts to secure free transit through the Strait of Hormuz. Mar
IDF · Assessment Assessment · Halfway Mark
IDF Chief Zamir: Iran Campaign at 'Halfway' Stage; Iran's Diego Garcia Strike Shows Missiles Can Reach Europe
IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir said in a video statement on March 21 that the Israeli military is approximately halfway through its campaign against Iran. He held an assessment with senior IDF officers following Iranian ballistic missile impacts in the southern cities of Dimona and Arad, and approved upcoming strikes 'across all fronts.' An Iranian ballistic missile carrying a large warhead struck Dimona, injuring 47 people including a 12-year-old boy left in serious condition at Soroka Hospital. Air defenses had engaged the missile but failed to intercept it. The IDF said it is investigating the interception failure. Iran's firing of two ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia — some 2,500 miles from Iran — prompted analysts to note that Iran's missiles have a greater range than Tehran has previously acknowledged, placing European assets theoretically within range.
📍Dimona & Arad, southern Israel (Iranian missile impacts); Diego Garcia, Indian Ocean 💀47 hospitalized after Dimona missile impact, including 12-year-old in serious condition ⚠️IDF: Air defense failure at Dimona under investigation; Zamir says campaign ~halfway complete
Sources: Times of Israel Stars and Stripes
23
Mar
Diplomacy · Breakthrough Diplomatic · Ceasefire Talks / Power Plant Deadline Extended
Trump Extends Hormuz Deadline 5 Days; Cites 'Productive' Iran Talks Toward 'Complete Resolution'
US President Donald Trump announced on March 23 that he was postponing his threatened strikes on Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for five days, citing what he described as 'very good and productive conversations' held over the prior two days between the United States and Iran aimed at a 'complete and total resolution' of hostilities. Trump posted his announcement on Truth Social in all-capitals, stating the pause was contingent on the success of ongoing discussions set to continue throughout the week. However, Iran's news agencies denied that Tehran had formally requested talks or a ceasefire, with Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi insisting Iran 'did not request a ceasefire' and would 'continue this resistance without any hesitation.' Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Baqer Ghalibaf simultaneously warned that any US strikes on Iranian power plants would cause 'irreversible destruction' of energy facilities across the Middle East, while Iran's Revolutionary Guards threatened to target power plants supplying US bases if Iran's plants were hit. Despite the diplomatic signaling, Israeli and US strikes on Tehran continued on March 23, with the IDF announcing a fresh 'wide-scale wave of strikes' on Iranian government infrastructure in the capital.
📍Truth Social / Tehran / Washington DC 🕊️5-day suspension of power plant strike threat — conditional on talks ⚠️Iran denies requesting ceasefire; strikes on Tehran continue simultaneously
Sources: Bloomberg Times of Israel Al Jazeera Iran International
23
Mar
Israel · Offensive Offensive · IDF Wide-Scale Wave on Tehran; Strikes on Tabriz & Khorramabad
'Unprecedented' IDF Wave Hits Tehran; Tabriz & Khorramabad Struck — At Least 7 Killed in Residential Areas
The Israeli military launched what Al Jazeera's correspondent in Tehran described as 'unprecedented' strikes on the Iranian capital on March 23, with explosions particularly concentrated on the eastern side of the city. The IDF confirmed it had 'begun a wide-scale wave of strikes' targeting Iranian government infrastructure sites in Tehran. Iran's air defense systems were observed activating in eastern Tehran. Separately, Iran's Fars news agency reported that a strike on a residential building in Khorramabad, west of Tehran, killed one child and wounded several others. At least six additional people were killed in strikes on residential areas in Tabriz, with the director general of crisis management for East Azerbaijan province confirming two deadly attacks in the city. The US military also separately confirmed it targeted a turbine engine production site in Qom province used for IRGC drone and aircraft components. CENTCOM chief Admiral Brad Cooper, in his first one-on-one interview of the war with Iran International, accused Iran of 'operating in a sign of desperation,' stating Iran had 'attacked civilian targets very deliberately, more than 300 times' in recent weeks.
📍Tehran (eastern districts), Tabriz (East Azerbaijan), Khorramabad, Qom Province 💀At least 7 killed in Tabriz & Khorramabad residential strikes (Fars); Iranian civilian death toll surpasses 1,407 confirmed (HRANA) 🎯Targets: Iranian govt infrastructure, IRGC turbine engine plant (Qom), Tehran government sites
Sources: Al Jazeera Daily Post Iran International
23
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · IRGC Strikes Prince Sultan AB (Saudi Arabia) & US 5th Fleet (Bahrain)
IRGC Launches Fresh Missile & Drone Salvo at Prince Sultan Air Base and US 5th Fleet; UAE Intercepts Ballistic Missile Near Al-Dhafra
On March 23, an IRGC spokesperson for the Khatam al-Anbiya headquarters confirmed that Iranian forces had attacked Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia and the US Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain using a combined barrage of missiles and drones as part of continued retaliatory operations. Separately, UAE authorities reported that an Indian national living in the UAE was injured by falling shrapnel following the interception of a ballistic missile over an industrial area near Al-Dhafra Air Base in Abu Dhabi. United Hatzalah emergency responders were also photographed at the scene of reported shrapnel impacts across central Israel following a renewed Iranian missile barrage. Iran's Revolutionary Guards also reiterated that the Strait of Hormuz would remain blocked to shipping until Iranian power plants damaged in the conflict are rebuilt, even as Foreign Minister Araghchi maintained publicly that the strait was 'not closed' — attributing ship hesitance to insurer risk assessments rather than Iranian blockade enforcement.
📍Prince Sultan Air Base (Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia), US 5th Fleet HQ (Bahrain), Al-Dhafra Air Base (Abu Dhabi, UAE), Central Israel 💥1 Indian national injured by intercept debris near Al-Dhafra (UAE); shrapnel impacts in central Israel 🚢IRGC reiterates Strait of Hormuz closure threat pending power plant reconstruction
Sources: Al Jazeera Jerusalem Post Iran International
23
Mar
CENTCOM · Update Statement · CENTCOM Cooper: Iran Targeting Civilians '300+ Times' in 'Sign of Desperation'; ~140 Vessels Sunk or Damaged
CENTCOM Chief Cooper: Iran 'Operating in Desperation'; Coalition Has Sunk ~140 Vessels; Campaign 'Ahead or On Plan'
In his first one-on-one interview of the war — given to Iran International — CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper stated that Iran was 'operating in a sign of desperation,' having 'attacked civilian targets very deliberately, more than 300 times' in recent weeks. Cooper confirmed that coalition operations had 'sunk or severely damaged about 140 vessels since the start of the campaign,' updating the previously reported figure of 130. He reaffirmed that the Strait of Hormuz is 'physically open to transit' but that ships are not using it because Iran is 'shooting at them with drones and missiles.' Cooper also noted that Iran's military chiefs are 'in deep bunkers' and that the overall campaign remains 'ahead or on plan,' citing a significant slowdown in Iranian incoming fire as the war enters its fourth week. President Trump separately stated over the weekend that Iran was 'ready to end the war' but that 'the terms aren't good enough yet,' while declining to specify exact conditions beyond regime change, nuclear disarmament, and what he has called 'unconditional surrender.'
📍CENTCOM HQ / MacDill Air Force Base, Tampa, FL 🚢~140 Iranian vessels sunk or severely damaged (updated from 130) 📉Iranian missile/drone launch rate collapsed ~92% from Day 1 peak; campaign 'ahead or on plan'
Sources: Iran International Times of Israel Stars & Stripes
23
Mar
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · 1.2M Displaced; Lebanon Death Toll 1,024+; IDF Givati Brigade Ops Ongoing
Lebanon Death Toll Reaches 1,024+; 1.2 Million Displaced — IDF Givati Brigade Continues Southern Lebanon Ops
As of March 23, Lebanon's Ministry of Public Health has confirmed at least 1,024 people killed since Israel renewed widespread attacks on March 2, including at least 118 children, with more than 2,740 wounded. The Lebanese government confirmed that over 1.2 million people — nearly one-fifth of the country's entire population — have been displaced, creating what humanitarian agencies are calling the worst internal displacement crisis Lebanon has seen. The UN Refugee Agency (UNHCR) separately estimates 3.2 million people have been internally displaced in Iran. IDF Givati Brigade soldiers were reported operating in southern Lebanon on March 23, continuing ground operations against Hezbollah targets. The IDF also struck the Qasmiyeh Bridge in southern Lebanon, which it said was being used by Hezbollah. Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun condemned Israeli attacks on Lebanese infrastructure as a 'prelude to a ground invasion.' The UK's RAF Typhoon and F-35 jets continued defensive air patrols over Jordan, Qatar, the UAE and Bahrain. As of March 19, approximately 140,000 people had crossed from Lebanon into Syria.
📍Southern Lebanon, Beirut suburbs, Bekaa Valley, Qasmiyeh Bridge 💀1,024+ killed in Lebanon (incl. 118+ children); 2,740+ wounded (Lebanese Health Ministry, Mar 21) 🏚️1,200,000+ displaced in Lebanon (Lebanese govt); 140,000+ fled to Syria (IRC, Mar 19)
Sources: Al Jazeera Time Arab News PK Jerusalem Post
23
Mar
Iran · Diplomatic Diplomatic Standoff · Hormuz Deadline
Iran Denies Talks as Bahrain Sirens Sound; Hormuz Deadline Suspended 5 Days
Hours after the last CENTCOM briefing confirmed Iran is 'operating in desperation,' Iranian air-raid sirens rang out again over Bahrain — the first since Trump's surprise announcement. On Monday morning (Mar 23), Trump posted in all-caps on Truth Social that the U.S. and Iran had 'very good and productive conversations' over the prior 48 hours toward 'a complete and total resolution,' and ordered the Pentagon to pause all strikes on Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for a five-day period. Trump told reporters that special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner had conducted talks Sunday into the evening with a 'respected' Iranian leader he declined to name, saying he 'did not want to get him killed.' An Israeli official told Axios the interlocutor was Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf. Iran's Foreign Ministry flatly denied any talks had taken place, with state media calling Trump's announcement 'psychological warfare' designed to manipulate energy markets. Iranian parliament speaker Ghalibaf himself denied negotiations were underway. U.S. stocks surged roughly $1.7 trillion in minutes before recovering some losses as Iran's denial circulated. Oil prices dropped from ~$109 to $92 Brent before partially recovering. Trump said any deal would require Iran to end its nuclear program — 'no nuclear bomb, not even close to it' — and that he and new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei would 'together be in charge' of the Strait of Hormuz. Israel was notified of the talks and is expected to follow the U.S. in suspending strikes on Iranian power plants.
📍Truth Social / Washington DC · Bahrain (sirens resumed post-announcement) 🕊️5-day pause on Iran power plant strikes; Hormuz reopening demanded as condition ⚠️Iran denies talks; IRGC warns Strait closure continues until damaged power plants rebuilt
Sources: Axios Washington Post PBS NewsHour France 24
23
Mar
IDF · Ground Ops Offensive · Lebanon Expansion
HRW: IDF Signals 'Stepped-Up Atrocities' as Ground Ops Expand South of Litani; Katz Orders Home Demolitions
Human Rights Watch issued a report on March 23 stating that Israeli forces have expanded ground operations in southern Lebanon 'after indicating an intent to forcibly displace residents, destroy civilian homes and conduct strikes that could target civilians.' On March 22, Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz announced that he and Prime Minister Netanyahu had 'ordered the acceleration of the demolition of Lebanese houses in the border villages in order to thwart threats to Israeli communities — in accordance with the Beit Hanoun and Rafah models in Gaza.' Between March 11 and 15, the IDF issued at least five displacement orders for seven neighborhoods in Beirut's southern suburbs, with warnings that the military 'will not hesitate to target anyone who is present near Hezbollah members, facilities, or means of combat.' The IDF's chief of staff Eyal Zamir said the army is determined to 'deepen' the Lebanon operation until 'all of our objectives are achieved.' Over the weekend of March 20–21, 110 Hezbollah attack waves against Israel were identified, with 83 waves using rockets and missiles. The Radwan Unit's special forces commander, Abu Khalil Barji, was eliminated in Majdal Selm. As of March 19, approximately 140,000 people had crossed from Lebanon into Syria. Lebanon's total death toll stands at 1,024+ with more than 1.2 million displaced — roughly 1-in-5 of the population.
📍Southern Lebanon / Beirut southern suburbs — ground ops south of Litani expanding 💀1,024+ killed in Lebanon (incl. 118 children); 1.2M+ displaced; 140,000 fled to Syria ⚠️HRW: forcible displacement and wanton destruction may constitute war crimes
Sources: Human Rights Watch The Intercept Al Jazeera

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Energy Escalation
IRGC Wave 70+ Targets Haifa Refinery, Gulf Energy Sites; Iran Death Toll Tops 2,000
Iran's death toll has surpassed 2,000, its Ministry of Health confirmed on or around March 23, with HRANA independently verifying at least 1,407 civilian deaths (including 214 children). Iran's health ministry separately reported ~210 children killed and 1,500+ under-18s injured, with 300 health and emergency facilities damaged. The IRGC on March 21 announced its 70th wave of strikes under 'Operation True Promise 4,' targeting more than 55 locations across the region with Khorramshahr-4 and Qadr multi-warhead missile systems, including strikes on five US military installations in Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, Iraq, and Bahrain. Iran also struck the Haifa oil refinery in northern Israel (causing localized damage), fired cluster-bomb-tipped missiles at central Israel injuring dozens, and targeted two Iranian ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia — the joint US-UK Indian Ocean base some 4,000km from Iran — marking the first operational use of a missile at that range. Cooper confirmed the missiles missed; no damage was caused. Meanwhile Iran's IRGC warned that the Strait of Hormuz would remain shut until its damaged power plants are rebuilt. On Day 23, HRANA confirmed at least one additional civilian killed and three injured within the 24-hour period.
📍Haifa refinery (Israel) · Diego Garcia (Indian Ocean) · Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, Iraq, Bahrain (Wave 70) 💀Iran: 2,000+ killed total (MoH); 18,551+ injured · Israel: 15 killed, 4,697+ injured 🚀IRGC Wave 70: Khorramshahr-4 & Qadr missiles; cluster munitions used on central Israel; first Diego Garcia ballistic missile strike

Diplomacy · Trump Pause Political · Ceasefire Signal
Trump Delays Iran Power-Plant Strikes 5 Days as Ceasefire Talks Begin
US President Donald Trump postponed threatened strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure and power plants for five days, pending the outcome of what he described as talks with Iran to end the 24-day war. The pause came as Bloomberg reported that ceasefire negotiations had gotten underway, though no framework has been publicly confirmed. Iran's IRGC parliament speaker Mohammad Baqer Ghalibaf had warned that attacks on Iranian power plants could lead to 'irreversible destruction' of energy facilities across the Middle East, while Foreign Minister Araghchi struck a softer tone, stating that the Strait of Hormuz was not formally closed. Separately, CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper confirmed coalition operations have sunk or severely damaged approximately 140 Iranian vessels since the campaign began, describing the destruction as 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.'
📍Tehran / Washington DC / Regional ⏱️5-day window on power-plant strike threat; Hormuz negotiations ongoing 💀Iran death toll surpassed 2,000+ (Health Ministry); 1,500+ confirmed by MoH as of Mar 22

IRGC Strikes Prince Sultan Airbase, US 5th Fleet Bahrain; Arad & Dimona Hit Overnight
An IRGC Khatam al-Anbiya headquarters spokesperson confirmed that Iranian forces attacked Prince Sultan Airbase in Saudi Arabia and the US Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain using missiles and drones. Separately, in one of its most severe attacks on Israel to date, Iran targeted the Israeli southern towns of Arad and Dimona — near the Negev Nuclear Research Center — leaving dozens injured according to Israeli authorities. Iranian parliament speaker Ghalibaf warned on X that any strikes on Iranian power plants would lead to 'irreversible destruction' of energy infrastructure across the Middle East, as CENTCOM Adm. Cooper noted that Iranian attack volumes have fallen sharply — now 'one or two at a time' versus the large waves seen at the conflict's outset, indicating significant degradation of Iran's launch capacity.
