Coalition · Strikes Offensive · Wide-Scale Wave
IDF Launches 'Unprecedented' Wide-Scale Wave on Tehran; Second Round Same Day
The Israeli military announced it had begun a 'wide-scale wave of strikes' on infrastructure targets in eastern Tehran, with Al Jazeera's correspondent on the ground describing the size and volume of the explosions as 'unprecedented,' especially on the eastern side of the city. The IDF then launched a second round of strikes the same day, hours after the first wave. Targets included IRGC facilities, regime infrastructure, and ballistic missile arrays. The IDF also conducted five successive airstrikes on a large-scale ballistic missile array site in western Iran, releasing footage of the strikes. Iran's death toll in the war surpassed 1,500 per the Ministry of Health as the strikes continued. At least six people were killed in strikes on homes in Tabriz and one child was killed in Khorramabad, according to Iranian state media reports.

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile & Drone Strike
IRGC Fires on Northern Israel; Cluster Missile Suspected Near Nesher
Iranian forces launched a fresh wave of missiles toward northern Israel in the early hours of March 24. The IDF confirmed that missiles launched from Iran were directed at northern Israel, with interception debris striking a building in the city of Nesher and causing structural damage. Magen David Adom reported a man in his 30s was lightly injured after stepping on a fragment. The IDF suspects Iran employed cluster munitions in the barrage — a practice banned by the Convention on Cluster Munitions but not by Iran. Sirens blared across multiple northern communities as Home Front Command search-and-rescue forces deployed to impact sites. The strikes follow a pattern of sustained, if degraded, Iranian launch activity; by Day 24 missile launches are down roughly 92% from the Day 1 peak of 480 ballistic missiles.

Mar
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Talks Political · Negotiations
Trump Delays Iran Power-Plant Strikes 5 Days; US–Iran Talks Eyed in Pakistan by Week's End
US President Donald Trump announced a five-day postponement of threatened strikes on Iranian power and energy infrastructure, citing what he described as productive diplomatic contacts with Tehran. The White House indicated the US is weighing talks with Iran or intermediaries as early as this week, with Israeli officials reporting Pakistan as a potential venue. Trump's press secretary Karoline Leavitt declined to confirm specifics, saying 'these are sensitive diplomatic discussions.' Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Baqer Ghalibaf called Trump's talk of ongoing negotiations 'fake news designed to manipulate markets,' while the Politico reported the Trump administration is quietly weighing Ghalibaf himself as a potential future Iranian leader. The US has set April 9, 2026 as its internal target date for ending the conflict. Netanyahu, in a video statement, said Trump 'believes there is a chance to leverage the massive achievements' of the campaign through an agreement, but Israel says it will continue strikes until instructed otherwise.

Mar
CENTCOM · Update Coalition · Strike Assessment
CENTCOM: 8,000+ Targets Struck, 140 Iranian Vessels Destroyed — 'Navy Not Sailing'
CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper released a video update on March 21–23 confirming that US forces have now struck more than 8,000 military targets in Iran since February 28 — constituting the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II. Cooper confirmed 130–140 Iranian naval vessels destroyed or severely damaged. 'Their navy is not sailing, their tactical fighters are not flying, and they've lost the ability to launch missiles and drones at the high rates seen at the beginning of the conflict,' Cooper said. Cooper also stated Iran has 'attacked civilian targets very deliberately, more than 300 times' in a 'sign of desperation.' More than 50,000 US service members are supporting the campaign across the region. Cooper's operational assessment: Iran's combat capability is 'on a steady decline as offensive strikes ramp up.' Iran's IRGC simultaneously warned that the Strait of Hormuz will remain blocked until damaged Iranian power plants are rebuilt, while Foreign Minister Araghchi maintained the strait is 'not closed' but ships face Iranian drone and missile fire.

IDF · Lebanon Ground & Air · Ops Expansion
HRW: IDF Expands Ground Ops South of Litani; Katz Orders Home Demolitions — 1M+ Displaced
Human Rights Watch released a report on March 23 documenting Israeli forces expanding ground operations in southern Lebanon and issuing new orders that raise serious war-crimes concerns. Defense Minister Israel Katz confirmed he and PM Netanyahu had 'ordered the acceleration of the demolition of Lebanese houses in border villages' citing the 'Beit Hanoun and Rafah models.' Between March 11 and 15, the IDF's Arabic spokesperson issued at least five near-identical displacement orders for seven Beirut southern-suburb neighborhoods. Lebanon's Ministry of Public Health now puts the Lebanese death toll at 1,024 killed — including 827 men, 79 women, and 118 children — and 2,740 wounded since March 2. More than 1 million people, approximately 1 in 5 of Lebanon's population, have been displaced. As of March 19, approximately 140,000 people crossed from Lebanon into Syria. The IDF also struck a structure used by Hezbollah's Radwan Forces in southern Lebanon and released footage on March 23. Hezbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem has been designated a target for elimination.

Iran · Strike Offensive · Missile Strike on Tel Aviv
Iranian Missiles Strike Tel Aviv; At Least 4 Impact Sites, 6 Wounded
On the morning of March 24, Iranian ballistic missiles struck central Tel Aviv, with emergency responders confirming at least four impact sites across the city. Israel's Magen David Adom reported six people wounded in the immediate aftermath, with Agency photos showing damaged and burning buildings. Separately, rocket trails were photographed over the coastal city of Netanya amid a fresh barrage. Iran's IRGC warned that 'special plans' targeting Tel Aviv and US regional allies would 'remove any hope of negotiation.' Israeli air defense engaged the incoming fire but debris and direct impacts caused widespread structural damage in residential neighborhoods.

Mar
IDF · Air Campaign Offensive · Wave Strikes on Iran & Lebanon
IDF Strikes 50+ Targets in Iran Overnight; Hits Isfahan, Missile Storage, Regime HQs; All Litani Bridges Blown
The Israeli Air Force carried out an 'extensive' wave of overnight airstrikes into Iran on March 24, targeting more than 50 sites including ballistic missile production facilities in Isfahan — home to a key Iranian nuclear complex — along with launch sites and Iranian Intelligence Ministry infrastructure. The IDF also confirmed that all bridges over the Litani River used by Hezbollah to move weapons south have been destroyed. Defense Minister Israel Katz stated that 'the IDF will control the remaining bridges and the security zone up to the Litani.' In parallel, IDF struck Hezbollah command infrastructure in Beirut's Haret Hreik neighborhood, releasing footage of the strike on a Radwan Force structure. Israel has now struck more than 3,000 targets in Iran since the war began, per IDF figures.
📍Isfahan (nuclear-linked sites), western/central Iran; Litani River crossings; Haret Hreik, Beirut 💥50+ Iran targets overnight; all Litani bridges destroyed; Radwan HQ in Beirut struck

Mar
Iran · Diplomatic Diplomatic · Lebanon Expels Iranian Ambassador
Lebanon Declares Iranian Ambassador Persona Non Grata; Orders Envoy Out by March 29
Lebanese Foreign Minister Youssef Raggi announced on March 24 that Iran's designated ambassador, Mohammad Reza Shibani, has been declared persona non grata and ordered to leave Lebanese territory no later than March 29, 2026. The ministry simultaneously summoned the Lebanese ambassador to Iran back for consultations, citing Tehran's violation of diplomatic norms. The move comes after Lebanon banned IRGC activity at the war's outset and demanded Iranian nationals obtain visas. Lebanon has been dragged into the war by Hezbollah's refusal to disarm and its continued missile attacks on Israel, triggering intensive Israeli bombing and the displacement of over one million people. Iran responded by withdrawing its own ambassador to Lebanon.
📍Beirut, Lebanon / Tehran, Iran 🕊️Ambassador expelled; diplomatic relations effectively suspended; Lebanon cites Iranian violation of norms
Sources: CNN Times of Israel

Mar
US-Coalition · Strike Offensive · PMF HQ Struck in Iraq
US-Israeli Strikes Kill 15 at Iran-Backed PMF HQ in Anbar; Operations Commander Among Dead
Apparent US-Israeli airstrikes targeted the military headquarters of the Popular Mobilization Forces (PMF) in Iraq's western Anbar province in the early hours of March 24, killing at least 15 people according to Iraq's Joint Operations Command. Saad Duwai Al-Baiji, the PMF's operations commander in Anbar province, was confirmed among those killed. The PMF condemned the strike as a 'flagrant violation of Iraqi sovereignty' and vowed the 'blood of the martyrs will not be wasted,' urging Iraqi political leaders to respond. The attack marks a significant escalation of US-Israeli strikes against one of Iran's primary regional proxy forces inside Iraqi territory.

Mar
Diplomacy · Mixed Signals Political · Iran Denies Talks; Trump Claims Deal Near
Trump Claims Iran 'Wants a Deal'; Tehran Calls It 'Fake News' — Talks in Pakistan Eyed
Mixed signals over potential negotiations dominated March 24 as US President Donald Trump stated that Iran 'wants to make a deal' and that a deal could be imminent, while simultaneously strikes and missile exchanges continued. Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf dismissed Trump's claims as 'fake news' designed to manipulate markets. Israeli officials, however, said they believe US and Iranian representatives could hold talks in Pakistan as early as this week. Netanyahu confirmed a phone call with Trump, saying the president believes 'there is a chance to leverage the massive achievements' of the campaign through an agreement protecting Israel's 'vital interests.' Iran's IRGC meanwhile threatened 'special plans' for Tel Aviv and US regional allies, signaling no imminent halt to hostilities. Trump extended his deadline on Iranian power plant strikes by five days to allow for diplomacy.

Mar
Coalition · Air Campaign Offensive · IAF Wave Strikes
IAF Drops 120+ Bombs on Iranian Targets; Hits Missile Sites, Weapons Factories, Air Defenses
The Israeli Air Force completed a fresh wave of strikes on March 24, dropping over 120 bombs across dozens of Iranian targets. The IDF confirmed the strikes hit ballistic missile launch sites, weapons production facilities, and air defense systems. Among the targets was a factory in Isfahan used to manufacture explosives for various weapons — a facility previously struck in the June 2025 war that Iran had worked to restore. IDF Spokesman Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin confirmed the cell responsible for the recent missile fire toward the southern Israeli city of Arad was eliminated in a separate airstrike. The Israeli military stated it is operating in accordance with directives from Israel's political leadership and will continue striking in Iran according to its plans until instructed otherwise.

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Ballistic Missile Barrage
Iran Fires 11th Missile Salvo of Day; Ballistic Missile Strikes Beirut; Cluster Munitions Hit Central Israel
Iran launched its 11th missile attack of the day on March 24, with sirens sounding across northern Israel as a ballistic missile struck the city of Safed with no immediate injuries reported. Rocket trails were observed over the coastal city of Netanya amid the fresh barrage. The IDF separately confirmed that a ballistic missile launched from Iranian territory struck Beirut — an extraordinary escalation targeting Lebanese soil. Iran also deployed cluster munition-equipped missiles against central Israel, with submunitions dispersing over multiple locations including Nesher. In Kuwait, power lines were hit from air defense shrapnel, causing partial electricity outages. Missile alert sirens sounded in Bahrain, and Saudi Arabia's Defense Ministry confirmed it destroyed 19 Iranian drones targeting its oil-rich Eastern Province. Iran's launches have slowed dramatically — down to roughly 10 missiles per day from approximately 90 on Day 1 — as the IDF reported it had destroyed or disabled about 330 of Iran's estimated 470 ballistic missile launchers.

Mar
Iran · Infrastructure Strikes Offensive · Energy Infrastructure
US-Israeli Strikes Hit Two Gas Facilities and Pipeline in Central Iran; Trump Postpones Power Plant Strikes 5 Days
Iranian media reported on March 24 that US-Israeli strikes hit two gas facilities and a gas pipeline, hours after President Trump postponed planned attacks on Iran's power infrastructure. The facilities in central Iran were reported as 'partially damaged' by Fars News Agency, Iran's only outlet to report the incident. An attack also struck the gas pipeline of the Khorramshahr power plant in southwest Iran. Trump extended his 48-hour ultimatum for Iran to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz by five days, citing 'productive' diplomatic contact and claiming the US is in talks with Tehran. Iran flatly denied any such talks were occurring, with sources linked to the IRGC warning of 'special plans' for Tel Aviv and US regional allies designed to 'completely remove any hope of negotiation.' Pakistan confirmed it is ready to host talks to end the war. US officials were said to be pushing to open a direct line to a senior decision-maker in Tehran, per Channel 12.
📍Central Iran (gas facilities); Khorramshahr, southwest Iran (power plant pipeline) 🕊️Trump postpones power plant strikes 5 days; Pakistan ready to host talks; Iran denies all negotiations ⚡Strait of Hormuz remains physically open but effectively blocked by Iranian drone/missile threat

Mar
Lebanon · Civilian Crisis Humanitarian · Displacement & Strikes
IDF Strikes Kill 3 in Beirut Suburb (Incl. 3-Year-Old Girl); 1.2M+ Displaced Across Lebanon; Hezbollah Fuel Station Destroyed
An Israeli airstrike on a residential apartment in Bchamoun, roughly 10km southeast of Beirut, killed at least three people including a three-year-old girl and wounded five others on March 24, according to Lebanon's Ministry of Public Health. The strike hit an area outside the previously designated evacuation zones, drawing widespread condemnation. The Israeli army also struck and destroyed a Hezbollah-affiliated Amana fuel station in Lebanon as part of its campaign against Hezbollah's economic infrastructure. A UN spokesman confirmed that more than 1.2 million people — approximately one in five Lebanese — have now been displaced by the conflict, with the crisis deepening daily. Defense Minister Israel Katz announced Israel will install a security zone stretching to the Litani River, with hundreds of thousands of southern Lebanese residents barred from returning until security for northern Israel is ensured. The IDF confirmed it had blown all bridges over the Litani River to sever Hezbollah resupply routes.
📍Bchamoun (Beirut suburb); southern Lebanon; Litani River bridges 💀3 killed (incl. 3-year-old girl), 5 wounded in Bchamoun airstrike; 1,039+ total Lebanese killed since March 2 🚨1.2M+ displaced (UN) — ~1 in 5 Lebanese; IDF demolishing border villages, all Litani bridges blown
Sources: Al Jazeera UPI Jerusalem Post
25
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile / Cluster Munition Strike
Iran Cluster Munitions Strike Bnei Brak; Ballistic Missiles Hit Tel Aviv & Safed
On March 25, Iranian forces continued sustained missile and cluster munition attacks across Israel. Cluster bomb submunitions struck the Tel Aviv suburb of Bnei Brak, wounding at least 9 people, according to Israeli first responder agency Magen David Adom. Separately, Iranian ballistic missiles impacted multiple areas of Tel Aviv, causing major building damage and at least four reported casualties, while a missile also struck the northern city of Safed. Israel's 'David's Sling' interceptor system had earlier suffered a reported malfunction over the weekend, allowing two Iranian ballistic missiles to reach southern Israel. The IDF's early-warning system issued an alert for Eilat amid a further apparent Iranian missile launch; the projectile was likely intercepted with no injuries reported.
📍Bnei Brak, Tel Aviv, Safed, Eilat — Israel 💀9 wounded (Bnei Brak cluster munitions); 4+ casualties Tel Aviv; David's Sling malfunction allowed 2 missiles to reach south Israel
Sources: Times of Israel
25
Mar
Israel · Strike Offensive · Air Strike
IAF Launches New Wave of Strikes on Iranian Regime Infrastructure in Tehran
The Israeli Air Force announced a fresh wave of strikes targeting Iranian regime infrastructure sites in Tehran on March 25. The IDF stated the airstrikes were conducted in accordance with directives from Israel's political leadership and would continue until instructed otherwise. Al Jazeera's correspondent in Tehran reported explosions across the city, with the IDF separately confirming a strike killed an IRGC official in Beirut and that five Hezbollah operatives were slain in southern Lebanon during the same operational period.
📍Tehran, Iran; Beirut, Lebanon; southern Lebanon 💥Iranian regime infrastructure sites struck; 1 IRGC official killed in Beirut; 5 Hezbollah operatives killed in southern Lebanon

Mar
US · Strike Offensive · Air Strike / Drone
Drone Strikes Fuel Tank at Kuwait International Airport; US Hits Iran-Backed Group HQ in Iraq
An Iranian drone struck a fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport on March 25, sparking a fire, as Iranian attacks on Gulf infrastructure continued into Day 26 of the conflict. Separately, the US military launched a strike in Iraq's Anbar province targeting the headquarters of an Iran-backed armed group and its senior commander, Saad Dawai, as Washington continued its 'deliberate but calibrated' campaign against Iranian proxy leadership across the broader theater of war.
📍Kuwait International Airport; Anbar Province, Iraq 🔥Fuel tank fire at Kuwait airport; Iran-backed group HQ destroyed; commander Saad Dawai targeted
Sources: Times of Israel Al Jazeera
25
Mar
Diplomacy · Pakistan Offer Diplomatic · Mediation
Trump Says US 'Talking to the Right People' in Iran; Pakistan Offers to Host Talks; Iran Denies
On March 24–25, President Trump stated the US is 'talking to the right people' in Iran, who 'badly' want to make a deal, and that Washington has submitted a 15-point plan to Tehran. An Israeli official confirmed the plan was shared with Israel before being sent to Iran, with a potential summit eyed in Pakistan within days. Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif publicly offered Islamabad as a host venue for comprehensive settlement talks, tagging Trump, Witkoff, and Iranian FM Araghchi on X. Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf dismissed Trump's talk of negotiations as 'fake news' designed to manipulate financial and oil markets, while the IRGC warned that 'special plans are arranged tonight for Tel Aviv.'
📍Washington D.C. / Islamabad, Pakistan (proposed venue) 🗣️US 15-point plan submitted; Pakistan ready to host; Iran parliament calls it 'fake news'; IRGC threatens new Tel Aviv strike
Sources: Times of Israel Signal SCV

Mar
Iran · Government Administrative · Leadership Appointment
Iran Names New Supreme National Security Council Secretary to Replace Slain Larijani
Iran appointed Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as the new secretary of the Supreme National Security Council on March 25, replacing Ali Larijani, who was killed in a US-Israeli strike on March 17. The appointment reflects the IRGC's growing influence over regime decision-making amid a leadership vacuum caused by decapitation strikes, with new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly struggling to exercise full authority. Two senior Israeli officials told Axios that the IRGC has largely filled the resulting power vacuum, with some of the most aggressive elements of the corps expanding their influence.
📍Tehran, Iran 👤Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr appointed SNSC Secretary; replaces Ali Larijani (KIA Mar 17); IRGC consolidates power
Sources: Al Jazeera

Mar
Lebanon · Hezbollah Offensive · Rocket Strike
Hezbollah Rocket Kills Woman in Northern Israel; Lebanon Expels Iran's Ambassador
A Hezbollah rocket attack on northern Israel on March 24–25 killed one woman and injured two others, identified as Nuriel Dubin, 27, a youth counselor who was about to be married. In a significant diplomatic development, Lebanon formally declared the Iranian ambassador persona non grata and ordered him to leave the country, also recalling its own envoy to Iran for consultations, citing Iran's violations of diplomatic norms. Lebanon's Council of Ministers also voted to ban Hezbollah's military activities and called on the group to hand over its weapons, as over 1 million Lebanese remain displaced by Israeli strikes.
📍Northern Israel (rocket strike); Beirut, Lebanon (diplomatic expulsion) 💀1 Israeli woman killed (Nuriel Dubin, 27); 2 injured; 1M+ displaced across Lebanon; Iran ambassador expelled
Sources: Times of Israel Al Jazeera
25
Mar
CENTCOM · Strike Update Offensive · Targets Struck Milestone
CENTCOM: 9,000+ Targets Struck, 9,000+ Combat Flights Flown
US Central Command confirmed that coalition forces have now struck more than 9,000 Iranian targets and flown more than 9,000 combat flights since Operation Epic Fury began on February 28. The milestone update also noted that 130 Iranian naval vessels have been destroyed or heavily damaged — described by CENTCOM commander Adm. Brad Cooper as 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' Cooper assessed that Iran's combat capability 'is on a steady decline,' with its navy not sailing, tactical fighters not flying, and the ability to launch missiles and drones at high rates effectively eliminated. The US also used multiple 5,000-pound bombs to destroy a hardened underground facility in the Strait of Hormuz area used to store anti-ship cruise missiles and missile radar relay equipment.
📍Iran-wide; Strait of Hormuz facility 💀No new US/Israeli casualties reported in this update 🚢130 Iranian vessels destroyed — largest navy elimination since WWII
Sources: Stars & Stripes Signal SCV Washington Examiner
25
Mar
Iran · Missile Barrage Offensive · Ballistic Missiles + Cluster Munitions
Iran Fires 13 Salvos at Israel; Cluster Bomb Munitions Strike Bnei Brak
Iran launched 13 separate missile salvos at Israel throughout Tuesday, March 25. Cluster bomb bomblets impacted across central Israel, with nine people wounded in Bnei Brak. Four people were lightly hurt and buildings damaged by an Iranian missile strike on Tel Aviv. Additional fragments from an Iranian missile fell in an unrecognized Bedouin village, injuring three. An IDF ambulance service reported no direct injuries from a separate Iranian ballistic missile attack targeting central Israel. The IDF stated it struck the 'main' explosives production facility near Isfahan in retaliatory strikes. The IRGC also threatened to launch heavy missile and drone attacks on Israeli military personnel in northern Israel near Gaza unless Israel ceases strikes in Lebanon and Gaza, stating Israel had crossed 'all red lines.'
📍Bnei Brak, Tel Aviv, central Israel; Isfahan (IDF counterstrike) 💀9 wounded in Bnei Brak; 4 lightly hurt in Tel Aviv; 3 injured near Bedouin village 🚀13 Iranian salvos fired at Israel on March 25 alone
Sources: Times of Israel TIME

Mar
IDF · Strike Wave Offensive · IAF Wide-Scale Wave
IAF Strikes IRGC Official in Beirut; IDF Hits 'Main' Explosives Facility Near Isfahan
The Israeli Air Force launched a new wave of strikes in Tehran in the early hours of March 25, targeting Iranian regime infrastructure sites. Separately, the IDF confirmed it struck and killed an IRGC official in Beirut, while also destroying five Hezbollah operatives in southern Lebanon. The IDF also struck what it described as the 'main' explosives production facility near Isfahan. Israeli PM Netanyahu stated that 'there's more to come' regarding strikes on Hezbollah and Iranian infrastructure, and Israel also announced it has now launched attacks against more than 3,000 targets in Iran since the start of the war. IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir previously assessed the campaign against Hezbollah as having 'only just begun' and said the war would go on several more weeks.

Mar
US · Diplomacy Diplomatic · 15-Point Proposal to Iran
Washington Sends Tehran 15 Conditions for War Settlement; US Eyes Direct Contact with Senior Iranian Decision-Maker
The United States confirmed it has transmitted 15 conditions to Iran as the basis for a comprehensive settlement to end the war. Channel 12 reported that Washington is pushing to move beyond indirect contact with Iran and open a direct line to a senior decision-maker in Tehran. Trump stated on Monday that talks had been 'very good and productive' over the prior two days and postponed planned strikes on Iranian power plants for five days pending diplomatic progress, with special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner involved. However, Iran's parliament speaker called reports of talks 'fake news,' and Iranian FM Araghchi told CBS that Iran 'never asked for a ceasefire' and 'never asked even for negotiation.' Trump has called Iran's response a 'very big present' and said negotiations could potentially culminate in a Pakistan-hosted summit within days, per Israeli reporting.
📍Washington D.C. / Tehran (indirect channel); Pakistan proposed as host 🕊️15-point US proposal transmitted; Iran publicly denies talks while backchannel continues ⚡Trump postpones Iranian power plant strikes 5 days pending diplomacy
Sources: Times of Israel Signal SCV Bloomberg
25
Mar
Iran · Dimona / David's Sling Malfunction Offensive · Ballistic Missile Strike on Nuclear-Adjacent Area
David's Sling Malfunction Allows Two Iranian Missiles to Strike Southern Israel; Dimona Area Struck
Israel's military confirmed that a malfunction in the 'David's Sling' aerial interceptor system allowed two Iranian ballistic missiles to strike southern Israel over the weekend (around March 22-23), wounding dozens of people. Separately, at least 180 people were wounded in Iranian missile strikes on the southern city of Dimona — home to Israel's main nuclear facility — and the nearby city of Arad, in one of the most severe single-salvo attacks on Israel since the conflict began. Iranian state television framed the Dimona-area strikes as a 'response' to a US-Israeli attack on the Natanz nuclear enrichment complex. Missile fragments also fell approximately 350 metres from the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound in occupied East Jerusalem, causing extensive damage to the religiously sensitive site.
📍Dimona, Arad (southern Israel); East Jerusalem (Al-Aqsa proximity) 💀180+ wounded near Dimona/Arad; dozens more wounded after David's Sling malfunction ⚠️David's Sling interceptor system malfunction — two ballistic missiles reached southern Israel
Sources: Al Jazeera Al Jazeera tracker Iran International
21
Mar
Iran · Diego Garcia Strike Attempt Offensive · Ballistic Missile — Record Range
Iran Fires Two Ballistic Missiles at Diego Garcia — Longest-Ever Iranian Strike Attempt
Iran launched two intermediate-range ballistic missiles targeting the US-UK joint military base at Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean on March 21, marking the furthest Iranian missile strike ever attempted at approximately 2,500 miles from Iranian territory. One missile failed in-flight; the second was intercepted by US defenses. Neither struck the strategic base — a hub for long-range US bombers, warships, and submarines. The incident shocked analysts by demonstrating that Iranian missiles can reliably reach beyond the 2,000-kilometer range the Iranian regime had long claimed to self-impose, raising new concerns about threats to targets in Europe. The UK described the attempted attack as 'a threat to British interests.'
📍Diego Garcia, Indian Ocean (~2,500 miles from Iran) 💀No casualties — one missile failed in-flight, one intercepted 🚀Longest-ever Iranian missile strike attempt; exceeded self-declared 2,000km limit
Sources: Stars & Stripes Eurasia (CTP-ISW)
24
Mar
Lebanon · Humanitarian Crisis Humanitarian · Mass Displacement Milestone
1.2 Million Displaced in Lebanon as IDF Expands Ground Op; Lebanon Kills 1,072+
The United Nations confirmed that more than 1.2 million people — approximately one in five Lebanese — have been displaced by Israeli military operations since March 2. Lebanon's Ministry of Public Health reported that at least 1,072 people have been killed and 2,966 injured in Israeli strikes since the war with Hezbollah resumed. An Israeli airstrike on a residential apartment in Bchamoun, southeast of Beirut, killed at least three people including a three-year-old girl, and wounded five others, striking an area outside previously issued evacuation zones. Defense Minister Katz announced Israel would install a security zone in southern Lebanon up to the Litani River, and that displaced Lebanese would not be permitted to return south of the Litani until Israel's security objectives are met. As of March 19, approximately 140,000 people had crossed from Lebanon into Syria.
📍Southern Lebanon, Beirut (Bchamoun); Litani River security zone declared 💀1,072+ killed in Lebanon (MoPH); 3 killed in Bchamoun strike incl. 3-yr-old girl 🏚️1.2M+ displaced (UN); 140,000 fled to Syria as of Mar 19 (IRC)
Sources: Al Jazeera IRC UPI

Mar
Iran · Natanz Strike Offensive · Nuclear Site Struck Again
US Strikes Natanz Nuclear Facility Again; Iran Accuses US-Israel of Bombing Bushehr Nuclear Site
The United States conducted another strike on the Natanz nuclear enrichment facility in Isfahan Province on or around March 21-25, with anti-regime media reporting the use of a bunker buster bomb. The UN's nuclear watchdog, the IAEA, said it had been informed by Iran that Natanz was attacked, though it detected no increase in off-site radiation levels. Separately, Iran accused the United States and Israel of bombing the Bushehr nuclear power plant — a facility operated with assistance from Russia's Rosatom. The Bushehr strike claim drew intense international attention given the risk to civilian populations and Russian personnel on-site. Israeli and US officials did not immediately confirm or deny striking Bushehr.
📍Natanz, Isfahan Province; Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant (Iranian allegation) ☢️IAEA: Natanz attacked; no off-site radiation increase detected. Bushehr allegation unconfirmed by US/Israel. 🇷🇺Rosatom personnel remain on-site at Bushehr; Russia previously evacuated ~100 staff
Sources: Stars & Stripes (CENTCOM Day 22) Times of Israel Eurasia (CTP-ISW)

Mar
Iran · Gulf Attacks Offensive · Multi-Country Drone & Missile Barrage
Iran Continues Drone & Missile Strikes Across Gulf: Kuwait Sirens 7x in One Night; Saudi Arabia Intercepts ~20 Drones
Iran sustained a prolonged campaign of drone and missile attacks across Gulf states on and around March 24-25. Kuwait air defenses responded to multiple incoming attacks with sirens sounding at least seven times in a single night. Saudi Arabia intercepted approximately 20 drones targeting its oil-rich Eastern Province. Bahrain's forces reported intercepting 153 missiles and 301 drones since the start of the war. The UAE defense ministry confirmed that a Moroccan contractor working with the Emirati military was killed in Bahrain in an Iranian attack. The UN Human Rights Council announced it would hold an urgent meeting to address Iran's strikes on Gulf countries. Trump's approval rating fell to 36% — a second-term low — attributed in part to rising fuel costs (US national average: $3.97/gal) and growing public opposition to the war.
📍Kuwait, Saudi Arabia (Eastern Province), Bahrain, UAE 💀1 Moroccan contractor killed in Bahrain (UAE defense ministry) 🛢️Brent crude above $100/bbl; US gas avg $3.97/gal; Trump approval at 36%
Sources: Al Jazeera Day 25 TIME

Mar
Iran · Day 25 Civilian Toll Humanitarian · Casualty Update
HRANA: 1,407+ Iranian Civilians Confirmed Killed Including 214 Children; 82,000+ Civilian Structures Damaged
The Human Rights Activists News Agency (HRANA) confirmed at least 1,407 civilian deaths in Iran as of Day 24-25, including at least 214 children. Iran's Health Ministry separately said approximately 210 children have been killed and more than 1,500 people under 18 have been injured. The Iranian Red Crescent Society reported that strikes have damaged or destroyed more than 82,000 civilian structures. Iran's Health Ministry also confirmed that 300 health and emergency facilities were damaged during the fighting, including hospitals, pharmacies, and emergency services. HRANA reported at least 36 civilians killed and 138 injured on Day 24 alone (March 23). Human rights groups warned the true toll may be significantly higher due to communication disruptions and restricted access.
📍Iran-wide (30 of 31 provinces affected per CENTCOM) 💀1,407+ civilians killed (HRANA); 214 children; 82,000+ civilian structures damaged (Red Crescent) 🏥300 health/emergency facilities damaged; 1,500+ under-18s injured (Iran Health Ministry)
Sources: Iran International / HRANA HRANA Day 24 Report Al Jazeera Day 25

Mar
US · Diplomacy Diplomatic · 15-Point Ceasefire Plan Delivered to Iran via Pakistan
US Sends Iran 15-Point Ceasefire Proposal via Pakistan; 82nd Airborne Deploying
The Trump administration submitted a comprehensive 15-point ceasefire framework to Iran through Pakistani intermediaries. The plan covers sanctions relief, civilian nuclear cooperation, full dismantlement of enrichment facilities at Natanz, Isfahan and Fordow, IAEA access, missile program limits, an end to proxy militia support, and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif publicly offered Islamabad as a host for direct US-Iran talks, tagging Trump and Witkoff on X. Iranian officials — including parliament speaker Ghalibaf and an IRGC spokesperson — denied any negotiations were underway and vowed to continue fighting. Simultaneously, the Pentagon ordered 2,000–3,000 paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne Division's Immediate Response Force to deploy to the Middle East within days, supplementing ~50,000 US troops already in the region. Two Marine Expeditionary Units adding ~5,000 Marines are also en route. Israeli officials were reportedly surprised by the diplomatic outreach.
📍Plan transmitted via Islamabad; summit potentially in Pakistan within days 🪖82nd Airborne (2,000–3,000 troops) + 2 Marine Expeditionary Units (~5,000 Marines) deploying
Sources: AP / KSAT NPR Chicago Tribune

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · IRGC Fires Missiles & Drones at Israel, Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain; Kuwait Airport Fuel Tank Ablaze
Iran Fires Dozens of Waves at Israel & Gulf; Drone Ignites Fuel Tank at Kuwait International Airport
Iran's IRGC announced it launched missiles and drones targeting US military bases hosting American forces in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain, as well as 'targets in the heart of the occupied territories' (Israel). In Kuwait, a drone struck a fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport overnight, sparking a large fire. Kuwait's civil aviation authority confirmed 'limited' material damage and no casualties; the Kuwait National Guard intercepted six drones. Saudi Arabia's Defense Ministry reported destroying at least 8 drones in its oil-rich Eastern Province. In Israel, Iran fired at least a dozen salvos throughout the day; three people were wounded in southern Israel and four others suffered minor wounds in Tel Aviv. Iran also launched cluster munitions that struck Bnei Brak, injuring 12 people including six children. The IRGC declared it will continue operations, with its spokesperson mocking US ceasefire efforts as 'negotiating with themselves.'
📍Kuwait Intl Airport (fuel tank fire); Bnei Brak, Tel Aviv, southern Israel; Saudi Eastern Province; Bahrain; Jordan 💀Israel: 3 wounded (south) + 4 minor wounds (Tel Aviv) + 12 injured (Bnei Brak cluster munitions); Kuwait: no casualties (airport)
Sources: Al Arabiya CBS News Jerusalem Post

Mar
IDF · Strike Offensive · IAF Strikes Iranian Production Sites; Submarine R&D Facility in Isfahan Destroyed
IDF Strikes Tehran Regime Infrastructure & Destroys Iran's Only Submarine Development Center in Isfahan
The Israeli Air Force conducted multiple waves of airstrikes in Tehran on March 25 targeting Iranian regime infrastructure sites, including IRGC headquarters and weapons production facilities. The IDF also announced that Iran's sole submarine development facility in the Isfahan area had been bombed — described as 'the only site in Iran responsible for the planning and development of submarines and auxiliary systems for the Iranian navy,' where various models of unmanned vessels were also produced. Separately, the Israeli Navy struck and killed Muhammad Ali Kourani, a Quds Force operative in Beirut who was 'advancing terror attacks directed by Iranian intelligence officials.' Five additional Hezbollah operatives were killed in strikes in southern Lebanon. The IDF also struck Hezbollah fuel stations (Al-Amana front company) across Lebanon to cut the group's weapons logistics chain.

Mar
Iran · Cluster Munitions Offensive · Iran Fires Cluster Munitions at Bnei Brak; Hezbollah Kills Israeli Woman in North
Hezbollah Rocket Kills Nuriel Dubin, 27, in Northern Israel; Iran Cluster Bombs Hit Bnei Brak (12 Injured incl. 6 Children)
On March 24, a 27-year-old woman, Nuriel Dubin — a youth counselor engaged to be married in September 2026 — was killed by a Hezbollah rocket near the Mahanayim Junction in the Galilee. Two others were lightly wounded. Hours later, Iran fired a cluster-munitions-carrying ballistic missile at Bnei Brak in central Israel that was not intercepted. Twelve people including six children and two elderly women were hospitalized with shrapnel injuries; one 23-year-old man was in moderate condition with head and abdominal injuries. Iran fired at least 13 salvos throughout the day. An Iranian missile also struck Tel Aviv, lightly injuring four people and mangling buildings. A Soroka Hospital doctor was seriously wounded near Beersheba by intercept debris.
📍Mahanayim Junction, Galilee (Hezbollah); Bnei Brak, Tel Aviv, Beersheba area (Iran) 💀1 killed (Nuriel Dubin, 27, Hezbollah rocket); 12 injured Bnei Brak cluster munitions; 4 lightly hurt Tel Aviv; 1 doctor seriously wounded Negev

Mar
Lebanon · Diplomacy Diplomatic · Lebanon Expels Iran's Ambassador Persona Non Grata
Lebanon Declares Iranian Ambassador Mohammad Reza Shibani Persona Non Grata, Orders Departure by March 29
Lebanon's Foreign Minister Youssef Raggi formally declared Iranian Ambassador-designate Mohammad Reza Shibani persona non grata on March 24, giving him until March 29 to leave. Lebanon's ambassador to Tehran, Ahmed Sweidan, was also recalled. The decision was framed as a response to Iran violating diplomatic norms and interfering in Lebanese internal affairs, including holding meetings with unofficial Lebanese bodies. Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam has publicly accused Iran's IRGC of directly commanding Hezbollah's military operations from within Lebanon. The Lebanese government did not sever full diplomatic ties. Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar praised the expulsion as a 'justified and necessary step.'
📍Beirut, Lebanon 🏛️Ambassador Shibani ordered out by March 29; Lebanon's envoy to Tehran recalled for consultations

CENTCOM · Milestone Operational Update · CENTCOM: 9,000+ Targets Struck, 140+ Iranian Vessels Destroyed
CENTCOM Day-24 Update: 9,000+ Targets Struck, 9,000+ Combat Flights, 140+ Iranian Naval Vessels Destroyed
In a March 23 operational update, US Central Command confirmed that Operation Epic Fury has surpassed 9,000 targets struck and 9,000 combat flights conducted since February 28. More than 140 Iranian naval vessels have been damaged or destroyed — described as 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' CENTCOM said it is continuing to strike 'targets to dismantle the Iranian regime's security apparatus.' Adm. Brad Cooper noted that Iran's ballistic missile launch rate has fallen by over 90% from Day 1, its tactical fighters are grounded, and its navy is no longer sailing. The IDF separately confirmed striking more than 3,000 Iranian targets since the operation began.

25
Mar
Iran · Diplomacy Diplomatic · Rejection & Counter-Proposal
Iran Rejects US 15-Point Ceasefire Plan; Demands Strait of Hormuz Sovereignty
On March 25, 2026 (Day 26), Iran formally rejected the Trump administration's 15-point ceasefire proposal, which had been transmitted to Tehran via Pakistan. Iran's state broadcaster Press TV, citing a senior political-security official, reported that Tehran had issued a five-point counteroffer demanding: (1) a halt to US–Israeli 'aggression and assassinations'; (2) establishment of mechanisms to prevent resumption of war; (3) payment of war reparations; (4) an end to attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon and pro-Iranian militias in Iraq; and (5) international recognition of Iran's authority over the Strait of Hormuz. An IRGC military spokesperson mocked the US peace effort, saying: 'Don't dress up your defeat as an agreement.' Iran continued to insist no direct or indirect negotiations were underway, despite Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif offering Islamabad as a mediation venue. President Trump told reporters the US was talking to 'the right people' in Iran and that the Iranians were eager for a deal. Israel made no official reaction to the ceasefire proposal, though an unnamed Israeli official told Axios it aligns with Israel's position.
📍Tehran / Islamabad (via Pakistan back-channel) 📋Iran's 5-point counter demands sovereignty over Strait of Hormuz & war reparations
Sources: CNBC TIME AP via Britannica
25
Mar
IDF · Strike Update Offensive · Strategic Shift — Military Industry Targets
IDF Shifts Focus: Strikes Tehran Cruise Missile & Arms Production Sites; 70% of Designated Targets Hit
Israeli defense sources confirmed on March 25, 2026 that the IAF has formally shifted its targeting doctrine away from Iranian regime infrastructure — initially struck to spur a popular uprising — toward weapons production sites and military-industrial assets. Overnight strikes hit military industries and cruise missile production facilities in Tehran, as well as missile manufacturing plants and air defense systems across Iran. Army sources stated that approximately 70% of targets designated at the start of the operation have now been struck, and that the IDF is operating at full available operational capacity. The IDF also reported striking bridges in Lebanon to interdict Hezbollah weapons transfers southward, alongside financial entities and fuel stations tied to Hezbollah's economic infrastructure. On the Lebanese front, the IDF said it had eliminated approximately 700 Hezbollah terrorists and that Hezbollah continues to sustain a rate of roughly 100 rocket launches per day. IRGC air defense units separately claimed to have shot down multiple intruding drones over Khuzestan province, including two Hermes drones over Andimeshk County, with Iran's total hostile drone interception count surpassing 130.
📍Tehran (military-industrial zones), southern Lebanon (bridges & Hezbollah infrastructure), Khuzestan (drone intercepts) 💀IDF: ~700 Hezbollah terrorists eliminated to date; 70% of Iran target set struck
Sources: Haaretz Israel Hayom Press TV
25
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Ballistic Missile Strike on Central Israel
Iran Fires Ballistic Missiles at Central Israel; IDF Detects Incoming Attack
On March 25, 2026, Iranian forces launched a ballistic missile attack targeting central Israel, with the IDF detecting the incoming barrage and activating air defenses. The Israeli ambulance service (Magen David Adom) reported no direct injuries from the missile impact in central Israel, though the IDF confirmed the attack. Fragments of a separate Iranian missile fell in an unrecognized Bedouin village in Israel, injuring three people. Four additional people were lightly injured and buildings were damaged in Tel Aviv from a separate Iranian missile attack the same day. Iran has now fired at Israel in what Israeli broadcaster Kan 11 described as at least 13 salvos throughout the previous day (March 24–25). Iran's military stated its forces have carried out over 80 waves of retaliatory missile and drone strikes against US interests and Israeli positions since the start of the war.

Mar
US · Diplomacy & Deployment Diplomatic + Military · Ceasefire Outreach & Troop Movement
US Sends 15-Point Iran Peace Plan via Pakistan; Begins Moving Paratroopers to Middle East
The United States sent a 15-point ceasefire proposal to Iran via Pakistan as a back-channel intermediary, according to Associated Press reporting on March 25, 2026. The proposal reportedly addresses Iran's ballistic missile and nuclear programs and touches on maritime routes including the Strait of Hormuz. Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif offered Islamabad as a neutral venue for talks. Simultaneously, the US began moving parachute troops to the Middle East to supplement a contingent of Marines already heading to the region. Channel 12, citing three Western sources, reported the Trump administration proposed a one-month ceasefire to allow negotiation of final terms, similar to the Gaza deal framework. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed that while negotiations are being explored, the military mission 'continues unabated.' Iran's IRGC military spokesperson rejected the overture, and Iran's five-point counter-proposal demanded sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and war reparations. NPR reported that Trump's stated war objectives have shifted over time — from 'unconditional surrender' toward a position of US dominance regardless of formal Iranian capitulation.
📍Washington D.C. / Islamabad (Pakistan back-channel); US paratroopers deploying to undisclosed Middle East location 📋15-point US plan transmitted via Pakistan; Iran rejected; US deploying additional ground forces to region
Sources: Washington Post TIME NPR

Mar
Iran · Diplomacy Diplomatic · Rejection
Iran Rejects 15-Point US Ceasefire Plan; Offers 5 Counter-Conditions
Iran formally rejected the US-backed 15-point ceasefire proposal transmitted via Pakistan, with Iranian state broadcaster Press TV citing an anonymous official confirming the rejection. Iran simultaneously published its own five counter-conditions: a full halt to US and Israeli 'aggression and assassinations'; establishment of mechanisms guaranteeing the war does not resume; payment of war damages and reparations to Iran; an end to all attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon and pro-Iranian militias in Iraq; and international recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz. Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi denied any formal negotiations were underway, characterising the message exchanges as 'not negotiations or dialogue.' An Iranian military spokesman mocked the US proposal, questioning Washington's credibility. Israel expressed concern that Washington might unilaterally declare a one-month ceasefire to facilitate talks.
📍Tehran, Iran / Islamabad back-channel 📋Iran's 5 counter-demands include war reparations and Strait of Hormuz sovereignty recognition
Sources: Time NPR CNN

Iran · Strike Offensive · Ballistic Missile Attack
IRGC Fires Multi-Wave Ballistic Missiles & Drones at Central Israel; Tel Aviv Hit
The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps conducted multi-wave missile and drone operations targeting sites across Israel on the night of March 24–25, with widespread alerts across central and southern regions. The IRGC's Khatam al-Anbiya headquarters spokesperson confirmed the strikes were part of ongoing offensive-defensive measures. Israeli media reported that a rocket strike on Tel Aviv caused nine initial casualties, with damage observed in several urban locations. Iran also simultaneously launched missile and drone attacks against Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia and the US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain as part of what it described as a coordinated offensive. Israel maintained heightened alert status and deployed additional air-defense assets.

US · Deployment Logistics · Force Posture
US Begins Moving 82nd Airborne Paratroopers to Middle East Amid Stalled Peace Talks
The United States began repositioning 82nd Airborne Division paratroopers toward the Middle East to back up a contingent of Marines already heading to the region, according to the Washington Post. The deployment occurred simultaneously with transmission of the 15-point ceasefire proposal to Iran via Pakistan. The move signals that Washington is preparing contingency options even as it pursues a diplomatic track, and suggests the possibility of expanded ground presence is being considered if conditions deteriorate or ceasefire negotiations collapse entirely.

Israel · Strike Offensive · Targeting Shift
IDF Shifts Strikes from Regime Targets to Weapons Production & Military Assets
Israeli defense sources confirmed on March 25 that strikes targeting Iran's regime infrastructure have been sidelined in recent days in favor of a systematic campaign against weapons production sites, military-industrial assets, and the remaining ballistic missile array. IDF strikes hit a turbine engine production site in Qom province used for IRGC drone and aircraft components, along with five near-simultaneous strikes against western Iran's ballistic missile array, eliminating a large number of personnel. Multiple Iranian Air Force bases were targeted in Dezful and Isfahan. A UAV production facility in Isfahan was also destroyed. IDF Chief of Staff Eyal Zamir said the campaign is at the 'halfway' stage, with thousands of additional targets ahead. Israel has reportedly hit at least 77% of 107 tunnel entrances to Iran's underground missile facilities.
📍Qom (turbine plant), western Iran (missile array), Dezful & Isfahan (air bases), Natanz (enrichment) 🎯70%+ of designated targets struck; 77% of 107 tunnel entrances to underground missile sites hit (CNN/Israel)

Mar
Iran · Kharg Island Defensive · Force Repositioning
Iran Fortifies Kharg Island; Charges Fee for Strait of Hormuz Passage
US intelligence reporting, cited by CNN, revealed that Iran has been laying traps and moving military personnel and air defense systems to Kharg Island in preparation for a possible US operation to seize control of the critical oil-export hub. The island handles 90% of Iran's oil exports. Separately, Iran announced it would charge countries a fee for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, with tracking data showing multiple commercial vessels transiting the strait following the offer. US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent described Washington as 'fine' with Iranian-controlled tanker traffic currently permitted through the strait, including Chinese tankers. Over a dozen countries have called for the strait to be reopened, though none has pledged military assets to enforce free passage.
📍Kharg Island (Persian Gulf); Strait of Hormuz ⚠️Iran deploying air defenses & traps on Kharg; charging commercial passage fee through Hormuz
Sources: CNN Signal SCV / CENTCOM

Lebanon · Ground Ops Offensive · Ground Operations
IDF Intensifies Lebanon Ground Ops; 1.2 Million Displaced; Bchamoun Strike Kills 3
Israeli forces continued to expand ground operations in southern Lebanon, with Defense Minister Katz announcing on March 24 a 'security zone' stretching to the Litani River and ordering the acceleration of demolition of Lebanese border-area houses. An Israeli airstrike on a residential apartment in Bchamoun — approximately 10km southeast of Beirut and outside designated evacuation zones — killed at least three people including a three-year-old girl and wounded five others with no prior warning issued. The UN confirmed more than 1.2 million people have been displaced, representing one in five Lebanese residents. Lebanese authorities report 1,072 killed and 2,966 wounded since March 2. IRGC warned it would launch 'heavy missile and drone strikes' on Israeli forces if attacks on Lebanese civilians continued. IDF announced it killed Abu Khalil Barji, Radwan Unit special forces commander, in Majdal Selm.

Simultaneously, the IRGC issued a stark public warning against any US-Israeli ground incursion into Iranian territory, directly addressing the American people in a statement reading: 'Don't send your children to hell with the deceit of Netanyahu and Trump.' Earlier, the Israeli Air Force said it had completed an intelligence-guided overnight strike wave targeting weapons production sites in the Tehran area, hitting several production facilities for air and sea weaponry. In parallel, Israeli forces struck Iranian air defense systems and a missile launcher position. Diplomacy · Deadlocked Political · Peace Proposal Rejected
Iran Publicly Rejects Trump's 15-Point Peace Plan; Counters with 5 Conditions Including Hormuz Sovereignty
On March 25 (Day 25), Iran publicly rejected the United States' 15-point ceasefire and war-termination proposal, which had been delivered to Tehran via Pakistan as intermediary. Iranian state broadcaster Press TV cited an anonymous official confirming the rejection. Iran simultaneously released its own five-point counter-proposal, which demands: a full halt to US-Israeli 'aggression and assassinations'; enforceable mechanisms guaranteeing the war will not resume; payment of war damages and reparations; a comprehensive end to attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon and pro-Iranian militias in Iraq; and international recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi denied any direct negotiations were taking place, characterizing message exchanges via mediators as not constituting talks, and saying Washington's shift toward dialogue amounted to 'an admission of defeat.' IRGC military spokesperson Lt. Col. Ebrahim Zolfaghari mocked the US proposal on state television. Trump said the US was talking to 'the right people' in Iran and that Iranians were eager for a deal.
📍Mediated via Pakistan; messages also relayed through Egypt and Turkey per reports 📋Iran's 5 conditions include war reparations and sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz 🗣️Araghchi: message exchanges 'do not mean negotiations'; calls US tone 'an admission of defeat'

CENTCOM: 10,000+ Targets Struck; Two-Thirds of Iran's Arms Manufacturing Destroyed
On March 25 (Day 25), CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper announced that US forces have now struck over 10,000 targets in Iran since the start of Operation Epic Fury — up from the 9,000+ figure reported days prior. Cooper also stated that the strikes have destroyed two-thirds of Iran's military production facilities. The coalition has conducted over 8,000 combat flights, maintains full air superiority over Iran, and has sunk or severely damaged approximately 140 Iranian naval vessels — which Cooper has described as 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' Iranian missile launches have fallen by approximately 90% and drone launches by 83% from Day 1 rates, according to CENTCOM.

Lebanon · Buffer Zone Political · Israeli Ground Policy
Israel Announces Security Buffer Zone in Southern Lebanon; 1M+ Displaced, 1,000+ Killed
On March 25 (Day 25), Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz announced that Israel will establish a security buffer zone across large parts of southern Lebanon stretching to the Litani River, roughly 30 km north of the Israeli border. Katz stated that hundreds of thousands of displaced Shiite residents of southern Lebanon 'will not return south of the Litani River until the security of northern residents is ensured.' He also confirmed that all bridges over the Litani used by Hezbollah to transport weapons and fighters have been destroyed, and that Israeli forces will 'control the remaining bridges and the security zone up to the Litani.' Lebanese President Joseph Aoun condemned the buffer zone as collective punishment. The UN Secretary-General's spokesperson confirmed that more than 1.2 million people — approximately one in five Lebanese — have been displaced since the conflict began on March 2. Lebanon's Health Ministry puts the death toll at over 1,072. Israel has also ordered the acceleration of the demolition of Lebanese border villages, citing the Beit Hanoun and Rafah models.

Tangsiri was the senior IRGC officer responsible for Iran's chokehold operations in the Strait of Hormuz, overseeing the blockade of commercial shipping and the naval campaign against coalition and Gulf-state vessels. The IDF also completed a new wave of extensive strikes in Isfahan targeting Iranian regime infrastructure, continuing a 48-hour surge of targeting against Iran's arms factories ordered by Prime Minister Netanyahu ahead of any potential ceasefire. On March 26, 2026, Iran launched at least seven successive ballistic missile salvos targeting central Israel, with sirens sounding across central Israel and the outskirts of Jerusalem throughout the day. The IDF reported that some missiles were intercepted by air defenses while others caused impact, with rescue forces responding to reports of a strike in central Israel possibly caused by falling fragments. A projectile also struck the Arab-Israeli city of Kfar Qassem. At least 11 people were injured in the successive Iranian salvos combined with a Hezbollah drone attack during the same period. The strikes come even as Trump publicly insisted Tehran is eager to make a deal, a claim Iranian officials flatly denied. CENTCOM noted that Iranian launch rates have dropped dramatically — down roughly 90% from Day 1 — but Iran's remaining capabilities continue to threaten Israeli population centers.

Diplomacy · Trump vs. Reality Diplomatic · Contradictory Signals
Trump Claims Iran 'Eager to Deal' as Tehran Rejects Plan and Demands Hormuz Sovereignty
On the morning of March 26, 2026, President Trump publicly insisted that Iran is eager to make a deal, even as Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi flatly rejected the US 15-point ceasefire proposal and declared Tehran would end the war only on 'our own terms.' Araghchi, in a televised interview on IRIB, mocked Washington's shift toward negotiation language, stating: 'Didn't the Americans say unconditional surrender? So why are they talking about negotiation now?' He characterized messages exchanged through mediating nations — including Pakistan — as an 'exchange of messages,' not negotiations, and framed Washington's pivot as 'an admission of defeat.' Iran's five conditions for ending the war include: a full halt to US-Israeli aggression; concrete guarantees against recurrence; payment of war damages and reparations; a comprehensive end to the war across all fronts including Hezbollah and Iraqi militias; and international recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz. Iran is also reportedly advancing a parliamentary plan to formally codify Hormuz revenue collection — a direct violation of international maritime law according to the Gulf Cooperation Council. Israel's UN Ambassador Danny Danon separately stated that Israel is not part of US-Iran talks and that military operations will continue until Iran's nuclear and missile capabilities are fully eliminated.
🕊️US 15-point plan delivered via Pakistan — rejected by Iran as 'excessive' ⚠️Iran's 5 conditions include Hormuz sovereignty — GCC calls current fee-charging a violation of international law 🇮🇱Israel's UN envoy: Israel not party to US-Iran talks; operations continue until full capability elimination

As of March 26, 2026, the Times of Israel reported that the US is weighing 'final blow' options that could involve deploying troops into Iran. The Pentagon is reportedly planning to deploy elements of the 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East. Iranian officials responded by increasing military defenses on Kharg Island — which handles approximately 90% of Iran's crude oil exports — in anticipation of a possible US operation to seize the territory, according to sources familiar with US intelligence reporting cited by CNN. Iran has been laying traps, moving IRGC personnel, and boosting air defenses on the island. The IRGC issued a stark public warning against any ground incursion: 'The invading soldiers will be deeply drowned and disappear in the million-strong sea of the Iranian nation.' A Pakistani official separately confirmed that Israel had backed off targeting Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Qalibaf at Pakistan's and the US's request, as both figures are considered key to any diplomatic resolution.

The UN Secretary-General's spokesperson confirmed that more than 1.2 million people, roughly one in five Lebanese, have been displaced. Iran formally rejected the US 15-point ceasefire proposal — transmitted via Pakistan — on March 25–26, publicly calling the plan 'extremely maximalist and unreasonable.' Iran issued its own five-condition counter-proposal: a halt to US-Israeli 'aggression and assassinations'; guarantees preventing the war's resumption; payment of war reparations; a comprehensive end to attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon and Iraqi militias; and international recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced the launch of its 82nd wave of retaliatory operations — dubbed the most extensive in scale since the war began — using a combined force of missiles, suicide drones, and proxy launches. According to IRGC statements, fighters from across the Axis of Resistance conducted 230 total operations in the preceding 24 hours: Iranian Armed Forces carried out 110 direct missile and drone strikes, Lebanese Hezbollah launched 87 operations, and the Iraqi Islamic Resistance conducted 23 offensives. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz stated that displaced Lebanese residents south of this line will not be permitted to return until Hezbollah no longer poses a threat to northern Israel. IDF · Targeted Killing Offensive · IRGC Navy Commander Eliminated in Bandar Abbas
IDF Kills IRGC Navy Chief Alireza Tangsiri & Intelligence Head Behnam Rezaei in Bandar Abbas Strike
In a precision overnight airstrike on Bandar Abbas in the early hours of March 27 (local time), the Israeli Air Force eliminated Admiral Alireza Tangsiri, the eight-year commander of Iran's IRGC Navy, along with Behnam Rezaei, the IRGC-N Intelligence Division chief, and other senior naval officers. Israel's Defense Minister Israel Katz confirmed the killing, calling Tangsiri 'the man directly responsible for the terrorist operation of mining and blocking the Strait of Hormuz.' CENTCOM confirmed the death hours later, with Admiral Brad Cooper stating the elimination 'makes the region safer' and noting that since Operation Epic Fury launched, '92% of the large ships in the Iranian Navy have been eliminated.' Prime Minister Netanyahu hailed the strike, noting Tangsiri 'led the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and had a great deal of blood on his hands.' Iran had not publicly commented as of the time of reporting.

IDF · Air Superiority Offensive · 60-Jet Wave — Parchin, Isfahan, Tehran Arms Industry Blitz
IDF 48-Hour Blitz: 60 Jets Drop 150+ Bombs on Parchin, Isfahan & Tehran Weapons Sites Ahead of March 27 Deadline
With Trump's March 27 energy-strikes deadline looming and ceasefire talks yielding no agreement, Prime Minister Netanyahu ordered the IDF to destroy as much of Iran's arms industry as possible within 48 hours. On the night of March 25–26, 60 IAF fighter jets dropped more than 150 bombs on weapons manufacturing facilities near Tehran and in central Iran. In the Parchin area east of Tehran, the IDF struck 'the Iranian regime's most central facility for the production of missiles and defense systems,' including a site for developing air defense systems, a warhead casting and filling plant, and a solid-fuel mixing facility. In Isfahan, the IAF struck multiple IRGC Quds Force weapons manufacturing sites, an air defense production facility, and — in a separate confirmed operation — Iran's sole submarine research and development center, described by the IDF as 'the only site in Iran responsible for the planning and development of submarines and auxiliary systems for the Iranian navy.' IDF Spokesman Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin stated the military had now struck more than 1,000 targets in Iran's weapons production industries and vowed eliminations would not stop.
📍Parchin (east Tehran), Isfahan Province, central Tehran — multiple arms industry complexes 💥Iran's sole submarine development center destroyed; Parchin central missile production facility heavily struck; 60 IAF jets, 150+ munitions

US · Diplomacy Diplomatic · Trump Extends Energy-Strike Pause to April 6; Witkoff Confirms 15-Point Plan Delivered via Pakistan
Trump Extends Iran Energy-Plant Strike Pause 10 Days to April 6; Iran Responds to 15-Point Proposal Through Intermediaries
On March 26, President Trump announced via Truth Social a second extension of his pause on strikes targeting Iran's energy infrastructure, pushing the new deadline to Monday, April 6, 2026, at 8 p.m. Eastern Time. Trump stated the pause was extended 'as per Iranian Government request,' adding that talks are 'ongoing' and 'going very well.' Speaking at a Cabinet meeting, Special Envoy Steve Witkoff confirmed the US had presented Iran with a '15-point action list that forms the framework for a peace deal,' delivered through Pakistan as lead intermediary, with Egypt and Turkey also playing supporting roles. Witkoff said Iran had 'repeatedly rebuffed on everything we asked for' but expressed cautious optimism. A source with knowledge of the mediation said that despite public rejections, Iranian officials have signaled interest in negotiations, though Iran's top leadership had yet to formally decide. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi publicly stated Tehran would continue 'resistance' and did not intend to negotiate, while Tasnim news agency reported Iran had responded to the 15-point plan through intermediaries and was awaiting Washington's reply. Brent crude closed at $108.01/barrel; markets fell 1.74%.
📍Washington D.C. / Islamabad (Pakistan mediation) / Tehran 🕊️New deadline: April 6, 2026, 8 p.m. ET; Iran reviewing 15-point US proposal; no deal reached
Sources: CNBC CBS News Axios Al Jazeera
26
Mar
Lebanon · IDF Ops Offensive · Lebanon Death Toll Hits 1,116; IDF Golani Brigade Advances; Hezbollah Fires on Nahariya
Lebanon Death Toll Rises to 1,116; IDF Golani Brigade Expands Ground Ops; Hezbollah Strikes Nahariya
Israel's ongoing ground and air campaign in Lebanon continued on March 26, with the Lebanese Ministry of Health reporting that Israeli strikes have killed 1,116 people and injured 3,229 others since March 2, including at least 121 children and 42 health workers. IDF Golani Brigade troops were reported operating in southern Lebanon, with Israel striking Hezbollah financial infrastructure including fuel stations belonging to the Al-Amana front company. The IDF also struck bridges to prevent weapons transfers southward. Hezbollah continued firing rockets at northern Israeli towns including Nahariya, with first responders treating casualties. The 2026 Lebanon war has displaced more than 1 million people — approximately one-fifth of Lebanon's population — according to the Lebanese government and UN. IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir separately warned government ministers that the IDF is under severe strain due to manpower shortages and expanded operational demands across multiple fronts.
📍Southern Lebanon, Beirut suburbs, Nahariya (Israel), Kfar Roummane 💀1,116 killed / 3,229 wounded in Lebanon (Health Ministry, Mar 26); 1M+ displaced
Sources: CNN Israel Hayom Al Jazeera Live Tracker
27
Mar
Iran · Wave 83 Offensive · Missile & Drone Barrage
IRGC Launches 83rd Wave (Op. True Promise 4): Ashdod Oil Depot, Al-Dhafra, Ali Al-Salem Targeted
In the early hours of Friday, March 27, the IRGC announced the execution of Wave 83 of Operation True Promise 4, employing long-range and medium-range ballistic missiles, precision multi-warhead missiles, and suicide/loitering drones against US and Israeli targets across the region. Stated targets included oil storage tanks and the fuel depot at Ashdod port in Israel, a military personnel site at the Modi'in settlement, a US military information exchange center, American bases at Al-Dhafra (UAE) and Al-Udeiri (Saudi Arabia), maintenance/storage hangars for transport aircraft and drones at Ali Al-Salem Air Base (Kuwait), jet fuel tanks for US aircraft, and the Patriot missile system maintenance hangar at Sheikh Isa Base (Bahrain). The IRGC also separately confirmed the Iranian Army launched a 'massive drone attack' targeting the Haifa port naval vessel manufacturing and maintenance center and fuel storage for Israeli warplanes on Wednesday night. The IRGC said the Axis of Resistance conducted 230 total operations in the prior 24 hours — described as the 'most extensive' against US and Israeli positions since the war began.
📍Ashdod (Israel), Modi'in (Israel), Al-Dhafra (UAE), Ali Al-Salem (Kuwait), Sheikh Isa (Bahrain), Al-Udeiri (Saudi Arabia), Haifa Port (Israel) 💀Casualties not yet confirmed; no Israeli mass-casualty reports from this wave at time of filing
Sources: ANI / Press TV via Tribune India NewsFIRST Press TV / GlobalSecurity
27
Mar
IDF · Overnight Blitz Offensive · Wide-Scale Air Strikes
IAF Strikes Iran's Primary Naval Missile & Sea Mine Factory in Yazd; Dozens of Weapons Sites Hit Across Tehran & Western Iran
Overnight into Friday, March 27, the Israeli Air Force completed a wide-scale wave of strikes 'in the heart of Iran,' targeting ballistic missile production sites, aerial defense systems, and IRGC weapons manufacturing infrastructure across Tehran and western Iran. In Tehran, the IAF struck dozens of weapons manufacturing sites including a military base used to store anti-aircraft systems, a production site for key ballistic missile components, a battery manufacturing site for various weapons systems, and an IRGC weapons production facility. In western Iran (Kermanshah/Dezful area), the IAF struck IRGC fire arrays throughout the night, hitting launchers and missile storage sites. In a separate highlighted operation, the IAF — guided by Military Intelligence and the Navy's Intelligence Directorate — struck what the IDF described as Iran's 'most central' facility for the production of missiles and naval mines, located in Yazd. The site was used for the planning, development, assembly, and storage of advanced missiles launched from cruise platforms, submarines, and helicopters against maritime targets. The IDF said the strike dealt 'a significant blow to the production capabilities of the naval forces.' The IAF has now dropped nearly 15,000 munitions on Iran since Operation Roaring Lion began February 28, rising to over 25,000 when combined with US strikes under Operation Epic Fury.
📍Tehran (weapons manufacturing & IRGC sites), Western Iran / Kermanshah, Dezful (fire arrays), Yazd (naval missile & sea mine production facility) 💀Iranian IRGC soldiers operating at launch sites reported killed in western Iran strikes (IDF statement)
Sources: Times of Israel Jerusalem Post Fox News
27
Mar
IDF · Escalation Warning Diplomatic-Military · Threat of Expansion
Defense Minister Katz: IDF Will 'Escalate and Expand' Strikes on Iran to New Targets & Domains
Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz announced on Friday, March 27, in a formal situational assessment with military officials, that the IDF would intensify and expand its strikes on Iran in direct response to Tehran's continued ballistic missile fire against Israeli civilian population centers. Katz stated: 'The prime minister and I warned the Iranian terror regime to stop the missile fire toward the civilian population in Israel. Despite the warnings, the fire continues, and therefore IDF strikes in Iran will intensify and expand to additional targets and domains that assist the regime in building and operating weapons against Israeli civilians.' The warning came amid a joint Iranian-Hezbollah missile barrage triggering sirens in Tel Aviv, northern, central, and southern Israel, and the West Bank. An Israeli missile was intercepted by air defenses; one fell in an open area in central Israel. The IDF reported no mass casualties from the morning barrage. On the same day, an IDF officer and soldier were seriously wounded by a friendly-fire grenade accident during an encounter with Hezbollah operatives in southern Lebanon.
📍Jerusalem (IDF HQ announcement); impacts reported Tel Aviv, Netanya, central & southern Israel, West Bank 💀No mass-casualty reports from Mar 27 barrage; 2 IDF troops seriously wounded in Lebanon grenade accident
Sources: Haaretz Times of Israel India TV News
27
Mar
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Stalemate Diplomatic · Talks / Counter-Proposals
Pakistan Confirms Indirect US-Iran Talks as Tehran Rejects 15-Point Plan, Issues 5-Point Counter; Trump Extends Energy-Strike Pause to April 6
Pakistan's Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar publicly confirmed on March 26–27 that indirect US-Iran ceasefire talks are ongoing through Pakistan as the primary channel, with Egypt and Turkey also playing supporting roles — the first time Islamabad officially acknowledged its mediating role. The US 15-point peace plan — delivered via Pakistan — demands Iran commit to never pursuing nuclear weapons, dismantle nuclear capabilities, cap missile arsenal, and reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for sanctions relief. Iran formally rejected the plan as 'extremely maximalist,' with Foreign Minister Araghchi stating 'there are no negotiations underway' and 'we do not want a ceasefire.' Tehran issued its own five-point counter-proposal demanding: an end to all aggression, concrete guarantees against future war, war reparations from the US, Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and a comprehensive end to the war across all fronts including against Hezbollah. Trump extended his pause on energy-plant strikes by 10 days to April 6 at 8 PM Eastern Time, citing the Iranian request and claiming talks are 'going very well.' Trump separately revealed Iran had allowed at least eight oil tankers (reportedly Pakistani-flagged) through the Strait as a gesture of good faith. Despite the diplomatic activity, both US/Israeli strikes and Iranian missile waves continued unabated. The Pentagon is considering deploying an additional 10,000 ground troops to the Middle East.
📍Islamabad (Pakistan mediation), Tehran (Iranian counter-proposal), Washington (Trump Truth Social post), G7 France (Rubio) 💀N/A — diplomatic track; kinetic operations continue on all fronts simultaneously
Sources: LiveNOW Fox NPR CNN CS Monitor
27
Mar
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · Displacement & Casualties
UNICEF: 370,000+ Lebanese Children Displaced; 121 Children Killed; Lebanon Declares Iranian Ambassador Persona Non Grata
UNICEF's representative in Lebanon reported on Friday, March 27, that more than 370,000 children have been forcibly displaced across Lebanon amid the Israeli offensive against Hezbollah, with at least 121 children killed and 399 injured. Total displacement in Lebanon has surpassed 1 million people — roughly 20% of the country's entire population — according to Lebanese government figures. As of March 21, Lebanese authorities reported 1,024 people killed, 2,740 injured, and approximately 1.2 million displaced. Separately, the Lebanese government declared the Iranian ambassador persona non grata, ordering his departure by Sunday March 29; Hezbollah and Amal ministers boycotted the subsequent cabinet session in protest. Netanyahu announced IDF forces would advance further into south Lebanon 'to establish a broader buffer zone,' with Defense Minister Katz stating the zone would extend to the Litani River. Israeli overnight airstrikes also hit the Burj al-Barajneh neighborhood of Beirut's southern suburbs on March 27.
📍Southern Lebanon, Beirut southern suburbs (Burj al-Barajneh), Litani River zone, Lebanese-Iranian diplomatic row 💀1,024+ Lebanese killed, 2,740+ wounded, 1 million+ displaced (Lebanese authorities, as of Mar 21); 370,000+ children displaced (UNICEF, Mar 27); 121 children killed
Sources: Al-Monitor / Reuters Arab News / Arab News Japan The National

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Wave Strike
IRGC Launches 83rd Wave: Haifa Port, Gulf Bases, Israeli & US Sites Struck
The IRGC announced the 83rd wave of Operation True Promise 4 early Friday, March 27, employing a mix of long-range and medium-range missiles and loitering drones. Declared targets included oil storage tanks and a fuel depot in Ashdod, a military personnel facility in the Modi'in settlement, and a U.S. military information exchange center. U.S. bases at Al-Dhafra and Al-Udeiri were also listed, along with aircraft maintenance and storage hangars at Ali Al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait and fuel storage and Patriot missile system maintenance facilities at Sheikh Isa Air Base in Bahrain. Separately, the Iranian Army claimed a massive drone attack against strategic and sensitive sites in Israel's Haifa port, targeting what it described as naval vessel manufacturing and maintenance facilities and fuel storage for warplanes. The IRGC stated that Axis of Resistance fighters conducted 230 total operations in the preceding 24 hours — described as the most extensive in scale — including 87 Hezbollah operations, 23 Iraqi Islamic Resistance strikes, and 110 Iranian missile and drone strikes.
📍Ashdod (Israel), Al-Dhafra (UAE), Ali Al-Salem & Sheikh Isa (Kuwait/Bahrain), Haifa port (Israel) 💀Casualties not confirmed by coalition; Iran declared strike impact on Israeli civilian shelter routines
