Mar
IAF · Nuclear & Industrial Strikes Offensive · Strategic Strikes
IAF Bombs Arak Heavy Water Reactor, Yazd Uranium Plant & Two Major Steel Plants
On the morning of March 27 (Day 28), the Israeli Air Force struck Iran's Arak heavy water reactor following an evacuation warning to civilians in the area. The IDF cited 'repeated reconstruction attempts by the Iranian terror regime' as justification, noting the facility had previously been struck during the June 2025 Twelve-Day War. Shortly after, the IAF also struck a uranium extraction and processing facility in Yazd and Ardakan, which the IDF described as 'the only one of its kind where raw materials are processed for uranium enrichment,' stating the strike 'deprives the Iranian terror regime of the raw material needed for uranium enrichment processes.' Israel additionally confirmed strikes on two of Iran's largest steel plants, which it said have links to the IRGC and the Iranian military-industrial sector. Iran threatened retaliatory strikes on Israeli and Gulf steel plants in response, naming the Yehuda Group facility in Ashdod and five others across the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Qatar, and Bahrain. Strikes were also reported in the heart of Tehran targeting command and weapons sites. The IDF stated it intercepted over 90% of drones launched by Iran and Hezbollah.
📍Arak (Khondab heavy water reactor), Yazd/Ardakan (uranium plant), Tehran (command centers), IRGC-linked steel plants 💀At least 4 civilians killed at targeted sites including 2 at a cement plant in Firouzabad; no casualties reported at Arak per Iranian media
Sources: CNN Haaretz Times of Israel
27
Mar
Diplomacy · G7 & Iran Counter-Proposal Diplomatic · Multilateral
G7 Foreign Ministers Demand End to Civilian Attacks; Iran Counter-Proposal Expected; Trump Extends Energy Strike Pause to April 6
G7 foreign ministers, meeting at the Abbaye des Vaux-de-Cernay in France on March 26–27, issued a joint statement calling for an immediate halt to attacks on civilians and critical infrastructure in Iran, warning there is 'no justification for the deliberate targeting of civilians.' Secretary of State Marco Rubio attended but dismissed suggestions that U.S. policy would be shaped by its allies. Separately, a source briefed on the matter told the Times of Israel that Iran's counter-proposal to the U.S. 15-point ceasefire plan was expected to arrive on March 27. Trump administration officials said the White House had been told via interlocutors that Iran's response would likely come that day. Trump extended his pause on strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure by 10 days to April 6, writing on Truth Social: 'Talks are ongoing and... they are going very well.' Iran had previously rejected the 15-point plan outright and issued a 5-point counter-demand that included retaining its Strait of Hormuz stranglehold and receiving war reparations — both widely seen as nonstarters for Washington. Pakistan continued to serve as the primary diplomatic intermediary.
📍G7 meeting: Abbaye des Vaux-de-Cernay, France; Iran counter-proposal via Pakistan channel 🕊️Trump energy strike pause extended to April 6, 2026 at 8PM ET; Iran's formal counter-proposal expected same day
Sources: Haaretz CNN (Day 26) NPR PBS NewsHour

Mar
IDF · Lebanon Escalation Offensive · Beirut Airstrikes
IAF Launches New Wave of Strikes on Hezbollah Infrastructure in Beirut; UNHCR Warns of 'Humanitarian Catastrophe' with 1.2M+ Displaced
The Israeli Air Force launched a new wave of strikes against Hezbollah infrastructure sites in Beirut's Dahiyeh southern suburbs on March 27, the IDF announced without providing immediate further details. The strikes followed weeks of sustained bombardment and a growing ground presence south of the Litani River. The UN Refugee Agency (UNHCR) warned on Friday that Lebanon faces a 'humanitarian catastrophe,' with more than 1.2 million people — roughly one in five Lebanese — forced from their homes since Israel's intensified campaign began in early March. UNHCR's Lebanon representative Karolina Lindholm Billing noted that Israeli strikes and forced displacement orders have affected people across the country from southern Lebanon to the Bekaa Valley, Beirut, and further north, and that the destruction of key bridges has cut off entire districts. Lebanon's Culture Minister described the situation as an 'invasion' and said the government would file a complaint with the UN Security Council. The Lebanese Health Ministry's death toll in Lebanon stands at over 1,116 killed; the war has also wounded at least 3,229 people.
📍Dahiyeh (southern Beirut), southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley; 1.2M+ displaced across Lebanon 💀1,116+ killed in Lebanon (Health Ministry); 3,229+ wounded; 121 children killed (UNICEF)
Sources: Al Jazeera Times of Israel Euronews

Mar
Iran · Warning Threat · Civilian Evacuation Notice
IRGC Issues Evacuation Warning to Civilians Near U.S. Forces; Iranian Parliament Speaker Warns of Amphibious Threat
Iran's IRGC issued a statement on March 27 warning civilians in the region to evacuate areas near U.S. military positions, accusing American forces of 'hiding in hotels and civilian areas as a protective shield' — a warning that implicitly threatened strikes on civilian-adjacent sites hosting U.S. personnel. The statement read: 'Since it is our duty to eliminate the terrorist forces of the U.S. and the usurping (Zionist) regime...wherever we find them, we recommend that you urgently leave the locations of the American forces to avoid any harm coming to you.' Separately, Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf issued a veiled warning on social media in response to reports of additional U.S. amphibious warships and thousands of Marines being deployed to the region, questioning whether the U.S. could protect its forward-deployed forces.
📍Region-wide; directed at civilians near U.S. military sites in Gulf states and Israel ⚠️No confirmed strikes from this warning at time of publication; thousands of additional U.S. Marines en route per reports
Sources: Time CNN

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Wave 84 — Al-Kharj Base Strike
IRGC Launches 84th Wave; Claims Destruction of US Tanker Fleet at Prince Sultan Air Base
The IRGC announced the 84th wave of Operation True Promise 4 on March 27, describing it as a 'special joint missile and drone operation' conducted by the IRGC Aerospace Force with assistance from the IRGC Navy. The IRGC claimed its forces 'destroyed anti-missile defense systems and successfully penetrated the Al-Kharj base with solid- and liquid-fueled missiles,' targeting the 'stationed refueling and air support fleet.' The IRGC characterized the mission as an 'impact-driven' operation, asserting strikes led to the destruction or severe damage of multiple wide-body and heavy support aircraft, including KC-135 tankers, belonging to US forces at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia. The 84th wave was dedicated to employees and martyrs of Iran's state broadcaster IRIB. CENTCOM had not independently confirmed aircraft losses at time of writing.
📍Prince Sultan Air Base (Al-Kharj), Saudi Arabia 💀US aircraft losses unconfirmed by CENTCOM; IRGC claims multiple tanker/support aircraft destroyed or severely damaged
Sources: ANI / Tribune India Press TV
27
Mar
Israel · Strike Offensive · Nuclear Infrastructure
IDF Strikes Arak Heavy Water Reactor, Ardakan Yellowcake Plant & Chemical Weapons Research Facility; Missile Hits Bushehr Grounds
On March 27 (Day 28), the IDF struck the Khondab Heavy Water Complex in Arak in two stages, as well as the uranium yellowcake production facility in Ardakan, Yazd Province. The IDF said heavy water 'can also be used as a neutron source for nuclear weapons' and that Iran had deliberately avoided converting the reactor to prevent weapons-grade plutonium production. Iran's Fars News Agency reported no casualties and no danger to the local population. The IDF also separately confirmed it struck an Iranian chemical weapons research facility, though it did not clarify whether Iran possessed finished chemical weapons or was still at the research stage. Earlier on Friday, the IDF had struck an Iranian missile and sea-mine production facility in Yazd. Separately, a missile also struck the grounds of Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant — 350 metres from the reactor, per the IAEA — causing no damage, casualties, or technical disruption. Russia's Rosatom began a further personnel evacuation and condemned the near-miss. The IAEA called for 'maximum restraint to avoid nuclear safety risks.'
📍Arak (Khondab), Yazd / Ardakan, Yazd Province; Bushehr NPP, Persian Gulf coast ⚠️No radiation release confirmed; IAEA Director-General called for 'maximum restraint'; Rosatom evacuating staff
Sources: Jerusalem Post Al Jazeera IAEA / World Nuclear News
26
Mar
Israel · Leadership Kill Decapitation · IRGC Navy Command
IDF Kills IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri & Entire Naval Leadership in Bandar Abbas Strike
The IDF confirmed on March 26 that Admiral Alireza Tangsiri, commander of the IRGC Navy since 2018, was killed in a precise overnight airstrike on Bandar Abbas at 3:00 a.m. local time. The IDF stated the strike also killed IRGC Navy Intelligence Chief Behnam Rezaei and 'the rest of the Navy's top leadership.' CENTCOM confirmed the killing. Defense Minister Katz said Tangsiri was 'the man directly responsible for the terrorist operation of mining and blocking the Strait of Hormuz.' Netanyahu stated: 'Last night, we eliminated the commander of the Revolutionary Guards' navy.' Tangsiri had personally overseen efforts to blockade the Strait and post updates on which ships were permitted to transit. FDD analysts assessed the strike represented 'an additional massive blow to the Islamic Republic,' noting it came after 'destruction of over 90 percent of the Iranian Navy's large ships.' Israel reportedly removed Iranian FM Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Qalibaf from its hit list after Pakistan's request via Washington.
📍Bandar Abbas, Hormozgan Province, Iran 💀Adm. Alireza Tangsiri (IRGC Navy Commander), Behnam Rezaei (Navy Intel Chief), and additional senior naval officers KIA
Sources: Times of Israel CNBC France 24

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Tel Aviv Ballistic Strike
Iranian Missile Kills One in Tel Aviv; Israel Vows to 'Intensify and Expand' Campaign
A missile fired from Iran struck a building in Tel Aviv on the night of March 27–28, killing one person and injuring dozens, despite advance notice of the launch. Israeli sirens in the area did not sound in time to allow residents the standard 90-second window to reach shelters. Defense Minister Katz responded by declaring that 'IDF strikes in Iran will intensify and expand to additional targets and domains that assist the regime in building and operating weapons against Israeli civilians.' Israel also said it struck an Iranian missile and sea-mine production facility in Yazd that was used for 'planning, development, assembly, and storage of advanced missiles' targeting maritime infrastructure. The IRGC simultaneously issued warnings to civilians across the Middle East to evacuate areas near US forces, threatening to strike 'wherever we find them.' Iran's Aerospace Commander Moosavi warned 'the equation will no longer be an eye for an eye.'
📍Tel Aviv, Israel; Yazd Province missile/mine facility, Iran 💀1 Israeli killed, dozens injured in Tel Aviv strike
Sources: Jerusalem Post TIME Al Jazeera

Mar
Diplomacy · Deadlock Political · Iran Rejects US 15-Point Plan; Counter-Proposal Expected
Iran Rejects US 15-Point Peace Proposal; Issues 5-Point Counter-Demand; Trump Extends Energy Strike Pause to April 6
Iran formally rejected the US 15-point ceasefire proposal delivered via Pakistan, calling it 'one-sided, maximalist, and unreasonable.' Iran's five-point counter-demand includes: cessation of aggression with concrete guarantees, war reparations and compensation, Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, a comprehensive end to hostilities across all fronts including all resistance groups, and clear determination of peace terms. Foreign Minister Araghchi stated 'We do not want a ceasefire' and dismissed US messaging through intermediaries as 'not negotiations.' He also framed Washington's shift toward talks as 'an admission of defeat.' Trump extended his deadline for striking Iranian energy infrastructure by 10 days to April 6, stating talks were 'going very well.' Iran's UN ambassador separately agreed to 'facilitate and expedite' humanitarian aid through the Strait. US Secretary of State Rubio attended G7 foreign ministers talks in France but dismissed the idea that US policy would be shaped by allies. A source confirmed Iran's counter-proposal was expected to arrive in Washington on March 27.
🕊️Pakistan, Egypt, Turkey acting as intermediaries; US Special Envoy Witkoff and Jared Kushner involved 📅Trump energy-strike pause extended to April 6, 2026 at 8 PM Eastern
Sources: NPR Times of Israel Al Jazeera

Mar
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · Mass Displacement Crisis
Lebanon: 1.2 Million Displaced; UN Warns of 'Humanitarian Catastrophe'; Israel Declares Occupation of South up to Litani
More than 1.2 million people have been displaced in Lebanon — one in five residents — since Israel intensified strikes in early March, according to the UN. The UNHCR warned on March 27 of a 'humanitarian catastrophe,' with Lebanon's shelter system overwhelmed, bridges destroyed, and entire districts cut off. Israel formally declared it will occupy southern Lebanon up to the Litani River, blocking hundreds of thousands of displaced civilians from returning until Hezbollah is 'removed.' Defense Minister Katz stated: 'Where there is terror and missiles, there will be no homes and no residents.' Israeli forces have destroyed five bridges over the Litani since March 13, cutting supply routes. Human Rights Watch warned the mass expulsion 'raises serious risks of forced displacement' and called the tactics potential war crimes. Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam warned the UN of the 'risk of annexation' of Lebanese territory. The IDF's Chief of Staff Zamir stated forces will 'deepen the operation' in Lebanon. Hezbollah's Naim Qassem rejected any negotiation under fire as 'surrender.'
📍Southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs 💀1,116+ killed in Lebanon (Health Ministry); 3,229+ wounded; 1.2M+ displaced (UN); 125,000+ fled to Syria
Sources: Al Jazeera Euronews NewStar / HRW

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Ballistic Missile & Drone Strike
Iran Strikes Prince Sultan Air Base; 10 US Troops Wounded, Refueling Aircraft Damaged
In the early hours of March 28 (Day 28), Iran struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia with a combination of ballistic missiles and drones, wounding at least 10 U.S. service members — two seriously — and damaging several U.S. refueling aircraft on the ground. All 10 personnel were reportedly inside an installation building at the time of the attack. No fatalities were reported in the strike. U.S. Central Command separately confirmed that more than 300 service members have now been wounded in total since the start of Operation Epic Fury on February 28, with 30 still out of action and 10 considered seriously wounded. The strike on Prince Sultan is the second major Iranian attack on the base, following the March 1 attack that killed Army Sgt. Benjamin Pennington.
📍Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia 💀10 US troops wounded (2 seriously); several US refueling aircraft damaged; no KIA
Sources: Times of Israel CNN Military Times
28
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Ballistic Missile Cluster Strike
Iranian Cluster Missile Kills One in Tel Aviv; Houthis Enter War with First Ballistic Missile Launch at Israel
In the early hours of March 28, an Iranian ballistic missile carrying a cluster warhead struck multiple sites across Tel Aviv and its vicinity, killing one man (aged 52, a security guard) and wounding at least two others. Bomblet debris from the cluster warhead damaged buildings across at least six separate impact sites in central Israel. Separately, for the first time since Operation Epic Fury began on February 28, the Houthi movement in Yemen officially entered the war by launching a ballistic missile toward southern Israel, triggering air raid sirens in Beersheba. The IDF intercepted the missile and reported no injuries. The Houthi attack followed a speech by their leader and fulfills the group's prior warnings that they would join the conflict if the situation continued to escalate. The UAE also activated air defenses in response to a simultaneous Iranian cruise missile and drone barrage, intercepting multiple projectiles. The IDF continued overnight strikes on what it described as 'targets of the Iranian terror regime throughout Tehran,' including ballistic missile production sites and launchers in western Iran.

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Ballistic Missile Strike
Iran Strikes UAE: Targets 'American Commander Hideouts' and Ukrainian Anti-Drone Depot in Dubai
On March 28, Iran's military central operational command Khatam Al-Anbiya announced in a statement carried by state television that it had targeted 'hideouts of American commanders and soldiers in Dubai,' and separately struck a Ukrainian anti-drone system depot in the UAE which it claimed was being used to assist US forces. The IRGC framed the strikes as retaliation for ongoing US-Israeli operations. Bahrain and the UAE confirmed overnight Iranian drone activity, with both countries reporting successful interceptions of incoming projectiles.

Iran Fires Ballistic Missile at Eshtaol; Sirens Over Jerusalem as New Barrage Detected
On March 28, the IDF detected a ballistic missile launched from Iran that impacted in the Eshtaol area, in the Jerusalem hills west of the capital. Air defense batteries were observed launching interceptors visible over Jerusalem. A separate Iranian ballistic missile attack was detected by the IDF targeting southern Israel, with sirens expected to sound in affected zones. The strikes came as the war reached its one-month mark, with Israeli and US officials vowing to continue and intensify operations.

Mar
IDF · Strike Offensive · Targeted Killing
IDF Kills Hezbollah-Affiliated Journalist Ali Shoeib, Member of Elite Radwan Force Intelligence Unit
On March 28, the Israeli military confirmed it had killed Ali Hassan Shoeib in an airstrike in the southern Lebanese town of Jezzine. The IDF said Shoeib was an operative in Hezbollah's elite Radwan Force intelligence unit, working under the guise of a journalist for the Hezbollah-linked Al-Manar TV channel. The IDF alleged Shoeib was involved in exposing IDF troop positions in southern Lebanon and disseminating Hezbollah propaganda. The same strike also killed Fatima Ftouni, a reporter for the Hezbollah-affiliated Al-Mayadeen channel, according to the network.

Mar
Coalition · CENTCOM Strategic · One-Month Assessment
War Reaches One Month: CENTCOM Reports 10,000+ Targets Struck, 92% of Iran Navy Destroyed; Rubio Says Another 2–4 Weeks Needed
As Operation Epic Fury reached its one-month mark on March 28, CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper confirmed that US and coalition forces had struck more than 10,000 military targets in Iran and destroyed 92% of the Iranian Navy's largest vessels. Over two-thirds of Iran's drone, missile, and naval production shipyards and facilities have been destroyed. Secretary of State Marco Rubio told G7 foreign ministers that the campaign remains 'ahead of schedule' but that another 2 to 4 weeks of operations are needed. Nearly 300 US service members have been wounded since the conflict began. More than 850 Tomahawk Land Attack Missiles have been expended during the first month of the conflict, raising concerns about long-term US munitions inventories. Iranian missile and drone launch rates have fallen dramatically — down approximately 92% from Day 1 peaks — though Iran launched its 82nd wave of retaliatory strikes (Operation True Promise 4) on March 26.
📍Regional — Iran, Gulf states, Lebanon 💀~300 US WIA total; 10,000+ Iranian targets struck; 140+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed
Sources: SOF News Times of Israel NPR
28
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Ballistic Missile & Drone Strike
Iran Strikes Prince Sultan Air Base; 12–15 Americans Wounded, KC-135 Destroyed
Iran launched a wave of at least six ballistic missiles and 29 drones at Prince Sultan Air Base outside Riyadh, Saudi Arabia — the third strike on the installation this week. At least 12–15 U.S. service members were wounded, with two to five in serious condition. Iranian military spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaghari stated that one KC-135 refueling aircraft was 'completely destroyed' and three others were damaged and taken out of service. Iran released Chinese satellite imagery purporting to show burning aircraft at the base. A U.S. official confirmed to NPR that 'some aircraft were apparently damaged.' The strike marks a significant escalation in Iran's targeting of U.S. air-refueling assets supporting Operation Epic Fury.
📍Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia 💀12–15 U.S. service members wounded (2–5 serious); 1 KC-135 destroyed, 3 damaged
Sources: Fox News NPR Al Jazeera
28
Mar
Israel · Air Strike Offensive · Nuclear Infrastructure Strike
IDF Strikes Arak Heavy Water Reactor and Ardakan Yellowcake Plant
The Israeli Air Force conducted simultaneous strikes on nuclear-related infrastructure across three areas of Iran on March 27–28. The IDF confirmed it struck the Shahid Khondab Heavy Water Complex in Arak — a facility which Iran had rebuilt since its destruction in the June 2025 war — and the Ardakan yellowcake production facility in Yazd Province. The IDF stated: 'Heavy water is a unique material used to operate nuclear reactors… [and] can also be used as a neutron source for nuclear weapons.' Iranian state media reported no radioactive material was released. A third site, an Iranian Defense Ministry facility for advanced explosive devices, was also struck. Israel's Defense Minister Katz vowed to 'intensify and expand' operations in Iran.
📍Arak (Khondab Heavy Water Complex) and Ardakan, Yazd Province, Iran ☢️No radioactive release reported; Iran confirms damage to yellowcake and heavy water facilities
Sources: Jerusalem Post Al Jazeera Wikipedia
28
Mar
Houthis · First Strike Offensive · Ballistic Missile Launch
Houthis Enter the War: First Ballistic Missile Salvo Fired at Southern Israel, Intercepted
Iran-backed Houthi forces in Yemen formally entered Operation True Promise 4 on March 28, conducting what they described as their 'first military operation' in support of Iran since the war began. Houthi Brigadier General Yahya Saree announced that ballistic missiles were fired at 'sensitive Israeli military sites' in southern Israel. The Israeli military confirmed it had intercepted a missile launched from Yemen; sirens sounded in the Beer Sheba area and near Israel's Negev Nuclear Research Center in Dimona. Missile traces were visible over the West Bank. This is the first confirmed Houthi attack on Israel since the launch of Operation Epic Fury on Feb. 28, significantly widening the geographic scope of the conflict.
📍Launched from Yemen; targeted Beer Sheba area and Negev/Dimona region, southern Israel 🛡️Intercepted by Israeli air defenses; no casualties reported
Sources: Fox News CNN Wikipedia
28
Mar
Israel · Air Strike Offensive · Targeted Kill
IDF Kills Three Journalists in Southern Lebanon in Targeted Strike on Press Vehicle
An Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon killed three journalists traveling in a clearly marked press vehicle on March 28. Among those killed were Fatima Ftouni, a correspondent for Beirut-based Al Mayadeen television, and her brother, as well as Ali Shoeib, a correspondent for Hezbollah-owned Al Manar television who the IDF identified as a member of the Radwan Force intelligence unit (already noted on the dashboard). Al Jazeera confirmed at least three journalists were killed in the single strike on their car near Deir al-Zahrani, southern Lebanon. The IDF said Shoeib 'operated within the Hezbollah terrorist organization under the guise of a journalist.' The killings drew international condemnation from media freedom groups.
📍Near Deir al-Zahrani, southern Lebanon 💀3 journalists killed: Fatima Ftouni (Al Mayadeen), her brother, and Ali Shoeib (Al Manar/Radwan Force)
Sources: Al Jazeera ABC News
28
Mar
US/Israel · Assessment Strategic · IDF Military Industry Targeting Update
IDF: 90% of Iran's Key Weapons Development Sites Now Targeted; Completion Days Away
The Israeli Defense Forces announced on March 28 that the IAF has now targeted approximately 70% of all assets in Iran's defense industrial base, and is close to having struck around 90% of key sites used to develop weapons threatening Israel. The IDF assessed it would complete targeting of nearly all of Iran's key military industry sites 'in the coming days.' Defense industries targeted include ballistic missiles, air defense systems, naval weapons, cyber capabilities, and spy satellites. This assessment aligns with VP JD Vance's statement on March 27 that the US has 'accomplished the vast majority of our military projects' and that the war would continue 'a little while longer' to 'neuter' Iran for a 'very, very long time.'
📍Iran-wide; multiple provinces 📊~70% of Iran defense-industrial assets struck; ~90% of key weapons-threat sites now targeted (IDF estimate)
Sources: Times of Israel
28
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Ballistic Missile Barrage
Iran Fires Six Ballistic Missile Barrages Since Midnight; Hits Tel Aviv (1 Killed), Targets Eilat
Iran continued its persistent ballistic missile campaign against Israel throughout March 28, firing at least six barrages since midnight. One missile struck Tel Aviv, killing one person. Additional barrages targeted the Eilat area in southern Israel, with at least one missile intercepted by air defenses. Footage showed damaged shops and buildings in central Israel following strikes late Friday night. The IDF confirmed 'several impact sites' in the Tel Aviv district shortly before midnight with no additional casualties in those areas. Iran's launch rate, while dramatically reduced from Day 1 (down ~92%), remains sufficient to sustain pressure on Israeli civilian life.
📍Tel Aviv (1 killed), Eilat (southern Israel), Jerusalem area 💀1 killed in Tel Aviv; multiple sites impacted across central and southern Israel
Sources: Times of Israel CNN
27
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Ballistic Missile & Drone Strike
Iran Strikes Prince Sultan Air Base — 15 US Troops Wounded, KC-135s and E-3 Sentry Damaged
Iran launched six ballistic missiles and 29 drones at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia — a key US Air Force hub some 96 km from Riyadh — wounding at least 15 American service members, five of them seriously. The attack damaged multiple aerial refueling KC-135 Stratotankers and reportedly struck an E-3G Sentry AWACS command-and-control aircraft, one of the most valuable US airborne intelligence assets in theater. IRGC spokesperson Ibrahim Zolfaghari stated that one refueling aircraft was 'completely destroyed' and three others put out of service. Iranian satellite imagery appearing to show burning aircraft was released by Tehran. US officials confirmed more than two dozen total troops wounded at the base in the past week across multiple strikes. CENTCOM declined to comment on aircraft damage specifics. The IRGC issued warnings to Gulf civilians to vacate areas near US forces ahead of the strikes. The attack followed Iran's 82nd wave of Operation True Promise 4 retaliatory strikes on March 26, which the IRGC called its 'most extensive' to date.
📍Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia 💀15 US troops wounded (5 seriously); multiple KC-135s + E-3G Sentry damaged or destroyed 🚀6 ballistic missiles + 29 drones fired by IRGC
Sources: AP / NPR Al Jazeera The Aviationist Air & Space Forces Magazine
26
Mar
IDF · Leadership Decapitation Strike · High-Value Target
IDF Kills IRGC Navy Commander Alireza Tangsiri and Entire Naval Leadership in Bandar Abbas Strike
An overnight Israeli Air Force strike in the Iranian port city of Bandar Abbas, at approximately 3 a.m. local time on March 26, killed Admiral Alireza Tangsiri — the longest-serving commander of the IRGC Navy — along with his intelligence chief Behnam Rezaei and the rest of the IRGC Navy's top command structure. Prime Minister Netanyahu confirmed the killing, stating Tangsiri had 'a great deal of blood on his hands' and was personally responsible for leading the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper confirmed the death and issued an unprecedented call for IRGC Navy personnel to 'abandon their post and return home.' The IDF said the strike 'constitutes an additional significant blow to the command-and-control arrays of the IRGC.' Tangsiri had served as IRGC Navy commander since August 2018, during which time he oversaw the major buildup of Iran's naval capabilities and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz after Operation Epic Fury began. Since the start of the conflict, over 92% of the IRGC Navy's large ships have been eliminated.
📍Bandar Abbas, Hormozgan Province — adjacent to the Strait of Hormuz 💀IRGC Navy Commander Adm. Alireza Tangsiri KIA; Intel Chief Behnam Rezaei KIA; full IRGC Navy top command eliminated ✈️IAF precision airstrike, ~03:00 local time
Sources: Times of Israel Jerusalem Post CNBC Gulf News / CENTCOM
28
Mar
Houthi · Escalation Offensive · Ballistic Missile Salvo (2nd Wave)
Houthis Fire Second Missile Salvo at Israel; Threaten Bab el-Mandeb Closure — Intercepted by IDF
Hours after their initial confirmed entry into the war on March 28, Yemen's Houthi rebels fired a second barrage of ballistic missiles toward what Houthi spokesman Brig. Gen. Yahya Saree described as 'sensitive Israeli military sites' in southern Israel. The IDF said it intercepted the incoming projectile. Saree stated that strikes 'will continue until the declared objectives are achieved' and until aggression against 'all fronts of the resistance' ceases. Houthi Deputy Information Minister Mohammed Mansour warned that 'closing the Bab al-Mandeb strait is among our options,' raising the prospect of a second global shipping chokepoint being threatened alongside the still-blockaded Strait of Hormuz. The Houthi entry into the Iran war marks a significant expansion of the conflict's geographic scope. Israel's USS Gerald R. Ford aircraft carrier, previously withdrawn to Crete for fire-related repairs, arrived in Split, Croatia — complicating any potential Red Sea redeployment. The threat to Bab el-Mandeb drew immediate concern from shipping industry analysts tracking the $1 trillion in annual Red Sea commerce.
📍Fired from Yemen toward southern Israel (Beer Sheba area/Negev); intercepted by IDF Arrow system ⚠️Houthis threaten Bab el-Mandeb Strait closure — second global chokepoint at risk 🚀Multiple ballistic missiles fired; IDF confirmed 2nd wave interception
Sources: CNBC Al Jazeera CNN Live Fortune / AP
28
Mar
IDF · Nuclear Infrastructure Offensive · Strategic Strike
IDF Hits Central Tehran Weapons Production Sites; Iran Industrial Facilities Struck Across Multiple Provinces
On March 28, Israel focused its strikes on sites 'in the heart of Tehran' where ballistic missiles and other weapons are produced, the IDF said. The military also struck missile launchers and storage sites in western Iran. Witnesses in eastern Tehran reported a partial power outage following the airstrikes. Iranian state-affiliated media reported heavy bombardment across multiple districts of Tehran, including the northeastern, western, central, and eastern parts of the city. In a parallel development, residents circumventing Iran's internet blackout sent footage to diaspora media showing US and Israeli strikes hitting steel and cement factories across Iran, including in southern and central Iran as well as central Isfahan. Explosions were reported across eight impact sites in Tel Aviv overnight into March 28, killing one person and injuring two — the result of Iranian retaliatory ballistic missile fire. Israel's Fire and Rescue Service responded to 11 impact sites in the Tel Aviv metro area. Earlier on March 28, Iran also allowed 20 Pakistani-flagged vessels to pass through the Strait of Hormuz — a limited but notable diplomatic signal amid Pakistan-mediated indirect talks.
📍Heart of Tehran (weapons production); Western Iran (missile sites); Isfahan (factories) — IDF strikes 💀1 killed, 2 injured in Tel Aviv from Iranian retaliatory missile fire (8 impact sites) 🌐Iran allows 20 Pakistani ships through Strait of Hormuz; US deadline extended to April 6
Sources: CNBC / AP CNN Live NPR
28
Mar
CENTCOM · Reinforcement Deployment · Ground Forces
2,500 US Marines and Sailors Arrive in Middle East; Kharg Island Operation Speculation Mounts
CENTCOM announced on March 28 that thousands of US sailors and Marines had arrived in the Middle East theater, marking a significant reinforcement surge as the war entered its second month. The deployment destination was not officially disclosed, but speculation mounted among defense analysts and media that the forces may be assigned to a potential amphibious operation to seize or neutralize Iran's Kharg Island — the hub of roughly 90% of Iran's oil exports. A US senator publicly referenced the island in urging an attack, and CNN reported that sources indicated Iran had been 'laying traps and moving military personnel and air defenses to Kharg Island in preparation for a possible US operation.' Secretary of State Marco Rubio also stated on March 28 that the US would 'destroy their ability to make missiles and drones in their factories' and 'dramatically reduce the number of missile launchers.' He reiterated that the operation remained 'ahead of schedule.' The deployment adds to the formidable existing CENTCOM presence, which as of March 25 had struck over 10,000 targets and two-thirds of Iran's military production facilities.
📍Middle East theater — destination undisclosed; Kharg Island operation speculation 🪖2,500+ US Marines and sailors deployed to region 🎯CENTCOM: 10,000+ targets struck; 2/3 of Iran's military production facilities destroyed (as of Mar 25)
Sources: CNN Live Fortune / AP Fox News Live
29
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile & Drone Barrage
IRGC Launches 82nd Wave — 230 Axis of Resistance Operations in 24 Hours
On March 26, the IRGC announced the 82nd wave of Operation True Promise 4, describing it as the most extensive coordinated strike package since the war began. The Axis of Resistance conducted 230 operations in a single 24-hour period: Iranian Armed Forces carried out 110 missile and drone strikes against US bases and Israeli positions, Lebanese Hezbollah launched 87 operations, and the Iraqi Islamic Resistance conducted 23 offensives. The IRGC said strike targets included a military command center inside Israel and facilities linked to Israel's nuclear program near the Dead Sea. The wave was dedicated to civilians in Iran's northern provinces of East Azerbaijan, Ardabil, Gilan, Mazandaran, and Golestan, which had sustained heavy US-Israeli bombardment.
📍Targets: Israeli military positions, US bases across West Asia, Israeli nuclear-linked sites near Dead Sea 🚀Scale: 110 Iranian strikes + 87 Hezbollah ops + 23 Iraqi Resistance ops = 230 total in 24 hrs (IRGC, Mar 26)
Sources: Press TV / GlobalSecurity NewFirst.lk
28
Mar
US · Casualties Defensive · Personnel
Iran Strikes Prince Sultan Air Base — 15 US Troops Wounded, 5 Seriously; Refueling Aircraft Destroyed
On March 28, Iran launched an attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia — the third such strike on the base in 10 days — wounding at least 15 US service members, with five in serious condition. The attack involved at least six ballistic missiles and 29 drones, according to the Associated Press. Iranian military spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaghari stated in a video that one US refueling aircraft was 'completely destroyed' and three others were damaged and put out of service. The soldiers were inside a building at the base when it was struck. The Pentagon and CENTCOM did not immediately comment on the Iranian spokesperson's claims regarding aircraft damage. The base typically hosts 2,000–3,000 US soldiers primarily involved in missile defense systems and logistical support.
📍Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia 💀15 US troops wounded (5 seriously); 1 refueling aircraft destroyed, 3 damaged (per Iranian MoD) 🚀Attack vector: 6 ballistic missiles + 29 drones (AP)
Sources: Al Jazeera NPR
26
Mar
Iran · Diplomacy Political · Ceasefire Rejection
Iran Formally Rejects US 15-Point Peace Proposal; Issues 5 Counter-Conditions Including War Reparations
On March 25–26, Iran formally rejected a 15-point US ceasefire proposal that had been delivered via Pakistan as an intermediary. The US proposal, as reported by The New York Times and Israel's Channel 12, required Iran to permanently end its nuclear program and dismantle all nuclear capabilities in exchange for sanctions relief. Iran countered with five conditions of its own: a full end to aggression, concrete guarantees preventing future war, payment of war reparations and compensation for damages, a comprehensive ceasefire across all fronts including against all 'resistance groups,' and recognition of Iranian sovereignty. Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Iran does not want a ceasefire but rather a permanent end to war 'on our own terms.' Iran's military spokesperson mockingly questioned whether the US was 'negotiating with itself.' White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt warned Iran not to miscalculate, saying Trump 'is prepared to unleash hell.' Meanwhile, Pakistan, Turkey, and Egypt emerged as potential mediator states for indirect talks.
📍Delivered via Pakistan; Iran's counter-conditions announced via state media 🗣️Iran FM Araghchi: 'We do not want a ceasefire' — demands war reparations and Strait of Hormuz sovereignty
Sources: NPR NPR (rejection)
28
Mar
Coalition · Strikes Offensive · Industrial Targets
CENTCOM Confirms 11,000+ Targets Struck; US-Israeli Strikes Hit Iran University of Science and Technology, Water Infrastructure
As of late March, US Central Command confirmed that coalition forces have struck over 11,000 targets in Iran since the start of Operation Epic Fury on February 28. On March 28, US-Israeli airstrikes severely damaged the Iran University of Science and Technology in Tehran and struck a 10,000-cubic-meter water reservoir in Haftkel, Khuzestan Province, drawing condemnation from humanitarian organizations. CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper also confirmed that the coalition has destroyed approximately two-thirds of Iran's arms manufacturing facilities. Iran has reported damage to at least 120 historical and cultural heritage sites as of March 27. US-Israeli forces have also targeted industrial production facilities across multiple provinces, with residents inside Iran — overcoming the internet blackout — posting footage of the strikes' extent.
📍Iran University of Science and Technology (Tehran); water reservoir in Haftkel, Khuzestan; multiple provinces 💥11,000+ total targets struck (CENTCOM); ~2/3 of Iranian arms manufacturing destroyed (Adm. Cooper, Mar 25)
Sources: Wikipedia / 2026 Iran War Times of Israel (Mar 25 liveblog)
28
Mar
Lebanon · Humanitarian Civilian · Displacement Crisis
1.2 Million Displaced in Lebanon; Over 1,100 Killed as Israel Issues Expanded Evacuation Orders South of Zahrani River
As of late March, Lebanon's government confirmed at least 1.2 million people have been displaced — approximately one quarter of the country's entire population — as Israel expanded its forced evacuation zone beyond the Litani River to areas south of the Zahrani River, roughly 40 kilometers from the Israeli border. Lebanon's Health Ministry reported more than 1,100 killed and over 3,200 wounded since the war began on March 2. More than 140,000 people have crossed from Lebanon into Syria, nearly half of them children. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz stated that displaced Lebanese civilians south of the Litani would not be allowed to return until Hezbollah no longer poses a threat, and on March 22 ordered the 'acceleration of demolition of Lebanese houses in border villages' modeled on Israeli operations in Rafah and Beit Hanoun in Gaza. On March 28, an Israeli drone strike killed three Lebanese journalists: Ali Shoeib, Fatima Ftouni, and a third unnamed colleague. Hezbollah continues to fire rockets at northern Israel, with Karmiel struck and two wounded. The UN warned that the humanitarian crisis in Lebanon is now on a 'whole different level' from 2024.
📍Southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs (Dahiyeh); displacement camps along Beirut coastline 💀1,100+ killed, 3,200+ wounded (Lebanon Health Ministry); 1.2M displaced (Lebanese government, late March) 🏕️140,000+ fled to Syria; 3 journalists killed by IDF drone strike Mar 28 (AP/NPR)
Sources: Al Jazeera Euronews
29
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Houthi Entry / Saudi Strike Follow-up
Houthis Formally Enter War; Iran-Backed Strikes Intensify Across Region
As Operation Epic Fury approached its fifth week, Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen signaled formal entry into the conflict, launching missiles toward Israeli territory and firing on US-affiliated assets in the Red Sea corridor. Israeli air defenses intercepted at least one Houthi ballistic missile. Simultaneously, the fallout from the March 28 Iranian strike on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia continued, with CENTCOM confirming at least 15 US service members wounded — including five seriously — in the attack, which also destroyed a refueling aircraft. Iran's IRGC state media framed the ongoing campaign as part of its 'Operation True Promise 4,' with 230 Axis of Resistance operations claimed in the preceding 24-hour window (Wave 82). White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt warned Iran that President Trump 'does not bluff' and is 'prepared to unleash hell' if Tehran does not accept a deal.
📍Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia; Red Sea corridor; northern Israel 💀15+ US troops wounded (5 seriously) in Saudi base strike; Houthi missile intercepted over Israel 🚀Houthis fire ballistic missile at Israel; IRGC claims 230 Axis ops in prior 24 hrs (Wave 82)
Sources: NPR Fox News
29
Mar
CENTCOM · Strike Update Defensive/Offensive · Targeting Iran Arms Industry
CENTCOM: Two-Thirds of Iran's Arms Manufacturing Destroyed; Strikes Continue on Industrial Base
CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper confirmed that US and Israeli forces have destroyed approximately two-thirds of Iran's military production facilities since the start of Operation Epic Fury. More than 11,000 targets have been struck in total, including — most recently on March 28 — the Iran University of Science and Technology and a 10,000-cubic-meter water reservoir in Haftkel, Khuzestan. Israel's military said it is accelerating targeting of Iranian arms factories over the next 48 hours in anticipation of a potential ceasefire declaration, trying to maximize damage to Iran's remaining defense-industrial base before any halt in operations. Iran's missile launch rate and drone attack rate remain sharply reduced from Day 1 levels — down approximately 90% and 83% respectively — though the IRGC continues to launch smaller, precision strikes using heavier payloads of 1,000 kg or more.
📍Iran-wide; Iran University of Science & Technology; Haftkel, Khuzestan 🎯11,000+ total targets struck; ~2/3 of Iran's arms manufacturing destroyed (CENTCOM) 📉Iranian ballistic missile launch rate down ~90%, drone rate down ~83% vs. Day 1
Sources: Times of Israel Wikipedia – 2026 Iran war
29
Mar
Diplomacy · Impasse Diplomatic · Iran Counter-Conditions / Pakistan Mediation
Iran Issues 5 Counter-Conditions; Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt Join Mediation Effort as Talks Stall
Following Iran's formal rejection of the US 15-point peace proposal (delivered via Pakistan), Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that Iran does not want a ceasefire and demanded a permanent end to hostilities on Iranian terms. Tehran's five counter-conditions include: cessation of all aggression, concrete non-recurrence guarantees, clear determination of war responsibility, guaranteed payment of war reparations and damages, and a comprehensive end to the war across all fronts — including against all Axis of Resistance groups. Araghchi dismissed US diplomatic outreach as an 'admission of defeat,' saying message exchanges through third-country intermediaries do not constitute negotiations. Pakistan has emerged as the primary mediator, with Turkey and Egypt also involved in indirect talks. Israel, meanwhile, has made clear it wants weeks more of fighting and is ramping up strikes regardless of diplomatic activity.
📍Tehran / Islamabad (mediation hub) 🤝Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt mediating; US 15-point plan rejected; Iran issues 5 counter-conditions ⚠️Iran demands war reparations and sovereignty over Strait of Hormuz as preconditions
Sources: NPR CNN
29
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · IRGC 82nd Wave / Bandar Khamir Strikes
IRGC Launches 82nd Wave; US–Israel Strike Bandar Khamir Port; Iranian Missile Hits Ne'ot Hovav Chemical Plant
On March 29, U.S.–Israeli strikes bombed the port city of Bandar Khamir in southern Iran, killing at least five people. Separately, an Iranian missile struck a chemical plant in the Ne'ot Hovav industrial zone in southern Israel, injuring one person and causing fears of a hazardous chemical leak. A convoy of Iraqi Shia militia PMF fighters was seen entering Khorramshahr in southwestern Iran, suggesting Iran is drawing on regional proxy ground forces. The IRGC's 82nd wave of retaliatory operations — launched March 26 and described as the 'most extensive' to date — included 230 combined Axis of Resistance operations in a 24-hour period: 110 Iranian missile and drone strikes, 87 Hezbollah operations, and 23 Iraqi Islamic Resistance offensives targeting US and Israeli assets across the region.
📍Bandar Khamir port (S. Iran) struck; Ne'ot Hovav industrial zone, Negev, Israel hit; PMF convoy at Khorramshahr 💀5 killed in Bandar Khamir; 1 injured at Ne'ot Hovav chemical plant (hazmat fears) 🚀82nd IRGC wave: 230 total Axis of Resistance ops in 24 hrs — 110 Iranian, 87 Hezbollah, 23 Iraqi IR strikes
Sources: Wikipedia – 2026 Iran War Press TV / GlobalSecurity NewsFirst.lk
29
Mar
Coalition · Strike Update Offensive · Saudi Base Attack / US Wounded
Iranian Strike Wounds 15 US Troops at Saudi Air Base; Houthis Formally Enter War with Missile on Israel
An Iranian strike on a Saudi Arabian air base hosting US troops wounded at least 15 American service members, including at least five in serious condition, according to the Associated Press. Israel confirmed it intercepted a missile launched by Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen — marking the Houthis' formal entry into the conflict. The attack on the Saudi base came on approximately the 29th day of the war, near the one-month mark of Operation Epic Fury's launch on February 28. Secretary of State Rubio reiterated that the conflict would not become 'prolonged' and could be resolved 'without any ground troops,' even as thousands of Marines and Army troops are en route to the region. The USS Gerald R. Ford, having sailed for Crete for repairs after a non-combat fire on March 12, remains sidelined.
📍Saudi Arabia (air base hosting US forces); Houthi missile launch from Yemen intercepted over Israel 💀15 US service members wounded (5 serious) in Saudi base strike — Fox News/AP, Mar 28 ⚠️Houthis fire first missile at Israel, formally entering the war on Iran's side
Sources: NPR Fox News Live Blog
29
Mar
Diplomacy · Talks Stall Political · Iran Rejects 15-Point Plan / Pakistan-Turkey-Egypt Mediation
Iran Rejects US 15-Point Peace Plan; Araghchi Says Negotiations Amount to 'Admission of Defeat'
Iran formally rejected a 15-point US ceasefire proposal that was delivered via Pakistan, which included a demand that Iran permanently end its nuclear program. Iran issued five counter-conditions of its own, including: a full end to hostilities, concrete guarantees against future aggression, war reparations and compensation, a comprehensive end to war across all fronts including against all resistance groups (i.e., Hezbollah), and Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated the US shift toward negotiation was 'exactly an admission of defeat,' while also saying Iran wants the war to end permanently — not via a temporary ceasefire. Pakistan has emerged as the primary mediator, with Turkey and Egypt also participating in indirect talks. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt warned Iran not to miscalculate, saying 'President Trump does not bluff.' Israel, for its part, is accelerating strikes on Iranian arms factories, hoping to hit as many targets as possible before any ceasefire is declared.
🕊️US 15-point proposal (via Pakistan): Iran to end nuclear program, sanctions relief in return — rejected by Tehran 📋Iran's 5 counter-conditions include war reparations, Strait of Hormuz sovereignty, and end to attacks on Hezbollah 🌍Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt join as mediators; indirect message channels active via 'friendly countries'
Sources: NPR NPR CNN
29
Mar
Coalition · Strike Offensive · Port Strike
US–Israel Strike Bandar Khamir Port; PMF Convoy Enters Iran
On March 29, US–Israeli airstrikes bombed the port city of Bandar Khamir in southern Iran, killing at least five people. Separately, a convoy of Iraqi Shia militia PMF fighters was observed entering Khorramshahr in southwestern Iran, raising concerns about a new ground-level axis of resistance forming inside Iran's border. The strikes on Bandar Khamir continued the coalition's campaign against Iranian naval and logistics infrastructure in the Strait of Hormuz region.
📍Bandar Khamir, Hormozgan Province, southern Iran 💀At least 5 killed in port strike (Wikipedia / Al Jazeera) ⚠️PMF convoy enters Khorramshahr, SW Iran

29
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Chemical Plant Strike
Iranian Missile Hits Ne'ot Hovav Chemical Plant; Houthis Fire on Israel
An Iranian ballistic missile struck the Ne'ot Hovav industrial zone chemical plant in southern Israel on March 29, injuring one person and triggering fears of a hazardous materials leak at one of Israel's most sensitive industrial sites. The same day, the Houthis formally entered the conflict by launching a missile at Israel, marking their first direct strike since the war began on February 28. Sirens sounded around Be'er Sheba and near Israel's Dimona nuclear research center as part of the same overnight wave.

Sources: Wikipedia – 2026 Iran war Fox News Live Updates
29
Mar
Diplomacy · Talks Stalled Diplomatic · Counter-Conditions
Iran Issues 5 Counter-Conditions; Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt Mediate as Talks Collapse
Iran formally rejected the US 15-point peace plan transmitted via Pakistan and issued five counter-conditions of its own, including a demand that any ceasefire cover the 2026 Lebanon war and a halt to Israeli operations against Hezbollah. Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi declared that American calls for negotiations amount to 'an admission of defeat,' noting that Washington had previously demanded unconditional surrender. Pakistan, Turkey, and Egypt joined mediation efforts but talks stalled immediately over the incompatible preconditions. The White House press secretary warned Iran not to 'miscalculate again,' while Israel's military said it was accelerating strikes on Iranian arms factories in anticipation of a potential ceasefire declaration.
