29
Mar
CENTCOM · Assessment Operational · Industrial Destruction
CENTCOM: Two-Thirds of Iran's Arms Manufacturing Destroyed; Strikes Continue
CENTCOM commander Admiral Brad Cooper confirmed that as of late March, over 10,000 targets have been struck in Iran since the start of Operation Epic Fury, and that two-thirds of Iran's military production facilities have been destroyed. The US maintains full air superiority over Iran, with more than 9,000 combat flights logged. Iranian ballistic missile launch rates have fallen 90% from Day 1 and drone launch rates are down 83%, according to CENTCOM. Israel's military stated it plans at least three more weeks of operations and has thousands of additional targets identified, including arms factories it is racing to strike before any potential ceasefire is declared.
📍Pan-Iran — strike campaign now in Week 5 💥10,000+ targets struck; 2/3 of arms manufacturing destroyed (CENTCOM) ✈️Air superiority maintained; 9,000+ combat flights flown
Sources: Times of Israel – Mar 25 Liveblog Stars and Stripes – CENTCOM Day 22 Times of Israel – IDF 3 More Weeks
30
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile & Drone Salvos
Iran Fires Four Overnight Ballistic Salvos at Israel; IRGC Claims MQ-9 Shootdown & F-16 Hit
Between midnight and dawn on March 29–30 (Day 30), Iran's IRGC launched four separate ballistic missile salvos at Israel. One missile struck a commercial street in Tel Aviv; others landed in open areas with no reported injuries. The IRGC separately claimed to have shot down a US MQ-9 Reaper drone over Shiraz and to have damaged a US F-16 fighter jet over southern Fars Province. CENTCOM appeared to refute the F-16 claim by posting an image of an F-16 landing safely at a regional base after a combat mission. The IRGC's claim, if confirmed, would represent the first acknowledged loss of a manned US combat aircraft in the campaign. Saudi Arabia and the UAE both reported intercepting fresh drone and missile threats overnight, with the Saudi Defence Ministry confirming the destruction of 10 drones in the early hours of March 30.
📍Tel Aviv commercial district (missile impact); Shiraz airspace (MQ-9 claim); southern Fars Province (F-16 claim) 💀No confirmed casualties from overnight Israeli strikes; CENTCOM has not confirmed aircraft loss
Sources: Global Security / Day 30 Update Al Jazeera — Day 30
29
Mar
IAF · Strike Wave Offensive · Airstrikes
IAF Wave Hits Tehran Ballistic Missile Sites; Power Blackouts Reported Across Capital
On March 29, the Israeli Air Force carried out a wave of strikes in and around Tehran, causing brief power blackouts in parts of the capital and the city of Karaj. The IDF stated the strikes hit sites where Iran stored and launched ballistic missiles alongside air defense systems. Specific targets included a site used for 'essential' ballistic missile component development, an IRGC complex used for research and development of ballistic missiles and satellite launchers, and a weapons research, development, and production site of the Iranian army. The IAF also struck additional ballistic missile storage and launch sites during the same wave. PM Netanyahu, speaking from IDF Northern Command HQ in Safed, said Israeli actions were creating 'visible cracks in the terror regime in Tehran.'
📍Tehran & Karaj, Iran — IRGC missile R&D complexes, ballistic missile storage and launch sites, air defense systems 💥Power blackouts reported across parts of Tehran and Karaj following strikes
Sources: Times of Israel — Mar 29 Liveblog
29
Mar
Iran · IRGC Ultimatum Diplomatic · Threat
IRGC Sets March 30 Noon Deadline: Condemn University Strikes or US Gulf Campuses Will Be Targeted
Iran's IRGC issued a formal ultimatum demanding the US government condemn reported strikes on Iranian universities via an official statement by noon on March 30, threatening to expand missile and drone attacks to American university campuses in the Gulf if conditions are not met. US universities with Gulf campuses include Texas A&M and Northwestern in Qatar and New York University in the UAE. The deadline coincides with diplomatic efforts underway in Islamabad, where foreign ministers of Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt convened on March 29 for a two-day summit representing the most visible multilateral push to end the conflict. Turkish FM Fidan stated the four nations would discuss establishing a de-escalation mechanism.
📍IRGC communiqué targeting US university campuses in Qatar (Texas A&M, Northwestern) and UAE (NYU) 🕛Deadline: March 30, 2026 — noon local time
Sources: CNN — Day 29 Global Security Day 30
29
Mar
Houthis · Entry into War Offensive · Missile & Drone Attack
Houthis Enter War on Day 29: Fire Ballistic Missiles & Drones at Israel from Yemen
Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the Iran war on Day 29, firing a barrage of ballistic missiles and drones at Israel — their first such strikes since the US-Israel war on Iran began on February 28. Houthis claimed responsibility for strikes on Eilat and Tel Aviv, using a mix of ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, and attack drones. GlobalSecurity analysis described the Houthi entry as the opening of a sustained campaign rather than a symbolic gesture, noting the rapid follow-up of two separate operations using different weapon systems within fewer than 24 hours. The development significantly expands the southern axis threat to Israel and raises fears of a dual-chokepoint scenario involving both the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea.
📍Launched from Yemen; targeted Eilat and Tel Aviv, Israel ⚠️First Houthi strikes on Israel since Operation Epic Fury began Feb 28; signals sustained campaign from southern axis
Sources: Al Jazeera — Day 30 Global Security Day 30
30
Mar
Diplomacy · Islamabad Summit Diplomatic · Multilateral Talks
Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey & Egypt Convene in Islamabad to Seek De-escalation Mechanism
Foreign ministers of Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt convened in Islamabad on March 29 for a two-day summit representing the most visible multilateral diplomatic push to end the 30-day-old conflict. Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan stated the four nations would discuss establishing a de-escalation mechanism and assess 'where the negotiations in this war are heading.' The summit came as Trump's deadline for striking Iranian energy infrastructure was extended to April 6, and as Iran's 15-point ceasefire rejection remained in effect. The IRGC's simultaneous noon March 30 ultimatum threatening US university campuses in the Gulf cast a shadow over the diplomatic effort.
📍Islamabad, Pakistan — two-day summit with Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Egypt 🤝Goal: establish de-escalation mechanism; Pakistan acting as lead mediator between US and Iran
Sources: Global Security Day 30 Al Arabiya
30
Mar
Coalition · Nuclear Strike Offensive · IAF Destroys Arak Heavy Water Plant
IAEA Confirms Arak Khondab Heavy Water Plant Destroyed; No Longer Operational
On March 27–28, over 50 Israeli Air Force fighter jets conducted three waves of strikes on Iran's Arak (Khondab) Heavy Water Production Facility, the Ardakan Yellowcake Plant in Yazd, and additional weapons-production sites. The IAEA confirmed on March 30 via satellite imagery analysis that Iran's Khondab Heavy Water Plant has sustained severe damage and is no longer operational. The IDF stated the facility was 'designed to have the capability to produce weapons-grade plutonium' and that Iran had not fully converted the reactor despite international commitments. Strikes also hit the Ardakan yellowcake facility — a raw-material source for uranium enrichment — as well as multiple ballistic missile and advanced explosives production sites across the three strike areas.
📍Arak (Khondab), Yazd (Ardakan), and additional weapons sites across Iran 💀No casualties reported per Iranian authorities; IAEA confirms facility non-operational ✈️50+ IAF fighter jets; three strike waves over several hours
Sources: IAEA / Business Standard IDF Official Site Times of Israel
30
Mar
Iran · Missile Strike Offensive · Iranian Missile Hits Haifa Refinery
Iranian Missile Impacts Haifa Oil Refinery; Fire Erupts on Day 31
On the morning of March 30, Israeli Channel 12 and other outlets reported an impact at an oil refinery in the northern city of Haifa shortly after the IDF detected a fresh salvo of Iranian ballistic missiles. Thick black smoke billowed from the refinery site; the fire was subsequently described as extinguished. The IDF said search-and-rescue forces were dispatched. The incident marked the second fire at a Haifa refinery during the month-long war. Sirens sounded across northern Israel immediately prior to the impact, consistent with the pattern of Iranian salvos targeting Israeli industrial and energy infrastructure.
📍Haifa Oil Refinery, northern Israel 🔥Fire reported and subsequently extinguished; no casualty figures immediately confirmed 🚀Part of fresh IRGC ballistic missile salvo detected by IDF on Mar 30
Sources: France 24 NBC News
30
Mar
Diplomacy · Talks Update Diplomatic · Trump Claims Iran Agreed to 'Most' of 15-Point Plan; Pakistan to Host Talks
Trump: Iran Agreed to 'Most' of 15-Point Demands; Pakistan Ready to Host Direct Talks
Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One on March 29–30, President Trump said Iran had responded positively to the US 15-point peace framework transmitted via Pakistan, claiming 'They gave us most of the points. Why wouldn't they?' Trump simultaneously raised the prospect of seizing Iran's Kharg Island oil hub — which handles 90% of Iran's oil exports — saying 'Maybe we take Kharg Island, maybe we don't. We have a lot of options.' Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmail Baghaei flatly denied direct negotiations, calling US proposals 'unrealistic and unreasonable,' and said Iran had only received messages through intermediaries including Pakistan. Pakistan's Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar announced Islamabad would be 'honored to host and facilitate meaningful talks between the two sides in the coming days.' Trump also extended his pause on strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure to April 6, 2026, citing ongoing talks.
🕊️Pakistan to host US–Iran talks; Islamabad four-nation summit concluded March 29–30 ⚠️Iran denies direct negotiations; calls 15-point plan 'unrealistic and unreasonable' 🛢️Trump floats Kharg Island seizure; Iran allowed 20 Pakistan-flagged ships through Hormuz as gesture
Sources: Times of Israel NBC News CNN
30
Mar
Iran · Ultimatum Deadline Threat · IRGC University Ultimatum Deadline Passes; US Universities Move Online
IRGC Noon Deadline Passes: US Refuses to Condemn University Strikes; Gulf Campuses Go Virtual
The IRGC's noon Tehran-time deadline on March 30 for the United States to officially condemn strikes on Iranian universities (including the University of Science and Technology and Isfahan University of Technology) passed without a US statement of condemnation. The IRGC had warned that all American and Israeli universities in West Asia are 'legitimate targets' until two are struck in retaliation. In response, the American University of Armenia, the American University in Iraq Baghdad (AUIB), the American University of Beirut, and other Gulf-region campuses moved to full remote/online learning as a precautionary measure. The IRGC had urged all staff, students, and nearby residents to maintain a distance of at least 1 km from US and Israeli university campuses in the region.
📍Gulf region: Texas A&M Qatar, NYU Abu Dhabi, AUA Armenia, AUIB Baghdad, AUB Beirut ⚠️IRGC: all Israeli and American universities in West Asia are 'legitimate targets' until two are struck 🕛Deadline: 12:00 noon Tehran time March 30 — passed without US condemnation
Sources: France 24 Iraqi News / AUIB J.Post
30
Mar
Coalition · IDF Strike Offensive · IDF Attacks Tehran Government Infrastructure; Power Blackout Reported
IDF Strikes Tehran Government Infrastructure on Day 31; Overnight Blackout Reported
The Israeli military announced on March 30 that it was 'currently attacking the infrastructure of the Iranian government throughout Tehran.' Overnight US-Israeli strikes hit Tehran and other cities including Karaj, Shiraz, Qom, Abadan, and Tabriz. The attacks caused a power blackout in the Iranian capital, which Iranian authorities subsequently said had been restored. Iranian state media reported impacts in the Saadat Abad neighbourhood of northern Tehran and western Tehran residential areas. Earlier on March 29, IDF strikes also hit mobile IRGC command centers mounted on rail cars — a novel target type reflecting the near-total destruction of Iran's fixed command infrastructure. Trump posted on Truth Social that 'many long sought after targets have been taken out and destroyed' by US forces in Iran over the past 24 hours. CENTCOM has now confirmed over 11,000 cumulative targets struck since February 28.
📍Tehran (Saadat Abad, western districts), Karaj, Shiraz, Qom, Abadan, Tabriz 💡Power blackout in Tehran capital — since restored per Iranian authorities 🚂IDF struck IRGC mobile command centers on rail cars — first such reported target type
Sources: Al Jazeera Times of Israel Kenya Times / CENTCOM
30
Mar
Iran · Ground Invasion Warning Escalation · Iran Warns It Is 'Waiting' for US Ground Troops
Iran Parliament Speaker Warns US Ground Invasion Plans Underway; 'We Are Waiting'
Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf issued an aggressive warning on March 29–30, accusing the United States of 'openly sending messages of negotiation and dialogue, but secretly planning a ground attack.' Ghalibaf stated: 'The US is unaware that our men are waiting for American soldiers to enter on the ground so they can set them ablaze and punish their regional partners forever.' His statement came as the Washington Post reported the Pentagon has drawn up plans for weeks of ground operations in Iran short of a full-scale invasion, and as USS Tripoli arrived in the region with approximately 3,500 US service members and the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt stated Trump had not made a final decision on deploying ground troops. Iran also conditioned any ceasefire deal on an end to the 2026 Lebanon war against Hezbollah.
⚠️Iran Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf: 'Our men are waiting for American soldiers to enter' 🚢USS Tripoli + 31st MEU (~3,500 troops) arrived in Middle East, March 28 🕊️Iran conditions ceasefire on end to Lebanon war; 5 Iranian counter-conditions presented to mediators
Sources: NPR NBC News CNN
31
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · IRGC Wave 85 Strike Package
IRGC Launches Wave 85 of Op. True Promise 4; US F-16 Reported Struck Over Pars Province
The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced the 85th wave of Operation True Promise 4, conducting joint IRGC Navy and Aerospace Force strikes targeting US and Israeli-linked heavy industrial sites. The operation deployed long- and medium-range solid and liquid-fuel missile systems alongside one-way loitering drones under the codename 'O Messenger of Allah.' The IRGC also claimed a US MQ-9 Reaper drone was shot down over Shiraz and that a US F-16 fighter jet was struck in southern Pars province and destroyed before landing in Saudi Arabia. CENTCOM acknowledged the F-16 sustained heavy damage. Additionally, the 84th wave included coordinated IRGC Navy attacks on US Landing Craft Utility vessels at Kuwait's Shweikh Port and a drone/ballistic missile assault on Bubiyan Island, with the IRGC claiming multiple US casualties transferred to Kuwaiti hospitals.
📍Pars Province / Shiraz / Bubiyan Island, Kuwait / Dubai coastline 💀US F-16 destroyed (IRGC claim, CENTCOM confirms damage); MQ-9 shot down; multiple US casualties on Bubiyan (IRGC claim, unconfirmed by CENTCOM)
Sources: Al Mayadeen
30
Mar
IDF · Strike Offensive · Long-Range Air Defense Suppression
IDF Strikes Iranian Air Defense Sites Near Caspian Sea, Over 1,600 km from Israel
The Israel Defense Forces announced that the Israeli Air Force struck Iranian air defense systems in northern Iran near the Caspian Sea — more than 1,600 kilometers (approximately 1,000 miles) from Israeli territory. The IDF said the targeted site, concealed in a forested area, housed air defense systems that posed a threat to Israeli aircraft operating deep inside Iran. The IDF stated the strikes 'preserve, and further expand, the Israeli Air Force's aerial superiority over Iranian airspace.' The operation was conducted as part of Operation Roaring Lion and was based on IDF intelligence. No Israeli aircraft were reported lost.
📍Northern Iran, near Caspian Sea ✈️No Israeli aircraft lost; Iranian air defense site destroyed (IDF)
Sources: VINnews
30
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Maritime Strike
Iranian Drone Strikes Kuwaiti Crude Tanker 'Al-Salmi' Anchored in Dubai Port; Fire Erupts
Iranian forces struck the Kuwaiti crude carrier 'Al-Salmi,' anchored in a Dubai port, with a drone, igniting a fire aboard the fully loaded vessel. Kuwait's state news agency KUNA reported no crew casualties — all 24 crew members were confirmed safe — but Kuwait Petroleum Corporation warned of the possibility of an oil spill into surrounding waters. The Dubai Media Office confirmed the incident involved a drone and that maritime firefighting teams were deployed to control the blaze. The tanker was fully loaded at the time of the strike.
📍Dubai Port, UAE 🚢Kuwaiti crude tanker 'Al-Salmi' struck and set ablaze; 24 crew safe; oil spill risk assessed
Sources: CNN
30
Mar
Israel · Lebanon Offensive · UNIFIL Incident
Three UN Peacekeepers Killed in Southern Lebanon Over Weekend; France Calls Emergency UN Security Council Meeting
Three United Nations UNIFIL peacekeepers were killed in southern Lebanon over the course of the weekend and Monday, March 30 — the deadliest period for UN peacekeepers since the Lebanon war began. A UNIFIL statement confirmed one peacekeeper was killed Saturday night when a projectile exploded near Adchit al-Qusayr, and another was critically wounded. Two more UN peacekeepers were killed Monday. France's Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot called the incidents 'unacceptable and unjustifiable' and requested an emergency UN Security Council meeting. The IDF said it was reviewing the incidents to determine whether casualties resulted from IDF or Hezbollah activity. NPR reported three UN peacekeeper deaths in the past 24 hours as of Monday morning, March 30.
📍Adchit al-Qusayr / Southern Lebanon 💀3 UNIFIL peacekeepers killed (weekend–Mar 30); 1 critically wounded; perpetrator unconfirmed
Sources: CNN NPR
30
Mar
Diplomacy · Trump Ultimatum Political · Truth Social Warning
Trump Threatens to Obliterate Iran's Power Plants, Oil Wells, and Kharg Island if No Deal Reached
President Trump posted on Truth Social on March 30 that if a deal was not reached soon and the Strait of Hormuz not reopened, the United States would conclude the war by destroying all of Iran's power plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, and possibly all desalination plants. The warning came amid ongoing indirect message exchanges between Washington and Tehran through third-country intermediaries. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi rejected the US 15-point peace plan and characterized the US shift toward negotiations as 'an admission of defeat,' noting the communications do not constitute negotiations. Pakistan has offered to broker direct talks. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt stated that US troops in the region are being positioned to give Trump 'maximum optionality.'
🏛️Washington D.C. / Tehran (via intermediaries) ⚡Iran rejects 15-point plan; Trump threatens energy infrastructure destruction; Pakistan offers mediation
Sources: CNN Wikipedia – 2026 Iran war
31
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Cluster Bomb BM Launch
Iran Fires Cluster-Warhead Ballistic Missile at Central Israel; IDF Completes New Tehran Strike Wave
On the morning of March 31, the IDF detected and announced a new ballistic missile attack from Iran targeting central Israel. Initial IDF assessments indicated the missile carried a cluster bomb warhead, with emergency services dispatched to several possible bomblet impact sites across central Israel; no casualties were immediately reported. Separately, the Israeli military completed an additional wide-scale wave of strikes targeting Iranian regime infrastructure throughout Tehran, with Iranian media reporting power outages in parts of the capital and Alborz province following damage to electrical facilities. The IDF said it had struck 170 Iranian regime targets over the preceding 24 hours, including weapons production sites, drone engine manufacturing facilities, and key IRGC military headquarters in western Iran. Israeli Health Ministry figures noted 121 people treated at hospitals in the previous 24 hours as a result of the combined Iran and Hezbollah attacks.
📍Central Israel (missile impact zone); Tehran & Alborz Province (IDF strikes) 💀No immediate Israeli casualties confirmed from Mar 31 cluster BM; 121 hospitalised in previous 24-hour period (Israel Health Ministry)
Sources: Times of Israel CBS News
31
Mar
Israel · Ground Combat Defensive · IDF Casualties Lebanon
Four IDF Soldiers Killed in Close-Range Hezbollah Ambush in Southern Lebanon
The IDF announced on March 31 that four of its soldiers were killed and two others wounded in a close-range firefight with Hezbollah gunmen in the western sector of southern Lebanon on the evening of March 30. The engagement occurred at approximately 18:30 local time when troops of the Nahal Brigade's Reconnaissance Unit spotted a cell of Hezbollah operatives and exchanged fire at close range, killing several of the attackers. Three of the four fallen soldiers were identified as Cpt. Noam Madmoni, 22, from Sderot; Staff Sgt. Ben Cohen, 21, from Lehavim; and Staff Sgt. Maxsim Entis, 21, from Bat Yam. The name of the fourth soldier was to be released later. One soldier was seriously wounded and one reservist moderately hurt. A separate incident on March 30 also saw a soldier killed by a Hezbollah anti-tank missile strike on a tank of the 401st Armored Brigade's 9th Battalion.
📍Western sector, southern Lebanon (Nahal Reconnaissance Unit engagement) 💀4 IDF KIA, 2 wounded (Nahal Brigade Recon); 1 additional KIA (anti-tank strike, 401st Armored)
Sources: Times of Israel Jerusalem Post
30
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Energy Infrastructure Strike
Iran-Hezbollah Joint Missile Salvo Ignites Haifa Bazan Oil Refinery; Kuwait Power Plant Hit, Indian Worker Killed
On March 30, a coordinated missile and rocket salvo by Iran and Hezbollah struck northern Israel, triggering sirens as far south as Herzliya and setting off a blaze at the Bazan oil refinery in Haifa — a facility previously hit on March 19. At least six impact sites were confirmed across Kiryat Ata, Haifa, and Shfaram. Two people were injured in Israel, one seriously. Separately, an Iranian missile or drone struck a power and water desalination plant in Kuwait, killing one Indian worker — the second foreign national killed in Kuwait during the conflict. A Kuwaiti military base was also struck, injuring ten Kuwaiti soldiers. Iran continued a pattern of targeting critical energy and water infrastructure across the Gulf throughout the day.
📍Haifa (Bazan refinery fire); Kiryat Ata; Kuwait power/desalination plant 💀2 injured in Israel (1 seriously); 1 Indian worker killed in Kuwait; 10 Kuwaiti soldiers wounded
Sources: Times of Israel Al Jazeera
31
Mar
US · Diplomacy & Escalation Political · Trump Threats & Talks
Trump Threatens to 'Completely Obliterate' Iran's Energy, Water & Desalination Plants; Claims 'Great Progress' in Talks as Tehran Denies Negotiations
On March 30–31, President Trump escalated his ultimatum to Iran, threatening via Truth Social to 'completely obliterate' all of Iran's energy sources — electric plants, oil wells, and possibly desalination plants — if a deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz is not reached. Trump simultaneously claimed 'great progress' in talks, asserting Iran had agreed to 'most of' the US 15-point ceasefire plan and had shipped 20 boatloads of oil to demonstrate seriousness. Tehran flatly contradicted those claims: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said there had been 'no negotiations with America' over the 31 days of war, only an exchange of messages via Pakistani intermediaries. Trump also told the Financial Times his preference would be to 'take the oil' in Iran and he was still weighing seizure of Kharg Island. The Pentagon confirmed a Tuesday briefing by Hegseth and Gen. Caine — the first in nearly two weeks — was scheduled for 8 a.m. ET. Pakistan said it was preparing to host 'meaningful talks' in Islamabad in the coming days, with the April 6 grace period for negotiations still in effect.
📍Washington DC / Islamabad (Pakistan mediation track) / Tehran ⚠️April 6 deadline for deal still active; USS Tripoli (3,500 Marines) arrived in theater; 50,000+ US special forces now in Middle East region
Sources: NBC News CNN
30
Mar
Israel · Strike Offensive · Infrastructure Shift
IDF Leaders Ordered to Cause Economic Damage to Iran After Nearing Completion of Military Target List
According to a Times of Israel report on March 31, Israeli political leaders have ordered the IDF to begin causing deliberate economic damage to the Iranian regime after the military came close to finishing strikes on all military targets outlined at the campaign's start. The IDF hit 170 regime targets in the preceding 24 hours, including the Imam Hussein University IRGC complex and a Tabriz petrochemical plant. Reports confirmed widespread power outages in Tehran and Alborz province following strikes on electrical infrastructure. Iranian opposition sources reported the killing of Hassan Hassanzadeh, commander of the Rasul-Allah IRGC Corps in Tehran, and the death from wounds of General Majid Zakariaei, commander of Iran's Natural Resources Protection Forces, injured in a March 28 strike. The IDF completed an additional extensive wave of strikes against Tehran infrastructure on the morning of March 31, with the military issuing a pre-strike warning to residents of the Vard Avar district on X in Farsi.
📍Tehran (Vard Avar district warned); Tabriz (petrochemical plant); Alborz Province (power grid) 💀Hassan Hassanzadeh (IRGC Rasul-Allah Corps Tehran cmdr) killed; Gen. Majid Zakariaei died of wounds (Mar 30)
Sources: Times of Israel Alma Research Center
29
Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Ballistic Missile / Chemical Plant Strike
Iran Strikes Ne'ot Hovav Chemical Plant; IRGC Wave 82–85 Hits US-Israeli Heavy Industry
On March 29, Iran launched the 82nd through 85th waves of Operation True Promise 4. The IRGC announced the 82nd wave on March 26 as the 'most extensive' axis-of-resistance operation to date, with 230 combined operations in 24 hours — including 87 Hezbollah sorties, 23 Iraqi Islamic Resistance strikes, and 110 IRGC missile and drone strikes. On March 27–28, the 84th and 85th waves targeted US and Israeli-linked heavy industrial sites using ballistic missiles and Ghadr-380 cruise missiles; an MQ-9 Reaper was claimed shot down over Shiraz and a US F-16 claimed struck in southern Pars province. A separate Iranian missile struck the Ne'ot Hovav industrial zone chemical plant in southern Israel on March 29, injuring one person and triggering fears of a hazardous leak. Joint US–Israeli strikes also bombed the port city of Bandar Khamir in southern Iran the same day, killing at least five people.
📍Ne'ot Hovav Industrial Zone, Israel; Bandar Khamir, Iran; Bubiyan Island, Kuwait; Shiraz, Iran 💀5+ killed in Bandar Khamir; 1 injured at Ne'ot Hovav chemical plant; multiple US casualties reported at Bubiyan Island (IRGC claim, unverified by CENTCOM)
Sources: Wikipedia – 2026 Iran War Al Mayadeen – Wave 85 PressTV – Wave 82
30
Mar
IDF · Strike Offensive · Air Defense Suppression
IDF Strikes Iranian Air Defenses Near Caspian Sea — 1,600 km from Israel
On March 30, the Israel Defense Forces announced that the Israeli Air Force struck Iranian air defense systems in northern Iran near the Caspian Sea — more than 1,600 kilometers (approximately 1,000 miles) from Israeli territory. The IDF stated the targeted site, concealed in a forested area, housed air defense systems that posed a threat to Israeli aircraft operating over Iran. The operation was based on IDF intelligence and was described as part of a wave of attacks carried out that Sunday. The IDF said: 'The strikes preserve, and further expand, the Israeli Air Force's aerial superiority over Iranian airspace.' No Israeli aircraft were reported lost. This action marks Israel's deepest confirmed air defense suppression strike of the conflict to date.
📍Northern Iran — near Caspian Sea, >1,600 km from Israeli territory ✈️No Israeli aircraft losses reported; IAF aerial superiority over all Iranian airspace confirmed extended
Sources: VINnews
30
Mar
Iran · Diplomacy / Threat Political · Trump Ultimatum / Peace Plan Rejection
Iran Rejects US 15-Point Peace Plan; Trump Threatens to Destroy All Power Plants & Oil Infrastructure
On March 30, Trump posted on Truth Social that if a deal was not reached soon and the Strait of Hormuz reopened, the US would conclude the war by destroying all of Iran's power plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, and possibly all its desalination plants. However, halfway through a 48-hour deadline Trump had previously set, he announced a five-day postponement of strikes on Iranian power plants, saying the US was negotiating with Iran to end the war. Iran categorically denied the talks were taking place and called the president 'deceitful.' Iran also formally rejected the US 15-point peace plan, and insisted that any ceasefire must include an end to the 2026 Lebanon war as a precondition. Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi said the US sending messages through 'friendly countries' did not constitute negotiation, calling Washington's shift in tone 'an admission of defeat.' On the same day, Turkey and NATO intercepted the fourth Iranian missile directed toward Turkish territory.
📍Washington D.C. / Tehran — diplomatic exchange via third-party intermediaries; Turkish airspace ⚠️Iran rejected 15-point US plan; Trump postponed power-plant strikes 5 days; 4th Iranian missile intercepted over Turkey
Sources: Wikipedia – 2026 Iran War CNN – Day 26 Live The Intel Drop
29
Mar
Israel · Strike Offensive · Industrial / Military-Industrial
Israel Strikes IRGC-Linked Steel Plants in Isfahan, Ahvaz; Palm Sunday Multi-Domain Escalation
On March 29 — Palm Sunday and Day 31 of Operation Epic Fury — Israel executed extensive strikes against Iranian military-industrial infrastructure in Tehran, Isfahan, and Ahvaz, specifically targeting IRGC-linked steel plants and academic facilities with alleged weapons research ties. Iran retaliated with 16 waves of ballistic missiles against Israel and struck UAE EMAL aluminium plants with 16 ballistic missiles and 42 UAVs over a 24-hour period. The Houthis formally entered the war on March 28, launching their first ballistic missile attacks against southern Israel, all of which were intercepted. CENTCOM confirmed a running total of 303 US troops wounded and 13 killed as of Day 30. An Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeeper was also killed near Adchit al-Qusayr in southern Lebanon — the first UN peacekeeper fatality of the conflict.
📍Isfahan & Ahvaz, Iran (IRGC steel plants); UAE EMAL aluminium plants; Southern Israel (Houthi missiles); Adchit al-Qusayr, South Lebanon (UNIFIL KIA) 💀1 Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeeper KIA (South Lebanon); US: 303 wounded / 13 KIA (cumulative CENTCOM Day 30)

31
Mar
Lebanon · Humanitarian Crisis Humanitarian · Mass Displacement / UNIFIL Casualties
Lebanon Displacement Surpasses 1.2 Million; Three UN Peacekeepers Killed in 24 Hours; Netanyahu Expands Invasion
As of March 30–31, Israeli forced evacuation orders and intensive bombardment of southern Lebanon, the Bekaa Valley, and Beirut's southern suburbs have displaced more than 1.2 million people — approximately one quarter of Lebanon's total population — in what NPR described as 'one of the biggest and fastest displacements in Lebanon's history.' Three United Nations peacekeepers were killed in the past 24 hours, though it remained unclear whether by Israeli or Hezbollah fire, according to NPR reporting on March 30. More than 1,200 Lebanese civilians have been killed since March 2. Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu announced an expansion of the Lebanon ground invasion. More than 120,000 of the displaced are sheltering in collective shelters, with many sleeping in streets or cars. The Lebanese government's figure of 1.2 million displaced represents a dramatic increase from 1 million registered on March 17.
📍Southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs (Dahiyeh); Jezzine area 💀1,200+ Lebanese killed (Health Ministry); 3 UNIFIL peacekeepers KIA in 24 hours (Mar 30); 1.2 million+ displaced
Sources: NPR – Lebanon Invasion NPR – Fear and Exhaustion Al Jazeera – Lebanon Attacks
31
Mar
Iran · Wave 87–88 Offensive · IRGC Naval & Aerospace Strike
IRGC Launches Waves 87 & 88 — Israeli Container Ship Hit in Persian Gulf; UAE Coast Targeted
On March 30–31, 2026, the IRGC executed the 87th and 88th waves of Operation True Promise 4. Wave 87, dedicated to slain IRGC Navy commander Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri, struck US command centers and military installations across the region, including a claimed hit on a C2 node near Al Minhad Air Base in the UAE and the US Fifth Fleet hideout in Bahrain. Wave 88, conducted in the early hours of March 31, saw IRGC naval forces strike the Israeli-flagged container ship Express Halfong with ballistic missiles in mid-Persian Gulf waters. Additionally, IRGC drones targeted a reported US Marine position along the UAE coast, and two early-warning radars at the Jaber al-Ahmad base in Kuwait were hit. The IRGC reiterated that the Strait of Hormuz remains under its control and warned that any enemy movement will be met with immediate strikes.
📍Persian Gulf (container ship) · UAE coast · Kuwait (Jaber al-Ahmad base) · Bahrain (5th Fleet area) 💥Israeli vessel Express Halfong struck by ballistic missiles; US C2 center and UAE Marine position targeted; Kuwait radars hit
Sources: Press TV / IRGC PR Al Mayadeen
31
Mar
US · Strike Offensive · USAF/B-2 Strike on Nuclear-Adjacent Site
US Strikes City Housing Key Iranian Nuclear Site; Massive Fireball Reported
In the early hours of March 31, 2026, US aircraft struck a city in Iran that serves as home to one of the country's main nuclear sites, sending a massive fireball into the sky, according to the Associated Press. The strike came as Trump simultaneously threatened to obliterate all of Iran's electric generating plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, and possibly all desalination plants if the Strait of Hormuz is not immediately reopened and a deal is not reached by an April 6 deadline. CENTCOM has now confirmed over 11,000 total targets struck since the start of Operation Epic Fury on February 28. The IDF stated it expects to complete targeting all critical Iranian military production sites — approximately 70% of Iran's defense industrial base — by April 1.
📍Iran nuclear-adjacent city (undisclosed, AP-confirmed) · Iranian defense industrial sites nationwide 💥Massive fireball reported; CENTCOM total: 11,000+ targets struck; IDF: ~70% of Iran's military production industry targeted
Sources: AP / KCTV5 Times of Israel
31
Mar
Iran · Maritime Attack Offensive · Tanker Strike off Dubai
Iran Strikes Kuwaiti Oil Tanker Off Dubai; Iran Hits Israeli Cargo in Persian Gulf
Overnight into March 31, 2026, Iran attacked the Kuwait Petroleum Corporation-owned very large crude carrier Al Salmi while it was anchored at the Dubai port anchorage area. The attack caused damage and sparked a fire on board. The UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) also confirmed a separate projectile impact on a tanker approximately 31 nautical miles northwest of Dubai, causing a fire on the vessel's starboard side. All crew were accounted for and the blaze was later extinguished with no oil spill. Separately, the UAE Defense Ministry announced it was intercepting new Iranian missiles and drones in the early hours of Tuesday. Iran also struck an ADAMA/Makhteshim chemical plant in the Ne'ot Hovav industrial zone of southern Israel on March 31, with no injuries reported and damage extent unclear. An Indian worker was killed in an Iranian attack on a Kuwaiti desalination and power plant on March 30.
📍Dubai port anchorage (VLCC Al Salmi) · Strait area NW of Dubai · Ne'ot Hovav, southern Israel · Kuwait desalination plant 💀1 Indian worker killed in Kuwait desalination attack (Mar 30); tanker crew safe; Israel plant strike — no injuries
Sources: Fox News Live NPR
31
Mar
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Talks Political · US–Iran Indirect Negotiations & Trump Kharg Island Threat
Trump Floats Seizing Kharg Island; Indirect Talks Progressing Toward April 6 Deadline; Iran Rejects 15-Point Plan
On March 30–31, 2026, Trump told the Financial Times he wants to 'take the oil' in Iran and that the US could capture Kharg Island, which handles 90% of Iran's crude exports. He simultaneously reiterated his threat to destroy all of Iran's electric plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, and possibly all desalination plants if a deal is not reached 'shortly' and the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed that Trump set an April 6 deadline for a deal and said talks are progressing, noting that what Iran says publicly differs from what it tells the US privately. Iran's foreign minister publicly rejected any dialogue and Iran formally dismissed the US 15-point peace plan as 'excessive and unreasonable.' Iran also conditioned any ceasefire on an end to the 2026 Lebanon war against Hezbollah. Pakistan and China released a joint five-point peace proposal calling for an immediate ceasefire and resumption of talks. Spain closed its airspace to all US warplanes involved in the Iran war on March 30. Secretary of State Rubio blasted NATO allies for 'abandoning' the US.
📍Washington D.C. · Tehran · Muscat (indirect channel) · Madrid (Spain airspace closure) 🕊️April 6 US deadline; Iran rejects 15-point plan; Pakistan–China 5-point ceasefire proposal issued; Spain bars US warplanes
Sources: Al Jazeera CBS News NPR
31
Mar
Israel · Strike Offensive · IDF Eliminates Hezbollah Unit 1800 Deputy Commander in Beirut
IDF Strike Kills Hezbollah Unit 1800 Deputy Commander Hamza Raqin and Operations Officer in Beirut
On March 31, 2026, the IDF carried out a targeted airstrike in Beirut that eliminated Hamza Ibrahim Raqin, the deputy commander of Hezbollah's Unit 1800, along with the unit's operations officer and another operative, according to the Alma Research and Education Center. Unit 1800 is Hezbollah's dedicated unit responsible for managing operations against Israel from within the Palestinian territories. The strike adds to a growing list of senior Hezbollah commanders killed since March 2. Separately, on March 31 the Lebanese army reported that an Israeli drone attack killed a Lebanese soldier and wounded five others at a checkpoint on the Qlileh–Tyre road. IDF Chief of Staff Zamir, in remarks released following his meeting with CENTCOM's Admiral Cooper at IDF HQ on March 29, stated that 'the Iranian regime's axis of terror is beginning to collapse.' Israel also confirmed it expects to complete strikes on all critical Iranian military production sites by April 1.
📍Beirut (Unit 1800 HQ) · Ameriyeh checkpoint, Tyre road, southern Lebanon 💀Hamza Ibrahim Raqin (Hezb Unit 1800 deputy cmdr) + ops officer + 1 operative killed; 1 Lebanese army soldier KIA, 5 WIA
Sources: Alma Research Center CNN Times of Israel
31
Mar
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · IDF Lebanon Expansion & UNIFIL Casualties
Three Indonesian UNIFIL Peacekeepers Killed in 24 Hours; IDF Kills 4 in South Lebanon; Katz Announces Permanent Buffer Zone
On March 30–31, 2026, three United Nations UNIFIL peacekeepers — all Indonesian nationals — were killed in southern Lebanon within a 24-hour period. One died from indirect artillery fire; two others were killed when an explosion of unknown origin destroyed their vehicle near Bani Haiyyan village. Indonesia's foreign minister called for a swift and transparent investigation. Israel said it is determining whether IDF fire or Hezbollah was responsible. Separately, on March 31 Israeli drone strikes killed four people in southern Lebanon — two in a car near Tyre and two in Derikifa. The IDF has now killed 10 soldiers in Lebanon since March 2, with 5 IDF soldiers killed in the past two days alone. Defense Minister Katz formally stated Israel will occupy southern Lebanon up to the Litani River indefinitely after the war's end, vowing to demolish border villages using the 'Rafah and Beit Hanoun model.' The Lebanese government sent a letter to the UN classifying Hezbollah's military wing as illegal. Displacement stands at over 1.2 million Lebanese, representing approximately one quarter of Lebanon's population.
📍Bani Haiyyan & Aadchit el-Qsair (UNIFIL) · al-Wasita / Tyre area · Derikifa, S. Lebanon 💀3 Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers KIA; 4 Lebanese civilians killed by IDF drones on Mar 31; 1,238+ total killed in Lebanon; 1.2M+ displaced
Sources: NPR Al Jazeera Alma Research Center
32
Mar 31
CENTCOM · Strike Offensive · Bunker-Buster Strike on Isfahan Ammunition Depot
US Drops 2,000-lb Bunker-Busters on Isfahan Ammo Depot; Massive Fireball Erupts
US forces struck a major ammunition depot in Isfahan — a city of 2.3 million and home to one of Iran's main nuclear enrichment sites — using 2,000-pound (907 kg) GBU-31 JDAM bunker-buster bombs, triggering a series of secondary explosions and towering fireballs visible for miles. President Trump shared footage of the strike on Truth Social without comment. The US Joint Chiefs chairman Gen. Dan Caine simultaneously confirmed that B-52 Stratofortress strategic bombers had begun flying over Iranian territory for the first time since the war began — a signal that Iran's air defenses have been severely degraded. The attack came as Defense Secretary Hegseth declared at a Pentagon briefing that 'the upcoming days will be decisive' and that Iran has 'almost nothing they can militarily do about it.'
📍Isfahan, central Iran — nuclear enrichment hub and Badr military airbase 💥2,000-lb bunker-buster bombs; B-52s overflying Iranian territory for first time 📊CENTCOM total: 11,000+ targets struck inside Iran since Feb. 28
Sources: Wall Street Journal / 10TV Foreign Policy Military.com

Mar 31
Iran · Escalation Offensive · IRGC Threatens 18 US Tech & Corporate Targets
IRGC Issues Unprecedented Threat Against 18 US Companies in Middle East; Strikes to Begin April 1 at 8 PM Tehran Time
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps issued a sweeping and unprecedented threat on March 31, naming 18 major American corporations as legitimate military targets in the Middle East, effective 8:00 PM Tehran time on April 1, 2026. The list includes Apple, Google, Microsoft, Meta, IBM, Dell, Intel, Oracle, HP, Cisco, Nvidia, Palantir, JP Morgan Chase, Tesla, Boeing, GE, Spire Solutions, and UAE-based AI firm G42. The IRGC stated the strikes were retaliation 'for every assassination' of Iranian officials, and advised employees to immediately evacuate their workplaces and urged all civilians within a one-kilometer radius of these companies' regional facilities to move to safety. Iranian forces had earlier struck the Amazon Web Services data centers in Bahrain and UAE early in the war. The threat came as US Secretary of State Rubio said 'fractures' had emerged in Iran's leadership structure and as both sides traded contradictory signals on whether talks were actively progressing toward Trump's April 6 deadline.
📍Gulf-wide — UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar corporate offices and data centers targeted ⚠️18 US companies named; civilians within 1km radius urged to evacuate 🕗Strike deadline: 8:00 PM Tehran time, April 1, 2026 (16:30 UTC)
Sources: Times of Israel Foreign Policy The Hill
32
Mar 31
Iran · Naval Strike Offensive · Drone Strike on Kuwaiti Oil Tanker Off Dubai
Iranian Drone Sets Kuwaiti Crude Tanker 'Al-Salmi' Ablaze Off Dubai Port; Oil Prices Spike
An Iranian drone struck and ignited the fully loaded Kuwaiti crude carrier Al-Salmi while at anchor in the Dubai Port area in the UAE, setting the vessel ablaze in one of the most significant attacks on a merchant vessel since the war began. Kuwait's state-owned oil company KUNA confirmed the attack was 'direct and malicious.' Dubai authorities subsequently reported the fire was contained and all 24 crew members were safe, with no oil spill detected. The attack sent oil prices spiking, with Brent crude ending March at $119.01/barrel — a record monthly gain of 64%. The strike came hours after Trump renewed his threat to obliterate Iran's energy infrastructure and power plants if Tehran did not reopen the Strait of Hormuz and agree to a peace deal before the April 6 deadline. The UAE air defense additionally intercepted 8 ballistic missiles, 4 cruise missiles and 36 drones launched from Iran on the same day.
📍Dubai Port anchorage area, UAE — Persian Gulf 🛢️Fully loaded crude carrier; fire contained, 24 crew safe, no spill 💹Brent crude hit $119/barrel (+64% month-on-month) following strike
Sources: CNN Outlook India CBS News
32
Mar 31
Pentagon · Briefing Strategic · Hegseth & Caine: 'Upcoming Days Will Be Decisive'; Ground Options Not Ruled Out
Hegseth & Joint Chiefs Chair Caine Warn 'Decisive Days' Ahead; Refuse to Rule Out Ground Invasion
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chairman Gen. Dan Caine held a high-stakes Pentagon briefing on March 31, declaring 'the upcoming days will be decisive' and warning that US firepower is 'only increasing' while Iran's is 'decreasing.' Hegseth confirmed the US would 'negotiate with bombs' until Iran surrenders its nuclear ambitions and reopens the Strait of Hormuz. When pressed on whether the 82nd Airborne paratroopers now arriving in the region could be used in a ground assault, Hegseth declined to rule it out, stating 'there are 15 different ways we could come at them with boots on the ground.' Hegseth also criticized NATO allies, noting that requests for 'simple access, basing and overflight' had met 'questions or roadblocks or hesitations.' Secretary Rubio separately told ABC News that 'fractures' had emerged inside Iran's leadership, though he declined to identify the officials Washington was secretly engaging with, citing safety concerns.
📍Pentagon, Washington D.C. — Hegseth press briefing ⚔️82nd Airborne paratroopers arriving in region; ground options openly discussed 🤝Rubio: 'fractures' in Iranian leadership; secret interlocutors not named
Sources: Military.com Air Force Times CBS News

Mar 31
Iran · IRGC Leadership · IRGC Confirms Killing of Maj. Gen. Jamshid Eshaghi
IRGC Confirms Death of Maj. Gen. Jamshid Eshaghi, Head of Budget & Finance at Iran's Armed Forces General Staff
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps confirmed the killing of Brigadier General Jamshid Eshaghi, the head of budget and financial affairs at Iran's armed forces general staff, in a US-Israeli airstrike. The IRGC cited Eshaghi's death as the direct trigger for its unprecedented threat against 18 American corporations in the Middle East. His killing is part of a broader campaign that Israel says has eliminated at least 52 senior Iranian leaders since the war began on February 28, including Supreme Leader Khamenei, IRGC commander Pakpour, Defense Minister Nasirzadeh, national security adviser Larijani, intelligence minister Khatib, and IRGC Navy commander Alireza Tangsiri.

IRGC Deadline Passes: April 1 8 PM Tehran Time — 18 US Companies Named for Strikes
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) set a hard deadline of 8:00 PM Tehran time on April 1, 2026, to begin striking the Gulf-region facilities of 18 named US technology and defense companies. The full list published via the IRGC's official Sepah News outlet includes Cisco, HP, Intel, Oracle, Microsoft, Apple, Google, Meta, IBM, Dell, Palantir, Nvidia, JPMorgan Chase, Tesla, General Electric, Boeing, Spire Solutions, and UAE-based AI firm G42. The IRGC framed the action as retaliatory escalation for what it called 'targeted assassinations' of Iranian commanders, including Maj. Gen. Jamshid Eshaghi, killed in a US-Israeli strike on March 31. The statement urged all employees of the named companies in the Gulf to leave their workplaces immediately and advised civilians within a one-kilometer radius of company facilities to evacuate. The US State Department separately issued a shelter-in-place advisory for US citizens in Saudi Arabia near hotels, US businesses, and educational institutions. No strikes had been confirmed at time of dashboard update.

Mar
US · Diplomacy Diplomatic · Energy Deadline Extension
Trump Extends Pause on Iran Energy Strikes to April 6; Claims Talks 'Going Very Well'
President Donald Trump on March 26, 2026 announced via Truth Social that he was extending his pause on US strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure by 10 days, to April 6 at 8:00 PM Eastern Time. Trump claimed the extension came 'as per Iranian Government request,' and insisted that talks were 'going very well.' The move followed an earlier five-day pause Trump had announced on March 23 after he claimed the US and Iran were having 'very good and productive conversations.' Iran publicly denied that any direct negotiations with the US had taken place, with Tehran calling the US proposals 'unrealistic.' The 15-point US peace proposal, transmitted via Pakistani intermediaries, reportedly demands a permanent end to uranium enrichment, limits on Iran's missile capacity, and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Iran countered with five conditions of its own, including reparations for war damages and international recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz — terms the US dismissed. On March 29, Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf publicly rejected the ongoing negotiations, saying Iran could not be forced into submission. Trump claimed the same day that Iran had agreed to 'most' US demands.
📍Washington DC / Tehran (via Pakistani mediators) 🕗New deadline: April 6, 2026 at 8:00 PM ET 🇺🇸15-point US proposal: zero enrichment, missile limits, Strait of Hormuz reopening 🇮🇷Iran denies direct talks; parliament speaker Ghalibaf rejects negotiations
Sources: CNBC Al Jazeera CBS News

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Houthi Entry
Houthis Enter War with First Ballistic Missile Strikes on Israel; Iran Hits Gulf Aluminium Plants
On March 28, 2026 — the one-month anniversary of Operation Epic Fury — Yemen's Houthi movement formally entered the conflict by launching their first ballistic missile strikes on southern Israel. All missiles were intercepted. In parallel, Iran struck the Aluminium Bahrain and Emirates Global Aluminium plants with 16 ballistic missiles and 42 UAVs over a 24-hour period. CENTCOM separately confirmed on Day 28 that 13 US troops had been killed in action and 303 wounded. The Pentagon also confirmed the staging of 4,500 additional US Marines to the region. Israeli and US airstrikes continued to pound Iranian military-industrial targets across Tehran, Isfahan, and Ahvaz.
📍Southern Israel (Houthi), Bahrain, UAE (Iranian aluminium plant strikes) 💀CENTCOM: 13 US KIA confirmed, 303 wounded as of Day 28 🚀Houthi: first ballistic missile salvos at Israel — all intercepted 🏭Iran struck Aluminium Bahrain and Emirates Global Aluminium with 16 BMs + 42 UAVs

Mar
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Mass Missile Barrage
Iran Fires 16 Missile Waves at Israel on Palm Sunday; First UN Peacekeeper Killed in Lebanon
On March 29, 2026 — Palm Sunday, Day 31 of Operation Epic Fury — Iran launched 16 waves of ballistic missiles against Israel over a 24-hour period, one of the highest single-day launch totals in weeks despite the overall 90%+ suppression of Iranian missile capacity. Israel executed counter-strikes against IRGC-linked steel plants and academic facilities with alleged weapons research ties in Tehran, Isfahan, and Ahvaz. An Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeeper was killed near Adchit al-Qusayr in southern Lebanon, becoming the first UN peacekeeper fatality of the conflict. Iran also struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, wounding at least 15 US service members. Separately, Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf publicly rejected ongoing ceasefire negotiations with the United States, declaring Iran could not be 'forced into submission.' Trump claimed on the same day that Iran had agreed to 'most' of America's demands — a characterization Iran denied.

On March 30, 2026, President Trump posted on Truth Social that if a deal was not reached and the Strait of Hormuz reopened, the US would 'destroy all of Iran's power plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, and possibly all its desalinization plants.' On the same day, the US flew B-52 strategic bombers over Iranian territory for the first time in the conflict, targeting an ammunition depot and air base in Isfahan with massive explosions. Turkey and NATO intercepted the fourth Iranian missile directed toward Turkish territory; Iran denied firing it, calling the incident a false-flag attack. Apr
Iran · Escalation Offensive · IRGC Deadline Passes / Three Missile Waves on Israel
IRGC April 1 Deadline Passes; Iran Fires Three Missile Waves at Israel at Dawn
The IRGC's self-imposed deadline of 8:00 PM Tehran time on April 1 — threatening to destroy the Middle East facilities of 18 named U.S. companies including Microsoft, Apple, Google, Meta, IBM, Tesla, Boeing, Nvidia, Palantir, and JPMorgan — passed amid heightened regional security alerts. The IRGC had warned employees to evacuate workplaces and urged civilians within one kilometer of targeted facilities to relocate, citing the companies' alleged role in 'planning and tracking assassination targets' of Iranian officials. Separately, Iranian state media confirmed the Revolutionary Guards fired three waves of missiles at Israel within a single hour in the early morning of April 1, with Israeli police confirming damage in Tel Aviv. An oil tanker off Qatar's coast was also struck by one of three Iranian missiles launched from Iran, with no injuries reported. Meanwhile, President Trump said the war could be 'finished' in two to three weeks and that no deal was required, while Defense Secretary Hegseth said the U.S. was 'negotiating with bombs.' Iran's FM Araghchi told Al Jazeera that messages had been exchanged with Washington but declared Tehran has 'zero' trust in the U.S. and that there are no negotiations. China and Pakistan jointly proposed a five-point peace plan calling for an immediate ceasefire, reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and a UN-backed peace process.
📍Tel Aviv (missile damage confirmed); tanker struck off Qatar's coast; IRGC company-target deadline covers UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar 💀No confirmed casualties from April 1 IRGC company strikes or tanker hit as of filing time 🚀Three IRGC missile waves on Israel within one hour; 3 missiles fired toward Qatar coast, 1 hit tanker

Mar
US/Israel · Strikes Offensive · Isfahan Ammunition Depot & IRGC Navy Commander Killed
US Bombers Destroy Isfahan Ammunition Depot; IDF Kills IRGC Navy Chief Tangsiri
US B-52 bombers conducted massive overnight strikes on a major IRGC ammunition depot and air base in Isfahan on the night of March 30–31, producing vast explosions visible from miles away and confirmed by NASA thermal imagery. CNN geolocated fires near Mount Soffeh in Isfahan. Separately, the IDF announced the killing of IRGC Navy Commander Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri in a strike on Bandar Abbas, a confirmation also relayed by Pakistani officials involved in mediation. The strikes marked the north-eastern-most coalition operations conducted so far in the war, according to ISW analysis. Israel simultaneously accelerated targeting of Iranian arms factories — including an IRGC headquarters near Bonab in East Azerbaijan Province and the 7th Artesh Air Force Tactical Airbase in Shiraz — in anticipation of a possible ceasefire declaration. An Indian worker was separately killed in an Iranian attack on a Gulf power and desalination plant on March 30, and Iran struck the Zanjan Grand Hosseinieh religious center, killing three people.
📍Isfahan (ammunition depot/air base); Bandar Abbas (IRGC Navy HQ); Bonab, East Azerbaijan; Shiraz, Fars Province 💀Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri (IRGC Navy Commander) KIA; 3 killed in Iranian strike on Zanjan mosque; 1 Indian worker killed in Gulf desalination plant strike

Apr
Iran · Diplomatic Diplomatic · Ceasefire Dispute
Iran Denies Ceasefire Request; Trump Conditions Halt on Hormuz Opening
On April 1, 2026, President Trump posted on Truth Social claiming that Iran's 'New Regime President' had asked the United States for a ceasefire, stating the US would 'consider' it only once the Strait of Hormuz was 'open, free, and clear,' threatening to otherwise continue 'blasting Iran into oblivion.' Tehran flatly denied the claim. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi told Al Jazeera that Iran was not seeking a ceasefire and that 'at present there is no negotiation.' A senior Iranian official separately denied Trump's claim through state channels. Qatar's Defense Ministry meanwhile reported that one of three missiles launched from Iran struck an oil tanker in Qatari territorial waters, with no injuries reported. Trump also announced a 10-day extension of the pause on strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure, pushing the next deadline to April 6.
📍Truth Social / Al Jazeera / Qatar — Multiple theaters 💬Iran denies ceasefire request; Qatar tanker struck by Iranian missile, no casualties

Apr
CENTCOM · Milestone Offensive · Air Operations Milestone
CENTCOM: US Forces Surpass 12,000 Combat Flights Over Iran
On April 1, 2026, U.S. Central Command announced that American forces had flown over 12,000 combat flights directly supporting Operation Epic Fury since operations began on February 28. CENTCOM also confirmed that Iran's ballistic missile launch rate had fallen 90% from Day 1 and drone launch rates had dropped 83%, reflecting the sustained degradation of Iranian offensive capabilities across more than a month of continuous air operations. The announcement came alongside Trump's prediction that combat operations could cease within two to three weeks. CENTCOM also denied Iranian and some media allegations that US forces had struck civilian areas in Lamerd, stating the munition depicted in circulating footage was consistent with an Iranian Hoveyzeh cruise missile, not a US weapon.

Iran Fires Three Missiles at Qatar; Oil Tanker Hit in Qatari Waters
On April 1, 2026 — the day Iran's self-imposed IRGC deadline expired — the IRGC launched a fresh missile salvo toward Qatar. Qatar's Defense Ministry confirmed that one of three missiles launched from Iran struck an oil tanker in the country's territorial waters. No injuries were immediately reported. The strike represents a continued Iranian pattern of targeting Gulf energy and maritime infrastructure despite sharply degraded launch capabilities. Iran's 83rd-plus wave of retaliatory strikes has now encompassed oil tankers, Gulf military bases, and Israeli territory across more than 30 days of sustained operations.

Apr
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · Mass Displacement
Over 1 Million Displaced in Lebanon; Beirut Overwhelmed One Month Into War
As Operation Epic Fury entered its 33rd day, Lebanon's displacement crisis reached a staggering scale. More than 1 million people — roughly 20% of Lebanon's entire population — have fled southern Lebanon, the Beqaa Valley, and Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahiyeh) since Israeli strikes and ground operations began on March 2. The UN refugee agency's spokesperson in Lebanon described the figure as 'almost certainly an undercount.' Israel has declared its intent to establish a permanent security buffer zone up to the Litani River, some 15–20 miles north of the Israeli border. IDF ground forces are currently holding positions up to 10 kilometers deep inside Lebanon. Hezbollah continues to fire hundreds of rockets daily at northern Israel. Lebanon's Health Ministry put the civilian death toll at 1,268, while approximately 400 Hezbollah fighters have also been killed. The UN's UNFPA representative described the 2026 crisis as operating on 'a whole different level' compared to the severe 2024 conflict.
📍Southern Lebanon, Dahiyeh (Beirut), Beqaa Valley 👥1,000,000+ displaced (≈20% of Lebanon's population); 1,268 killed per Lebanon Health Ministry
Sources: Philadelphia Inquirer / AP CNN NPR
1
Apr
US · Diplomatic / Military Diplomatic · Address to Nation
Trump Addresses Nation; Claims Iran Requested Ceasefire, Ties Acceptance to Hormuz Opening
President Trump delivered a prime-time address to the nation on April 1 — his first major national address since the war began on February 28 — promising to reaffirm a two-to-three-week timetable for concluding US operations. Earlier in the day, Trump posted on Truth Social claiming Iran's 'New Regime President' had asked for a ceasefire, which he said the US would 'consider' only once the Strait of Hormuz is 'open, free, and clear.' Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson immediately called Trump's claim 'false and baseless,' and a spokesperson for President Pezeshkian's office stated Iran remains 'determined' to fight on. Pakistani officials — serving as intermediaries — confirmed continued back-channel messaging between the two sides, though direct talks have not been arranged. Trump also told British newspaper The Telegraph he was 'absolutely' considering withdrawing the US from NATO, following European allies' refusal to join the war effort or police the Strait of Hormuz.
📍Washington D.C. · White House address, 9 p.m. ET 🕊️Iran denies ceasefire request; Pakistan serving as back-channel intermediary ⚠️Trump threatens NATO withdrawal over allied non-participation

Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile Strike
Iran Fires Missiles at Bnei Brak; UAE Intercepts 438th Ballistic Missile; USS George H.W. Bush Carrier Group Deploys
On April 1, Iranian forces continued multi-front strikes across the theater. Israeli emergency services photographed a missile impact site in Bnei Brak, a densely-populated suburb of Tel Aviv, triggering sirens across the region. The UAE Ministry of Defense updated its cumulative interception tally to 438 ballistic missiles, 19 cruise missiles, and 2,012 drones intercepted since the war began. Meanwhile, the USS George H.W. Bush carrier strike group — comprising more than 6,000 sailors — was confirmed en route to the Middle East alongside three destroyers, and thousands of soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division began arriving in theater, further expanding the US force presence beyond the 50,000+ already confirmed by CENTCOM.

Apr
US · Intelligence / Casualties Investigative · Pentagon Cover-Up Reported
Intercept: Pentagon Concealing True US Casualty Count; At Least 520+ Wounded, 15+ KIA Confirmed
The Intercept published an investigation on April 1 reporting that the Pentagon has been sending outdated and incomplete casualty statements, resulting in significant undercounts. At least 15 US troops have been confirmed killed since the war began — including six in the March 1 Port Shuaiba drone strike in Kuwait and one at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia on March 1. The investigation found that more than 520 US personnel have been wounded, a figure substantially higher than the '303 wounded' figure cited in CENTCOM's most recent official statement. The report noted that the official count also excludes more than 200 sailors treated for smoke inhalation following a fire aboard the USS Gerald R. Ford. Additionally, at least 15 more troops were wounded on March 28 in an Iranian strike of six ballistic missiles and 29 drones on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia — a strike that also damaged several US refueling aircraft and an E-3 Sentry surveillance aircraft.
📍Prince Sultan Air Base (Saudi Arabia) · Port Shuaiba (Kuwait) · USS Gerald R. Ford 💀15+ US KIA confirmed; 520+ wounded per The Intercept investigation ⚠️CENTCOM official figure: '303 wounded' — excludes USS Ford smoke casualties

Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · New Missile & Drone Wave
Iran Announces New Wave of Strikes Targeting Israel and US Gulf Bases
On the evening of April 2, 2026, Iran's military central command, Khatam Al-Anbiya, announced a new wave of missile and drone attacks targeting Israeli cities including Tel Aviv and Eilat, as well as US military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait. Israel's air defenses reported responding to three waves of Iranian missile fire in the early hours, with media reporting several light injuries in the Tel Aviv area. The IDF confirmed multiple impact sites across central Israel. Iranian attacks also continued across Gulf states, with nearly 90 incidents recorded between March 28 and April 1 alone, demonstrating increased accuracy against airports, energy infrastructure, and ports.
