1
Apr
Israel · Strike Targeted Assassination · Hezbollah Southern Front Commander
IDF Navy Kills Hezbollah Southern Front Commander Hashem in Beirut Jnah Strike
On April 1, 2026, the Israeli Navy executed a targeted strike on Beirut's Jnah district, killing Haj Youssef Ismail Hashem, commander of Hezbollah's Southern Front — described by a Hezbollah source as 'the highest-ranking official to be targeted since the start of the war.' Hashem, a senior commander with over 40 years of experience, had taken over the Southern Front from Ali Karaki following Karaki's death alongside Hassan Nasrallah in September 2024. Hezbollah member Mohammad Baqir al-Naboulsi was also killed in the same strike. The attack killed at least 7 people and wounded 26 others according to Lebanese health authorities. Hezbollah confirmed Hashem's death, calling him a 'beacon of the Islamic Resistance.' More than 400 Hezbollah fighters have been killed since March 2, per Reuters sources.
📍Jnah district, Beirut, Lebanon 💀7 killed, 26 wounded (Lebanese Health Ministry); Hashem + al-Naboulsi confirmed KIA
Sources: Reuters / US News Al Jazeera Times of Israel
1
Apr
Iran · Diplomatic/Strike Strike on Diplomat · Ceasefire Dispute
Airstrike Wounds Former FM Kharazi, Kills Wife; Iran Calls US Demands 'Maximalist'; CENTCOM Reports 12,300+ Targets Struck
On April 1–2, 2026, a US-Israeli airstrike targeted the Tehran home of Kamal Kharazi — former Iranian foreign minister (1997–2005) and current head of Iran's Strategic Council on Foreign Relations — seriously wounding him and killing his wife. Iranian officials described the attack as an attempt to derail diplomacy, noting Kharazi was overseeing backchannel engagement with Pakistan for a possible meeting between Iranian officials and US Vice President JD Vance. Separately, CENTCOM announced it had struck over 12,300 targets in Iran since the start of the war. Iran called US ceasefire demands 'maximalist and irrational' and denied any negotiations were taking place, while Trump insisted on the Strait of Hormuz opening as a precondition. Airstrikes on Hengam Island in the Strait of Hormuz caused seven additional injuries. Netanyahu asserted US-Israeli strikes had eliminated Iran's ability to produce nuclear weapons and ballistic missiles.
📍Tehran (Kharazi residence); Hengam Island (Strait of Hormuz) 💀Kharazi seriously wounded, wife killed; 7 injured on Hengam Island
Sources: AFP / Dawn Middle East Eye Wikipedia – 2026 Iran war France 24
2
Apr
Iran · Maritime Strike Offensive · Cruise Missile / Tanker Attack
Iran Hits Oil Tanker Off Qatar; Strikes Kuwait Airport — Day 34
On April 2, 2026, Iran fired a cruise missile that struck an oil tanker off the coast of Qatar while simultaneously attacking Kuwait's airport, continuing multi-domain pressure on Gulf energy and logistics infrastructure. The strikes came as CENTCOM confirmed its 12,300+ target figure remained current and as airstrikes continued to batter Tehran. Iran's FM Abbas Araghchi told Al Jazeera there are 'no negotiations' and that Iran did not request a ceasefire, directly contradicting a Trump Truth Social claim that Iran's 'New Regime President' had asked for a truce. Trump responded that the U.S. would only 'consider' a ceasefire once the Strait of Hormuz is 'open, free, and clear,' adding 'Until then, we are blasting Iran into oblivion.' Iran's own five-point counterproposal retains sovereignty over the strait and demands war reparations — terms the U.S. called nonstarters.
📍Strait of Qatar / Kuwait International Airport / Tehran (continued strikes) 💥Oil tanker struck by cruise missile off Qatar coast; Kuwait airport attacked 🕊️Diplomatic: Trump claims ceasefire request; Iran denies; no talks confirmed
Sources: Military.com / AP CNBC
1
Apr
Coalition · Strategic Strike Offensive · Government Infrastructure
Israel Completes Wave on Iranian Govt Infrastructure; Houthis Enter War with First Ballistic Missile on Israel
On April 1, 2026, Israel announced it had completed a wave of strikes targeting government infrastructure inside Iran, with Iranian state media reporting explosions across Tehran in the early morning hours. Separately, the IDF intercepted the first ballistic missile fired at southern Israel by Yemen's Houthis, formally widening the conflict. Iran continued its retaliatory strikes, launching several ballistic missile barrages at Israel — all intercepted by the IDF according to Israeli military statements. The CFR conflict tracker confirmed both events. Meanwhile, Trump stated the war would be concluded within two to three weeks and announced he was set to address the nation regarding the conflict.
📍Tehran (IAF wave); southern Israel (Houthi missile); multiple Israeli intercept sites 💀No immediate casualties reported in IAF Tehran wave; Houthi missile intercepted, no casualties 🚀First Houthi ballistic missile targeting Israel confirmed intercepted by IDF
Sources: CFR Global Conflict Tracker Times of Israel
29
Mar
Iran · Multi-Domain Retaliation Offensive · 16-Wave Ballistic Barrage + UAE EMAL Strike
Palm Sunday — Iran Fires 16 Missile Waves at Israel; Strikes UAE Aluminium Plants with 16 BMs + 42 UAVs
On March 29, 2026 — Palm Sunday and Day 31 of Operation Epic Fury — Iran launched 16 waves of ballistic missiles against Israel and struck UAE EMAL aluminium plants with 16 ballistic missiles and 42 UAVs over a 24-hour period. Israel simultaneously executed extensive strikes against Iranian military-industrial infrastructure in Tehran, Isfahan, and Ahvaz, targeting IRGC-linked steel plants and academic facilities with alleged weapons-research ties. An Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeeper was killed near Adchit al-Qusayr in southern Lebanon — the first UN peacekeeper fatality of the conflict. Iran also struck Prince Sultan Air Base, wounding at least 15 US service members.
📍Israel (16 missile waves); UAE EMAL aluminium plants; Tehran/Isfahan/Ahvaz (IAF); Prince Sultan AB, Saudi Arabia 💀15+ US service members wounded at Prince Sultan AB; 1 Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeeper KIA in southern Lebanon 🏭UAE EMAL hit by 16 ballistic missiles + 42 UAVs; IRGC-linked steel plants in Isfahan and Ahvaz struck by IAF
Sources: IranWarLive OSINT Recap Al Jazeera Tracker
28
Mar
Iran · War Anniversary / Houthi Entry Escalation · Houthis Formally Enter Conflict
One-Month Anniversary: Houthis Launch First Strikes on Israel; Iran Hits Gulf Aluminium Plants; Pentagon Stages 4,500 Marines
On March 28, 2026 — the one-month anniversary of Operation Epic Fury — Yemen's Houthis formally entered the war by launching their first ballistic missile attacks on southern Israel, all of which were intercepted. Iran simultaneously struck Aluminium Bahrain and Emirates Global Aluminium. CENTCOM confirmed 303 US troops wounded and 13 killed as of this date. The Pentagon began staging 4,500 additional Marines in the region in preparation for potential contingencies, even as Trump denied any plans for a ground invasion of Iran.
📍Southern Israel (Houthi BM); Aluminium Bahrain; Emirates Global Aluminium (UAE) 💀CENTCOM: 303 US wounded, 13 KIA confirmed as of Mar 28; all Houthi missiles intercepted ⚓Pentagon stages 4,500 Marines in theater; CENTCOM confirms 50,000+ total US troops in region
Sources: IranWarLive OSINT Recap Kurdistan 24 / CENTCOM
33
Apr
Israel · Strike Offensive · Pharmaceutical Factory / Fentanyl-CW Program
Israel Strikes Tofigh Daru Factory Allegedly Supplying Fentanyl to IRGC Chemical Weapons Program
On Tuesday, April 1, 2026 (Day 33), the Israel Defense Forces struck the Tofigh Daru Research and Engineering Company factory in Tehran, alleging it served as a central supplier of fentanyl to the SPND organization — Iran's body tasked with chemical weapons development. The IDF stated that the factory 'presented itself as a civilian company' but systematically transferred fentanyl to the IRGC for research and development of chemical weapons. Iran acknowledged the strike but insisted the facility only produced 'hospital drugs' for medical purposes. Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi posted images of the factory on X, writing that Israel was 'openly and unashamedly bombing pharmaceutical companies.' The IDF also completed two separate waves of airstrikes on Iranian regime infrastructure sites on the morning of April 1, with footage showing widespread damage in central Tehran including shattered shop windows and debris-strewn streets near the strike site. Strikes also reportedly hit an area near the former US Embassy compound in Tehran — currently controlled by the IRGC — with witnesses reporting shattered windows and damage within the walled facility on Taleghani Street. Iranian media additionally reported US-Israeli strikes hit steel industry complexes at the Mobarakeh Steel Company in Isfahan and a subsidiary in Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari province.
📍Tofigh Daru Factory, Tehran; former US Embassy compound area, Tehran; Mobarakeh Steel Co., Isfahan 💀No confirmed casualties from factory strike; casualties from parallel Tehran strikes TBD 🎯IDF: 400+ targets struck in Iran over preceding 48 hours (IAF statement)
Sources: Times of Israel AP / News4Jax Al Jazeera
4
Apr
Iran · Maritime Strike Offensive · Tanker Strike + Airport Attack
Iran Fires Three Cruise Missiles at Qatar, Hitting LPG Tanker; Drone Ignites Fire at Kuwait International Airport
On April 1–2, 2026 (Day 34), Iran fired three cruise missiles at Qatar. According to Qatar's Ministry of Defence, two were intercepted while the third struck the Indian-flagged LPG carrier Jag Vasant off Qatar's coast. All 21 crew members were safely evacuated with no injuries or environmental damage reported. Separately, a drone struck a fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport, sparking a large fire per Kuwait's state-run KUNA news agency. Jordan's military said it intercepted a ballistic missile and two drones fired from Iran in the preceding 24 hours. Two drones were also intercepted in Saudi Arabia. In the UAE, one person was killed in Fujairah when hit by debris from an intercepted drone. Iran also struck an oil tanker off Dubai the previous day, one of more than 20 ships attacked by Iran during the war. Missile sirens sounded across central Israel, with AP reporters hearing loud booms in Tel Aviv. Iran's ongoing attack tempo — despite its launch rate being degraded roughly 90% since Day 1 — continues to disrupt Gulf shipping and energy infrastructure, maintaining the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
📍Off Qatar coast (Jag Vasant LPG tanker); Kuwait International Airport; Fujairah, UAE; central Israel 💀0 casualties on struck tanker (crew evacuated); 1 killed in UAE (interceptor debris); Kuwait airport fire — no casualties confirmed 🚢Jag Vasant (India-flagged LPG carrier) struck by Iranian cruise missile — 21 crew evacuated safely
Sources: AP / Washington Times Euronews ABC27 / AP
4
Apr
Israel · Lebanon Strike Offensive · Targeted Assassination — Senior Hezbollah Commander
IDF Kills Hezbollah Southern Front Commander Ali Youssef Hashem in Beirut Strikes; 9 Total Killed
In the early hours of April 1–2, 2026 (Day 34), Israeli forces conducted two strikes in the Beirut area targeting senior Hezbollah members. The IDF later confirmed it killed Ali Youssef Hashem, commander of Hezbollah's southern front — the most senior Hezbollah commander killed since Lebanon was drawn into the war on March 2. The first strike hit a vehicle in the Khaldeh area south of Beirut, killing two people and wounding three. A second naval-origin strike hit parked vehicles on a street in the Jnah area, which borders Hezbollah's stronghold in south Beirut, killing at least seven people and wounding 26. Hezbollah confirmed its senior member Mohammad Baqir Al Nabulsi — nephew of the group's spokesman killed in 2024 — was also killed in the Jnah strike. A third strike was carried out in Beirut's Hadath district, with no immediate reported fatalities. Israel simultaneously struck multiple sites in southern Lebanon and the West Bekaa area. At around midnight, Hezbollah fired a barrage of more than 40 rockets at northern Israel's Galilee region. Lebanon's total death toll since March 2 rose to 1,318 killed and 3,935 wounded per the Lebanese Health Ministry as of April 2.
📍Jnah district, Beirut (naval strike); Khaldeh, south of Beirut; Hadath district, Beirut; southern Lebanon; West Bekaa 💀9 killed (7 in Jnah, 2 in Khaldeh); 26+ wounded in Beirut strikes; Lebanon cumulative: 1,318 killed / 3,935 wounded 🎯Ali Youssef Hashem (Hezbollah southern front commander) confirmed killed; Mohammad Baqir Al Nabulsi also killed
Sources: The National Al Jazeera SANA / Lebanese Health Ministry
4
Apr
US · Diplomacy/Speech Political · Trump National Address on Operation Epic Fury
Trump Addresses Nation: War 'Nearing Completion,' Vows 'Extremely Hard' Strikes Over Next 2–3 Weeks; Conditions Ceasefire on Hormuz Opening
On the evening of April 1, 2026 (Day 34), President Trump delivered a 19-minute prime-time address from the White House Cross Hall — his first such address since the war began on February 28. Trump declared that US military objectives were 'nearing completion' and that he expected to complete the operation 'very shortly,' while simultaneously threatening to hit Iran 'extremely hard' over the next two to three weeks. He threatened to obliterate all of Iran's electricity-generating plants and oil sites if Iran's leaders refuse to make a deal. Earlier in the day, Trump posted on Truth Social claiming Iran's 'New Regime President' had asked the US for a ceasefire — a claim Iran flatly denied. Iran's FM Araghchi told Al Jazeera: 'At present there is no negotiation.' Trump conditioned any ceasefire consideration on the Strait of Hormuz being 'open, free, and clear.' The speech came as the Pentagon confirmed 348 US troops wounded and 13 KIA. CENTCOM simultaneously announced it had struck over 12,300 targets and damaged or destroyed over 155 Iranian vessels since the war began. The IDF confirmed 800+ Israeli strikes in Iran. Pakistan and China issued a joint statement calling for a ceasefire and reopening of Hormuz, with Pakistani FM Ishaq Dar having visited Beijing for talks. Markets reacted negatively: Brent crude jumped to ~$106/barrel and S&P 500 futures fell 0.75% after the speech.
📍White House, Washington D.C. — nationally televised address 📊CENTCOM: 12,300+ targets struck, 155+ Iranian vessels damaged/destroyed, 13,000+ combat flights flown 🛢️Brent crude: ~$106/bbl post-speech; US avg gas price >$4/gallon for first time in 3+ years
Sources: CBS News CNBC CNBC / Ceasefire Daily Post Nigeria / CENTCOM
1
Apr
Iran · Strike Offensive · Assassination Attempt / Diplomacy Disruption
Airstrike Kills Wife of Former FM Kharazi; Backchannel Peace Talks Disrupted
On April 1, Mehr News Agency — a semi-official Iranian government outlet — reported that senior policy adviser and former Iranian Foreign Minister Kamal Kharazi was seriously wounded and his wife was killed when an airstrike struck his private residence. Iranian officials described the attack as a deliberate attempt to derail ongoing diplomatic engagement: Kharazi had been overseeing a backchannel through Pakistani intermediaries aimed at arranging a possible meeting between Iranian officials and U.S. Vice President JD Vance. Separately, airstrikes struck the strategically vital Hengam Island in the Strait of Hormuz, causing seven injuries. Trump, in the same window, indicated he was no longer concerned about Iran's enriched uranium stockpiles buried underground, signaling a possible shift in stated war objectives.
📍Tehran (Kharazi residence) + Hengam Island, Strait of Hormuz 💀1 killed (Kharazi's wife); Kharazi seriously wounded; 7 injured on Hengam Island 🕊️Backchannel via Pakistan to arrange Vance–Iran talks disrupted per Iranian officials
Sources: Wikipedia – 2026 Iran War Kenya Times – CENTCOM Update
1
Apr
CENTCOM · Strike Update Defensive / Offensive · Milestone Report
CENTCOM Confirms 12,300+ Targets Struck, 155+ Iranian Vessels Destroyed, 13,000+ Combat Flights Flown
U.S. Central Command issued its latest operational dashboard update on April 1, confirming that coalition forces have struck more than 12,300 targets across Iran and surrounding waters since Operation Epic Fury commenced on February 28. CENTCOM also reported conducting over 13,000 combat flights and damaging or destroying more than 155 Iranian naval vessels across the course of the campaign. The April 1 release highlighted sustained high-tempo operations involving B-2 stealth bombers, F-35 fighters, aircraft carrier strike groups, guided-missile destroyers, and multiple drone systems. CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper noted that Iranian naval forces have largely ceased sailing, tactical aircraft sorties have dropped sharply, and the rate of Iranian missile and drone launches has declined significantly versus the opening days of the conflict. Iran's armed forces warned in response of 'crushing, broader, and more destructive' attacks to come as the war enters its fifth week.
📍Iran-wide + Persian Gulf / Arabian Sea theater 🎯12,300+ targets struck; 155+ Iranian naval vessels damaged or destroyed; 13,000+ combat flights ⚠️Iran warns of escalated retaliation; CENTCOM releases strike footage of drones, tanks, missile launchers
Sources: Middle East Eye – CENTCOM 12,300 Targets Al Jazeera – CENTCOM Strike Video Kenya Times
1
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Wave 89, Operation True Promise 4
IRGC Fires 100+ Missiles & Drones in Wave 89; Targets Israeli Tanker, USS Abraham Lincoln Carrier Group
Iran's IRGC announced the 89th wave of Operation True Promise 4 in the pre-dawn hours of April 2, claiming it launched over 100 heavy missiles and drones at enemy positions. Iranian naval forces conducted five separate naval strike operations as part of the wave, including a claimed precision strike on the Israeli-affiliated tanker Aqua One in the central Persian Gulf, which the IRGC stated was set ablaze. Additionally, multiple waves of explosive drones were launched toward the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group operating in the northern Indian Ocean. The IRGC claimed in a statement — citing satellite imagery — that the carrier group had retreated from its previous position deeper into the Indian Ocean. U.S. officials had not confirmed damage to the carrier group. Iran's armed forces simultaneously warned that the intensity of Iranian strikes contradicted U.S. and Israeli claims that launch rates had fallen 90%.
📍Central Persian Gulf (Aqua One tanker); Northern Indian Ocean (USS Abraham Lincoln); Israel + Gulf states 🚢Israeli-linked tanker Aqua One reported struck and burning; USS Abraham Lincoln group reportedly targeted ⚠️IRGC claims carrier group retreated — unconfirmed by U.S. officials
Sources: GlobalSecurity.org – Wave 89 Statement
2
Apr
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Political · Trump Ceasefire Conditions / Tehran Denial
Trump Claims Iran Asked for Ceasefire; Tehran Denies — Hormuz Opening Set as Precondition; April 6 Deadline Looms
On April 1–2, a sharp public dispute erupted over ceasefire diplomacy. President Trump claimed on Truth Social that Iran's 'New Regime President' Masoud Pezeshkian had asked the U.S. for a ceasefire. Trump stated the U.S. would 'consider' the offer only once the Strait of Hormuz is 'open, free, and clear,' warning that until then the U.S. was 'blasting Iran into oblivion.' Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi flatly denied the claim to Al Jazeera: 'At present there is no negotiation.' Tehran has separately rejected the U.S. 15-point ceasefire plan — transmitted via Pakistani intermediaries — which analysts described as amounting to unconditional surrender terms covering zero nuclear enrichment, missile restrictions, and an end to support for regional armed groups. Trump had issued a deadline of April 6 for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face strikes on power plants. Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has made no public appearances since assuming leadership following his father's killing.
🕊️Trump: ceasefire only after Hormuz opens; April 6 deadline for power plant strikes 🇮🇷Iran denies any ceasefire request or ongoing negotiations; rejected 15-point U.S. plan 📋U.S. plan reportedly covers: zero enrichment, missile caps, end to proxy support
Sources: CNBC Foreign Affairs PBS NewsHour
2
Apr
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · Displacement Crisis
Lebanon Displacement Reaches 1.2 Million; 14 European FMs Warn of 'Breaking Point'; UNIFIL Peacekeepers Killed
A joint statement by 14 European foreign ministers released April 1 described Lebanon as at a 'breaking point,' confirming that 1.2 million people — approximately 25% of Lebanon's entire population — are now internally displaced. The statement cited the Lebanon Health Ministry figure of more than 1,000 killed, the majority civilians including children, aid workers, and journalists. A UN Security Council emergency session was called by France after three Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers were killed within a 24-hour period in two separate incidents in southern Lebanon — the worst single-day peacekeeper losses of the conflict. The UN relief chief warned that 'a cycle of coercive displacement is unfolding,' with entire villages south of the Litani River flattened and most bridges destroyed. Hezbollah continues to fire rockets into northern Israel while Israel deepens its ground operation, with Defense Minister Katz stating that displaced Lebanese will not be allowed to return south until the Hezbollah threat is eliminated.
📍Southern Lebanon, Beqaa Valley, Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahiyeh) 👥1.2 million displaced (25% of Lebanese population); 1,000+ killed per Health Ministry 🇺🇳3 Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in 24-hour period; UN Security Council emergency session convened
Sources: GlobalSecurity.org – Joint Statement GlobalSecurity.org – Lebanon Breaking Point NPR Inquirer / Washington Post
2
Apr
Iran · Missile & Drone Offensive · Reduced Salvo
IDF Reveals Iran's Passover Attack Plan Failed; Launches 400+ Targets Over Two Days
The IDF disclosed that during the 24-hour Passover window (approx. 6 a.m. to 6 a.m. Apr 2), Iran managed to fire fewer than half of its planned ~20 morning launches and fewer than 10 of its planned ~20 evening launches — a dramatic sign of degraded capability. Concurrently, beginning around 4 p.m. local time on Apr 2, the Israeli Air Force completed 20 attack sorties dropping more than 140 munitions on approximately 50 ballistic missile sites in Iran. Separately, the IDF confirmed it struck more than 400 targets in Iran over the two days ending Apr 2, hitting air defense systems and a Defense Ministry compound used for weapons production. Lebanon saw roughly 130 launches cross into Israel from Hezbollah during the same period.
📍Iran-wide (ballistic missile sites); Lebanon (Hezbollah rocket launches into northern Israel) 💀No confirmed new mass-casualty figures from this specific salvo; Iranian launches down ~90%+ vs. Day 1
Sources: Israel Hayom NPR
2
Apr
US · Address to Nation Political · Presidential Address
Trump Declares War 'Nearing Completion'; Threatens Iranian Power Grid if No Deal; 2–3 Week Timeline
In his first primetime address to the nation on the Iran war, delivered on the evening of April 2 from the White House Cross Hall, President Trump said Operation Epic Fury is 'nearing completion' and projected two to three more weeks of combat. He warned that if no deal is reached, the US would strike 'each and every one of their electric generating plants very hard and probably simultaneously.' Trump reiterated that he has not struck Iranian oil — calling it 'the easiest target of all' — but left open the threat. He claimed regime change had been accomplished through the deaths of Iran's original leadership, and said Iran's new leaders are 'less radical and much more reasonable.' Earlier that day, Trump had posted on Truth Social that Iran's 'New Regime President' asked for a ceasefire, which the US would only consider after the Strait of Hormuz is 'open, free, and clear.' Tehran denied both the ceasefire request and any negotiations. Secretary of State Rubio and Joint Chiefs Chairman Gen. Dan Caine flanked Trump during the address.
📍White House, Washington D.C. 🕊️Strait of Hormuz reopening set as precondition; April 6 deadline context; Pakistan mediating backchannel messages
Sources: CNN CNBC Axios
2
Apr
Iran · Infrastructure Coalition Strike · Dual Infrastructure
US Strikes Collapse B1 Bridge Between Tehran and Karaj; 8 Killed, 95 Wounded
Two US airstrikes struck the B1 bridge connecting Tehran and Karaj — described as the highest bridge in the Middle East — causing it to collapse. Iranian authorities reported eight people killed and 95 wounded. The IDF separately denied responsibility for the strike. The attack represented a major escalation in targeting dual-use civilian and strategic infrastructure near the Iranian capital. The bridge had served as a critical artery between Tehran and the satellite city of Karaj.
📍B1 Bridge, Tehran–Karaj corridor, Iran 💀8 killed, 95 wounded (Iranian authorities)
Sources: Wikipedia / 2026 Iran War Al Jazeera
2
Apr
IDF · Leadership Strike Targeted Kill · IRGC Command
IDF Strike in Kermanshah Kills Iranian Ballistic Missile Chief Makram Atimi and Senior Commanders
The Israel Defense Forces announced that a precision airstrike in the Kermanshah area of western Iran killed Makram Atimi, chief of Iran's ballistic missile forces, along with several battalion commanders from his central Iranian ballistic missile unit. The strike was part of the IDF's intensified campaign to systematically dismantle Iran's entire missile production and command chain. Israeli defense sources confirmed that strikes have shifted from regime decapitation to focused degradation of weapons production sites and military assets, with IDF officials noting they had already hit over 1,700 military-industrial assets.
📍Kermanshah area, western Iran 💀Makram Atimi (IRGC Ballistic Missile Chief) confirmed killed; multiple battalion commanders killed
Sources: Wikipedia / 2026 Iran War Haaretz
2
Apr
Iran · Strait Economic · Hormuz Legislation
Iran Parliament Approves Bill to Charge Vessels for Hormuz Transit; IRGC Warns of 'Crushing' Broader Strikes
Iran's parliament this week approved legislation to charge vessels for crossing the Strait of Hormuz, formalizing its economic blockade strategy. The move came as CENTCOM released footage of fifth-week air strikes and Iran's armed forces issued a warning of 'crushing, broader and more destructive' retaliatory attacks to come. Oil tanker traffic through the strait remained at a near standstill: no tankers transited on Tuesday April 1, with only isolated bulk carriers making the voyage. An estimated 20,000 seafarers remained stranded aboard vessels in the active war zone, with the IMO negotiating their evacuation. The World Food Programme warned that tens of thousands of tons of food aid were stuck in regional ports.
📍Strait of Hormuz; Iranian parliament, Tehran 🛢️~20% of global oil supply blocked; Brent crude hovering near $100/barrel
Sources: NPR CNBC Al Jazeera
2
Apr
Iran · Infrastructure Coalition Strike · Port & Desalination
IAF Bombs Shahid Haqqani Passenger Pier in Bandar Abbas; Qeshm Desalination Plant Knocked Out
Israeli Air Force jets struck the Shahid Haqqani passenger pier in the strategic port city of Bandar Abbas, according to Ahmad Nafisi, a senior official in Iran's Hormozgan province, who condemned the attack on civilian infrastructure. No casualties were immediately reported from the pier strike. Separately, a desalination plant on Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz was struck and put entirely out of service, Iranian media reported, disrupting fresh water access to surrounding communities. The strikes reflected the continued broadening of coalition target sets to include civilian-adjacent dual-use infrastructure along Iran's southern coast.
📍Bandar Abbas passenger pier; Qeshm Island desalination plant, Strait of Hormuz 💀No casualties confirmed from pier strike; desalination outage affects surrounding villages
Sources: Al Jazeera
3
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Saudi Air Base Attack
Iran Strikes Saudi Air Base; 15+ US Troops Wounded — Pentagon Accused of Covering Up Casualty Count
At least 15 U.S. troops were wounded in an Iranian missile and drone attack on a Saudi air base hosting American forces, according to two government officials who spoke with The Intercept. The report also alleged that CENTCOM has been issuing outdated casualty statements resulting in significant undercounts. A CENTCOM spokesperson had previously stated approximately 303 US service members had been wounded since the start of Operation Epic Fury — a figure The Intercept said was already outdated. The attack occurred amid broader Iranian strikes continuing to target the infrastructure supporting US airpower across at least seven bases in Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia and the UAE. A U.S. Air Force E-3 Sentry AWACS was among aircraft previously damaged in a March 27 strike on Prince Sultan Air Base; five KC-135 tankers were damaged on March 13.
📍Saudi Air Base (location unspecified by officials); multiple US forward bases across Gulf region 💀15+ US troops wounded (Apr 1 attack, The Intercept); 303+ total US wounded per outdated CENTCOM statement
Sources: The Intercept / Joe My God Defense News
2
Apr
Lebanon · Humanitarian Crisis · Mass Displacement
Lebanon Displacement Exceeds 1.2 Million; Three Indonesian UNIFIL Peacekeepers Killed; 14 European FMs Issue Joint Warning
Lebanon's humanitarian crisis deepened dramatically through early April, with the number of internally displaced persons surpassing 1.2 million — approximately 25% of the overall population — according to a joint statement by 14 European foreign ministers. More than 1,318 people have been killed in Lebanon per the Health Ministry, with over 3,700 injured. Three Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers were killed in two separate incidents within a 24-hour period, prompting France to call an emergency UN Security Council meeting. UN peacekeeping chief Jean-Pierre Lacroix warned that the Council's support for UNIFIL is 'indispensable.' The IRC reported over 300,000 displaced children, while some 180,000 people had crossed the Lebanon–Syria border by late March. Israel's Defense Minister Katz reiterated plans for a permanent 'security zone' up to the Litani River, with officials suggesting some areas may remain occupied post-war.
📍Southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs; UNIFIL zone 💀1,318+ killed in Lebanon (Health Ministry, Apr 1); 3 UNIFIL peacekeepers killed; 1.2M+ displaced
Sources: GlobalSecurity / Joint FM Statement GlobalSecurity / UNIFIL Breaking Point IRC
3
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Wave 89 / Infrastructure Strike
IRGC Fires Wave 89; Kuwait Mina Al-Ahmadi Refinery Hit; Iranian F-35 Shootdown Claim
On Day 35 of Operation Epic Fury, the IRGC announced the 89th wave of Operation True Promise 4, claiming to have fired 100+ heavy missiles and drones at enemy positions. Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery was struck by a drone early on April 3, sparking fires across several sections of the facility; emergency teams were deployed by Kuwait News Agency accounts. Separately, Iranian media claimed a second US F-35 was shot down over central Iran — a report the US did not confirm. Israel's Home Front Command issued warnings of possible rocket and missile fire near Malkiya, Kiryat Shmona, Tel Hai, and Metula in Upper Galilee, and also alerted residents to a 'hostile aircraft intrusion' in the Western Galilee area. Iran also released a list of eight major bridges across the Middle East it threatened to target, following the US collapse of the B1 bridge in Karaj.
📍Kuwait (Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery); Upper Galilee, Israel; Persian Gulf theater 💥Wave 89 of Operation True Promise 4; 100+ missiles and drones claimed; refinery fires confirmed by KUNA ⚠️Iranian media claims second F-35 downed — unconfirmed by CENTCOM
Sources: India TV News (Live) GlobalSecurity / PressTV (Wave 89)
3
Apr
US · Command Shakeup Political · Pentagon Leadership
Hegseth Fires US Army Chief Gen. Randy George
On Day 35, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked US Army Chief General Randy George to step down and take immediate retirement, a significant leadership shakeup during an active major combat operation. The move drew immediate scrutiny given the ongoing prosecution of the Iran campaign and unresolved questions about Pentagon casualty undercounts. The Intercept has reported that CENTCOM has been sending outdated statements resulting in underreported US casualties, including a Monday statement citing approximately 303 US service members wounded — a figure critics say is already out of date.
📍Pentagon, Washington DC 👤Gen. Randy George, US Army Chief of Staff — forced retirement ⚠️Occurs amid Pentagon criticism over casualty reporting transparency
Sources: India TV News The Intercept / Joe.My.God
2
Apr
Iran · Diplomacy Political · Ceasefire Dispute
Trump Claims Iran Requested Ceasefire; Tehran Denies — Zarif Floats Exit Roadmap
On April 2, President Trump posted on Truth Social that Iran's president had asked the US for a ceasefire, but said the US would only 'consider' any offer once the Strait of Hormuz was 'open, free, and clear.' Tehran flatly denied the claim, with Foreign Minister Araghchi telling Al Jazeera 'At present there is no negotiation.' Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei made no public appearances. Separately, former FM Javad Zarif went public with a roadmap he said could allow Iran to 'declare victory and stop the war,' explaining he felt conflicted but believed the war should end in a way that protects Iranian national interests. The Trump administration's previously transmitted 15-point peace plan via Pakistani intermediaries — described by Foreign Affairs as amounting to unconditional surrender terms — remains rejected by Tehran, which has issued its own counter-demands including Hormuz control and war reparations.
📍Washington DC / Tehran / Pakistani intermediary channel 🕊️Iran denies ceasefire request; 15-point US plan rejected; Zarif roadmap floated publicly ⚠️Trump grace period for negotiations extended to ~April 6 per Foreign Affairs
Sources: CNBC Foreign Affairs India TV News
2
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Infrastructure
Iran Strikes UAE EMAL Aluminium Plant and B1 Tehran–Karaj Bridge Collapses After US Strike
On April 2, Trump addressed the nation saying strikes have 'dramatically curtailed' Iranian missile and drone systems and vowed to escalate operations over the next two to three weeks. Two US strikes on the B1 bridge between Tehran and Karaj — described as the highest bridge in the Middle East — killed eight people and wounded 95, causing the structure to collapse. Iran listed eight major regional bridges as future retaliation targets in response. On the same day, CENTCOM released new strike footage targeting drones, tanks, and missile launchers, as Iran's armed forces warned of 'crushing, broader and more destructive' attacks ahead. The IRGC also claimed a tanker named Aqua One linked to Israel was hit and set ablaze in the Persian Gulf, and launched multiple waves of drones toward the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group in the northern Indian Ocean.
📍B1 Bridge (Tehran–Karaj); Persian Gulf; Indian Ocean (USS Abraham Lincoln area) 💀B1 bridge collapse: 8 killed, 95 wounded (Iranian report) 🚢IRGC claims Aqua One tanker hit; USS Abraham Lincoln group targeted with drone waves
Sources: Wikipedia – 2026 Iran War Al Jazeera (CENTCOM video) GlobalSecurity / IRGC Wave 89
1
Apr
Iran · Leadership Strike Blowback · Diplomacy
Kamal Kharazi Seriously Injured in Airstrike; Wife Killed — Iran Says Attack Targeted Backchannel to Vance
On April 1, Mehr News Agency reported that prominent policy expert and former Iranian foreign minister Kamal Kharazi was seriously injured and his wife killed when an airstrike hit his home. Iranian officials described the attack as a deliberate attempt to derail diplomacy, noting that Kharazi had been overseeing preparations for a possible meeting between Iranian officials and US Vice President JD Vance. Separately, airstrikes on the strategically important Hengam Island in the Strait of Hormuz caused seven injuries. Trump indicated on April 1 that he is 'no longer concerned' about Iran's enriched uranium buried underground, signaling a possible shift in stated war aims. Netanyahu separately asserted that the strikes have eliminated Iran's ability to produce nuclear weapons and ballistic missiles.
📍Tehran (Kharazi residence); Hengam Island, Strait of Hormuz 💀Kharazi wife killed; Kharazi seriously injured; 7 injured on Hengam Island 🕊️Iran: strike targeted Vance backchannel diplomat; Trump shifts stance on buried uranium stockpiles

3
Apr
Iran · Wave 89 Offensive · Mass Missile & Drone Salvo
IRGC Fires Wave 89 — 100+ Heavy Missiles & Drones; Tanker Aqua One Struck in Persian Gulf
The IRGC announced the 89th wave of Operation True Promise 4, confirming the launch of over 100 heavy missiles and drones targeting US military bases and Israeli positions across the region. Iranian naval forces conducted five separate operations as part of Wave 89, including striking the Israeli-linked tanker Aqua One in the central Persian Gulf, which was left burning. At dawn, multiple waves of explosive drones were also launched toward the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group in the northern Indian Ocean; Iran claimed via satellite imagery that the carrier group had repositioned. The IRGC statement said further strikes would continue throughout the day. Iranian media disputed US-Israeli claims of a 90% reduction in Iranian launch rates, calling the characterisation false.
📍Persian Gulf / Indian Ocean / West Asia — multi-domain salvo 🚢Israeli-linked tanker Aqua One struck and burning in Persian Gulf 🛥️USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group reportedly forced to reposition
Sources: GlobalSecurity / PressTV
3
Apr
US · Carrier Ops Defensive · Naval Repositioning
USS Gerald R. Ford Departs Croatia After Repairs — Destination Undisclosed
The USS Gerald R. Ford, the largest aircraft carrier deployed to the Arabian Sea theater, departed Croatia after five days in port undergoing repairs following a fire that broke out on board on March 12. The US Navy confirmed the departure but did not reveal the carrier's next destination, citing operational security as US forces continue to reposition assets across the Middle East. The fire, which injured multiple sailors, originally forced the Ford to transit to Souda Bay, Greece, and then to Split, Croatia. The Intercept also reported that CENTCOM casualty figures may be understated, noting that the 200+ sailors treated for smoke inhalation from the Ford fire do not appear in official wounded counts.
📍Croatia (Split) → destination undisclosed ⚠️200+ sailors treated for smoke inhalation not reflected in official CENTCOM wounded tally (The Intercept)
Sources: Al Jazeera The Intercept
3
Apr
Diplomacy · Zarif Roadmap Political · Exit Roadmap Elaborated
Zarif Elaborates Exit Roadmap on X — Says War Must End Protecting Iran's National Interests
Former Iranian Foreign Minister Javad Zarif elaborated publicly on his proposed roadmap for Iran to declare victory and exit the war, explaining on X why he decided to make the proposal public. He said he felt conflicted — angry over Trump's rhetoric but proud of Iran's military and people — yet believed the war should end in a way that protects Iran's national interests. His intervention follows Iran's rejection of the US 15-point peace plan on March 25, which Tehran described as tantamount to unconditional surrender. Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf separately rejected the negotiations on March 29, saying Iran could not be 'forced into submission.' Trump extended the Strait of Hormuz ultimatum deadline to April 6, citing 'very good and productive conversations,' which Iran denied took place.
🕊️Zarif proposes public 'exit roadmap' — Tehran denies any active ceasefire talks 📅Trump's Hormuz ultimatum extended to April 6, 2026
Sources: India TV News / Live Blog Foreign Affairs

Apr
Iran · Threat Escalatory · Infrastructure Targeting List
Iran Releases List of 8 Major Middle East Bridges as Potential Retaliation Targets
Iran's semi-official Fars News Agency published a list of eight major bridges across the Middle East that could become Iranian military targets in retaliation for recent US-Israeli strikes, including the collapse of the B1 Tehran–Karaj bridge on April 2. The IRGC is implied to be responsible for any future strikes. The announcement signals a potential tit-for-tat infrastructure campaign after Trump's April 2 address threatened further escalation over the next two to three weeks. Iran's armed forces simultaneously warned that the war would continue until the US and Israel face 'humiliation' and 'surrender,' and cautioned Washington against any ground invasion.

Apr
Iran · Shootdown Claim Air Combat · F-35 Shootdown Claim (Unconfirmed)
Iranian Media Claims Second US F-35 Shot Down Over Central Iran — Pentagon Silent
Iranian state media claimed that a second US F-35 fighter jet was shot down over central Iran, with the US military having not confirmed the report. Al Jazeera's Day 35 live blog noted that 'Iranian state media says a US fighter jet has been shot down over Iran' and that the US military launched a search-and-rescue operation in the area. The US has not confirmed the loss. This follows Iran's earlier Wave 89 claims and a prior unconfirmed F-35 shootdown report already noted on the dashboard from April 3 morning. The US denial of the first claim remains standing; the second claim is assessed as preliminary and unconfirmed.
📍Central Iran — exact location unspecified ⚠️Unconfirmed — US military has not acknowledged; SAR operation reportedly launched

3
Apr
Lebanon · Humanitarian Crisis · Mass Displacement Update
Lebanon Displacement Tops 1.2 Million — 25% of Population; Beirut at Breaking Point
European foreign ministers issued a joint statement on April 1 noting that Lebanon now has 1.2 million internally displaced persons — approximately 25% of the entire population — calling the situation 'appalling.' Lebanon's Health Ministry confirmed more than 1,318 people killed, including civilians, children, aid workers, and journalists. The IRC reported that over 1 million people, including 300,000+ children, have been displaced, with evacuation orders covering an estimated 15% of the country's territory. Around 180,000 people have crossed from Lebanon into Syria as of March 26. Three Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers were killed in two separate incidents within 24 hours, prompting an emergency UN Security Council meeting called by France. Displaced Shiite families from southern Lebanon and Beirut's Dahiyeh suburb are crowding into Beirut proper, raising fears of long-term demographic shifts. The UN refugee agency spokesperson called the situation 'an imminent humanitarian catastrophe.'
📍Southern Lebanon, Beqaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs — nationwide displacement 👥1.2M+ displaced (25% of population); 1,318+ killed; 3,700+ injured (IRC, Apr 1) 🇺🇳3 Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers KIA — first UN peacekeeper fatalities of conflict
Sources: GlobalSecurity / EU Joint Statement IRC GlobalSecurity / UN Briefing
3
Apr
USAF · Combat Loss Defensive · SAR Operation
Iran Shoots Down F-15E Strike Eagle; A-10 Downed in SAR Mission
On April 3 (Day 36), Iranian air defenses shot down a U.S. Air Force F-15E Strike Eagle over Iran, prompting an immediate search and rescue operation by U.S. helicopters. The two-seat jet was crewed by a pilot and a weapon systems officer (WSO); the pilot was successfully rescued by U.S. forces while the search for the WSO remained ongoing. During the SAR mission, an A-10 Warthog was also shot down — its pilot was successfully rescued — and two Black Hawk helicopters were struck by small-arms fire but returned safely. The incident marks the most significant confirmed U.S. fixed-wing aircraft combat loss of Operation Epic Fury beyond the earlier Kuwait friendly-fire incident.
📍Central Iran (exact location undisclosed) ✈️F-15E crew: pilot rescued, WSO search ongoing; A-10 pilot rescued 🚁2× Black Hawks hit by small-arms fire during SAR
Sources: Wikipedia – 2026 Iran War The Intercept

Apr
Iran · Wave 89 Offensive · Multi-Domain Strike
IRGC Declares Wave 89 of Operation True Promise 4 — 100+ Missiles & Drones
The IRGC announced Wave 89 of Operation True Promise 4, involving over 100 heavy missiles and drones fired at enemy positions, including naval operations targeting what Iran described as an Israeli-linked tanker ('Aqua One') in the central Persian Gulf, which was reported burning. Iranian naval forces also launched multiple waves of explosive drones toward the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group in the northern Indian Ocean, with IRGC state media claiming the carrier group 'fled its previous position.' CENTCOM and the U.S. Navy did not confirm damage to the vessel. Iran also struck Abu Dhabi gas facilities at Habshan, halting operations, and the IRGC announced attacks on American steel industries in the UAE.

Apr
IDF · Targeted Killing Offensive · Leadership Strike
IDF Eliminates IRGC Oil HQ Commander Jamshid Eshaqi & IRGC Commando Chief Fath Ali Zadeh
Israeli airstrikes conducted on April 1–2 eliminated two senior IRGC figures. Jamshid Eshaqi, commander of the oil headquarters of the Iranian regime and responsible for financing IRGC forces and Iran's proxy network, was killed. In the same strike package, Mohammad Ali Fath Ali Zadeh, commander of the 'Fatehin' commando unit of the IRGC ground forces, was also eliminated. The IDF simultaneously struck IRGC weapons production sites and a central Ministry of Defense complex in Tehran, a 'Khordad 15' missile base in Isfahan, fuel storage at Mashhad International Airport, and IRGC targets in Mahshahr.

Apr
Iran · Diplomacy Strike Strike · Diplomatic Escalation
Airstrike Kills Wife of Former FM Kharazi; Iran Claims Attack Targeted Peace Backchannel
On April 1, Mehr News Agency reported that former Iranian Foreign Minister Kamal Kharazi was seriously injured and his wife was killed when an airstrike struck his home. Iranian officials described the attack as an attempt to derail diplomacy, noting that Kharazi had been overseeing a proposed meeting between Iranian officials and U.S. Vice President JD Vance. The strike drew international condemnation and complicated already-stalled ceasefire negotiations. The Trump administration had previously sent a 15-point ceasefire proposal through Pakistani intermediaries, which Iran rejected on March 25, countering with five conditions of its own including sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and war reparations.

Apr
US · Command Shakeup Command · Leadership
Hegseth Fires U.S. Army Chief General Randy George Amid Active Wartime Operations
U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth abruptly dismissed U.S. Army Chief of Staff General Randy George and two other senior Army officers on April 3, ordering immediate retirement. The move sparked significant concern among military analysts and lawmakers, occurring on Day 35 of active combat operations with Iran. No official explanation was provided beyond a terse statement from the Pentagon. The firing follows earlier congressional friction over casualty reporting and comes amid The Intercept's reporting that CENTCOM has sent outdated statements on troop casualties, with at least 520+ U.S. personnel wounded and 15+ killed — figures disputed as undercounts by multiple officials.
🏛️Pentagon, Washington D.C. ⚠️Gen. Randy George (Army Chief) + 2 senior officers dismissed; immediate retirement ordered 📋CENTCOM casualty undercount controversy ongoing — 520+ wounded per The Intercept

3
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Energy Infrastructure
Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi Refinery Hit by Drone Strike; Fires Erupt Across Multiple Sections
In the early hours of April 3, Iranian drones struck Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery, sparking fires across several sections of the facility. Emergency response and firefighting teams were rapidly deployed to contain the blaze. The Kuwait News Agency (KUNA) confirmed the attack and the ongoing firefighting response. The strike follows Iran's continuing campaign against Gulf energy infrastructure, including prior attacks on UAE aluminum plants, Qatar's LNG export capacity, and Saudi oil fields. The IRGC also separately announced attacks on American steel industries in Abu Dhabi on April 2–3.
📍Mina Al-Ahmadi Refinery, Kuwait 🔥Fires ignited in multiple refinery sections; emergency crews deployed 🛢️Part of Iran's sustained campaign against Gulf energy infrastructure
Sources: India TV News – Apr 3 Live
2
Apr
Iran · Houthis Offensive · New Front
Houthis Enter War — 4th Missile Detected Launched from Yemen Toward Israel
Israel detected a fourth ballistic missile launched from Yemen by the Houthis, the latest in an escalating series of Houthi attacks since the group formally entered the conflict on March 28 — the one-month anniversary of Operation Epic Fury. All prior Houthi missiles were intercepted. Israel's multi-layer air defense system, including the Arrow system, has engaged Houthi projectiles alongside Iranian and Hezbollah fire. The Houthis have framed their involvement as solidarity with Iran and Palestinian resistance, and have pledged to continue attacks on Israeli territory.
📍Trajectory: Yemen → Israel (southern/central region) 🚀4th Houthi missile since Mar 28; prior 3 intercepted
Sources: India TV News – Apr 3 Live IranWarLive OSINT Recap
3
Apr
USAF · Aircraft Loss Defensive · SAR Operation
Iran Shoots Down USAF F-15E; A-10 Downed During SAR Mission
On April 3, Iranian air defenses shot down a U.S. Air Force F-15E Strike Eagle over Iranian airspace, prompting an immediate search and rescue mission by U.S. helicopters. The jet carried two crew members — a pilot and a weapon systems officer (WSO). The pilot was successfully extracted by U.S. forces; the search for the WSO remains ongoing, with American special forces reported to have entered Iran to locate him. During the SAR operation, an A-10 Warthog close-air-support aircraft was also downed near the Strait of Hormuz, though its pilot was rescued. Two Black Hawk helicopters were struck by small-arms fire during the operation but landed safely. Iran broadcast a $60,000 bounty for the missing WSO's capture and released footage purporting to show the downed A-10. The IDF separately halted strikes in the search zone to avoid endangering rescue personnel.
📍Central Iran / Strait of Hormuz area ✈️1 F-15E lost (SAM) + 1 A-10 lost (SAR) + 2 Black Hawks damaged 🔍Pilot rescued; WSO search ongoing — US special forces on ground in Iran

1
Apr
Iran · Wave 89 Offensive · Mass Missile & Drone Barrage
IRGC Launches Wave 89: 100+ Missiles & Drones; Tanker Struck in Persian Gulf
The IRGC announced Wave 89 of Operation True Promise 4 on April 1, firing over 100 heavy missiles and drones at Israeli and U.S.-linked targets across West Asia. In the Persian Gulf, an Israeli-linked tanker commercially identified as Aqua One was struck and set ablaze. The IRGC also claimed to have launched multiple waves of explosive drones toward the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group in the northern Indian Ocean, asserting via satellite imagery that the carrier group had repositioned deeper into the ocean. Strikes continued against Gulf-state infrastructure, with the UAE recording 19 ballistic missiles and 26 UAVs intercepted on April 2 alone. Since the war's start, 457 ballistic missiles, 19 cruise missiles, and 2,038 UAVs have been launched at the UAE. Iran's military vowed attacks would continue 'continuously, uninterruptedly, and shot after shot.'
📍Persian Gulf, Indian Ocean (USS Abraham Lincoln area), UAE, Israel 🚢Israeli-linked tanker 'Aqua One' struck and burning in Persian Gulf 💀Ongoing regional casualties; UAE intercepted 19 BMs + 26 UAVs on Apr 2

1
Apr
IDF · Tehran Strikes Offensive · Multi-Site Strike Package
IDF Strikes IRGC HQs, Missile Bases & Defense Ministry in Tehran, Isfahan, Mashhad, Mahshahr
Over April 1–2, the IDF conducted extensive strike packages across multiple Iranian cities. In Tehran, targets included a central complex of the Iranian Ministry of Defense, an IRGC ground forces base, a mobile IRGC headquarters, central IRGC financial headquarters responsible for funding proxy groups worldwide, air defense systems, launch sites, and ballistic missile production and storage facilities. In Isfahan, the 'Khordad 15' missile base was struck. In Mashhad, fuel storage facilities at the international airport were hit. In Mahshahr, additional IRGC targets were destroyed. The IDF separately struck over a dozen Hezbollah infrastructure targets in southern Lebanon on April 1–2, including headquarters, weapons depots, and Hezbollah financial transfer nodes. On April 3, the IDF issued evacuation warnings for two bridges in the Sahmar area of the southern Bekaa to interdict Hezbollah reinforcement routes.
📍Tehran, Isfahan, Mashhad, Mahshahr (Iran); southern Lebanon / Bekaa Valley 💥IRGC Ministry of Defense complex, Khordad 15 missile base, Mashhad airport fuel depot, 12+ Hezbollah sites 🎯IDF confirms 12,300+ total targets struck across war (CENTCOM, Apr 1)
Sources: Alma Research Center Voice of Emirates / CENTCOM
1
Apr
Iran · Diplomatic Diplomatic · Ceasefire Dispute
Trump Claims Iran Asked for Ceasefire; Tehran Flatly Denies — Hormuz Precondition Set
On April 1, President Trump announced via Truth Social that Iran's 'New Regime President' had requested a ceasefire, but stated the U.S. would only 'consider' it once the Strait of Hormuz is 'open, free, and clear.' Iranian FM Araghchi flatly denied any ceasefire request or negotiations were underway, telling Al Jazeera 'At present there is no negotiation.' Trump then addressed the nation from the White House Cross Hall, stating core strategic objectives were 'nearing completion' and that the U.S. military would strike Iran 'extremely hard' over the next 2–3 weeks. He floated either a deal or all-out infrastructure bombardment as the endgame. The U.S. 15-point peace plan transmitted via Pakistani intermediaries — demanding zero enrichment, missile limits, and an end to Strait blockade — was formally rejected by Iran, which issued its own counter-demands including reparations and continued Hormuz control. Foreign Affairs assessed the U.S. terms 'basically amount to asking for unconditional surrender.'
📍Washington D.C. / Tehran / Pakistani intermediaries 🕊️US 15-point plan rejected; Iran counter-demands include reparations & Hormuz control ⏳Trump sets 2–3 week window: deal or escalation to infrastructure strikes
Sources: CNBC CNBC – Trump Address Recap Foreign Affairs PBS NewsHour
2
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Cluster Bomb Strike
Iranian Cluster Bomb Hits Tel Aviv Metro; Wave 93 Launched with Solid-Fuel Missiles
On April 2–3, Iran launched 'Wave 93' of Operation True Promise 4, utilizing a combination of solid and liquid-fuel ballistic missiles and suicide drones against Israeli military sites and the Tel Aviv metropolitan area. A cluster-munition warhead Iranian missile struck central Israel, with impacts recorded across Bnei Brak and surrounding areas — a girl was critically injured and two infants were wounded by shrapnel. A separate Iranian ballistic missile struck and damaged an Israeli drone factory in Petah Tikva. The Alma Research Center confirmed 434 cumulative Iranian attack waves against Israel had been identified since February 28, with the central region continuing to be Iran's primary target. Hezbollah fired more than 50 rockets from Lebanon into northern Israel on the morning of April 2 — Passover — forcing hundreds of thousands of Israelis into shelters. One rocket struck Kiryat Shmona, lightly injuring two people.
📍Tel Aviv metro (Bnei Brak, Petah Tikva); northern Israel (Kiryat Shmona) 💀Critical injury (girl, Bnei Brak); 2 infants wounded; drone factory in Petah Tikva damaged 🚀Wave 93 launched; 434 cumulative Iranian attack waves on Israel confirmed; 50+ Hezbollah rockets on Passover morning
Sources: Alma Research Center NewKerala / IRGC Statement Times of Israel Haaretz
3
Apr
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · Mass Displacement
Lebanon Displacement Surpasses 1 Million; 1,345+ Killed as Israel Expands Buffer Zone
As of April 3, more than 1 million people — roughly 20% of Lebanon's population — have been forcibly displaced by Israeli aerial and ground operations targeting Hezbollah in the south, the Bekaa Valley, and Beirut's southern suburbs. Lebanon's Health Ministry confirms 1,345+ killed and over 4,000 injured since the war began. Israel's IDF has killed approximately 1,000 Hezbollah operatives, including hundreds from the elite Radwan Force. Israel is establishing a 'security zone' up to the Litani River, with Defense Minister Katz warning displaced Lebanese will not be able to return until Hezbollah's threat is removed. The IRC reports more than 300,000 children among the displaced; around 180,000 people have crossed from Lebanon into Syria. Sectarian tensions in Beirut are rising sharply as displaced Shiites face evictions, inflated rents, and suspicion of Hezbollah ties in Christian-majority neighborhoods.
📍Southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, Beirut southern suburbs 💀1,345+ killed, 4,000+ injured (Lebanese Health Ministry, Apr 1) 🏠1,000,000+ displaced (20% of population); 300,000+ children; buffer zone to Litani River
Sources: NBC News Al Jazeera – Casualties Tracker Times of Israel IRC

Apr
Iran · Aerial Offensive · SAM Shootdown
Iran Downs US F-15E Over Central Iran; WSO Missing, SAR Underway
On April 3 (Day 35), Iranian air defenses shot down a US Air Force F-15E Strike Eagle over central Iran during strike operations. The jet carried two crew members — a pilot and a weapons systems officer (WSO). The pilot was successfully rescued by US forces. An A-10 Warthog dispatched to provide close air support during the search-and-rescue mission was itself shot down, though its pilot was also recovered. Two UH-60 Black Hawk helicopters were struck by small arms fire during the SAR operation. The IDF suspended strikes in the area of the downed aircraft to facilitate the ongoing search for the missing WSO. CBS News and Reuters confirmed the incident via unnamed US officials. Iranian state media framed the shootdown as proof that US air dominance claims were overstated.
📍Central Iran (exact coordinates undisclosed, CENTCOM) 💀WSO status unknown; pilot + A-10 pilot rescued; 2 Black Hawks damaged ✈️US aircraft lost: +1 F-15E (SAM), +1 A-10 (SAR) — total coalition losses updated

3
Apr
US · Electronic Warfare Offensive · EW Escalation
US Activates EA-37B Compass Call to Jam Iranian Command & Radar Networks Across Theater
On April 3, the United States activated EA-37B Compass Call electronic attack aircraft, marking a qualitative shift in the air campaign. The aircraft actively jammed Iranian radar arrays, navigation systems, and command communications across the entire theater — the first sustained electronic attack of this type in the conflict. Simultaneously, coalition strikes hit the Khordad 15 missile complex in Isfahan, fuel storage at Mashhad International Airport, and the SA-Iran defense electronics hub in Shiraz. Three senior IRGC commanders were eliminated in the same strike package: ballistic missile unit chief Makram Atimi (Kermanshah), oil HQ commander Jamshid Eshaqi (responsible for financing proxy networks), and Fatehin commando unit chief Mohammad Ali Fath Ali Zadeh. The IDF also issued evacuation warnings for two bridges in the Sahmar area of the southern Bekaa district in Lebanon, striking them to cut Hezbollah reinforcement routes.
📍Isfahan (Khordad 15), Mashhad Airport, Shiraz (SA-Iran hub), Kermanshah, Bekaa Valley 💀3 IRGC commanders killed: Atimi, Eshaqi, Fath Ali Zadeh 📡EA-37B Compass Call activated — full-theater electronic jamming initiated
Sources: IranWarLive OSINT Alma Research Center
3
Apr
Iran · Ceasefire Diplomatic · Collapse
Trump Extends Power-Plant Strike Deadline to Apr 6; US 15-Point Plan Rejected; Iran Issues Counter-Demands
With the April 3 deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz approaching, Trump announced a 5-day extension to April 6 to allow space for Pakistani-mediated negotiations. The US had transmitted a 15-point ceasefire plan via Pakistani intermediaries; Iranian military spokesperson Lt. Col. Ebrahim Zolfaghari flatly rejected it. Iran issued its own counter-proposal demanding: continuation of the Strait of Hormuz blockade, full war reparations, and inclusion of Lebanon/Hezbollah in any deal. Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi confirmed 'no negotiation is underway' in an interview with Al Jazeera and NBC News. Trump responded by posting on Truth Social that there would be 'no deal with Iran except UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER' and that the US military will strike Iran 'extremely hard' over the next 2–3 weeks. Secretary of State Rubio said the war could end 'in a matter of weeks' without ground troops. A senior regional diplomatic source told PBS NewsHour that no agreement on direct talks or a venue had been reached.
📍Washington D.C. / Islamabad / Tehran — indirect channel via Pakistan 🕊️15-point US plan rejected; Iranian counter-demands issued; Hormuz deadline extended to Apr 6 ⚡Trump threatens infrastructure strikes (bridges, power plants, energy facilities) if no deal by Apr 6
Sources: Foreign Affairs CNBC PBS NewsHour

Apr

IRGC Wave 89+: Tanker 'Aqua One' Struck in Persian Gulf; Drone Swarm Targets USS Abraham Lincoln CSG
As part of Wave 89 of Operation True Promise 4, the IRGC confirmed multiple naval and air operations on April 1–3. An Israeli-linked tanker commercially named 'Aqua One' was struck and set ablaze in the central Persian Gulf. Multiple waves of explosive drones were launched at the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier strike group in the northern Indian Ocean; IRGC claimed satellite imagery confirmed the CSG had retreated deeper into the Indian Ocean. Separately, on April 1, Iran fired three cruise missiles at Qatar — two were intercepted and the third struck an oil tanker with no reported casualties. Iranian forces also struck Abu Dhabi gas facilities at Habshan, halting operations at the site. The UAE Air Defense Ministry confirmed 19 ballistic missiles and 26 UAVs intercepted on April 2 alone, with a cumulative total of 457 ballistic missiles, 19 cruise missiles, and 2,038 UAVs launched at the UAE since the war began.

Apr
Lebanon · Humanitarian Displacement · Crisis
Lebanon Displacement Exceeds 1 Million; 1,345+ Killed, 4,000+ Injured; Sectarian Tensions Surge in Beirut
By April 3, over 1 million people — approximately one-fifth of Lebanon's entire population — had been displaced by the Israeli ground and air campaign, confirmed by NBC News, NPR, and the Lebanese government. The Lebanese Health Ministry reports 1,345+ killed and 4,000+ injured since hostilities resumed on March 2. The IDF confirmed killing approximately 1,000 Hezbollah operatives including hundreds of Radwan Force members. Displaced Shiite families fleeing southern Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs are encountering sectarian hostility in majority-Christian and Sunni neighborhoods; landlords are demanding enormous upfront deposits and background security checks. The government has converted hundreds of schools to shelters and erected tents in Beirut's main stadium. On April 3, the IDF issued evacuation warnings and struck two bridges in the Sahmar area of the southern Bekaa to interdict Hezbollah reinforcement routes. As of March 26, approximately 180,000 people had crossed into Syria. The IRC and UNHCR have warned of an 'imminent humanitarian catastrophe.'

Apr

IRGC Wave 93: Iran Strikes Deep Israeli Military Sites with Solid/Liquid Fuel Missiles and Suicide Drones
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps launched 'Wave 93' of Operation True Promise IV, targeting Israeli military positions deep inside Israeli territory using a combination of solid and liquid-fuel ballistic missiles, long-range guided munitions, and suicide drones. The IRGC military command vowed the launches would 'continue continuously, uninterruptedly, and shot after shot.' Tehran framed the assault as retaliation for ongoing coalition strikes on Iranian energy and military infrastructure. Ceasefire talks simultaneously collapsed as Iran rejected meeting with US negotiators and called Washington's demands unacceptable, even as Turkish and Pakistani mediators continued backchanel contacts.

4
Apr
Coalition · Airstrike Offensive · Nuclear-Adjacent Infrastructure
Explosions Reported at Bushehr Nuclear Plant Auxiliary Building and Mahshahr Petrochemical Zone
On April 4, 2026, explosions were reported at an auxiliary building of the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant — the third time since March 17 the facility's grounds have been struck — as well as at the Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Zone in Khuzestan Province, Iran. This marks a continued pattern of pressure on Iranian energy and dual-use industrial infrastructure. Rosatom had previously evacuated nearly 100 staff from Iran, though Russian personnel remain on-site to keep the reactor operational. The AEOI condemned the strikes as attacks on peaceful nuclear facilities and issued further protest letters to IAEA Director-General Rafael Grossi.
📍Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant auxiliary building + Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Zone, Khuzestan Province, Iran ⚠️No casualties or reactor damage reported; Rosatom skeleton crew remains on-site
Sources: Wikipedia – 2026 Iran War CNN Live Updates
4
Apr
Iran · Diplomatic Diplomatic · Ceasefire Collapse
Ceasefire Push Fully Stalls: Iran Rejects US Negotiators; WSJ Reports Talks Dead
Efforts to reach a ceasefire between the US and Iran have collapsed as of April 4, with The Wall Street Journal citing mediators who confirmed Iran rejected direct talks with US officials and called Washington's 15-point demands — widely characterized as tantamount to unconditional surrender — unacceptable. Turkey and other regional actors continue diplomatic contacts but admit 'obstacles' remain. Trump's April 6 deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face power-plant strikes looms. Meanwhile, Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf reiterated that Iran cannot be forced into submission. Iran has presented its own five-point counter-framework requiring cessation of all US-Israeli strikes, compensation for damages, and international recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.
🕊️US 15-point plan rejected March 25; Iran counter-demands submitted via intermediaries ⏰Trump's power-plant strike deadline: April 6, 2026
Sources: Haaretz Wikipedia – Iran-US Negotiations Foreign Affairs
4
Apr
Coalition · SAR Search & Rescue · F-15E WSO Still Missing
SAR Continues for Downed F-15E WSO Behind Enemy Lines in Iran — One Crew Member Rescued
US forces rescued the pilot of the F-15E Strike Eagle shot down on April 3 over Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad Province in central Iran, but search and rescue operations for the Weapon Systems Officer (WSO) remain ongoing as of April 4. The missing airman has been on the run behind enemy lines for over a day and a half, possibly injured and operating with limited communications in mountainous terrain while being actively pursued by Iranian forces. Special operations rescue experts describe the mission as 'very dangerous' and 'more complex' than standard SAR. Night operations are considered most viable given US ISR dominance in low-light conditions.
📍Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad Province, central Iran — mountainous terrain 🚁Pilot rescued; WSO remains missing and evading capture — SAR ongoing
Sources: CNN Live Updates Wikipedia – 2026 Iran War
4
Apr
Iran · Ground Attack Offensive · Border Strike
Airstrike Hits Shalamcheh Iraq-Iran Border Crossing; Iraqi Civilian Killed, 5 Injured; Commercial Traffic Suspended
A coalition airstrike targeted the Iranian side of the Shalamcheh border crossing connecting Iraq and Iran on April 4, killing one Iraqi civilian and wounding five others near the Iraqi side of the crossing. Iraq's Border Ports director Omar al-Waeli announced the immediate suspension of all commercial traffic and passenger movement through the crossing. The Shalamcheh crossing is a major transit point for Iraqi and Iranian civilians, particularly during religious pilgrimage and holiday seasons. Video from the scene showed billowing black smoke and structural damage to the crossing's interior. Iran's state-run Tasnim News Agency confirmed the casualty figures.
📍Shalamcheh Border Crossing, Iraq-Iran border, Basra Governorate 💀1 Iraqi civilian killed, 5 injured; commercial and passenger traffic suspended
Sources: CNN Live Updates
4
Apr
Coalition · Airstrike Offensive · Red Crescent Casualty
4th Red Crescent Aid Worker Killed in Esfahan Province Airstrike — IFRC Condemns Attack
An Iranian Red Crescent aid worker, Abolfazl Dehnavi, was killed on April 4 in an airstrike in Esfahan Province, making him the fourth Red Crescent humanitarian to be killed in the US-Israeli air campaign. The International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies (IFRC) issued a formal statement condemning the strike and reminding all parties that the Red Crescent emblem constitutes an internationally recognized legal protection under the laws of armed conflict. The IFRC said protection of humanitarian workers 'is not only a moral issue, but an imperative international legal norm.'
📍Esfahan Province, Iran 💀Abolfazl Dehnavi, Iranian Red Crescent — 4th humanitarian aid worker killed in campaign
Sources: CNN Live Updates
36
Apr 4
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Wave 93 — Operation True Promise 4
IRGC Launches Wave 93: Joint Missile & Drone Swarm Strikes Northern Israel
On Friday afternoon, April 4, the IRGC announced the 93rd wave of Operation True Promise 4, striking Israeli military assembly points and combat support centers in Western Galilee, Haifa, Kafr Kanna, and the Krayot region. The operation was conducted as a joint endeavour with the Islamic Resistance and employed a combination of solid and liquid-fuel ballistic missiles, long-range guided munitions, and one-way attack suicide drones. The IRGC vowed the launches would 'continue continuously, uninterruptedly, and shot after shot.' Iranian and Houthi forces also coordinated simultaneous strikes toward the Dimona area in the Negev from Yemen. Iranian submunitions from an intercepted cluster-warhead missile fell near the IDF Kirya headquarters in Tel Aviv, causing minor damage to a nearby school building; no injuries at the military HQ were reported.
📍Western Galilee, Haifa, Kafr Kanna, Krayot (Israel); Tel Aviv Kirya area (submunitions) 🚀Solid/liquid-fuel ballistic missiles, long-range guided munitions, suicide drones — joint IRGC + Islamic Resistance operation 💀No confirmed Israeli fatalities from this wave; minor structural damage near Kirya base
Sources: ANI / Press TV CNN Jerusalem Post
36
Apr 4
US/Israel · Strike Offensive · Shahid Beheshti University & Azadi Stadium, Tehran
US-Israeli Strikes Hit Shahid Beheshti University Laser-Plasma Lab and Azadi Stadium, Tehran
On April 4, US-Israeli airstrikes struck the Laser and Plasma Research Institute at Shahid Beheshti University in Tehran, causing significant structural damage to the research facility and a nearby female students' dormitory, according to Iranian state media. Iran's Science Minister Hossein Simayi Sarraf condemned the strike as a 'crime against humanity,' stating that nearly 30 Iranian universities have been targeted since the war began. Iranian media also reported that the Azadi sports stadium in Tehran sustained damage in a separate strike on the same day. The IDF also carried out a fresh wave of strikes across Tehran targeting IRGC command nodes, mobile headquarters, air defense systems, missile launch sites, and ballistic missile storage facilities.
📍Shahid Beheshti University (Laser & Plasma Research Institute), Azadi Stadium — Tehran, Iran 🎯Dual-use scientific research facility; IRGC command nodes and missile infrastructure also struck across Tehran 💀Casualties unconfirmed; dormitory damage reported
Sources: ANI NPR WFAE/NPR
36
Apr 4
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Tech Infrastructure Strike — Dubai / Kuwait
Iran Strikes Oracle HQ Dubai, Kuwait Al-Ahmadi Refinery & Desalination Plant
Overnight into April 4, Iran's IRGC struck Oracle's office building in Dubai Internet City with a projectile; the Dubai Media Office confirmed debris from an aerial interception fell on the building's facade, leaving a large hole in the southwestern corner but causing no injuries. The IRGC had explicitly named Oracle — along with Apple, Google, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Boeing, and others — as legitimate targets for alleged 'terrorist espionage' assistance to US-Israeli operations. Separately, Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery (capacity ~350,000 bpd) was set ablaze by an Iranian drone attack in the early morning hours, while a Kuwaiti water desalination plant was also hit. Kuwait's state news agency KUNA reported fires in multiple operational units at the refinery; no employee injuries were reported. Iran denied responsibility for the desalination strike, blaming Israel.
📍Oracle HQ — Dubai Internet City, UAE; Al-Ahmadi Oil Refinery & Desalination Plant — Kuwait 🎯IRGC tech-company targeting escalation; Oracle, Amazon AWS (Bahrain) struck in consecutive days 💀No casualties reported at Oracle building; no refinery employee injuries confirmed
Sources: CNBC Al Jazeera Gizmodo
36
Apr 4
Diplomacy · Ultimatum Political · Trump 48-Hour Hormuz Ultimatum
Trump Issues 48-Hour 'All Hell' Ultimatum on Hormuz; Pakistan Ceasefire Mediation 'On Track'
On Saturday April 4, President Trump posted on Truth Social that Iran has 48 hours to 'MAKE A DEAL or OPEN UP THE HORMUZ STRAIT' or 'all Hell will reign down on them.' The post comes as Trump's self-imposed 10-day energy infrastructure strike pause — extended to April 6 at 8 PM ET — nears expiry. Sen. Lindsey Graham confirmed speaking with Trump, saying he is 'completely convinced' Trump will use 'overwhelming military force' if Iran continues to impede the strait. Iran's central military command General Ali Abdollahi Aliabadi dismissed the threat as 'helpless, nervous, unbalanced and stupid,' countering that 'the gates of hell will open for you.' Indirect negotiations continue behind the scenes: Pakistan's Foreign Ministry said ceasefire efforts are 'right on track,' with Pakistan, Turkey, and Egypt acting as mediators. Iran FM Araghchi said Tehran has 'never refused to go to Islamabad' but demands a 'conclusive and lasting' end to the war. Iranian parliament speaker Ghalibaf separately hinted at potentially extending Iran's maritime blockade to the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.
📍Washington D.C. / Islamabad / Tehran ⏰48-hour deadline expires ~April 6, 2026 at ~01:00 UTC 🕊️Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt mediating; Vance–Ghalibaf indirect channel active per Axios
Sources: Washington Times Axios Al Jazeera CBS News
36
Apr 4
US · SAR — Missing WSO Day 2 SAR · F-15E WSO Still Missing; Iran Offers Reward for Capture
Day-2 SAR: F-15E WSO Still Missing in Kohgiluyeh Province — Iran Offers $66,000 Reward
Search and rescue operations entered a second day on April 4 for the weapon systems officer (WSO) of the F-15E Strike Eagle shot down by Iranian fire on April 3 over Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad Province in southwestern Iran. One crew member (the pilot) was confirmed rescued by US forces on April 3. Iran's Tasnim News Agency stated the search 'had so far been unsuccessful' from their side, while multiple Iranian state media outlets reported the IRGC may have captured the WSO — unconfirmed by the US. Iranian authorities offered approximately $66,000 in reward to any citizen who captures and turns over an 'enemy pilot' alive. The US and Israel also struck the area of Iran where the missing airman was believed to be located, according to an Iranian outlet citing security sources. Iran separately claimed its joint military command struck two US UH-60 Black Hawk helicopters involved in the SAR effort on April 3 — the Associated Press could not independently verify this claim.
📍Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad Province, southwestern Iran (mountainous terrain) 💀1 crew member rescued (pilot); 1 WSO status unknown — possible IRGC capture unconfirmed ⚠️Iran offering ~$66,000 reward for live capture; IRGC claims two UH-60s also struck (AP unable to verify)
Sources: ABC News Fortune / Bloomberg RFE/RL CNN
36
Apr 4
Iran · Nuclear Escalation Nuclear Risk · Bushehr — Rosatom Evacuates 198 Staff
Rosatom Evacuates 198 Additional Staff from Bushehr as Projectile Kills Security Guard on Plant Grounds
Russia's state nuclear energy company Rosatom confirmed the evacuation of an additional 198 staff from Iran's Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant on April 4, the largest single-day evacuation since the war began. The IAEA confirmed Iran informed the agency that a physical protection staff member was killed by a projectile fragment at the plant perimeter, and that a building on-site was affected by shockwaves and fragments — the first confirmed fatality at a nuclear facility during the conflict. Neither the US nor Israel confirmed involvement in any attack on Bushehr. The IAEA's statement did not attribute blame. Separately, CNN reported Iran's FM Araghchi warned that repeated strikes near the Bushehr site could trigger 'a wider regional disaster.'
📍Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant — Bushehr Province, Iran (Persian Gulf coast) ☢️IAEA confirmed 1 security staffer killed by projectile fragment on plant grounds; building damaged by shockwave ⚠️Rosatom has now evacuated ~200+ total staff; some personnel remain on-site to keep reactor operational
Sources: RFE/RL CNBC CNN
36
Apr 4
Israel · Lebanon Ground/Air · Lebanon — IDF Strikes Tyre; Lebanon Death Toll Surpasses 1,400
Lebanon Death Toll Surpasses 1,400 as IDF Strikes Tyre; US Embassy Warns of Threat to American Universities in Beirut
The Lebanese Health Ministry reported on April 4 that the total number of people killed since Israel began strikes on March 2 has surpassed 1,400, including at least 126 children, 54 health workers, and 93 women. More than 1 million people remain displaced — roughly one-fifth of Lebanon's entire population. Israeli airstrikes hit a building in the southern port city of Tyre, with AP photographers documenting significant structural damage. Early on April 5, Israel's military said it was continuing strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure sites in Beirut. Separately, the US Embassy in Beirut elevated its threat warning, urging all US citizens to leave Lebanon immediately and warning that Iran may now target American university campuses including the American University of Beirut, which has shifted some classes online. The IDF says it has killed approximately 1,000 Hezbollah operatives since March 2, including hundreds from the elite Radwan Force.
📍Tyre (southern Lebanon), Beirut southern suburbs, Bekaa Valley — ongoing IDF air and ground ops 💀1,400+ killed in Lebanon (Health Ministry); 1M+ displaced; 10 IDF soldiers KIA in Lebanon ⚠️US Embassy: Iran may target AUB and other US university campuses — AUB shifts classes online
Sources: CNN NPR AP / ADN
4
Apr
US · Air Strike Offensive · Leadership Strike
IDF Kills IRGC Ballistic Missile Chief Makram Atimi in Kermanshah Strike
Israeli Air Force jets struck the Kermanshah area of Iran on April 3–4, eliminating Makram Atimi, commander of a central Iranian ballistic missile unit.
