The IDF said Atimi was personally responsible for dozens of missile launches targeting Israel. Several battalion commanders from the same northwestern Iran-based launch unit were also killed in the same strike package. The IDF stated the operations form part of its ongoing campaign to degrade Iran's missile command structure and prevent further launches.
📍Kermanshah Province, northwestern Iran 💀Makram Atimi (IRGC ballistic missile chief) + multiple battalion commanders KIA
Sources: Jerusalem Post IDF Official
4
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Cluster Munition Strike
Iran Fires Cluster-Bomb Missiles at Central Israel; Fragments Hit Near IDF Kirya HQ in Tel Aviv
On April 4, Iranian forces launched multiple salvos of ballistic missiles carrying cluster bomb warheads at central Israel. Submunitions struck more than a dozen sites across Tel Aviv, Ramat Gan, Bnei Brak, Petah Tikva, Givatayim, and Rosh HaAyin. Two cluster submunitions landed in a parking lot directly adjacent to the IDF's Kirya (HaKirya) military headquarters in Tel Aviv, causing damage to the parking lot and a nearby school with no injuries at that specific site. Cars were destroyed and several apartments damaged in Ramat Gan. Magen David Adom reported treating at least six people for light injuries overall. Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya command claimed the barrage also targeted the IDF's Home Front Command headquarters.
📍Tel Aviv (Kirya HQ), Ramat Gan, Bnei Brak, Petah Tikva — central Israel 💀6 lightly wounded; no fatalities confirmed at time of writing
Sources: Times of Israel Jerusalem Post
4
Apr
IAF · Strike Offensive · Industrial Infrastructure
IAF Destroys Mahshahr Petrochemical Zone; Strike Near Bushehr Nuclear Plant Kills Guard
Israeli Air Force jets struck the Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Economic Zone in Khuzestan Province on April 4, hitting the state-run Bandar Imam complex and facilities belonging to Fajr 1 and Fajr 2 petrochemical companies. Iranian state media confirmed at least five killed and 170 wounded. The IDF confirmed the sites produced chemical materials for ballistic missiles, stating the strikes halted all production and would cause billions of dollars in damage. Separately, a projectile struck the perimeter of the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant killing one security guard and damaging an auxiliary building — the fourth time the facility has been struck during the war. IAEA Director General Grossi expressed deep concern and called for maximum military restraint. Iranian FM Araghchi stated Bushehr had now been struck four times. Rosatom confirmed it was evacuating 198 additional staff.
📍Mahshahr, Khuzestan Province + Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant perimeter 💀5 killed, 170 wounded (Mahshahr); 1 security guard killed (Bushehr)
Sources: Al Jazeera Haaretz The Hill
4
Apr
US CSAR · Rescue CSAR · Downed Aircrew Rescue
Trump: 'WE GOT HIM!' — F-15E WSO Rescued Deep Inside Iran After 36-Hour Manhunt; 2 MC-130Js Destroyed at FARP
The Weapon Systems Officer (WSO) from the F-15E Strike Eagle of the 48th Fighter Wing, shot down by Iranian forces on April 3 over Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad Province, was successfully recovered by U.S. Special Operations Forces in the early hours of April 5 local time. The rescue was described by The Aviationist as one of the most complex CSAR missions in U.S. special operations history. The WSO used SERE training to evade capture, moving to elevated terrain and activating an emergency beacon. The CIA first launched a deception campaign inside Iran spreading word that the airman had already been found. Heavy clashes were reported near Dehdasht as IRGC and Basij forces converged on the area; significant Iranian casualties were reported. Two MC-130J Commando IIs became disabled at a forward arming and refueling point (FARP) established inside Iran and were deliberately destroyed to prevent capture. President Trump confirmed the rescue on Truth Social: 'WE GOT HIM!' stating the WSO 'sustained injuries, but he will be just fine.' Both crew members and the recovery team have since safely exited Iran.
📍Kohgiluyeh & Boyer-Ahmad Province / Dehdasht area, southwestern Iran 💀WSO injured but stable; significant IRGC/Basij casualties reported at rescue site; 2 MC-130Js destroyed
Sources: CBS News The Aviationist Fox News RFERL
4
Apr
Diplomacy · Ultimatum Political · Hormuz Deadline
Trump Issues Final 48-Hour 'All Hell' Ultimatum on Strait of Hormuz — Deadline: Apr 6, 8 PM ET
On April 4, President Trump posted on Truth Social warning Iran that its 10-day deadline (set March 26) to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or make a ceasefire deal expires in 48 hours — by April 6, 2026. 'Time is running out — 48 hours before all Hell will reign down on them. Glory be to GOD!' he wrote. Senator Lindsey Graham, after speaking directly with Trump, stated he is 'completely convinced that he will use overwhelming military force against the regime if they continue to impede the Strait of Hormuz.' Iran's Gen. Ali Abdollahi Aliabadi derided the post as 'a helpless, nervous, unbalanced and stupid action,' countering that 'the gates of hell will open for you.' Indirect negotiations via Pakistan, Egypt and Turkey — led by VP Vance and Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf — have so far failed to achieve a breakthrough. Iran has rejected any temporary ceasefire, seeking instead a permanent end to the war with reparations and sovereignty guarantees.
📍Washington DC / Indirect channel via Pakistan, Egypt, Turkey ⏰Deadline: April 6, 2026 at 8:00 PM Eastern Time
Sources: Axios Washington Times Al Jazeera
5
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Multi-Site Infrastructure Strike
Iran Drones Strike Kuwait Petroleum HQ, Two Desalination Plants & Ministries Complex
In the early hours of April 5, Iranian drones struck multiple critical infrastructure targets across Kuwait City. Kuwait Petroleum Corporation reported a fire at its Shuwaikh oil sector complex — which houses the oil ministry and KPC headquarters — after a drone attack. Kuwait's Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy confirmed two power generation and water desalination plants were hit, forcing two electricity-generating units offline and causing 'significant material damage.' The finance ministry separately confirmed the Ministries Complex government building in Kuwait City was struck by a hostile drone, also causing significant material damage. No casualties were reported in any of the incidents. Bahrain's Bapco Energies also reported a storage tank fire following an Iranian drone strike the same day, with the blaze later extinguished and no injuries reported.
📍Shuwaikh Oil Complex, two desalination plants, Ministries Complex — Kuwait City, Kuwait; Bapco storage facility — Bahrain 💥Significant material damage; 2 power generation units offline; KPC complex evacuated 💀No casualties reported (Kuwait); no injuries reported (Bahrain)
Sources: Oman Observer Al Jazeera The National
5
Apr
Coalition · Strike Offensive · Leadership Decapitation Strike
Trump Claims 'Massive' Tehran Strike Killed 'Many of Iran's Military Leaders'
On April 5, President Trump posted a video to Truth Social claiming a 'massive strike in Tehran' had 'terminated' many of Iran's military leaders, writing: 'Many of Iran's Military Leaders, who have led them poorly and unwisely, are terminated, along with much else, with this massive strike in Tehran!' The roughly minute-long video depicted explosions lighting up a city skyline at night. Trump did not specify the exact timing, targets, or names of those killed. ABC News did not independently verify the video's authenticity, and CENTCOM issued no immediate separate confirmation of leadership casualties. The post accompanied a new 48-hour ultimatum to Iran to 'MAKE A DEAL or OPEN UP THE HORMUZ STRAIT — before all hell will reign down on them.' Iranian officials called Trump's threats 'helpless and nervous,' with Foreign Minister Araghchi insisting Iran had never refused to negotiate in good faith.
📍Tehran, Iran ⚠️Trump claim unverified by CENTCOM; no independent confirmation of specific leaders killed 🕐48-hour Hormuz ultimatum countdown: deadline Apr 6, ~8 PM ET
Sources: ABC News Times of Israel YourNews
5
Apr
IDF · Beirut Offensive · Targeted Strike
IDF Strikes Hezbollah Infrastructure in Beirut's Dahiyeh; Evacuation Warning Issued for Ghobeyri
On April 5, the IDF issued an urgent evacuation warning to residents of the Ghobeyri neighborhood in Dahiyeh, Beirut's southern suburbs, warning that a building containing a Hezbollah facility was about to be struck and that residents should remain at least 300 meters away. The IDF subsequently announced it had struck Hezbollah infrastructure sites in Beirut, with local media reporting at least two strikes on the southern suburbs. This followed the IDF's broader April 3–5 operational tempo in which it carried out more than 70 strikes in Iran per day while simultaneously maintaining strike pressure on Hezbollah command and logistics nodes in Beirut and southern Lebanon. Lebanon's Health Ministry reported a cumulative death toll of 1,422 killed and 4,294 injured since March 2, including 125 children and 91 women. More than 1.2 million Lebanese remain displaced — approximately 25% of the country's population.
📍Dahiyeh / Ghobeyri — Beirut southern suburbs, Lebanon 💀Cumulative Lebanon toll: 1,422 killed, 4,294 injured (Lebanese Health Ministry, Apr 4) 🏚️1.2 million displaced — ~25% of Lebanon's population (European FMs joint statement)
Sources: Haaretz WAFA GlobalSecurity
4
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Tech Infrastructure Strike
Iran Strikes AWS Data Centers in UAE; Oracle Dubai Site Also Targeted
In a significant escalation of Iran's targeting doctrine, Amazon Web Services confirmed that two of its data centers in the UAE were 'directly struck' and a third in Bahrain was damaged by a nearby drone strike in early April. The attacks caused localized and limited disruption to AWS servers, according to the Associated Press. Iran's state-run IRNA news agency also reported that Tehran targeted an Oracle data center in Dubai on April 2 in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes that injured former Foreign Minister Kamal Kharazi and killed his wife on April 1. Dubai Media Office alleged the Oracle claim was 'fake news.' The strikes confirm a shift in Iranian targeting to US-affiliated tech infrastructure, part of a broader effort to maximize economic disruption across the Gulf.
📍UAE (Abu Dhabi / Dubai) and Bahrain 💥Two AWS data centers directly struck in UAE; third in Bahrain damaged — limited disruption reported 🎯Oracle Dubai targeted per IRNA; Dubai Media Office denied the claim
Sources: Al Jazeera Alma Research
2
Apr
IDF · Leadership Kill Offensive · Targeted Elimination
IDF Eliminates IRGC Oil HQ Commander Jamshid Eshaqi; Fatehin SF Commander Fathalizadeh Also Killed
The IDF confirmed the elimination of Major General Jamshid Eshaqi, commander of the Iranian regime's Oil Headquarters and adviser to the Chief of Staff of the Armed Forces, killed alongside several family members in a joint US-Israeli precision strike on or around March 31–April 1. The IRGC confirmed his death, describing him as a 'martyr' killed in a 'US-Zionist attack.' The IDF stated that Eshaqi 'led the financial arm of the regime's forces as well as the military industries responsible for the production of ballistic missiles,' and that he funneled hundreds of millions of dollars from shadow-fleet oil exports to fund IRGC operations, Hezbollah, and the Houthis. Separately, Brigadier General Mohammad Ali Fathalizadeh, commander of the IRGC's elite Fatehin Special Unit — a rapid-response commando formation — was killed on April 1–2 in a US-Israeli strike. Iran's Defense Ministry-affiliated Defa Press confirmed his death on April 2.
📍Tehran area (Eshaqi); Tehran / IRGC ground forces complex (Fathalizadeh) 💀Eshaqi killed with several family members; Fathalizadeh killed Apr 1–2 🎯Eshaqi oversaw IRGC oil-revenue laundering pipeline; Fathalizadeh led elite domestic security SF unit
Sources: Jerusalem Post Shafaq News GlobalSecurity
2
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Precision Strike
Iran Ballistic Missile Strikes Israeli Drone Factory in Petah Tikva
On April 2, an Iranian ballistic missile struck and damaged an Israeli drone factory in Petah Tikva, east of Tel Aviv. The strike represented a significant targeting of Israel's domestic defense-industrial base. No casualty figures were immediately confirmed by Israeli authorities. The attack came on Day 34 of Operation Epic Fury, as Iran continued its strategy of targeting both US Gulf bases and Israeli military-industrial infrastructure despite reports that up to 90% of Iran's ballistic missile launchers had been degraded by coalition strikes.
📍Petah Tikva, central Israel 💥Israeli drone manufacturing facility damaged ⚠️Attack on Israeli defense-industrial base; casualty figures not confirmed
Sources: Wikipedia — 2026 Iran War Wikipedia — Iranian Strikes on Israel
2
Apr
US · Strike Offensive · Infrastructure Strike
US Strikes B1 Karaj–Tehran Bridge During Nowruz Sizdah Be-Dar Holiday; 8 Killed, 95 Wounded
On April 2, US forces struck the B1 bridge connecting Tehran to the city of Karaj — the tallest bridge in the Middle East, still partially under construction. The bridge was described by US officials as a key Iranian military supply route used to transport missile and drone components. Two strikes on the span caused it to collapse. The attack killed eight people and wounded 95 others; Iranian officials noted that many families had gathered in parks below the bridge to celebrate the Persian Nowruz holiday of Sizdah Be-Dar. Trump gloated about the destruction on social media: 'The biggest bridge in Iran comes tumbling down, never to be used again.' International legal experts flagged the timing and method as potentially constituting a war crime, given the known civilian concentration during the holiday.
📍B1 Karaj–Tehran highway bridge, Alborz Province, Iran 💀8 killed, 95 wounded ⚠️Struck during Nowruz Sizdah Be-Dar holiday; international legal experts raised war crime concerns
Sources: Legal Insurrection Wikipedia — 2026 Iran War
1
Apr
US · Diplomacy Diplomatic · Presidential Address
Trump National Address: War 'Nearing Completion'; Threatens to Bomb Iran's Power Grid if No Deal by Apr 6
On April 1 (Day 33), President Trump addressed the nation from the White House Cross Hall in his first primetime address on the Iran War. Trump said the US is 'getting very close' to achieving its core strategic objectives and projected the conflict could end in two to three more weeks. He threatened that if no deal is reached, the US would 'hit each and every one of their electric generating plants very hard and probably simultaneously.' Trump repeated his claim that Iran's new regime president had asked for a ceasefire, a claim Iran denied. He simultaneously confirmed the US has not struck Iran's oil infrastructure. Earlier that day, Trump had posted on Truth Social that Iran's president requested a ceasefire and the US would only 'consider' it once the Strait of Hormuz was 'open, free, and clear.' Iran's Foreign Ministry swiftly denied any ceasefire request. Axios reported that US and Iranian officials were in fact discussing a potential ceasefire-for-Hormuz deal through intermediaries including Vice President Vance and Pakistani mediators, though no agreement had been reached.
📍White House, Washington D.C. — televised address ⏱️April 6 deadline: Trump threatened to strike Iran's power grid if Hormuz not reopened 🤝Axios: US-Iran discussing ceasefire-for-Hormuz deal through intermediaries; Iran publicly denies talks
Sources: CNBC CNN Axios
3
Apr
US · Electronic Warfare Offensive · EW Escalation
US Activates EA-37B Compass Call; Strikes Hit Mahshahr Petrochemical Zone — 5 Killed, 170 Wounded
On April 3 (Day 35), the US activated EA-37B Compass Call electronic attack aircraft to actively jam Iranian radar, navigation, and command communications across the entire theater — a qualitative shift from purely kinetic operations. Coalition strikes simultaneously hit the Khordad 15 missile complex in Isfahan, Mashhad Airport fuel storage, and the SA-Iran defense electronics hub in Shiraz. In Mahshahr, US-Israeli airstrikes struck the Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Economic Zone in Khuzestan Province — a critical industrial hub accounting for a significant share of Iran's national petrochemical output — killing at least 5 people and wounding approximately 170 others, per Iranian state media and confirmed by a provincial IRNA-cited security official. A total of 272 strikes were recorded across 14 provinces on the day, causing 184 casualties according to HRANA. Tehran's Shahid Beheshti University was also struck, with the medical research institute and laboratory building nearly destroyed; the student dormitory was severely damaged. IDF Defense Minister Katz vowed further escalation, and Israel postponed some planned strikes to support the ongoing SAR mission for the downed F-15E WSO still inside Iran.
📍Mahshahr, Khuzestan; Isfahan; Mashhad; Shiraz; Tehran (Beheshti University) 💀5 killed, ~170 wounded at Mahshahr petrochemical hub; 184 total casualties across 272 strikes in 14 provinces (HRANA) ⚡First use of EA-37B Compass Call in theater — full-spectrum EW jamming activated
Sources: Haaretz IranWarLive Alma Research
4
Apr
US · SAR / Strike Defensive · Search & Rescue
Bushehr Auxiliary Building & Mahshahr Hit; F-15E WSO Still Evading — CIA Deception Campaign Active
On April 4 (Day 36), explosions were reported at an auxiliary building of the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant and again at the Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Zone. Iran's armed forces also struck in the vicinity of the IDF's Kirya headquarters compound in Tel Aviv with a ballistic missile. The downed F-15E WSO — shot down April 3 over Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad province — remained at large inside Iran, evading capture by scaling terrain to a ridgeline approximately 7,000 feet above sea level, armed only with a pistol, communication device, and tracking beacon. A CIA deception campaign was activated to mislead Iranian forces into believing the WSO had already been recovered and was being transported by ground. Iran offered a reward of approximately US$60,000 to anyone who located and turned him in. Israel postponed planned strikes to avoid interference with the rescue. IDF drone strike also hit a residential area near Rafik Hariri Public Hospital in Beirut, killing at least 4 and injuring 40, per MSF on the ground.
📍Bushehr NPP (auxiliary building); Mahshahr, Khuzestan; Tel Aviv (near Kirya HQ); Beirut (near Hariri Hospital); Kohgiluyeh & Boyer-Ahmad province (SAR) 💀5+ killed, 170+ wounded (Mahshahr); 4 killed, 40 injured (Beirut airstrike, MSF); Iranian SAR contact — 3 killed by coalition in SAR zone (Tasnim) 🛩️F-15E WSO evading capture; 2x MC-130J destroyed at FARP inside Iran to prevent capture; CIA deception op active
Sources: CNN Wikipedia — 2026 Iran War Haaretz
5
Apr
US · Rescue Defensive · SAR Success
F-15E WSO Rescued After 'Heavy Firefight' Inside Iran; Israel Commends Operation
On April 5 (Day 37), the injured F-15E Strike Eagle WSO — who had been evading Iranian forces alone for over a day after his jet was downed by an Iranian SAM on April 3 — was successfully recovered by US forces following what CENTCOM described as a 'heavy firefight' inside Iran. Two UH-60 Black Hawks were hit by small arms fire during the mission but remained operational. The rescue involved the creation of a makeshift US remote airbase (FARP) inside Iran; two MC-130J Compass Call transport aircraft became stuck at the site and were intentionally destroyed by US forces to prevent capture, with three replacement aircraft flown in. A CIA deception campaign had been running since April 4 to confuse Iranian forces about the WSO's location. Trump described the mission as 'one of the most daring Search and Rescue Operations in U.S. History.' Israeli Defense Minister Katz commended American partners, calling the rescue 'another expression of the close cooperation between Israel and the United States, even in the most complex moments.' There were no US casualties during the rescue missions. Israel had postponed several planned strikes in Iran on April 4–5 to avoid interfering with the SAR effort and provided intelligence support.
📍Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad Province, Iran (ridgeline ~7,000 ft elevation) 💀No US casualties during SAR; 2x MC-130J destroyed at FARP; 2x UH-60 hit by small arms fire (operational) 🛡️CIA deception campaign active throughout; Israel delayed strikes and provided intel support
Sources: CNN Wikipedia — 2026 Iran War NBC News
5
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · IRGC Wave 93 — Operation True Promise 4
IRGC Launches 'Wave 93': Khurramshahr-4 MIRVs Strike Tel Aviv; Ramat David AB Hit; IRGC Claims F-35 Downed
On April 4–5, 2026, the IRGC announced the 92nd and 93rd consecutive waves of 'Operation True Promise 4,' executing large-scale combined missile and drone barrages against Israeli and US targets. The IRGC stated it launched Khurramshahr-4 multiple-warhead ballistic missiles, claiming to have struck more than 50 targets in central Tel Aviv and surrounding areas, as well as two ballistic missiles at Ramat David Air Base southeast of Haifa, home to Israeli F-16 squadrons. The IRGC additionally claimed to have destroyed a US F-35 fighter jet belonging to the Lakenheath squadron using its air defense systems over central Iran — an assertion the US has not confirmed. The IRGC's naval and air forces also claimed strikes against radar systems and naval equipment of US forces across the UAE, Kuwait, and Bahrain. Press TV reported the operation used a 'combination of solid and liquid fuel missiles, long-range and guided, and suicide drones,' with IRGC command vowing that 'launches will continue continuously, uninterruptedly, and shot after shot.'
📍Tel Aviv metro area, Ramat David AB (Haifa region), central Iran, UAE/Kuwait/Bahrain US bases 💀Casualties unconfirmed; IDF reported 1 lightly wounded; no confirmed US F-35 loss acknowledged by CENTCOM
Sources: ANI / Press TV via Trend.Az ANI News
5
Apr
IDF · Strike Package Offensive · IDF Beirut & Lebanon Strikes — 14 Killed
IDF Strikes Kill 14 Across Lebanon; Beirut Jnah Hit 100m from Major Hospital; Masnaa Border Crossing Threatened
On April 5, 2026, Israeli airstrikes across southern Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs killed at least 14 people and wounded dozens more. Four people were killed in Beirut's southern suburbs, while 10 people — including a family of six — were killed in attacks on southern Lebanon. A strike on the Jnah neighbourhood of Beirut wounded 39 people and struck approximately 100 metres from Rafik Hariri University Hospital, Lebanon's largest public medical facility. The previous day, Israel had threatened to strike the Masnaa border crossing between Lebanon and Syria — the country's primary civilian trade and transit route — prompting its closure. The IDF stated it was targeting Hezbollah infrastructure sites throughout the Beirut area and reissued evacuation warnings for dozens of southern Lebanese villages. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun publicly called for negotiations with Israel to spare southern Lebanon from further destruction.
📍Beirut (Jnah neighbourhood & southern suburbs), southern Lebanon, Masnaa border crossing (Syria border) 💀14 killed, 39+ wounded in Beirut/south Lebanon strikes (Lebanon MoPH, Apr 5)
Sources: Al Jazeera Alma Research Center (Apr 5 Daily Report)
5
Apr
Diplomacy · Trump Ultimatum Diplomatic · Power Grid Deadline Expires Today — No Deal Reached
Trump Apr 6 8PM ET Deadline: Power Grid Strikes Imminent — Mediators 'Less Optimistic'; Iran Rejects US Terms
As of April 5–6, 2026, Trump's self-imposed 10-day pause on strikes targeting Iranian energy infrastructure expires at 8:00 PM Eastern Time on April 6 (01:00 UTC April 7). In an interview with Axios published April 5, Trump warned 'I am blowing up everything' if no deal is reached, referring to power plants, bridges, oil wells, and possibly desalination facilities. Over the past 10 days the US and Iran have held indirect negotiations through Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey aimed at a ceasefire deal in exchange for reopening the Strait of Hormuz — but Axios confirmed 'no significant progress has been made.' Mediators told Axios they are 'less optimistic that a deal is close' but will 'work to the last minute.' The foreign ministers of Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey held calls on April 5 with both Witkoff and Araghchi but achieved no breakthrough. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi continued to publicly deny direct negotiations, stating 'the trust level is at zero.' Iran countered the US 15-point plan with its own five-point conditions, including retaining sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz. Israel separately was reported by Reuters and Israel Hayom to be preparing a comprehensive energy grid strike plan awaiting US approval. Tehran has threatened that any US power grid attack would trigger Iranian strikes on Israeli and Gulf energy infrastructure.
📍Indirect talks via Pakistan, Egypt, Turkey — no direct US-Iran contact confirmed ⚡April 6, 8PM ET: US pause on Iranian power grid strikes expires — outcome pending
Sources: Axios The Week India Israel Hayom
5
Apr
Iran · Internal Internal · Iranian Prosecutor Orders Seizure of Diaspora Assets
Iranian Prosecutor Orders Seizure of Assets and Bank Accounts of Iranians Living Abroad
On April 5, 2026, Iran's prosecutor ordered the seizure of assets and bank accounts of Iranians living abroad, a sweeping domestic legal measure widely interpreted as retaliation against diaspora Iranians perceived to be supporting or facilitating the US-Israeli war effort or anti-regime activity from outside Iran. The move escalates the regime's posture against its own citizens living internationally and is expected to affect hundreds of thousands of dual-national or expatriate Iranians whose families remain inside the country.
📍Iran (domestic legal decree — affects Iranian diaspora globally) 💀No casualties; significant legal and financial impact on Iranian diaspora community
Sources: Reactions to the 2026 Iran War — Wikipedia
6
Apr
Diplomacy · Deadline Day Ceasefire Talks · 45-Day Pause Framework
Trump Extends Deadline to Apr 7 8PM ET; US & Iran Discuss 45-Day Ceasefire via Pakistani, Egyptian & Turkish Mediators
With Trump's power-grid strike deadline originally set for Monday April 6 at 8PM ET, Trump on Sunday extended it by 20 hours to Tuesday April 7 at 8PM ET, posting the revision on Truth Social and labelling the day 'Power Plant Day' and 'Bridge Day' if no deal is reached. According to four US, Israeli and regional sources cited by Axios, the US, Iran and regional mediators are now discussing terms for a potential 45-day ceasefire as a first phase toward a permanent end to the war. Talks are being conducted through Pakistani, Egyptian and Turkish intermediaries as well as direct text exchanges between Trump envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi. Sources say chances of a partial deal within 48 hours are 'slim.' Iran's IRGC Navy stated Sunday the Strait of Hormuz will 'never return' to what it was before the war, and Iran's parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf dismissed Trump's threats as 'war crimes.' Trump told Fox News he thinks there is 'a good chance' of a deal, saying 'they are negotiating now.'
📍Negotiations via Oman/Pakistan/Egypt/Turkey back-channels; Trump Truth Social deadline post ⏳New deadline: Tuesday April 7, 2026 · 8PM ET — power grid & bridge strikes threatened if no deal
Sources: Axios The Week India NewsX
5
Apr
Iran · Wave 93 Offensive · Missile & Drone Barrage
IRGC Launches 'Wave 93': Mixed Ballistic Missiles & Suicide Drones Strike Israeli Military Sites; Carrier Group USS Abraham Lincoln Targeted
The IRGC launched its 93rd wave of Operation True Promise 4, employing a combination of solid- and liquid-fuel ballistic missiles, long-range guided munitions, and suicide drones targeting Israeli military staging areas deep inside Israel. The IRGC also announced that multiple waves of explosive drones were launched toward the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group in the northern Indian Ocean, claiming the carrier group 'fled its previous position and retreated into the depths of the Indian Ocean' — a claim unconfirmed by the US Navy. Additionally, the IRGC reported that an Israeli-linked tanker commercially named Aqua One was targeted and set ablaze in the central Persian Gulf. The IRGC military command vowed strikes 'will continue continuously, uninterruptedly, and shot after shot.' Israel's IAF intelligence unit assessed as of April 4 that Iran still retains over 1,000 ballistic missiles capable of reaching Israel, down from an estimated 2,500 at the start of the war, with daily launch rates now averaging 10–15 missiles versus ~90 on Day 1.
📍Targets: Israeli military sites (central Israel); USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group (N. Indian Ocean); Aqua One tanker (central Persian Gulf) 💀Iranian missile fire on Israel now ~10–15/day vs. ~90 on Day 1 (90% reduction per IDF/CENTCOM); IRGC claims heavy losses on US/Israeli side — unverified
Sources: NewKerala / IRGC via PressTV GlobalSecurity / PressTV Times of Israel
5
Apr
Coalition · Escalation Offensive · Economic Targets Expanded
IDF Defense Minister Katz Praises Petrochemical Strikes; Israel Shifts to 'Economic Targets'; Rosatom Evacuates 198 More Staff from Bushehr
Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz on April 5 praised the April 3–4 IDF strikes on the Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Economic Zone in Khuzestan Province — responsible for approximately 70% of Iran's domestic gasoline supply — calling the industry 'a central pillar of Iran's economy and the regime's ability to operate.' The strikes killed at least 5 people and wounded approximately 170 others per Iranian state media. Separately, Russia's Rosatom evacuated a further 198 staff from Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant on April 5, with senior IDF officials coordinating with Moscow for safe passage of Russian nationals. This marked the fourth attack in the area surrounding Bushehr. A Times of Israel report noted Israel's political leadership had instructed the IDF to shift toward striking 'economic targets' aimed at causing massive financial damage to the regime. Additional strikes were also confirmed in Karaj, Tabriz, Abadan, and continued target sets in Tehran including IRGC air defense systems, ballistic missile arrays, and R&D sites.
📍Mahshahr Petrochemical Zone (Khuzestan); Bushehr perimeter (4th strike); Tehran, Karaj, Tabriz, Abadan 💀5 killed, ~170 wounded at Mahshahr (IRNA); 198 Rosatom staff evacuated from Bushehr
Sources: Haaretz Times of Israel Alma Research
5
Apr
Lebanon · IDF Ground Ops Offensive · Beirut & South Lebanon Strikes
IDF Kills 14 Across Lebanon on Apr 5; Beirut Jnah Struck 100m from Rafik Hariri Hospital; Masnaa Border Crossing Evacuated After Israeli Threat
On April 5, Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon and Beirut killed at least 14 people. In Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahiyeh), four people were killed. In southern Lebanon, 10 people — including a family of six — were killed in separate strikes. A further 39 people were wounded in a strike on Beirut's Jnah neighbourhood approximately 100 metres from Rafik Hariri University Hospital, Lebanon's largest public medical facility. On April 4–5, Israel threatened to strike the Masnaa border crossing between Lebanon and Syria — a vital trade and humanitarian route — prompting its full evacuation and temporary closure. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun reiterated calls for talks with Israel. The Lebanese government has formally called for Hezbollah's disarmament and classified its military activities as illegal. France's UN ambassador stated: 'Hezbollah is conducting asymmetric hostilities in which civilians are the first to pay the price.' Over 1.2 million people remain displaced in Lebanon — roughly one-fifth of the entire population — amid dire humanitarian conditions. The conflict has killed at least 1,345 people and wounded over 4,000 in Lebanon since March 2. Three Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers have been killed.
📍Beirut (Jnah, 100m from Rafik Hariri Hospital; Dahiyeh suburbs); South Lebanon; Masnaa/Jdeidet Yabous border crossing (Syria–Lebanon) 💀14 killed (4 Beirut, 10 south Lebanon); 39 wounded near Rafik Hariri Hospital; 1,345+ total killed in Lebanon; 1.2M+ displaced
Sources: Al Jazeera The National Long War Journal
6
Apr
US–Israel · Strike Offensive · Leadership Kill
IDF Kills IRGC Intelligence Chief Maj. Gen. Majid Khadami; Sharif University Hit in Tehran Wave
On April 6, US-Israeli strikes killed at least 25+ people across Iran, including Maj. Gen. Majid Khadami, head of intelligence for Iran's IRGC, according to Iranian state media and confirmed by Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz. Thick black smoke rose near Tehran's Azadi Square after one strike hit the grounds of the Sharif University of Technology — described by US Rep. Yassamin Ansari as 'Iran's MIT.' Explosions rang out across Tehran for hours as low-flying jets continued strikes. Iran responded with at least five missile barrages on Israel during the morning, including strikes on Haifa where four people were found dead in rubble, and burning vehicles were reported in Petah Tikva. Trump threatened 'Power Plant Day and Bridge Day' by Tuesday 8PM ET if Iran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
📍Tehran (Sharif University, Azadi Sq.), Haifa, Petah Tikva 💀25+ killed in Iran (incl. IRGC intel chief Khadami); 4 dead in Haifa missile strike
Sources: AP / Dakota News Now CNN Live NBC News Live
6
Apr
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Proposal Diplomatic · Mediation
Egypt, Pakistan & Turkey Submit 45-Day Ceasefire Draft to Iran & US; Trump Extends Hormuz Deadline to Apr 7 8PM ET
Egyptian, Pakistani, and Turkish mediators formally sent both Iran and the US a draft ceasefire proposal calling for a 45-day cessation of hostilities and the partial reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, according to two Mideast officials speaking on condition of anonymity to AP. Pakistan's Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir reportedly led the mediation push, working the phones through Sunday night. A senior White House official confirmed to NBC News that a 45-day ceasefire is 'one of many things being discussed' but that President Trump has not signed off. Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei confirmed mediators were passing messages but refused to confirm specifics, stating that 'negotiation is in no way compatible with ultimatum, crime, or threats.' Trump extended his Strait of Hormuz deadline — originally set for April 6 — by approximately 20 hours to Tuesday, April 7 at 8PM ET, posting on Truth Social: 'Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one, in Iran.' Sources close to the mediation cautioned that chances of a deal before the deadline are slim, and warned that an Iranian retaliation to US-Israeli strikes on energy infrastructure could be catastrophic for Gulf oil and water facilities.
📍Mediated via Pakistan, Egypt, Turkey; proposal sent to Araghchi & Witkoff ⏰New Trump deadline: Apr 7, 2026 · 8PM ET — Power plants & bridges threatened
Sources: Axios AP / KSAT Bloomberg NBC News
6
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Multi-Wave Missile & Drone Barrage
IRGC Fires 5th+ Missile Alert of the Day on Israel; Haifa Residential Building Collapses, 4 Dead
On April 6, Iran launched at least five successive missile barrages against Israel — the fifth alert of the morning sounding before noon UTC. Israeli military confirmed four people killed in Haifa after being trapped under rubble of a residential building struck by an Iranian missile overnight (Apr 5–6). Iran simultaneously launched missiles and drones at Gulf Arab neighbors as Trump's Strait of Hormuz deadline loomed. The IRGC vowed a 'more severe and expansive' response if Trump follows through on his threatened strikes against Iranian power plants and bridges. Iran's Foreign Ministry confirmed it has formulated its response to the ceasefire proposals but refused direct talks while US-Israeli attacks continue, stating 'negotiation is in no way compatible with ultimatum.'
📍Haifa (residential building collapse), Tel Aviv region, Petah Tikva; Gulf states also targeted 💀4 confirmed dead in Haifa rubble; cars burned in Petah Tikva; dozens of missile/drone intercepts across Gulf
Sources: AP / KSAT Al Jazeera Live NBC News Live
6
Apr
Iran · Nuclear Protest Diplomatic · IAEA Warning
Iran Writes IAEA: Bushehr Reactor Struck 4 Times, Latest Kill Apr 4; Warns of 'Irreparable' Radioactive Risk
Iran's atomic energy chief Mohammad Eslami wrote to IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi on April 6, warning that Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant — Iran's only operational reactor — has been struck four times, the most recent on April 4, killing a security staff member and injuring others. Eslami warned the attacks risk the 'widespread release of radioactive materials from an operational reactor' with 'irreparable consequences' for civilians, the environment, and neighboring countries. He criticized the IAEA for offering only 'deep concern' rather than strong condemnation, warning that inaction may 'embolden the aggressors.' Russia's Rosatom had evacuated 198 additional staff on April 5, following prior evacuations.
📍Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant, Bushehr Province, Iran ☢️4 strikes on Bushehr reactor complex since war began; 1 security guard killed Apr 4; 198 Rosatom workers evacuated Apr 5
Sources: NBC News Live Al Jazeera
6
Apr
Iran · Rejection Diplomatic · Ceasefire Rejection
Iran Formally Rejects 45-Day Ceasefire Proposal; Calls Plan 'Illogical'
On April 6, Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmail Baghaei publicly rejected a proposed 45-day ceasefire framework drafted by Egyptian, Pakistani, and Turkish mediators and submitted to both Tehran and US special envoy Steve Witkoff. Baghaei stated the proposal was 'extremely excessive and unusual and illogical' and that any diplomatic talks were 'absolutely incompatible with ultimatums, crimes, and threats to commit war crimes.' Iran insisted it was seeking a permanent end to the war — not a temporary pause — warning that a ceasefire would allow the US and Israel to regroup. A White House official confirmed the 45-day plan was 'one of many ideas' but that 'the President has not signed off on it' and that 'Operation Epic Fury continues.' Pakistan's army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir reportedly stayed in contact overnight with VP JD Vance, envoy Witkoff, and Iranian FM Araghchi in a last-ditch mediation effort.
📍Tehran / Washington / Islamabad — diplomatic back-channel ⏰Trump Hormuz deadline: Tuesday Apr 7 at 8PM ET — extension described as 'highly unlikely' to move again
Sources: Axios CNBC Bloomberg
6
Apr
IDF · Air Strike Offensive · Multi-Airport Wave
IAF Strikes Three Tehran Airports Overnight Apr 5–6; Two Senior IRGC-QF Leaders Killed
The Israeli Air Force conducted a large-scale overnight wave of strikes targeting three airports across Tehran — Bahram Airport, Mehrabad Airport, and Azmayesh Airport — aimed at degrading Iranian Air Force and IRGC Air Force capabilities. The IDF described Mehrabad as a central hub used by the IRGC Quds Force for arming and financing the regime's proxy network across the Middle East. Separately, the IDF confirmed killing two senior IRGC Quds Force members in strikes over the past 24 hours, including an artillery chief of the Imam Hussein Division identified as 'Melhem,' who directed artillery launches targeting Israel and oversaw weapons procurement. Several additional senior aides to Division Commander Yahya Hussein were also killed in the same strike. The IDF simultaneously confirmed it struck over 120 air defense systems and ballistic missile arrays across western and central Iran, stating the destroyed systems 'preserves the Israeli Air Force's air superiority in the skies over Iran.'
📍Bahram, Mehrabad & Azmayesh Airports, Tehran; western and central Iran 💀2 senior IRGC-QF leaders killed + several aides; 120+ air defense & missile systems destroyed
Sources: Jerusalem Post Times of Israel
6
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Wave 94+
IRGC Fires Wave 94+ at Israel; Neot Hovav Industrial Zone Hit for Third Time; 4 Salvos on Apr 5
The IRGC launched Wave 94 of Operation True Promise 4 on April 5, targeting Israeli industrial and military sites across the south, center, and north of the country including Tel Aviv, Dimona, the Negev, Beersheba, and Ramat Gan. Iran fired four salvos of missiles at northern and southern Israel on April 5, with one projectile penetrating air defenses and striking the Neot Hovav industrial zone south of Beersheba — the third time the park has been targeted since the war began. Firefighters confirmed no hazardous material leak, with the missile striking an open area. Israeli air defenses failed to intercept the missile, which carried a conventional warhead. The IRGC stated strikes used Khorramshahr, Kheibar Shekan, and Emad ballistic missiles alongside suicide drones in 'deep, successive fire.' Meanwhile on April 6, the IAEA warned that ongoing military activity near Bushehr nuclear power plant — struck for the fourth time on April 4 — could cause a 'severe radiological accident' with consequences beyond Iran's borders.
📍Neot Hovav industrial zone (S. Israel), Dimona, Beersheba, Ramat Gan, Tel Aviv 💥No casualties at Neot Hovav; IAEA warning issued on Bushehr radiological risk (Apr 6)
Sources: Times of Israel CNN GlobalSecurity
6
Apr
IDF · Economic Strike Offensive · Petrochemical Infrastructure
Israel Strikes South Pars Petrochemical Complex; Jam & Damavand Facilities Rendered Inoperative
Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz announced on April 6 that Israel had struck the South Pars petrochemical complex in Iran, and that the Jam and Damavand petrochemical facilities — which collectively account for approximately 85% of Iran's petrochemical exports — have been rendered inoperative. Katz described the strike as 'a severe economic blow' that would cost Iran 'tens of billions of dollars' in lost petrochemical profits. The campaign against petrochemical infrastructure follows an earlier IAF strike on the Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Zone on April 3–4, which killed at least five and injured 170. The Mahshahr complex was responsible for approximately 70% of Iran's domestic gasoline supply. Prime Minister Netanyahu had signaled willingness to escalate economic strikes in a video address on April 5.
📍South Pars Petrochemical Complex; Jam & Damavand plants, Iran 💰~85% of Iran's petrochemical export capacity rendered inoperative; 'tens of billions' in losses (IDF/Katz estimate)
Sources: CNN Haaretz
6
Apr
Iran · IAEA Warning Diplomatic · Nuclear Risk Escalation
IAEA Warns Bushehr Strikes Risk 'Severe Radiological Accident'; Iran Writes Formal Protest Letter
The International Atomic Energy Agency issued an urgent warning on April 6 that ongoing military activity near Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant could cause a 'severe radiological accident' with potential consequences beyond Iran's borders. Iran's Atomic Energy Organisation confirmed in a formal letter to the IAEA Director General that the plant had been struck for the fourth time on April 4, and warned of 'irreparable' radiological risk. Earlier, Russia's Rosatom had begun evacuating personnel from the facility — approximately 198 Russian citizens working at the site — following the April 4 strike on an auxiliary building. The AEOI accused the IAEA of 'clear complicity' with the US and Israel by failing to condemn the strikes on the plant.
📍Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant, Bushehr Province, Iran ☢️4th strike on Apr 4; IAEA warns of cross-border radiological risk; 198 Rosatom staff evacuated
Sources: CNN Alma Research
6
Apr
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · Displacement Crisis
Lebanon: 1.2 Million Displaced (22% of Population); Death Toll Reaches 1,497+
Lebanon's Health Ministry confirmed on April 6 that at least 1,497 people have been killed and 4,430 wounded since the war in Lebanon began — 1,235 men, 97 women, and 129 children. Lebanese authorities and Al Jazeera reported that Israel's military campaign has forced roughly 1.2 million people from their homes, representing approximately 22% of Lebanon's total population — one of the fastest mass displacements in the country's history. Evacuation orders have been issued for over 100 villages, covering the entire area south of the Litani River and parts of Beirut's southern suburbs. IDF Chief of Staff Maj. Gen. Eyal Zamir told northern Israeli municipal officials on April 6 that displaced populations in southern Lebanon 'will not return until we ensure a demilitarized area south of the Litani River.' Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon killed at least 11 on Easter Sunday (April 6). Israel also killed at least 14 on April 5, including four in Beirut's Jnah neighbourhood and 10 across southern Lebanon. As of April 6, the IRC reports more than 1 million displaced include over 300,000 children.
📍Southern Lebanon, Beirut southern suburbs, Bekaa Valley 💀1,497+ killed / 4,430+ wounded in Lebanon; 1.2M displaced (22% of population) 👶300,000+ displaced children; over 180,000 crossed into Syria
Sources: GlobalSecurity/AFP Al Jazeera SpaceDaily/Al Jazeera IRC
6
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Ballistic Missile Strike
Iranian Missile Kills 4 in Haifa Residential Building; Cluster Munition Hits Ramat Gan
An Iranian ballistic missile scored a direct hit on a six-story residential building in Haifa overnight April 5–6, causing a partial collapse and killing four members of the same family: an elderly couple (Vladimir and Yelena Gershovich, both in their 80s), their son Dmitri (40s), and his partner Lucille-Jean (30s). The missile's warhead, estimated to contain several hundred kilograms of explosive material, failed to detonate on impact — preventing even greater casualties. Rescue operations lasted more than 18 hours. Separately, a cluster-munition-equipped Iranian ballistic missile later struck Ramat Gan in central Israel, causing extensive property damage and moderately injuring one person. Shrapnel from intercepted missiles struck at least 20 sites across central and northern Israel. Iran said it targeted the Haifa oil refinery, which it claimed supplies fuel to Israeli jets.
📍Haifa (direct hit, building collapse) & Ramat Gan (cluster munition), Israel 💀4 killed (Haifa); 1 moderately injured (Ramat Gan); 82-yr-old seriously wounded
Sources: Jerusalem Post Times of Israel NPR
6
Apr
IDF · Leadership Strike Offensive · Targeted Killing
IDF Kills IRGC Intelligence Chief Khademi & Quds Force Unit 840 Commander Bagheri in Overnight Strikes
In a pre-dawn precision strike on April 6, the IDF eliminated two of Iran's most senior remaining intelligence and special-operations figures. Brigadier General Majid Khademi — described by a senior Israeli official as 'effectively No. 2 within the IRGC' and one of the few commanders who had survived multiple previous targeting waves — was killed alongside Asghar Bagheri (also identified as Yazdan Mir / Sardar Bagheri), commander of the Quds Force's clandestine Unit 840, responsible for overseas covert and terrorist operations against Jews and Israelis worldwide. The IRGC confirmed Khademi's death via Tasnim News, calling him a 'martyr.' The IDF stated Khademi had been involved in attempts to penetrate U.S. systems including the Pentagon and coordinated extensively with Russia. Khademi had only recently assumed the IRGC Intelligence Organization's top post after his predecessor was also eliminated. The same day's strikes killed more than 25 people across Iran, per wire reports.
📍Tehran area, Iran 💀Khademi (IRGC Intel Chief) + Bagheri (QF Unit 840 Cmdr) confirmed killed; 25+ total killed in Apr 6 strikes
Sources: Fox News Washington Examiner CBS News
6
Apr
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Push Diplomatic · Ultimatum & Negotiation
Iran Rejects Temporary Ceasefire; Pakistan-Brokered 45-Day Proposal On Table; Trump Sets Hard Deadline of Tuesday 8 PM ET
With the war entering its 38th–39th day, a Pakistani-brokered two-phase ceasefire proposal was circulated to both the U.S. and Iran on Sunday April 6: an immediate 45-day ceasefire leading to permanent end-of-war negotiations, with the Strait of Hormuz to be addressed in that window. Pakistan's Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir held overnight contacts with U.S. VP JD Vance, envoy Steve Witkoff, and Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi. Trump called Iran's formal 10-point response through Pakistan 'a significant proposal' but 'not good enough,' and set a hard deadline of Tuesday April 7 at 8 PM Eastern Time (Wednesday 0000 GMT), threatening to destroy every Iranian power plant and bridge if no deal is reached. Iran's Foreign Ministry flatly rejected any temporary ceasefire, demanding a permanent end to hostilities, lifting of all sanctions, reconstruction payments, and inclusion of Lebanon in any deal. A White House official confirmed Trump had 'not signed off' on the 45-day proposal, calling it 'one of many things being discussed.' Iran warned of 'much more devastating and widespread' retaliation if civilian infrastructure is hit. The IRGC Naval Force simultaneously announced it was 'completing operational preparations for a new Persian Gulf order' in the Strait of Hormuz.
📍Washington D.C. / Islamabad / Tehran (indirect) ⚠️Trump deadline: Tue Apr 7 8 PM ET — power plants & bridges at risk if no deal
Sources: CNBC Axios NBC News NPR CNN
6
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Multi-Front Strikes
Iran Strikes Gulf Energy Infrastructure; Drone Attack Injures 15 U.S. Troops at Ali Al Salem AB Kuwait
On April 6, Iran continued sustained multi-front strikes across the Gulf. An Iranian drone strike on Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait injured 15 American service members, according to U.S. officials who spoke to CBS News. Iran also targeted a telecom building and a strategic port in the UAE vital for food imports (Dubai's main port remains inaccessible). UAE air defenses continued intercepting waves of Iranian missiles and drones through Monday. Saudi Arabia intercepted and destroyed seven ballistic missiles launched toward its Eastern Region, with debris falling near energy facilities. Iran's central military command (Khatam al-Anbiya HQ) warned on Monday that 'if attacks on civilian targets are repeated, the next stages of our offensive and retaliatory operations will be much more devastating and widespread.' The IRGC Naval Force declared it was completing preparations for a 'new Persian Gulf order' in the Strait of Hormuz.
📍Ali Al Salem AB (Kuwait); UAE telecom/port; Saudi Eastern Region energy facilities 💀15 U.S. troops injured at Ali Al Salem AB (CBS News); no fatalities confirmed
Sources: CBS News Haaretz NPR
6
Apr
Iran · Counter-Proposal Diplomatic · Ceasefire Rejection
Iran Rejects 45-Day Ceasefire; Sends 10-Clause Counter-Demand to Pakistan Mediators
Iran's state news agency IRNA reported Monday that Tehran formally rejected a 45-day temporary ceasefire proposal brokered by Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey, instead transmitting a 10-clause counter-proposal to Pakistani intermediaries demanding a permanent end to the war. Iran's demands include lifting all U.S. and international sanctions, a protocol guaranteeing safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, reconstruction compensation, and the explicit coupling of any Iran ceasefire to an end of hostilities in Lebanon. Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei told reporters the U.S. 15-point plan was "extremely excessive and unusual," and that any negotiations were "incompatible with ultimatums and threats to commit war crimes." President Trump, speaking on the White House South Lawn, acknowledged the proposal was "not good enough, but a very significant step," and reiterated his Tuesday 8 p.m. ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face strikes on "every bridge and power plant" in the country. Pakistan's Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir remained in contact throughout the night with VP Vance, envoy Witkoff, and Iranian FM Araghchi in an effort to bridge the gap before the deadline.
📍Pakistan-mediated channel · White House briefing room 🕐Deadline: Tuesday Apr 7 · 8:00 PM ET ⚠️Iran demands permanent end to war + Lebanon ceasefire linkage
Sources: NPR Axios PBS NewsHour CNN J.Post
6
Apr
CENTCOM · Escalation Warning Offensive · Strike Escalation Signal
Hegseth: 'Today Will Be Largest Volume of Strikes Since Day One; Tomorrow Even More'
U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, speaking Monday as Iran rejected the ceasefire proposal, declared that April 6 would see the highest volume of coalition airstrikes since the opening day of Operation Epic Fury on February 28, and that April 7 would exceed that figure. CENTCOM separately confirmed it has now struck over 13,000 targets in Iran since the war's start, surpassing the 12,300 figure announced on April 2. President Trump escalated his rhetoric at a White House press conference, warning that "every bridge in Iran will be decimated" and "every power plant in Iran will be out of business, burning, exploding" if Tehran does not comply with his Tuesday 8 p.m. deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Trump also stated, "The entire country can be taken out in one night, and that night might be tomorrow night." An Israeli political source told Haaretz that Israel believes U.S.-Iran talks are likely to fail, and that Israel has a prepared target bank for Iranian energy and civilian infrastructure should Trump authorize the next strike wave.
📍CENTCOM · White House · Washington D.C. 🎯13,000+ targets struck since Feb 28 (CENTCOM) ⚠️Hegseth signals largest single-day strike wave imminent on Apr 7
Sources: PBS NewsHour Middle East Eye Haaretz Time
6
Apr
IDF · Leadership Strike Offensive · HVT Elimination
IDF Strikes Three Tehran Airports; Kills IRGC-QF Artillery Chief Kamil Melhem & Senior Aids
The Israeli Air Force conducted a large-scale overnight wave of strikes on three airports across Tehran — Bahram Airport, Mehrabad Airport, and Azmayesh Airport — aimed at degrading remaining Iranian Air Force and IRGC Air Force capabilities. Dozens of IAF fighter jets participated. Mehrabad Airport, targeted multiple times during Operation Roaring Lion, had served as a central IRGC Quds Force hub for arming and financing proxy forces across the Middle East. Separately, the IDF confirmed the killing of Kamil Melhem, the Imam Hussein Division's artillery commander within the IRGC Quds Force, who had directed artillery launches targeting Israel and overseen weapons procurement for the IRGC. Several other senior aides to Imam Hussein Division Commander Yahya Hussein were also killed in the strike. Israel also claimed the killing of Yazdan Mir, a high-ranking IRGC Quds Force commander, in the same strike package that eliminated IRGC intelligence chief Majid Khademi on April 6.
📍Bahram, Mehrabad & Azmayesh Airports — Tehran, Iran 💀Kamil Melhem (IRGC-QF Imam Hussein Div. Artillery Chief) KIA · Yazdan Mir (IRGC-QF senior cmdr) KIA ✈️Dozens of IAF jets · large-scale airport suppression wave
Sources: Jerusalem Post Wikipedia / 2026 Iran War
6
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Wave 93 Multi-Domain Strike
IRGC Launches Wave 93 of Operation True Promise IV; Targets Israeli Sites & U.S. Carrier Group
The IRGC announced the launch of "Wave 93" of its sustained retaliatory campaign, Operation True Promise IV, employing a combination of solid- and liquid-fuel ballistic missiles and suicide drones against Israeli military positions across the occupied territories. The IRGC military command vowed launches would "continue continuously, uninterruptedly, and shot after shot." In a separate naval component of the same wave, IRGC naval forces conducted five maritime operations, including a strike on an Israeli-linked tanker commercially named Aqua One in the central Persian Gulf, which the IRGC reported was "burning." Additionally, multiple waves of explosive drones were launched toward the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group in the northern Indian Ocean; the IRGC claimed, citing satellite imagery, that the carrier group had "fled its previous position" and retreated deeper into the Indian Ocean. The IRGC framed the wave as a direct retaliation for continued U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iranian cities, infrastructure, and leadership.
📍Israeli occupied territories · Central Persian Gulf · Northern Indian Ocean 🚀Wave 93 · solid/liquid fuel ballistic missiles + suicide drones 🚢Tanker 'Aqua One' struck & burning in Persian Gulf · USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group targeted
Sources: GlobalSecurity / PressTV NewKerala / IRGC statement
6
Apr
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · Displacement & Casualties
Lebanon Death Toll Reaches 1,497+; 1.2 Million Displaced (22% of Population); IAEA Warns of Bushehr Radiological Risk
Lebanese health authorities reported that the death toll from Israeli strikes in Lebanon since March 2 has climbed to 1,497+, with over 4,430 wounded, including 126 children killed. UN data confirmed that 1.2 million Lebanese — approximately 22% of the entire national population — have been displaced, overwhelming shelter capacity and forcing hundreds of thousands to sleep in streets, vehicles, and public spaces. IDF ground operations continue to push deeper into southern Lebanon, with Israeli officials declaring intent to establish a security zone up to the Litani River. On April 5, Israel struck what it called a "terror target" in the predominantly Christian town of Ain Saadeh east of Beirut, killing a Lebanese Forces party official, his wife, and a friend — deepening sectarian fissures. Separately, the IAEA warned Monday that ongoing military activity near Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant — struck for the fourth time — could cause a "severe radiological accident" with potential consequences beyond Iran's borders; Russia began evacuating 198 citizens from the facility.
📍Southern Lebanon · Beirut suburbs · Ain Saadeh · Bushehr NPP (Iran) 💀1,497+ killed in Lebanon · 4,430+ wounded · 126 children 🏚️1.2 million displaced (22% of Lebanon's population) ☢️IAEA: Bushehr NPP 4th strike — radiological accident risk flagged
Sources: Al Jazeera GlobalSecurity / RFE-RL CNN SpaceDaily
7
Apr
US · Strike Offensive · Precision Strike
CENTCOM Strikes Kharg Island Military Targets (Second Wave); Oil Infrastructure Spared
U.S. Central Command confirmed a second major strike wave against Iran's Kharg Island, Iran's primary crude oil export hub responsible for approximately 90% of the country's oil exports. A U.S. official told NBC News that the military struck dozens of military targets on the island overnight, targeting the same category of military sites hit in the March 13 strike — including naval mine storage facilities and missile storage bunkers — while explicitly sparing oil infrastructure. The strikes came hours ahead of President Trump's 8 p.m. ET Tuesday deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face what Trump described as 'complete demolition' of Iranian bridges and power plants. VP JD Vance confirmed the strikes from Budapest, saying they did not represent 'a change in strategy.' Oil prices spiked more than 3%, with U.S. crude reaching $116/barrel.
📍Kharg Island, Persian Gulf — Iran's primary oil export terminal 💥Dozens of military targets struck: mine storage, missile bunkers, naval infrastructure 🛢️Oil infrastructure deliberately preserved; Brent crude surged past $110/barrel ⏰Strikes precede Trump's 8 PM ET Strait of Hormuz deadline
Sources: NBC News CNN Al-Monitor / Reuters
7
Apr
IDF · Strike Wave Offensive · Infrastructure & Logistics
IDF Completes Wide-Scale Wave Targeting Dozens of Iranian Infrastructure Sites; Issues Railway Warning
The Israel Defense Forces announced via X that it 'completed a wide-scale wave of strikes targeting dozens of infrastructure sites' across several areas of Iran on Tuesday. In advance of the strikes, the IDF's Persian-language X account issued an unprecedented warning to Iranian civilians to 'refrain from using and traveling by train throughout Iran' until 9 p.m. local time, citing that 'your presence on trains and near railway lines endangers your life.' Iranian security sources cited by Israeli Army Radio stated the rail network was being used by the IRGC to move and conceal weapons. Following the warning, train travel from Mashhad — Iran's second city — was cancelled until further notice. A railway bridge near the central Iranian city of Kashan was subsequently struck, killing two people and injuring three, per Iran's official IRNA news agency. A bridge in Qom was also struck. Iran's Ministry of Health confirmed at least 18 civilians, including two children, were killed in strikes on Alborz Province west of Tehran. The IDF also struck a petrochemical plant in Shiraz after attacking South Pars gasfield targets the night prior.
📍Railway bridge near Kashan struck; Qom bridge also hit; Alborz Province residential areas targeted 💀2 killed, 3 injured in Kashan bridge strike (IRNA); 18+ killed including 2 children in Alborz Province (IRNA/Mehr) 🚆IDF issued evacuation warning for all Iranian rail travel until 21:00 local time; Mashhad rail suspended ⚗️IDF struck petrochemical plant in Shiraz after South Pars gasfield hit overnight
Sources: CNN Live Al Jazeera Haaretz
7
Apr
IDF · Strike Collateral · Controversial Strike
Rafi-Nia Synagogue in Central Tehran Destroyed in US-Israeli Strike; Condemned During Passover
Iranian state media, including the official IRNA news agency and semi-official Mehr News Agency, reported that a US-Israeli strike on a residential building in central Tehran destroyed the adjacent Rafi-Nia Synagogue — a historic center for Tehran's Jewish community. Footage reviewed by the Associated Press showed civil defence workers amid the rubble, with Hebrew-language books scattered on the ground. Iran's semi-official Mehr news agency reported the synagogue was destroyed when the adjacent building was struck and that surrounding structures suffered severe internal and external damage due to the narrow streets. The IDF had not officially commented by publication time; Israel's Diaspora Affairs Ministry noted 'conflicting reports' as to whether the synagogue was damaged or completely destroyed. The Jewish community's representative in Iran's parliament, Homayoun Sameh Najafabadi, condemned the strikes, noting the attack occurred during the Passover holiday. Six bodies were recovered from rubble in the city of Pardis east of Tehran in related overnight strikes.
📍Central Tehran — Rafi-Nia Synagogue and adjacent residential buildings 💀No immediate casualties reported at synagogue; 6 bodies recovered in Pardis (Mehr News) ⚠️Struck during Passover; condemned by Jewish community representative in Iran's parliament 🔍IDF not confirmed; Israel's Diaspora Ministry investigating conflicting damage reports
Sources: Al Jazeera ABC News Times of Israel
7
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Cluster Munition Strike
IRGC Fires Cluster Warhead Ballistic Missiles at Tel Aviv, Ben-Gurion & Ramon Airports; Sharon Plain Hit
Iran's IRGC launched ballistic missiles armed with cluster warheads targeting Tel Aviv, central Israel, Ben-Gurion International Airport, Ramon Airport, and the West Bank on April 7, triggering widespread sirens. The Israel Defense Forces confirmed Iranian cluster munitions caused impacts at multiple sites across the Sharon plain and central Israel. Rescue teams responded to multiple impact sites. Hezbollah simultaneously launched rockets and drones toward northern Israel, triggering additional sirens, with one person lightly wounded. Explosions were also heard in Damascus as Israeli air defenses intercepted Iranian missiles transiting Lebanese and Syrian airspace. Seven ballistic missiles were intercepted heading toward eastern Saudi Arabia overnight, with debris falling near energy facilities. An Iranian drone strike on Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait injured 15 American service members, per CBS News citing U.S. officials.
📍Tel Aviv metro, Ben-Gurion Airport, Ramon Airport, Sharon Plain, West Bank; Kuwait (Ali Al Salem AB) 💀1 person lightly wounded in northern Israel (Hezbollah); 15 US troops injured at Kuwait base (CBS News) 💥Cluster submunitions impacted 17+ locations across central Israel (IDF); all airport strikes intercepted 🛡️7 Saudi-bound ballistic missiles intercepted overnight; debris fell near energy facilities
Sources: Haaretz Live CNN Times of Israel
7
Apr
Diplomacy · Ultimatum Diplomatic · Deadline Crisis
Trump Sets 8 PM ET Deadline: 'A Whole Civilization Will Die Tonight' Unless Iran Opens Strait
President Donald Trump escalated rhetoric to its most extreme pitch yet on April 7, posting on Truth Social that 'a whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again' unless Iran agrees to a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz before his 8 p.m. ET deadline. Trump warned the US has a plan to destroy 'every bridge' and 'every power plant' in Iran 'by 12 o'clock' in a four-hour campaign, while also leaving open a diplomatic off-ramp. Iran sent a 10-clause counter-proposal via Pakistan — rejecting a temporary ceasefire but calling for a permanent end to hostilities, a Strait safe-passage protocol, reconstruction commitments, and sanctions relief. Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei stated negotiations are 'in no way compatible with ultimatums.' Ceasefire talks were described as at a 'critical, sensitive stage.' Pakistan's army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir was in contact through the night with VP JD Vance, envoy Steve Witkoff, and Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi via direct text messages. Trump said Iran's offer was 'significant' but 'not good enough,' and called himself 'highly unlikely' to extend the deadline again.
📍White House / Tehran / Islamabad (Pakistan mediation channel) ⏰Deadline: Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 8:00 PM ET (0000 GMT Wednesday) 🕊️Iran sent 10-clause permanent peace counter-proposal via Pakistan; rejected 45-day temporary ceasefire ⚠️IRGC warned it will 'deprive the US and allies of the region's oil and gas for years' if Trump strikes infrastructure
Sources: CNBC RFE/RL NBC News
7
Apr
Lebanon · IDF Strike Offensive · Ground & Air
IDF Strikes Kill 8+ Across Lebanon; Paratroopers Brigade Active in South as Displacement Crisis Deepens
Lebanon's National News Agency reported Israeli strikes across the country on the morning of April 7, killing at least eight people. IDF footage published April 7 showed the Paratroopers Brigade conducting active operations in southern Lebanon. Hezbollah rocket and drone attacks triggered multiple sirens in northern Israel in response. Meanwhile, the broader Lebanese humanitarian crisis continues to worsen: as of late March 2026, the UN reported over 1.2 million people — approximately 22% of Lebanon's population — displaced from the south, Beirut's southern suburbs, and the Bekaa Valley. Lebanon's Health Ministry previously confirmed 1,497 killed and 4,430 wounded since March 2. IDF Chief of Staff Maj. Gen. Eyal Zamir told northern Israeli officials that evacuated Lebanese areas would not see population return until a demilitarized buffer south of the Litani River is established.
📍Southern Lebanon, Beirut suburbs, Bekaa Valley 💀8+ killed in April 7 strikes (NNA); 1,497+ total killed in Lebanon since Mar 2 (Health Ministry) 🏠1.2 million displaced — 22% of Lebanon's population (UN, late March 2026) 🛡️IDF Paratroopers Brigade active in south; Hezbollah rockets triggered sirens in northern Israel
Sources: CNN Al Jazeera Times of Israel
7
Apr
US · Offensive Strike Offensive · Infrastructure Strike
US Strikes Kharg Island Military Targets; Iran Vows to End Oil Infrastructure Restraint
On April 7, US forces commenced strikes on military targets on Kharg Island, Iran's key oil export hub responsible for roughly 90% of Iran's oil exports. The US official confirmed the strikes targeted military sites while oil infrastructure was deliberately spared. In direct response, Iran announced its prior restraint in targeting regional oil infrastructure would 'no longer apply,' a stark escalation threat with potentially catastrophic consequences for global energy markets. The strikes came hours ahead of Trump's 8 PM ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face destruction of power plants and bridges across the country.
📍Kharg Island, Persian Gulf — Iran's primary oil export hub (90% of exports) 💥90+ military sites struck; oil infrastructure deliberately spared per Trump ⚠️Iran threatens to end restraint on targeting regional oil infrastructure

7
Apr
Israel · Air Strike Offensive · Transport Infrastructure
IDF Strikes Iranian Railroads & Bridges; Netanyahu Confirms IRGC Transport Planes & Helicopters Destroyed
Israel's IDF carried out a broad wave of strikes targeting Iranian railroad lines and bridges used by the IRGC for military logistics, issuing a stark public warning to Iranian civilians: 'Your presence on trains and near railway lines endangers your life.' Railway activity in at least one Iranian city was suspended following the warning. Prime Minister Netanyahu confirmed in a video statement that the IDF had also destroyed IRGC transport aircraft and helicopters in the strikes. The IDF simultaneously struck Iran's 'most central site' for sonar and underwater detection system production in Shiraz, belonging to Iran's defense ministry, used for planning and manufacturing submarine detection systems. Israeli strikes in one province alone killed at least 18 civilians, according to an Iranian official, and at least 11 people were killed and 18 wounded in strikes near Tehran, per local reports.
📍Railway lines and bridges across Iran; sonar facility in Shiraz; Amin Abad rail bridge confirmed hit 💀At least 18 civilians killed in one province; 11 killed, 18 wounded near Tehran (local reports) ✈️IRGC transport planes and helicopters confirmed destroyed — Netanyahu statement
Sources: CNN Live – Apr 7 Times of Israel Liveblog CBS News Live Haaretz Live
7
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile Barrage
IRGC Fires Fresh Ballistic Missiles at Israel; Impacts Reported in Rosh Haayin, Ramat Hasharon & Southern Israel
On April 7, Iran launched additional ballistic missile salvos targeting multiple regions of Israel, including central and southern zones. The IDF confirmed a new ballistic missile attack targeting southern Israel and activated interception systems. Israeli media reported damage in Rosh Haayin and Ramat Hasharon, where emergency teams responded to multiple impact sites. Sirens sounded in Eilat, with one missile reportedly striking an open area without casualties. Separately, Iran fired seven ballistic missiles toward eastern Saudi Arabia overnight, all intercepted, with debris falling near energy facilities. The IRGC's continuing Operation True Promise 4 has now reached Wave 93 and beyond in its sustained campaign, using a combination of solid and liquid fuel ballistic missiles and suicide drones, with launches described as 'continuous, uninterrupted, and shot after shot' by IRGC command.
📍Rosh Haayin, Ramat Hasharon, Eilat (Israel); Eastern Saudi Arabia (overnight) 💀No confirmed new casualties from this wave; intercept debris reported in Holon, Rishon Letzion 🚀Operation True Promise 4 — Wave 93+; solid/liquid fuel BMs + suicide drones
