7
Apr
Iran · Diplomacy Diplomatic · Ceasefire Collapse
Iran Ends Ceasefire Negotiations; Informs Pakistan Talks Are Over as Trump Deadline Expires
In a dramatic diplomatic development on April 7, Iran formally informed Pakistani mediators that ceasefire negotiations with the United States are over, according to officials cited by The New York Times. This came after Iran had on April 6 rejected a US-backed 45-day ceasefire proposal brokered by Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey in Islamabad, insisting instead on a permanent end to the war. Iran's 10-clause counter-proposal demanded a protocol for Strait of Hormuz reopening, an end to all regional conflicts including Lebanon, lifting of all sanctions, and full reconstruction payment. Trump called Iran's counter-proposal 'a very significant step' but 'not good enough.' Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei declared Iran's forces would not be deterred by commander assassinations. With talks collapsed, Trump's 8 PM ET deadline for Iran to open the Strait or face destruction of power plants and bridges came into force. Hegseth warned April 7 would see 'the largest volume of strikes since day one.'
📍Islamabad (Pakistan mediators); Washington DC; Tehran 🕊️Iran rejects 45-day ceasefire; demands permanent end to war, sanctions relief, reconstruction ⚠️Trump 8 PM ET deadline expires — power plants and bridges threatened; Hegseth: 'largest strike volume since Day 1'
Sources: NPR Axios Haaretz Live PBS NewsHour
7
Apr
Iran · Civil Response Defensive · Human Shields
Iran Deploys Civilian Human Chains Around Power Plants Ahead of Trump's Destruction Deadline
With Trump's 8 PM ET deadline looming and explicit threats to destroy every Iranian power plant and bridge, Iran's deputy minister of youth and sports called on young people to form human chains around the country's power plants as a deterrent and act of civilian resistance. Iran's Fars News Agency posted a verified video showing hundreds of civilians who had answered the government's call, gathering in front of the Kazerun combined cycle power plant. The Iranian regime framed the campaign as 'Iranian Youth's Human Chain for a Bright Tomorrow.' UN spokesman Stéphane Dujarric warned that any attack on civilian infrastructure constitutes a clear violation of international law. Former Iranian FM Ali Akbar Velayati warned that strikes on power plants would plunge the entire region, including Saudi Arabia, into darkness. France condemned attacks on Iranian civil infrastructure, warning of global economic damage.
📍Kazerun combined cycle power plant and power stations across Iran 👥Hundreds of civilians forming human shields at power plants — Fars News / CBS verified ⚠️UN: striking civilian infrastructure is 'a very clear violation of international law'
Sources: CBS News Live CNN Live – Apr 7 PBS NewsHour
7
Apr
Israel · Lebanon Offensive · Ground & Air
IDF Orders Evacuation of Dozens of Villages South of Zahrani River; Warns of Maritime Zone Near Tyre–Rosh Hanikra
On April 7, the IDF ordered residents of dozens of villages in southern Lebanon to leave 'immediately' and flee north of the Zahrani River, citing Hezbollah activity. IDF Arabic-language spokesman Avichay Adraee posted that the military 'does not intend to harm' villagers but that Hezbollah activities are 'forcing strong action.' Separately, the IDF warned vessels in the maritime space from Tyre to Rosh Hanikra near Lebanon to evacuate north due to Hezbollah activity. As of April 7, over 1.2 million Lebanese — representing roughly one-fifth of the country's population — have been displaced. Lebanon's Health Ministry reported at least 8 people killed in Israeli strikes across the country this morning alone. The Norwegian Refugee Council noted Israel's evacuation orders now cover more than 1,470 sq km, approximately 14% of Lebanese territory.
📍South of Zahrani River, southern Lebanon; maritime zone Tyre–Rosh Hanikra 💀8+ killed in Lebanon on Apr 7 morning alone (NNA); 1,497+ total killed in Lebanon since Mar 2 🏠1.2 million+ displaced (20% of Lebanon's population); 14% of Lebanese territory under evacuation order
Sources: CNN Live – Apr 7 Al Jazeera – Lebanon Humanitarian Haaretz Live
7
Apr
Iran · Chemical Weapons Offensive · Targeted Strikes
IDF Strikes Marvdasht Petrochemical Complex Near Shiraz; Targets Linked to Chemical Weapons Program
On April 7, the Israeli Air Force struck the Marvdasht Petrochemical Complex near Shiraz, one day after striking Iran's largest petrochemical facility at Asaluyeh (South Pars) on April 6. The Foundation for Defense of Democracies assessed that both facilities — along with previously struck sites in the Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Economic Zone — are linked to Iran's chemical weapons research and precursor supply chains. Israeli strikes have also previously targeted Imam Hossein University and Malek Ashtar University of Technology, both sanctioned by the US for chemical weapons R&D, as well as the Tofigh Daru Research and Engineering Company at the Pasteur Institute in Tehran. Israeli Defense Minister Katz stated that the IDF has now struck sites responsible for 85 percent of Iran's petrochemical exports. The IDF simultaneously published footage confirming destruction of more than 130 Iranian air defense systems throughout the war.
📍Marvdasht, Fars Province, Iran (Apr 7); Asaluyeh / South Pars, Bushehr Province (Apr 6) 💥85% of Iran's petrochemical export capacity targeted; 130+ air defense systems destroyed per IDF ⚗️Facilities linked to chemical weapons precursor supply to IRGC's SPND; fentanyl weaponization program
Sources: FDD Analysis FDD — South Pars Strike
7
Apr
Diplomacy · Breakthrough Ceasefire · Two-Week Pause Announced
Trump Announces Two-Week Ceasefire After Iran Accepts 10-Point Framework as Basis for Negotiations
Shortly after Trump's 8 p.m. ET April 7 deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face destruction of its power plants and bridges, a dramatic diplomatic breakthrough occurred. Trump posted on Truth Social that he was suspending attacks on Iran for two weeks, contingent on Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz, describing the halt as a 'double-sided ceasefire.' Trump stated that the US had 'already met and exceeded all military objectives' and that the two sides were 'very far along' on a definitive long-term peace agreement. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi, speaking on behalf of the Supreme National Security Council, announced Iran's acceptance of the general framework of a 10-point proposal as a basis for negotiations. Iran's 10-point proposal included a protocol for reopening the Strait of Hormuz, an end to all regional conflicts, lifting of sanctions, and payment for reconstruction. Israel confirmed it agreed to the temporary ceasefire, subject to Iran's reopening of the strait. Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif welcomed the ceasefire and invited delegations to Islamabad on April 10 to continue negotiations toward a conclusive agreement. Oil prices plunged 8% on the news, sliding from $117 to approximately $103 per barrel.
📍Washington D.C. / Tehran — announced via Truth Social and Iranian state media 🕊️Two-week ceasefire; Iran accepts 10-point framework as basis for negotiations 🛢️Oil prices drop 8% to ~$103/bbl on ceasefire announcement; Strait of Hormuz reopening expected 🇵🇰Pakistan PM Sharif invites delegations to Islamabad April 10 for conclusive agreement talks
Sources: NBC News Live Blog CNN Live Updates
7
Apr
Iran · Missiles Offensive · Ballistic Missile Barrage
Iran Fires Ballistic Missiles at Beersheba and Central Israel Overnight; IDF Intercepts Missiles Over Southern Israel
Overnight April 7, Iranian forces fired a ballistic missile barrage targeting Beersheba and the surrounding area in Israel's south, following a lull of approximately 12 hours since Iran's previous assault. No injuries or confirmed impacts were reported as a result of the overnight attack. Separately, a missile fired toward central Israel earlier in the day was intercepted by the IDF; a 46-year-old man was lightly injured by falling fragments from the interception. Hezbollah rocket fire also struck the northern Israeli town of Nahariya from Lebanon, with Israeli rescue teams responding. Iran continued firing at an average pace of 7.7 attack waves per day over the past week, down from the war's overall average of 12.4 waves per day, with a total of 455 Iranian attack waves against Israel identified since the war's start.
📍Beersheba area (south Israel), central Israel; Nahariya (north Israel — Hezbollah rockets) 💀1 lightly injured (IDF interception shrapnel); no casualties from overnight Beersheba barrage 📊455 total Iranian attack waves vs. Israel since Feb 28; daily rate down to avg 7.7 waves/day

7
Apr
Coalition · IAF Offensive · Rail & Bridge Strikes
IDF Strikes 8–10 Rail Bridges and Road Sections Across Iran to Sever IRGC Weapons Transport Corridors
The Israeli Air Force completed an 'extensive' wave of strikes on April 7 targeting Iranian transportation infrastructure, destroying 8 to 10 rail bridges and sections of road used by the IRGC to transport missile launchers and weapons systems. The IDF stated it struck 'eight rail bridges and sections of roads used by the Iranian terror regime to transport weapons and military equipment.' Per Army Radio, 10 bridges and railways were targeted in total. Strikes were carried out across multiple Iranian cities including Tehran, Karaj, Tabriz, Tabriz-Zanjan corridor, Kashan, and Qom. Netanyahu confirmed the IDF was intensifying its attacks on the Islamic Republic. The IAF separately released new footage of strikes on Iranian air defense systems, confirming more than 130 destroyed since the start of the war. Additionally, CENTCOM previously struck Kharg Island's military sites (mine and missile storage) while sparing oil infrastructure. IDF also struck the Marvdasht and South Pars petrochemical complexes as part of the same operational surge.
📍Karaj, Qazvin (railways); Kashan, Tabriz-Zanjan (bridges); Tehran, Qom (air defense systems) 💥8–10 rail bridges/road sections destroyed; 130+ Iranian air defense systems eliminated to date 🚂IRGC weapon transfer capability significantly degraded per IDF security official briefing
Sources: Times of Israel IFCJ — IDF Rail Strikes
7
Apr
Iran · Retaliation Offensive · Missile Strikes Post-Ceasefire
Iran Fires Missiles at Israel and Gulf States After Ceasefire Takes Effect
Despite the two-week ceasefire declared on the evening of April 7, Iran launched additional ballistic missiles at Israel and struck Kuwait, UAE, and Bahrain in the hours immediately following the announcement. The IDF intercepted multiple incoming missiles over southern Israel. Shipping vessels began tentatively transiting the Strait of Hormuz following Iran's commitment to reopen the waterway as part of the ceasefire framework. Iran's Supreme National Security Council warned that its "hands remain upon the trigger" and that the ceasefire does not signify termination of the war.
📍Israel (southern & central), Kuwait, UAE, Bahrain — post-ceasefire launches 💀No confirmed new casualties from post-ceasefire salvoes; ceasefire in effect but sporadic launches ongoing

7
Apr
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Diplomatic · Pakistan-Mediated Pause
US–Iran Two-Week Ceasefire Declared; Islamabad Talks Set for April 10
Less than two hours before Trump's 8 p.m. ET deadline to strike Iran's power plants and bridges, Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif brokered a two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran. Trump posted on Truth Social that the US would suspend bombing for two weeks, contingent on Iran's "COMPLETE, IMMEDIATE, and SAFE OPENING" of the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi accepted on behalf of the Supreme National Security Council, stating that if attacks ceased, Iran's armed forces would halt defensive operations and allow safe passage through Hormuz "via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces." Trump said Iran had submitted a 10-point proposal calling it a "workable basis on which to negotiate." Iran's 10 points reportedly include US troop withdrawal from regional bases, lifting of all sanctions, and compensation for war damages. Peace talks in Islamabad are set for April 10, likely led by VP Vance. Israel's PM Netanyahu stated the ceasefire does not include Lebanon — a position disputed by Pakistan and other mediators. Secretary Hegseth called Operation Epic Fury "a historic and overwhelming victory." Oil prices plunged 13–16% on the announcement.
📍Washington D.C. / Islamabad — Pakistan-mediated framework 🕊️Two-week pause effective immediately; Islamabad talks April 10; Lebanon status disputed between Israel and Pakistan
Sources: CBS News NPR Foreign Policy
8
Apr
IDF · Ceasefire Declared Defensive · Ceasefire Entry
IDF Halts Strikes on Iran; Launches 'Largest Wave' on Hezbollah in Lebanon
The IDF announced it had ceased fire against Iran in accordance with directives from the political echelon, stating it was "highly prepared to respond defensively against any violation." The military said it carried out a final overnight wave targeting Iran's launching capabilities and infrastructure before the pause. However, Israel simultaneously confirmed it would continue targeting Hezbollah in Lebanon, launching what the IDF described as its largest single wave of strikes against the group since fighting began, hitting command centers in Beirut, the Beqaa Valley, and southern Lebanon. Hezbollah legislator Ibrahim Al-Moussawi warned of a response if Israel did not adhere to a ceasefire inclusive of Lebanon. Pakistan, France, and Egypt asserted Lebanon was included in the deal; Israel denied this.
📍Iran (cease-fire declared) / Beirut, Beqaa Valley, southern Lebanon (strikes continuing) ⚠️Lebanon ceasefire status disputed — IDF ground operations ongoing per Netanyahu's office
Sources: NBC News Times of Israel
6
Apr
Iran · Leadership Kinetic · IRGC Commander Eliminated
IDF Kills IRGC Intelligence Chief Maj. Gen. Seyed Majid Khademi
The IDF eliminated Maj. Gen. Seyed Majid Khademi, commander of IRGC intelligence, in overnight strikes on April 6. Khademi had been appointed in June 2025 after his predecessor was killed during the Twelve-Day War. His elimination represents the latest in a sustained Israeli campaign to decapitate the IRGC's command structure. On the same day, the IDF also struck Iran's largest petrochemical complex at Asaluyeh (South Pars), with Israeli Defense Minister Katz stating the IDF had now struck sites responsible for 85 percent of Iran's petrochemical exports. Iranian state media confirmed the strikes on the Mobin and Damavand utility companies supporting the complex.
📍Iran-wide — Asaluyeh (South Pars) petrochemical complex + IRGC intelligence HQ 💀Khademi KIA; 5 IRGC personnel also killed in northwest Iran per Iranian state media
Sources: FDD Haaretz
8
Apr
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Diplomatic · US–Iran Two-Week Ceasefire Holds; Islamabad Talks Confirmed for April 10
US–Iran Ceasefire Holds on Day 1; Hormuz Reopening Underway — Lebanon Excluded
Hours after the Pakistan-brokered two-week ceasefire took effect on April 7, both Iran and the United States confirmed the pause in hostilities was holding as of early April 8. Iran's Supreme National Security Council confirmed the agreement and claimed near-total achievement of its war objectives. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz would be 'possible via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces' for the two-week period. Oil prices plunged as much as 16% on the announcement, with U.S. crude sliding to around $103/bbl from a war-high of $117. Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif formally invited both delegations to Islamabad on April 10 to negotiate a conclusive settlement. Iran's 10-point proposal — which includes a protocol for reopening the Strait of Hormuz, lifting of sanctions, withdrawal of US forces from regional bases, and full payment of war damages — was accepted by Trump as a 'workable basis' for negotiation. Israeli PM Netanyahu's office confirmed Israel supports the two-week suspension of Iran strikes, but stated unequivocally that the ceasefire does NOT include Lebanon. Pakistan's PM had claimed it did, creating an immediate contradiction. Iran also threatened to resume operations if Israeli attacks on Lebanon continued.
📍Islamabad, Pakistan — talks set for Apr 10 · Strait of Hormuz — partial reopening underway 🕊️2-week ceasefire in effect; Lebanon explicitly excluded by Israel 💬Iran 10-point plan accepted as 'workable basis' by Trump (Truth Social, Apr 7)
Sources: CNBC Al Jazeera NPR
8
Apr
IDF · Operation Eternal Darkness Offensive · Largest Hezbollah Strike Wave of the War
IDF Launches 'Operation Eternal Darkness' — 100 Hezbollah Targets Hit in 10 Minutes Across Lebanon
Despite the US–Iran ceasefire announcement, Israel declared the truce does not apply to Lebanon and on April 8 launched what it called 'the largest concentrated blow Hezbollah has suffered since the pagers operation' (Defense Minister Katz). Within approximately 10 minutes, the IDF struck at least 100 Hezbollah command centers and military sites across southern Lebanon, Beirut's southern suburbs, the Bekaa Valley, central Beirut, and northern Lebanon — many without prior warning. Lebanon's Health Ministry issued a preliminary estimate of dozens killed and hundreds wounded. Israel struck Hiram Hospital in Tyre, causing extensive structural damage, and hit an ambulance in the city resulting in fatalities. Central Beirut neighborhoods including Barbour, Corniche Al Mazraa, Ain Al Mreisseh, and Burj Abi Haidar were struck. The IDF simultaneously issued fresh forced evacuation orders for Beirut's southern suburbs and the city of Tyre. Pakistan's PM Sharif insisted the ceasefire included Lebanon; Netanyahu's office contradicted this directly. Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam condemned the strikes as escalation at the moment Lebanese officials were seeking to negotiate. Hezbollah said no official ceasefire announcement had been made on their end and warned of a regional response if Israel continued.
📍Central Beirut, Tyre, southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley, northern Lebanon 💀Dozens killed, hundreds wounded (Lebanon Health Ministry preliminary estimate, Apr 8) 🏥Hiram Hospital Tyre damaged; ambulance struck with fatalities
Sources: PBS NewsHour Amnesty International The National
7
Apr
Iran · Ceasefire Breach Offensive · Post-Ceasefire Missile Launches
Iran Fires Final Missile Salvos at Israel and Gulf States as Ceasefire Takes Effect; Lavan & Sirri Oil Facilities Struck in Unattributed Attack
Just after the ceasefire declaration on April 7, Iran launched a final series of missiles at Israel and Gulf Arab states in what IRGC framing called its concluding retaliatory operations. Separately, Iran's Lavan Island oil refinery and Sirri Island crude export terminal — key petrochemical infrastructure — were struck in an 'enemy attack' with no party claiming responsibility. In response to ongoing Israeli attacks on Lebanon, Iran threatened to resume strikes on Israel 'if the aggressions against dear Lebanon are not brought to an immediate end.' Iranian media also reported that Iran paused Hormuz traffic coordination in protest of continued Israeli attacks on Lebanon, complicating the ceasefire's first hours.
📍Lavan Island refinery + Sirri Island crude export terminal, Persian Gulf ⚠️No party claimed responsibility for Lavan/Sirri strikes 🚨Iran threatens to resume anti-Israel strikes over Lebanon attacks
Sources: Wikipedia — 2026 Iran war Al Jazeera
7
Apr
IDF · Air Defense Degradation Offensive · S-300PMU2 Destroyed, Tehran Air Defenses Eliminated
IDF Destroys Iran's S-300PMU2 Air Defense System; IAF Publishes Footage of Tehran Air Defense Elimination
On April 7, the IDF released confirmed operational footage of Israeli Air Force strikes destroying Tehran's remaining air defense systems, including Iran's S-300PMU2 long-range surface-to-air missile battery — one of the most capable systems Russia had supplied to Iran. Army Recognition analysts confirmed the destruction through IDF-released footage dated April 7, 2026. The IDF stated these strikes 'preserve the Israeli Air Force's air superiority in the skies over Iran.' The IAF has now taken out more than 100 air defense systems and around 120 detection systems since the start of the war. Military officials confirmed IAF now holds air supremacy over most of Iran's airspace. Footage also showed strikes on Tehran at approximately 10:30 a.m. local time on April 7/8, and the Shi'ite holy city of Qom was also reportedly struck. Reports from The Times (London) stated that new Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei was located in Qom, unconscious, and unable to run the regime.
📍Tehran + Qom, Iran — air defense and command infrastructure 🛡️S-300PMU2 confirmed destroyed; IAF air supremacy over most of Iran declared 📡100+ air defense systems, 120+ detection systems destroyed (IDF, cumulative)
Sources: Army Recognition Jerusalem Post Times of Israel
7
Apr
CENTCOM · Drone Swarm Offensive · LUCAS Drone Swarms Deployed Against Iran
CENTCOM Deploys Hundreds of LUCAS One-Way Attack Drones Against Iran in Overnight Strike Wave
U.S. Central Command announced on April 7 that it had launched additional waves of one-way attack drones against Iran as part of Operation Epic Fury, confirming that LUCAS (low-cost, autonomous strike) platforms were included in the overnight assault. CENTCOM spokesperson Capt. Tim Hawkins confirmed LUCAS platforms' participation but declined to specify quantities, targets, or launch locations. CENTCOM's social media post noted that 'a large number of unmanned systems across various domains have been involved in the war effort.' LUCAS platforms have a reported range of 500 miles, wingspan of 8 feet, unit cost of $55,000 or less, and feature autonomy, anti-jamming, and swarming capabilities. The drones can be launched from catapults, rocket-assisted takeoff, and mobile ground vehicles. The overnight strikes preceded the ceasefire announcement by a few hours.
📍Iran — targets undisclosed; launched from undisclosed regional positions 🤖LUCAS autonomous swarming drones confirmed; 500-mile range, $55K/unit ⚡Part of final pre-ceasefire strike package (Apr 6–7 overnight)
Sources: DefenseScoop
8
Apr
Lebanon · Humanitarian Humanitarian · 1.2M Displaced; IDF Expands Buffer Zone
Lebanon Displacement Reaches 1.2 Million; IDF Issues Evacuation Orders Covering 14% of Territory as 'Operation Eternal Darkness' Expands
Israel's invasion of southern Lebanon and sustained aerial campaign has now displaced more than 1.2 million people — including 350,000 children — representing one-fifth of Lebanon's entire population, making it one of the world's fastest-growing displacement crises. Israeli evacuation orders now cover more than 1,470 sq km, or about 14% of Lebanese territory, per the Norwegian Refugee Council. ACLED recorded more than 1,840 Israeli attacks on Lebanon since March 2. Lebanon's Health Ministry reported 1,497 killed and more than 4,639 injured. More than 400 Hezbollah fighters have been killed (Reuters sources). At least 10 Lebanese Army soldiers have been killed. Three Indonesian UN peacekeepers were also killed in two separate incidents. More than 180,000 people have crossed from Lebanon into Syria. Israel's Defense Minister Katz declared the offensive similar in scope to Gaza and said displaced Lebanese cannot return south of the Litani River until Hezbollah is neutralized. On April 8 alone, following 'Operation Eternal Darkness,' Lebanon's Health Ministry reported dozens more killed and hundreds wounded.
📍Southern Lebanon, Beirut suburbs, Bekaa Valley — IDF occupation expanding 👥1.2M+ displaced (350,000 children); 180,000+ fled to Syria 💀1,497+ killed in Lebanon; 4,639+ injured (Lebanon Health Ministry, Apr 7)
Sources: Al Jazeera Amnesty International NBC News
7
Apr
US · Strike Offensive · Infrastructure
US Strikes Kharg Island Military Targets; IDF Bombs 8 Iranian Rail Bridges Ahead of Trump's Deadline
Hours before Trump's 8 p.m. ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, US forces conducted a second major strike on military targets at Kharg Island — Iran's key oil export hub through which roughly 90% of its crude oil flows — targeting military bunkers and storage sites while avoiding oil infrastructure. Simultaneously, the Israeli Air Force struck eight rail bridges and road sections across Iran including in Tehran, Karaj, Tabriz, Kashan, and Qom, with the IDF stating the crossings were used by the IRGC to transport weapons and military equipment. Iranian state media reported at least two fatalities from the railway strikes. The IAF also published footage showing more than 130 Iranian air defense systems destroyed since the start of the war. Additional IAF strikes hit dozens of Iranian regime infrastructure sites in a wide-scale wave completed by the afternoon, including a petrochemical compound in Shiraz described by the IDF as one of the last sites producing chemical components for ballistic missiles.
📍Kharg Island (US); Tehran, Karaj, Tabriz, Kashan, Qom, Shiraz (IDF) 💀At least 2 killed in rail strikes (Iranian state media); no oil infrastructure damage at Kharg 🛡️130+ Iranian air defense systems confirmed destroyed (IDF, cumulative)
Sources: Times of Israel CBS News Legal Insurrection
7
Apr
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Ceasefire · Pakistan-Brokered
US and Iran Agree to Two-Week Ceasefire; Strait of Hormuz to Reopen Under Pakistani Mediation
Less than two hours before Trump's apocalyptic 8 p.m. ET deadline — during which he had threatened to destroy Iran's 'whole civilization' if the Strait of Hormuz was not reopened — the United States and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir. Trump announced the deal on Truth Social, stating the US had 'already met and exceeded all Military objectives' and received a 10-point proposal from Iran he deemed a 'workable basis on which to negotiate.' Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi confirmed Tehran's acceptance, pledging that 'if attacks against Iran are halted, our Powerful Armed Forces will cease their defensive operations,' and that safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz would be permitted for two weeks under coordination with Iranian armed forces. Iran's Supreme National Security Council claimed the ceasefire as a victory, asserting it had forced the US to accept its 10-point plan. Both sides claimed victory. Oil prices plunged up to 16% on the announcement. Pakistan invited both delegations to Islamabad for talks on Friday, April 10. The White House later confirmed VP JD Vance, special envoy Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner would lead the US delegation.
📍Washington D.C. / Tehran / Islamabad (Pakistan mediation) 🕊️Two-week ceasefire; Hormuz to reopen; Islamabad peace talks begin Apr 10 ⚠️Iran's 10-point plan includes: Hormuz protocol, sanctions lifting, US base withdrawal, enrichment acceptance 🚨Netanyahu: ceasefire does NOT include Lebanon; Trump: Lebanon is 'a separate skirmish'
Sources: CNBC NPR NBC News Axios
8
Apr
IDF · Lebanon Offensive · Lebanon — Operation Eternal Darkness
IDF Kills 254 in Lebanon's Bloodiest Single Day; Strikes Central Beirut Without Warning Hours After Iran Ceasefire
In the deadliest single day of the Lebanon war, the Israel Defense Forces launched Operation Eternal Darkness on the afternoon of April 8 — just hours after the US-Iran ceasefire was announced — striking over 100 Hezbollah targets within 10 minutes across Beirut, southern Lebanon, and the Bekaa Valley. The Israeli military described it as the 'largest coordinated strike in the current war,' using approximately 50 fighter jets and dropping around 160 bombs. Strikes hit densely-populated residential areas in central Beirut including Corniche al-Mazraa, Barbour, Ain al-Mreisseh, and Burj Abi Haidar without prior warning, causing mass civilian panic. Lebanon's Civil Defence reported 254 killed and 1,165 wounded; in Beirut alone, 92 were killed and 742 injured. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz described the operation as 'the largest concentrated blow Hezbollah has suffered since the pagers operation.' An Israeli strike also hit near Hiram Hospital in Tyre, killing 4 and damaging the facility. Israeli media reported an assassination attempt on Hezbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem, though he was not believed to be at the target location. Netanyahu confirmed the ceasefire 'does not include Lebanon,' directly contradicting Pakistani PM Sharif, who said it did. Iran threatened to abandon the ceasefire over what it called Israeli violations, and Iranian media reported Tehran had temporarily paused Hormuz traffic in protest.
📍Beirut (Corniche al-Mazraa, Dahiyeh, Burj Abi Haidar), Sidon, Tyre, South Lebanon, Bekaa Valley 💀254 killed, 1,165 wounded (Civil Defence); Beirut: 92 killed, 742 wounded 🏥Hiram Hospital in Tyre damaged; ambulance struck in Tyre with fatalities (NNA) ⚠️Assassination attempt on Hezbollah SG Naim Qassem reportedly missed — Channel 12
Sources: UPI CBC News PBS NewsHour The National Al Jazeera
8
Apr
US · Diplomacy Diplomatic · Islamabad Talks Announced
Vance to Lead US Delegation to Islamabad Saturday; Highest-Level US-Iran Meeting Since 1979
White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed that President Trump is dispatching VP JD Vance, special envoy Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner to Islamabad, Pakistan for the first round of direct peace talks with Iran on Saturday, April 11 (local time). Axios described it as 'the highest-level meeting between the US and Iran since the 1979 Islamic revolution.' Vance, speaking from Budapest, described the ceasefire as a 'fragile truce' and urged Iran to negotiate in 'good faith,' warning that Trump is 'not one to mess around.' Iranian state media reported that Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf would lead Tehran's delegation, with FM Araghchi also participating. However, deep divisions remain: Trump publicly disavowed the 10-point plan Iran released publicly, saying the actual points being negotiated were different and would be discussed 'behind closed doors.' Iran insisted 'acceptance of enrichment' was a core demand; the US insisted there would be 'no enrichment of uranium.' Iran also alleged three of the 10 clauses had already been violated — citing continued strikes in Lebanon — and threatened to pull out of talks if Israeli attacks continued. Despite the fragility, China's involvement in securing the agreement was confirmed by the White House.
📍Islamabad, Pakistan (Saturday, Apr 11) 🤝US delegation: Vance, Witkoff, Kushner; Iran: Ghalibaf (Speaker), Araghchi (FM) ⚠️Iran claims 3/10 ceasefire clauses violated on Day 1; threatens to walk out over Lebanon strikes 🇨🇳White House confirms top-level US-China conversations played a role in securing ceasefire
Sources: CNN Axios Bloomberg NBC News
8
Apr
Iran · Ceasefire Strain Diplomatic · Ceasefire Violations
Iran Accuses US of Ceasefire Violations; IRGC Halts Hormuz Transit Over Lebanon Strikes
Hours after the US-Iran two-week ceasefire took effect on April 8, Iran's IRGC announced it was stopping shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, citing Israeli strikes in Lebanon as a violation of the agreement. Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf accused the US of breaching three tenets of the 10-point peace plan, including the continuation of Israeli strikes in Lebanon, an 'intruding drone' entering Iranian airspace, and denial of Iran's right to nuclear enrichment. Gulf states including Kuwait (28 drone attacks by 16:23 GMT) and the UAE (35 drone attacks) continued to absorb Iranian strikes throughout the day even as the ceasefire nominally held for Iran itself. Iranian-allied groups in Iraq also hit a diplomatic support centre at Baghdad International Airport, prompting a US embassy warning to citizens in the region. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi had confirmed Tehran's agreement to the ceasefire, stating that if attacks against Iran were halted, Iran's armed forces would cease defensive operations — but the Lebanon dispute immediately threatened to unravel the fragile deal.
📍Strait of Hormuz / Baghdad International Airport / Gulf states ⚠️Ceasefire declared but disputed — Hormuz transit halted by IRGC over Lebanon dispute
Sources: CNN The Hill Wikipedia – 2026 Iran war ceasefire
8
Apr
Israel · Operation Eternal Darkness Offensive · Largest Lebanon Strike
IDF Launches Largest-Ever Lebanon Strike: 50 Jets, 100 Targets in 10 Minutes; Hezbollah Intelligence Chief Killed
Hours after the US-Iran ceasefire was announced on April 8, the Israeli Air Force launched what it described as its largest coordinated strike against Hezbollah since the current war began. Fifty IAF fighter jets dropped approximately 160 bombs on over 100 targets within 10 minutes, hitting Hezbollah command centres, intelligence headquarters, the Radwan Force, and Hezbollah's aerial and naval unit infrastructure across Beirut, southern Lebanon, and the Bekaa Valley. Lebanon's Civil Defence confirmed 254 people were killed and 1,165 wounded — including 92 killed and 742 injured in Beirut alone. In the strikes, the IDF announced the killing of Hussein Makled, head of Hezbollah's intelligence headquarters, and Ali Yusuf Harshi, the personal secretary and nephew of Hezbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem. Defence Minister Katz described it as the 'largest concentrated blow Hezbollah has suffered since the pager operation.' The UN Human Rights Chief Volker Türk condemned the attacks, calling the scale of killing 'nothing short of horrific.' Netanyahu confirmed the ceasefire does not include Lebanon, stating Israel had its 'finger on the trigger' and would continue striking Hezbollah.
📍Beirut (central, southern suburbs), southern Lebanon, Bekaa Valley 💀254 killed, 1,165 wounded (Lebanon Civil Defence, Apr 8) — 92 killed in Beirut alone
Sources: Al Jazeera Times of Israel Haaretz
9
Apr
US · Diplomacy Diplomatic · Islamabad Talks
Vance, Witkoff & Kushner Depart for Islamabad; First Direct US-Iran Talks Since 1979 Begin Saturday
White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed that Vice President JD Vance will lead the US delegation to Islamabad, Pakistan, joined by Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. The first round of direct US-Iran talks is set for Saturday morning local time (April 11), under Pakistani mediation brokered by PM Shehbaz Sharif, who invited both delegations 'to further negotiate for a conclusive agreement to settle all disputes.' Pakistani authorities deployed hundreds of additional police and paramilitary forces to seal off the Red Zone in Islamabad ahead of the talks. Vance described the ceasefire as a 'fragile truce' and stated that if Iran does not follow through on promises to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the ceasefire will end. He also clarified that the US 'never made that promise' that Lebanon would be included in the ceasefire, calling the dispute a 'legitimate misunderstanding.' Iran's National Security Council confirmed participation, saying talks would be based on its 10-point proposal. White House confirmed Chinese involvement in truce negotiations was a factor. Trump stated that all US ships, aircraft, and military personnel would remain 'in place, in and around Iran' until a full agreement is reached.
📍Islamabad, Pakistan — Red Zone secured by Pakistani paramilitary forces 🕊️First direct US-Iran meeting since 1979; Vance leads delegation with Witkoff and Kushner
Sources: Bloomberg CBS News NPR
9
Apr
Iran · Proxy Action Offensive · Hezbollah Strikes North Israel
Hezbollah Fires Missiles at Northern Israel in Early Hours of April 9; IDF Kills Naim Qassem's Nephew in Beirut
In the early hours of April 9, 2026, Hezbollah announced it launched a missile attack against northern Israel, signalling it had not declared adherence to the ceasefire given Israel's continued strikes in Lebanon. Simultaneously, Israeli strikes continued across Lebanon overnight, with the IDF announcing the killing of Ali Yusuf Harshi, the personal secretary and nephew of Hezbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem, in a strike on Beirut. The IDF also stated that Hezbollah had left its traditional stronghold in Beirut's Dahiyeh and was repositioning toward northern Beirut and mixed civilian areas. Palestinian Islamic Jihad's Lebanon commander Adham Adnan al-Othman was also reported killed in Beirut's southern suburbs. Lebanon's health ministry cumulative toll stood at 1,784 killed and 5,977 wounded as of April 8.
📍Northern Israel (Hezbollah missile launch); Beirut (IDF strike on Harshi) 💀Ali Yusuf Harshi (Qassem's nephew/secretary) killed; Adham Adnan al-Othman (PIJ Lebanon cmdr) killed
Sources: Wikipedia – 2026 Lebanon war Jerusalem Post Wikipedia – Casualties of 2026 Iran war
6
Apr
US–Israel · Strike Offensive · University Strike
US–Israel Strike Sharif University of Technology, Tehran; 34 Killed Across Iran
On April 6, a joint US-Israeli airstrike struck the Sharif University of Technology in Tehran — Iran's foremost engineering and science institution, often likened to MIT — destroying the IT Centre, laboratories, a campus mosque, and a gas substation. Iranian Vice President Mohammad Reza Aref condemned the use of a bunker-buster bomb, calling it 'a symbol of Trump's madness.' Iran's Ministry of Science reported that over 30 universities had been struck since Feb. 28, with five professors and more than 60 students killed across all strikes. Simultaneously, strikes across Tehran province's Baharestan County killed at least 13 people, and a total of 34 people — including six children under 10 — were killed in that day's wave of attacks across Iran.
📍Sharif University, Tehran; Baharestan County, Tehran Province; Qom; multiple cities 💀34 killed (incl. 6 children under 10) across Iran in the Apr 6 wave; no casualties inside the university campus (empty due to online schooling)
Sources: Al Jazeera CBS News
7
Apr
US–Israel · Strike Offensive · Infrastructure
IAF Bombs 8 Iranian Rail Bridges; CENTCOM Launches LUCAS Drone Wave; IDF Strikes 'Dozens' of Sites Across Iran
On April 7, the Israeli Air Force bombed eight rail bridges and road sections across Iran — in Tehran, Karaj, Tabriz, Kashan, and Qom — that the IDF said were used to transport IRGC weapons and military equipment. CENTCOM separately confirmed the deployment of LUCAS one-way attack drones (range: 500 miles, unit cost ~$55,000) in overnight strikes, with hundreds of unmanned systems now involved across air, sea, and ground domains. The IAF announced it had completed an 'extensive' wave hitting dozens of Iranian infrastructure sites, and published footage showing the destruction of more than 130 Iranian air defence systems since the war began. Strikes on the Sharif University area continued, and Iran's VP condemned the university attack as bunker-buster use. Iran fired missiles toward southern Israel overnight and Hezbollah attacked northern Israel with rockets, injuring two.
📍Tehran, Karaj, Tabriz, Kashan, Qom (rail); multiple provinces across Iran 💀25+ killed across Iran overnight (incl. 6 children); 130+ Iranian air defence systems destroyed since Feb 28
Sources: Times of Israel DefenseScoop
7
Apr
Diplomacy · Ceasefire Diplomatic · Pakistan Brokered Ceasefire
US–Iran Two-Week Ceasefire Announced; Pakistan Brokers Deal; Hormuz to Reopen
On April 7 (April 8 local time in the Middle East), President Trump announced on Truth Social that the US had agreed to a 'double-sided ceasefire' with Iran for two weeks, less than two hours before his 8 p.m. ET deadline for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face destruction of all Iranian power plants and bridges. Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif mediated the deal, announcing it included 'an immediate ceasefire everywhere including Lebanon and elsewhere.' Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi confirmed Tehran's acceptance and stated that safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz would be possible for the two-week period. Iran presented a 10-point counterproposal as a framework for negotiations; the White House said Trump discarded the initial version and accepted a revised, more condensed plan as 'a workable basis on which to negotiate.' Trump confirmed no uranium enrichment would be permitted under any final deal. Negotiations are scheduled to begin Saturday April 11 in Islamabad, led by VP JD Vance, Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner on the US side, and Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf and FM Araghchi for Iran.
📍Washington D.C. / Islamabad, Pakistan (mediation) 🕊️Two-week ceasefire effective April 8 local time; Islamabad talks set for Saturday April 11
Sources: NPR CNN Al Jazeera
8
Apr
Iran · Ceasefire Violation Claim Escalation · Hormuz Closure / Ceasefire Dispute
Iran Re-Closes Strait of Hormuz After Israel's 'Operation Eternal Darkness' Lebanon Mega-Strike; Iran Accuses US of Three Ceasefire Violations
Hours after the ceasefire took effect on April 8, Israel launched 'Operation Eternal Darkness' — its largest coordinated strike on Lebanon since the war began — hitting 100+ Hezbollah targets with 50 jets dropping 160 bombs across Beirut, the Bekaa Valley and southern Lebanon in just 10 minutes, killing at least 303 people and wounding 1,150 (Lebanon's Health Ministry). Iran's Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf declared three ceasefire violations by the US: continued Israeli strikes on Lebanon, a drone entering Iranian airspace, and denial of Iran's right to uranium enrichment. Iranian state media (IRGC-linked Fars) reported Tehran had halted all tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz in response. The IRGC published an alternative route map citing 'possible sea mines' in the main traffic zone. The White House called the closure 'completely unacceptable' and denied the strait had shut. By April 9, there was no sign the Hormuz blockade had lifted, with only a handful of Iran-linked ships transiting. Oil futures climbed back above $100/barrel. Iran warned of a 'regretful response' if Israeli attacks on Lebanon continued. Pakistani sources said Iran was close to abandoning the ceasefire overnight April 8–9 but was talked back by Pakistani diplomats. Lebanon declared a national day of mourning on April 9.
📍Strait of Hormuz; Beirut, Bekaa Valley, southern Lebanon (Israeli strikes); Tehran (diplomatic) 💀303 killed / 1,150 wounded in Lebanon on Apr 8 alone (Lebanese Health Ministry) — deadliest single day of the Lebanon war; cumulative Lebanon toll: 1,888+ killed / 6,092+ wounded
Sources: CNN ABC News CBS News Wikipedia
9
Apr
Israel · Lebanon Offensive / Diplomatic · Lebanon
IDF Kills Naim Qassem's Aide Ali Yusuf Harshi; Israel Continues Lebanon Strikes; Netanyahu Opens Direct Lebanon Talks; Hezbollah Fires Rockets at North
On April 9, the IDF announced it had killed Ali Yusuf Harshi — Hezbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem's personal secretary and nephew — in strikes on Beirut the previous night, part of 'Operation Eternal Darkness.' The IDF issued new forced evacuation orders for southern Beirut suburbs and struck a bridge in southern Lebanon. Hezbollah, which had briefly halted fire after the Iran ceasefire announcement, resumed rocket attacks in the early hours of April 9, targeting Kiryat Shmona, Taibe, and Manara in northern Israel; the Israeli army said approximately 30 rockets were fired with no casualties reported. Prime Minister Netanyahu, at President Trump's request, announced Israel would open direct negotiations with Lebanon 'as soon as possible,' focused on disarming Hezbollah and establishing peaceful relations. Lebanon's PM Nawaf Salam instructed security forces to monopolise weapons in Beirut in state hands — a clear message to Hezbollah — while Lebanon's President Aoun described the negotiations as receiving a 'positive response' internationally. Lebanon responded that there would be 'no negotiations under fire,' as Israeli strikes continued on April 9.
📍Beirut (strike/talks); Kiryat Shmona, Taibe, Manara, northern Israel (Hezbollah rockets); southern Lebanon (IDF ground ops) 💀Ali Yusuf Harshi (Qassem's secretary/nephew) killed; ~30 rockets fired at northern Israel — no Israeli casualties reported; 1,888+ total Lebanon war dead
Sources: CNN CBS News Al Jazeera Wikipedia – 2026 Lebanon war
9
Apr
Diplomacy · Islamabad Talks Diplomatic · Islamabad Negotiations
Iranian Delegation Arrives in Islamabad; Vance Returns to Washington; Hormuz Still Effectively Closed; Trump 'Very Optimistic'
On April 9, Iran's ambassador to Pakistan confirmed the Iranian delegation — led by Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf and FM Araghchi — would arrive in Islamabad for peace talks. The Red Zone was sealed and key entry points in Islamabad closed by Pakistani security forces ahead of the talks. VP JD Vance, who had been in Budapest, returned to Washington on an overnight flight to prepare for the Saturday negotiations, saying Lebanese disagreements were a 'misunderstanding' and warning Iran that pulling out of talks 'would be dumb.' Trump told NBC he was 'very optimistic' about a peace deal from the Islamabad talks, saying Iran's leaders were 'much more reasonable' in private. Despite the ceasefire, the Strait of Hormuz remained effectively closed as of April 9, with the IRGC restricting traffic and publishing a mine-warning alternative route map; US oil futures climbed back above $100/barrel. Pakistan cautioned that Iranian restraint overnight was only preserved through active Pakistani diplomacy. The Intercept reported DCAS data showing 372 US troops wounded in action — 23% higher than CENTCOM's public figures — with both counts updated April 8.
📍Islamabad, Pakistan (talks venue); Washington D.C. (Vance return); Strait of Hormuz (closed) 📊DCAS: 372 US WIA (updated Apr 8) vs. CENTCOM public figure of 303+; Hormuz: only ~4 dry-cargo ships crossed Day 1 of ceasefire
Sources: Al Jazeera CNN The Intercept Bloomberg
41
Apr
Iran · Ceasefire Violation Offensive · Post-Ceasefire Missile & Drone Wave
IRGC Launches Post-Ceasefire Barrage on UAE & Kuwait Hours After Truce Announced
Hours after the US-Iran two-week ceasefire took effect on April 8, Iranian IRGC forces continued firing. Kuwait reported an "extensive wave" of at least 28 drones targeting oil facilities, power plants, and water desalination plants from 08:00 local time, causing "severe material damage." The UAE intercepted 17 ballistic missiles and 35 drones and reported a gas processing facility ablaze in Abu Dhabi. Qatar reported seven ballistic missiles and drones, all intercepted. Saudi Arabia activated civil defense alerts and intercepted five ballistic missiles targeting its eastern region. Iran framed the strikes as retaliation for a reported attack on its Lavan Island oil refinery, which it attributed to US or Israeli forces. The IRGC's 31 autonomous provincial commands were assessed as still executing pre-authorized operational plans at the time of the ceasefire announcement.
📍UAE, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain — Gulf-wide 💀3 lightly wounded in UAE; 2 minor injuries in Bahrain; 4 moderate injuries in Qatar incl. 1 child; Kuwait oil/power infrastructure severely damaged ⚠️IRGC Aerospace Force: 95 attack waves logged through April 8 ceasefire without interruption
Sources: Arab News Times of Israel NBC News House of Saud / IRGC Analysis
41
Apr
Israel · Operation Eternal Darkness Offensive · Largest Lebanon Strike of the War
IDF Launches 'Operation Eternal Darkness' — Largest Coordinated Strike on Lebanon; 254 Killed
On April 8, 2026 — within hours of the US-Iran ceasefire announcement — the Israeli Air Force launched what it called its largest coordinated strike on Lebanon since the start of Operation Roaring Lion, dubbed 'Operation Eternal Darkness.' Fifty IAF fighter jets dropped approximately 160 munitions on more than 100 Hezbollah targets across Beirut, the Bekaa Valley, and southern Lebanon in a 10-minute window. Strikes hit central Beirut without prior warning, including at least five neighborhoods in coastal and commercial areas during rush hour. Lebanese civil defense reported 254 killed and 1,165 wounded; Lebanon's Health Ministry put the toll at 203+ killed, adding to the 1,500+ already dead in the Lebanon war. The IDF said it killed Hezbollah intelligence chief Hussein Makled, along with over 40 militants. An Israeli airstrike hit a cemetery in Shmestar village during a funeral, killing at least 10 mourners. Israel also struck an ambulance near Tyre and caused extensive damage to Hiram Hospital. Lebanese authorities declared April 9 a national day of mourning. Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf declared the ceasefire framework violated, calling negotiations 'unreasonable.' Netanyahu stated the ceasefire does not cover Lebanon and Israel would continue strikes.
📍Beirut (Corniche al-Mazraa, Haret Hreik, southern suburbs), Bekaa Valley (Shmestar), Sidon, Tyre — Lebanon 💀254 killed (civil defense), 1,165+ wounded; IDF claims 40+ Hezbollah militants killed ✈️50 IAF fighter jets, ~160 munitions, 100+ targets struck in ~10 minutes
Sources: Al Jazeera NBC News Wikipedia — Apr 8 Lebanon Attacks Times of Israel UN News
42
Apr
Diplomacy · Islamabad Talks Diplomatic · Highest-Level US-Iran Talks Since 1979
US & Iranian Delegations Arrive in Islamabad for Historic Peace Talks; Ceasefire Strains Over Lebanon
The US and Iranian delegations converged on Islamabad, Pakistan on April 10, 2026, for what Axios described as the highest-level meeting between Washington and Tehran since the 1979 Islamic Revolution. The American delegation is led by Vice President JD Vance — who returned to Washington from Budapest on April 9 before departing for Pakistan — joined by special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. The Iranian delegation is led by Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, a former IRGC commander, alongside Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi. Pakistan secured Islamabad's Serena Hotel for the talks, sealing off the city's Red Zone and deploying hundreds of paramilitary forces. However, the framework entered talks under serious strain: Iran's Ghalibaf claimed three clauses of the 10-point ceasefire framework had been violated before talks began — Israel's continued strikes on Lebanon, a drone entering Iranian airspace, and Trump's rejection of Iran's right to uranium enrichment. Vance dismissed these concerns as a 'misunderstanding,' insisting Lebanon was never part of the ceasefire and warning Iran against letting negotiations fail. The ceasefire expires April 22, 2026, creating a hard deadline. Trump told NBC he was 'very optimistic' about a peace deal, saying Iran's leaders were 'much more reasonable' in private. Iran's Araghchi warned Washington it must choose between ceasefire or continued war-by-proxy through Israel.
📍Islamabad, Pakistan — Serena Hotel (Red Zone sealed) 🤝US: Vance (lead), Witkoff, Kushner · Iran: Ghalibaf (lead), Araghchi · Mediator: Pakistan PM Sharif & Army Chief Munir ⏳Ceasefire deadline: April 22, 2026 · Key dispute: Lebanon scope, uranium enrichment, Hormuz reopening
Sources: Al Jazeera Axios Bloomberg CNN The National Life News Agency
42
Apr
Iran · Hormuz & Ceasefire Crisis Escalation · Strait of Hormuz Re-Closes; Trump Warns Iran
Iran Re-Closes Hormuz After Israeli Lebanon Strikes; Trump Warns Iran 'Not Abiding by Terms'; Talks Still On
Following Israel's mass strikes on Lebanon (Operation Eternal Darkness) on April 8, Iranian state media reported the Strait of Hormuz closed to 99% of vessels. The IRGC warned it had 'no trust' in US promises and its 'finger is on the trigger.' Two ships — the NJ Earth and Daytona Beach — had briefly transited the strait just after the ceasefire, but traffic effectively halted again. On April 9, Trump posted on Truth Social that Iran was doing a 'very poor job' of allowing oil through Hormuz, stating 'That is not the agreement we have!' Vance reiterated that if Iran failed to reopen the strait, the ceasefire would end. Kuwait condemned fresh IRGC drone attacks on its vital oil installations on April 9, calling them a violation of its sovereignty. The IRGC also shot down a US long-range drone over Lar in Fars Province, declaring any aircraft entering Iranian airspace — even without a military mission — would be met with 'a decisive response.' Israel continued strikes on Lebanon on April 9, including on southern Beirut, with the IDF targeting approximately 10 Hezbollah rocket launchers. Lebanon's death toll from the April 8 strikes rose to 303 according to the Lebanese Health Ministry. Netanyahu announced Israel would open direct talks with Lebanon on Hezbollah disarmament and a possible peace deal.
📍Strait of Hormuz; Lar, Fars Province (IRGC drone shootdown); Kuwait oil infrastructure; southern Beirut ⚠️Lebanon Apr 8 strike death toll revised to 303 (Lebanese Health Ministry, Apr 9) 🛢️Hormuz re-effectively-closed; Saudi Arabia's East-West pipeline pumping station struck by drone (FT)
Sources: Haaretz Al Jazeera CBS News The War Zone Wikipedia — 2026 Iran War Ceasefire

Confirmed leadership killed
Ali Khamenei — Supreme Leader of Iran (86). Killed at his Tehran compound by Israeli precision strike. Daughter, son-in-law, grandson also killed. NPR
Ali Shamkhani — Secretary, Supreme National Security Council. Confirmed killed. CBS
Aziz Nasirzadeh — Defense Minister, oversaw missile industry and SPND nuclear/CBW research. Fox News
Hossein Jabal Amelian — SPND Director (Organization of Defensive Innovation and Research — nuclear/biological/chemical weapons). Wikipedia
Abdolrahim Mousavi — Chief of Staff of Iranian Armed Forces. Confirmed killed 1 March by Iranian state media. State funeral held in Tehran, March 11, 2026. Al Jazeera
Reza Mozaffari-Nia · Mohammad Shirazi · Salah Asadi — Former SPND head, Khamenei's military office chief, intelligence chief. Wikipedia
Hussein Makled — Head of Hezbollah's Intelligence Headquarters, Beirut. Killed in a precise IDF airstrike on Beirut's southern suburbs, confirmed by the IDF on March 12, 2026. (CBS News / Times of Israel / Jerusalem Post)
Mohammad Pakpour — IRGC Commander-in-Chief. Confirmed killed in opening strikes, per IDF's official list of regime officials killed Feb 28–Mar 1. State funeral in Tehran, March 11, 2026. IDF identified him as lead planner of the 'destruction of Israel' plan. Wikipedia
Khamenei's wife — Confirmed by Iranian sources to have died of injuries sustained in the strike on Khamenei's compound. Wikipedia
Mojtaba Khamenei (elected, injured — not killed) — Son of Ali Khamenei, reportedly elected 3rd Supreme Leader of Iran by Assembly of Experts under IRGC pressure on March 4. Not yet publicly announced by Iranian state media. IDF confirmed his underground bunker beneath Tehran compound was dismantled, March 7 (NBC/IDF). Wounded in opening strikes per Israeli sources; Defense Secretary Hegseth confirmed injury March 13. Israel has warned he is 'an unequivocal target for elimination.' Iran International
Rahman Mokaddam — Head of IRGC Special Operations Unit & planner of Trump assassination plot. Confirmed killed Mar 4 per IDF.
Zaid Ali Jamaa — Hezbollah fire and artillery commander, southern Lebanon. Killed by IDF (Critical Threats / CTP-ISW Mar 5).
Wasim Attallah Ali — Hamas commander for training/exercises in Lebanon. Killed by IDF Navy strike in Tripoli, Mar 5.
Abolqasem Babaian — Head of the Military Office of the Supreme Leader. Killed in an IDF strike, March 7, 2026, per Reuters/Times of Israel.
Majid Hassini — IRGC Quds Force Lebanon Corps Senior Financier (proxy funds to Hezbollah/Hamas). Killed in IDF Beirut hotel strike, March 8, 2026.
Ali Reza Bi-Azar — Chief of Intelligence, IRGC Quds Force Lebanon Corps. Killed in IDF Beirut hotel strike, March 8, 2026.
Ahmad Rasouli — Chief of Intelligence, IRGC Quds Force Palestine Corps. Killed in IDF Beirut hotel strike, March 8, 2026.
Hossein Ahmadlou — Intelligence Operative, IRGC Quds Force Lebanon Corps. Killed in IDF Beirut hotel strike, March 8, 2026.
Abu Muhammad Ali — Hezbollah’s representative in the IRGC Quds Force Palestine Corps. Killed in IDF Beirut hotel strike, March 8, 2026.
Daoud Alizadeh — Acting Commander, IRGC Quds Force Lebanon Corps. Killed in an Israeli airstrike in Tehran on March 3, 2026, per IDF announcement. Described by the IDF as 'the highest-ranking Iranian commander responsible for Lebanon.' Times of Israel
Ali Mohammad Naeini — IRGC Spokesperson, Brigadier General (age 68). Killed in a targeted IDF strike, March 20, 2026. Central figure in IRGC public communications and regime propaganda; repeatedly issued direct threats against US and Israeli targets. Hours before his death he publicly insisted Iran was still producing missiles at "a perfect score." Business Today
Mehdi Ghoreyshi — Commander, IRGC Aerospace Force (Isfahan). Killed in an IDF strike, March 20, 2026. The Aerospace Force is the principal operator of Iran's missile and drone arsenals, now assessed as functionally destroyed after three weeks of coalition strikes. Business Today
Esmail Ahmadi-Moghaddam — Head, Basij Intelligence Division (IRGC). Killed in an IDF precision strike on Basij leadership in central Tehran, March 20, 2026. Key figure in suppressing Iranian protests and running the regime's internal security apparatus. Business Today
Akbar Ghaffari — Iranian Deputy Intelligence Minister. Killed in US-Israeli strikes during the ongoing campaign. Confirmed by Wikipedia / 2026 Iran War sources.
Adham Adnan al-Othman — Head, Palestinian Islamic Jihad Al-Quds Brigades in Lebanon. Killed in Israeli strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs, confirmed March 12, 2026. (2026 Lebanon War Wikipedia)
Abu Dharr Mohammadi — IRGC Missile Unit Operations Commander (Hezbollah coordination). Killed in IDF strike in Beirut, March 13, 2026. Central figure rehabilitating Hezbollah's missile program post-Operation Northern Arrows.
Ali Muslim Tabaja — Senior Commander, Imam Hossein Division (IRGC/Hezbollah). Killed in IDF strike in Lebanon, March 13, 2026. Held multiple military roles within both Hezbollah and the IRGC Quds Force.
Abu Ali Riyan — Radwan Force Southern Lebanon Unit Commander, Hezbollah. Killed in IDF strike, March 13, 2026. Coordinated Radwan Force operations, recruitment, and weapons supply chain in southern Lebanon.
Abu Ali al-Askari — Security Commander, Kata'ib Hezbollah (Iraq). Killed in US/Israeli airstrike on Baghdad, March 14, 2026; confirmed publicly March 17. Key figure coordinating Iran-backed militia attacks on US forces in Iraq.
Abdullah Jalali Nasab — Brigadier General, Senior IRGC Military Figure. Confirmed killed in Israeli airstrike, reported by Al Jazeera March 15, 2026.
Ibrahim Muhammad Ghazali — Anti-Tank Weapons Commander, Hezbollah Badr Unit. Killed in IDF strike in southern Lebanon, confirmed by IDF March 16, 2026. Brother of Ayman Ghazali (Temple Israel synagogue shooter, Michigan); flagged by U.S. DHS. (IDF / Alma Research)
Ali Larijani — Secretary, Supreme National Security Council; Iran's de facto leader post-Khamenei. Killed in IDF precision strike on Tehran, overnight March 16–17, 2026. Died alongside son Morteza, deputy Alireza Bayat, and bodyguards. Confirmed by Iran's SNSC. Highest-ranking official killed since Khamenei.
Morteza Larijani — Son of Ali Larijani and SNSC aide. Killed in the same IDF strike as his father, Tehran, overnight March 16–17, 2026. Confirmed by Iran's SNSC.
Gholamreza Soleimani — Commander, IRGC Basij Paramilitary Force. Killed in IDF strike on a makeshift command area in Tehran, overnight March 16–17, 2026. Died alongside deputy Seyyed Karishi. Confirmed by IRGC via Sepah News.
Esmaeil Khatib — Minister of Intelligence, Iran. Killed in Israeli airstrike on Tehran, overnight March 17–18, 2026. Confirmed by President Pezeshkian and Israeli Defense Minister Katz. In post since 2021; oversaw internal repression and external ops against US/Israeli targets. Third senior official killed within 24 hours (after Larijani and Soleimani). Katz: "responsible for the regime's internal apparatus of repression and assassination."
Strategic context
Stated US objectives (Trump): (1) Prevent Iran acquiring nuclear weapon. (2) Destroy missile arsenal and production. (3) Degrade proxy networks. (4) Annihilate Iranian navy. (5) Regime change from within. CSIS
Scale vs June 2025: Operation Epic Fury targets leadership, full military/naval infrastructure, and government ministries — far beyond Operation Midnight Hammer's limited nuclear focus. CSIS calls it "an initial salvo of a longer conflict." CSIS
New US weapons: LUCAS (Low-cost Unmanned Combat Attack System) — reverse-engineered Shahed-136 drone, first combat use. Also HIMARS land-based rockets, Tomahawks from warships, B-2 bombers, Task Force Scorpion Strike drones. FlightGlobal
Risk: Nuclear material: Iran still holds ~400 kg of 60%-enriched uranium. CSIS warns exact location unknown after strikes. Collapse of Atomic Energy Organisation could scatter nuclear scientists — proliferation risk to non-state actors. CSIS
Succession crisis: CFR assesses that even if Khamenei dies, likely successors are hardline IRGC figures who control the terror and missile networks. CFR
Sources: Wikipedia (2026 Israeli–United States strikes on Iran), Al Jazeera, CNN, PBS News, USNI News, CSIS, CFR, NPR, CBS News, Times of Israel, Aviation Week, FlightGlobal, ICT SITREP, FDD Action, GlobalSecurity.org. All data reflects open-source reporting as of April 10, 2026 · 01:14 UTC. Figures are preliminary and subject to revision. This tab is updated daily.
